  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:\n\n## Kubernetes APIServer Library - Core Interfaces, Structs, and Filter Chain Architecture\n\nBased on my exploration of `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver`, here's a comprehensive analysis of the key components:\n\n---\n\n### **1. STORAGE INTERFACES** \n*File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go`*\n\n#### Core Interface: `Interface`\nThe primary storage interface that provides all database operations:\n- **Create** - Add object at key with optional TTL\n- **Delete** - Remove object at key with preconditions\n- **Get** - Retrieve object at key\n- **GetList** - Retrieve multiple objects with filtering\n- **GuaranteedUpdate** - Update with retry logic on conflicts\n- **Watch** - Stream changes to objects\n- **Stats** - Get storage statistics\n- **ReadinessCheck** - Verify storage is ready\n- **RequestWatchProgress** - Monitor watch stream progress\n- **GetCurrentResourceVersion** - Fetch current etcd revision\n- **CompactRevision** - Get latest compaction state\n\n#### Supporting Interfaces/Types:\n```\nVersioner - Manages resource versions (UpdateObject, UpdateList, PrepareObjectForStorage, ObjectResourceVersion, ParseResourceVersion)\nResponseMeta - Contains TTL and ResourceVersion from storage\nUpdateFunc - Function signature for GuaranteedUpdate mutations\nValidateObjectFunc - Pre-operation validation hook\nPreconditions - UID/ResourceVersion checks before operations\nGetOptions / ListOptions / DeleteOptions - Request configuration\nStats - Storage statistics (ObjectCount, EstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes)\n```\n\n---\n\n### **2. AUTHENTICATION INTERFACES**\n*File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator/interfaces.go`*\n\n#### Core Interfaces:\n```go\nToken interface {\n    AuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error)\n}\n\nRequest interface {\n    AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error)\n}\n```\n\n#### Response Struct:\n```go\ntype Response struct {\n    Audiences Audiences      // Set of audiences the token was validated against\n    User      user.Info      // Authenticated user information\n}\n```\n\n#### Adapter Functions:\n- `TokenFunc` - Function adapter implementing Token interface\n- `RequestFunc` - Function adapter implementing Request interface\n\n---\n\n### **3. AUTHORIZATION INTERFACES**\n*File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer/interfaces.go`*\n\n#### Core Interfaces:\n```go\nAttributes interface {\n    GetUser() user.Info\n    GetVerb() string                          // get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete, etc.\n    IsReadOnly() bool\n    GetNamespace() string\n    GetResource() string                      // e.g., \"pods\"\n    GetSubresource() string                   // e.g., \"logs\" in \"pods/logs\"\n    GetName() string\n    GetAPIGroup() string                      // e.g., \"apps\"\n    GetAPIVersion() string                    // e.g., \"v1\"\n    IsResourceRequest() bool                  // API resource vs non-resource (e.g., /healthz)\n    GetPath() string                          // Full request path\n    GetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error)\n    GetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error)\n}\n\nAuthorizer interface {\n    Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error)\n}\n\nRuleResolver interface {\n    RulesFor(ctx context.Context, user user.Info, namespace string) \n        ([]ResourceRuleInfo, []NonResourceRuleInfo, bool, error)\n}\n```\n\n#### AttributesRecord Implementation:\nComplete implementation of Attributes interface with fields for all attribute types.\n\n#### Decision Constants:\n```go\nDecisionDeny        // Explicitly deny access\nDecisionAllow       // Explicitly allow access\nDecisionNoOpinion   // Authorizer has no opinion (next in chain)\n```\n\n---\n\n### **4. AUDIT INTERFACES**\n*File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/types.go`*\n\n#### Core Interfaces:\n```go\nSink interface {\n    ProcessEvents(events ...*auditinternal.Event) bool\n}\n\nBackend interface {\n    Sink\n    Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error       // Initialize backend\n    Shutdown()                               // Graceful shutdown\n    String() string                          // Return backend name\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### **5. HTTP HANDLER CHAIN ARCHITECTURE**\n\n#### `APIServerHandler` Struct\n*File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go`*\n\n```go\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\n    FullHandlerChain http.Handler           // Complete filter chain (served directly)\n    GoRestfulContainer *restful.Container   // REST API handlers\n    NonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux    // Non-REST paths (healthz, etc.)\n    Director http.Handler                   // Routes between GoRestful and NonRestful\n}\n```\n\nThe Director intelligently routes requests:\n- To GoRestful if path matches registered web services\n- To NonGoRestful for health checks, discovery, and custom handlers\n\n#### `Config` Struct\n*File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go`*\n\nKey fields for filter chain configuration:\n```go\ntype Config struct {\n    SecureServing         *SecureServingInfo\n    Authentication        AuthenticationInfo\n    Authorization         AuthorizationInfo\n    BuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) http.Handler\n    AuditBackend          audit.Backend\n    AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\n    FlowControl           utilflowcontrol.Interface\n    AdmissionControl      admission.Interface\n    LoopbackClientConfig  *restclient.Config\n    EgressSelector        *egressselector.EgressSelector\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### **6. FILTER CHAIN - REQUEST FLOW**\n\nThe `BuildHandlerChainFunc` is the key customization point. The standard chain flows:\n\n```\nFullHandlerChain\n    \u2193\nDirector (route to GoRestful or NonGoRestful)\n    \u2193\nAPI Handlers (GoRestful or NonGoRestful)\n```\n\nWithin the chain (applied in typical order):\n\n#### Server-Level Filters (`/server/filters/`):\n1. **WithPanicRecovery** - Catches panics, logs stack traces\n2. **WithHTTPLogging** - HTTP request/response logging\n3. **WithCORS** - Cross-Origin Resource Sharing\n4. **WithHSTS** - HTTP Strict Transport Security\n5. **WithWatchTermination** - Graceful watch stream termination\n6. **WithRetryAfter** - Retry-After header on shutdown\n7. **WithGoaway** - HTTP/2 GOAWAY frames\n8. **WithLongRunning** - Identify long-running requests (watch, logs)\n9. **WithMaxInFlight** / **WithPriorityAndFairness** - Request queuing and prioritization\n10. **WithContentType** - Content type validation\n\n#### Endpoint-Level Filters (`/endpoints/filters/`):\n1. **WithRequestInfo** - Extract request metadata (verb, resource, namespace, etc.) \u2192 store in context\n2. **WithAuthentication** - Validate credentials, extract user info \u2192 store in context\n3. **WithAuthn Audit** - Record authentication events\n4. **WithAudit** - Record audit events (multiple stages: RequestReceived, ResponseStarted, ResponseComplete, Panic)\n5. **WithAuthorization** - Check RBAC/webhook authorization decisions\n6. **WithImpersonation** - Handle user/group/extra impersonation\n7. **WithMuxDiscoveryComplete** - Signal when discovery is ready\n8. **WithMetrics** - Record Prometheus metrics\n9. **WithRequestDeadline** - Set request context deadline\n10. **WithRequestReceivedTime** - Record request arrival time\n11. **WithStorageVersion** - Add storage version headers\n12. **WithCacheControl** - Set cache control headers\n13. **WithWarning** - Add warning headers to response\n\n---\n\n### **7. RECOMMENDED OPTIONS SETUP**\n*File: `/workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/recommended.go`*\n\n```go\ntype RecommendedOptions struct {\n    Etcd           *EtcdOptions\n    SecureServing  *SecureServingOptionsWithLoopback\n    Authentication *DelegatingAuthenticationOptions\n    Authorization  *DelegatingAuthorizationOptions\n    Audit          *AuditOptions\n    Features       *FeatureOptions\n    CoreAPI        *CoreAPIOptions\n    Admission      *AdmissionOptions\n    EgressSelector *EgressSelectorOptions\n    Traces         *TracingOptions\n}\n```\n\nThe `ApplyTo()` method applies options in this sequence:\n1. Etcd storage\n2. Egress selector\n3. Tracing\n4. Secure serving\n5. Authentication\n6. Authorization\n7. Audit\n8. Core API\n9. Features\n10. Admission\n\n---\n\n### **8. KEY FILTER IMPLEMENTATIONS**\n\n#### Authentication Filter\n*File: `authentication.go`*\n- Calls authenticator.Request interface\n- Stores user in context via `genericapirequest.WithUser()`\n- Removes Authorization header post-auth\n- Handles HTTP/2 DOS mitigation for unauthenticated requests\n- Records authentication latency metrics\n\n#### Authorization Filter\n*File: `authorization.go`*\n- Extracts attributes using `GetAuthorizerAttributes()`\n- Calls authorizer.Authorize()\n- Records decision (Allow/Deny) in audit annotations\n- Returns 403 Forbidden on denial\n- Supports feature gate for field/label selector authorization\n\n#### Audit Filter\n*File: `audit.go`*\n- Evaluates audit policy against request attributes\n- Records multiple event stages:\n  - **StageRequestReceived** - When request arrives\n  - **StageResponseStarted** - When response code set\n  - **StageResponseComplete** - When response sent\n  - **StagePanic** - If panic occurs\n- Intercepts response status/headers via `auditResponseWriter`\n- Tracks latency per layer and annotates audit event\n- Handles long-running requests specially\n\n#### RequestInfo Filter\n*File: `requestinfo.go`*\n- Parses HTTP request to extract Kubernetes request metadata:\n  - Verb (get, list, create, update, patch, delete, watch, proxy)\n  - Resource and subresource\n  - Namespace and name\n  - API group and version\n  - Selector strings\n- Stores RequestInfo in context via `request.WithRequestInfo()`\n\n---\n\n### **9. CONTEXT FLOW**\n\nFilters populate request context with:\n```\nrequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)              // RequestInfo\nrequest.UserFrom(ctx)                      // user.Info\nrequest.ReceivedTimestampFrom(ctx)        // time.Time\nrequest.DeadlineFrom(ctx)                 // context deadline\naudit.AuditContextFrom(ctx)               // AuditContext\nauthenticator.AudiencesFrom(ctx)          // Audiences\n```\n\nThis context is passed through the entire request chain and used by admission controllers, authorization, and response writers.\n\n---\n\n### **10. CONFIGURATION FLOW**\n\n```\nServerRunOptions\n    \u2193\nApplyTo(Config)\n    \u2193\nRecommendedOptions\n    \u2193\nApplyTo(RecommendedConfig)\n    \u2193\nConfig.Complete()\n    \u2193\ncompletedConfig.New() creates GenericAPIServer\n    \u2193\nUses BuildHandlerChainFunc to create FullHandlerChain\n    \u2193\nNewAPIServerHandler() wraps with Director\n```\n\n---\n\n### **SUMMARY**\n\nThe Kubernetes apiserver provides a modular, composable filter architecture where:\n- **Storage** layer handles all persistence operations\n- **Authentication** validates identity and extracts user info\n- **Authorization** enforces access control (RBAC, webhooks, etc.)\n- **Audit** records all API operations to sinks\n- **Filters** form an HTTP middleware chain\n- **Context** carries request metadata through all layers\n- **Options** pattern allows customization of all major components\n\nThis architecture supports multiple authentication schemes, authorization policies, audit backends, and storage engines through pluggable interfaces.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

35. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go | head -200"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\tgpath \"path\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tsystemd \"github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22/daemon\"\n\n\t\"golang.org/x/time/rate\"\n\tapidiscoveryv2 \"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer/cbor\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n\tutilwaitgroup \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n\tgenericapi \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n\tdiscoveryendpoint \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/statusz\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\trestclient \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n\tbasecompatibility \"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\"\n\tzpagesfeatures \"k8s.io/component-base/zpages/features\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\topenapibuilder3 \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/builder3\"\n\topenapicommon \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler3\"\n\topenapiutil \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/util\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n)\n\n// Info about an API group.\ntype APIGroupInfo struct {\n\tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\n\t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\n\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\n\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\n\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n\t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects.\n\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\n\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\n\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n\n\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\n\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\n\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\n\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\n\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\n\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\n\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n\n\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\n\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\n\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\n}\n\nfunc (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\n\tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n\t\tfor _, store := range stores {\n\t\t\tstore.Destroy()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\ntype GenericAPIServer struct {\n\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\n\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n\n\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n\n\t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\n\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\n\t// gracefully shutdown returns.\n\tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\n\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n\n\t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\n\tadmissionControl admission.Interface\n\n\t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\n\tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\n\n\t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\n\t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\n\tExternalAddress string\n\n\t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\n\t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\n\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n\n\t// \"Outputs\"\n\t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\n\tHandler *APIServerHandler\n\n\t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\n\t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\n\tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n\n\t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\n\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\n\n\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\n\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\n\n\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n\n\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\n\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\n\n\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\n\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n\n\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\n\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n\n\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\n\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n\n\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\n\t// during PrepareRun.\n\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\n\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\n\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n\n\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\n\n\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\n\n\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\n\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\n\n\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\n\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\n\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\n\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\n\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\n\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\n\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n\n\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\n\tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n\tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\n\n\t// healthz checks"
36. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go | head -100",
  "description": "Read storage interfaces from git"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage storage\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n)\n\n// Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\ntype Feature = string\n\n// RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\nvar RequestWatchProgress Feature = \"RequestWatchProgress\"\n\n// Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\n// onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\n// object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\n// a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\n// intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\n// type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\ntype Versioner interface {\n\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\n\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\n\t// from database.\n\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\n\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\n\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\n\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\n\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\n\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\n\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\n\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\n\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\n\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\n\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\n\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\n\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\n\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\n\n\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\n\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\n\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\n\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\n\t// \"1\", you will see updates from \"2\" onwards).\n\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\n}\n\n// ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\n// an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\n// database and to improve testability.\ntype ResponseMeta struct {\n\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\n\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\n\t// expiration time due to server lag).\n\tTTL int64\n\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\n\tResourceVersion uint64\n}\n\n// IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\n// `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\ntype IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\n\n// IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\n// for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\ntype IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\n\n// Everything accepts all objects.\nvar Everything = SelectionPredicate{\n\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\n\tField: fields.Everything(),\n}\n\n// MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\ntype MatchValue struct {"
37. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator/interfaces.go | head -100",
  "description": "Read authentication interfaces from git"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage authenticator\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n)\n\n// Token checks a string value against a backing authentication store and\n// returns a Response or an error if the token could not be checked.\ntype Token interface {\n\tAuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error)\n}\n\n// Request attempts to extract authentication information from a request and\n// returns a Response or an error if the request could not be checked.\ntype Request interface {\n\tAuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error)\n}\n\n// TokenFunc is a function that implements the Token interface.\ntype TokenFunc func(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error)\n\n// AuthenticateToken implements authenticator.Token.\nfunc (f TokenFunc) AuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error) {\n\treturn f(ctx, token)\n}\n\n// RequestFunc is a function that implements the Request interface.\ntype RequestFunc func(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error)\n\n// AuthenticateRequest implements authenticator.Request.\nfunc (f RequestFunc) AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error) {\n\treturn f(req)\n}\n\n// Response is the struct returned by authenticator interfaces upon successful\n// authentication. It contains information about whether the authenticator\n// authenticated the request, information about the context of the\n// authentication, and information about the authenticated user.\ntype Response struct {\n\t// Audiences is the set of audiences the authenticator was able to validate\n\t// the token against. If the authenticator is not audience aware, this field\n\t// will be empty.\n\tAudiences Audiences\n\t// User is the UserInfo associated with the authentication context.\n\tUser user.Info\n}"
38. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer/interfaces.go | head -100",
  "description": "Read authorization interfaces from git"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage authorizer\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n)\n\n// Attributes is an interface used by an Authorizer to get information about a request\n// that is used to make an authorization decision.\ntype Attributes interface {\n\t// GetUser returns the user.Info object to authorize\n\tGetUser() user.Info\n\n\t// GetVerb returns the kube verb associated with API requests (this includes get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete, deletecollection, and proxy),\n\t// or the lowercased HTTP verb associated with non-API requests (this includes get, put, post, patch, and delete)\n\tGetVerb() string\n\n\t// When IsReadOnly() == true, the request has no side effects, other than\n\t// caching, logging, and other incidentals.\n\tIsReadOnly() bool\n\n\t// The namespace of the object, if a request is for a REST object.\n\tGetNamespace() string\n\n\t// The kind of object, if a request is for a REST object.\n\tGetResource() string\n\n\t// GetSubresource returns the subresource being requested, if present\n\tGetSubresource() string\n\n\t// GetName returns the name of the object as parsed off the request.  This will not be present for all request types, but\n\t// will be present for: get, update, delete\n\tGetName() string\n\n\t// The group of the resource, if a request is for a REST object.\n\tGetAPIGroup() string\n\n\t// GetAPIVersion returns the version of the group requested, if a request is for a REST object.\n\tGetAPIVersion() string\n\n\t// IsResourceRequest returns true for requests to API resources, like /api/v1/nodes,\n\t// and false for non-resource endpoints like /api, /healthz\n\tIsResourceRequest() bool\n\n\t// GetPath returns the path of the request\n\tGetPath() string\n\n\t// ParseFieldSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.\n\t// It returns an error if the field selector cannot be parsed.\n\t// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.\n\tGetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error)\n\n\t// ParseLabelSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.\n\t// It returns an error if the label selector cannot be parsed.\n\t// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.\n\tGetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error)\n}\n\n// Authorizer makes an authorization decision based on information gained by making\n// zero or more calls to methods of the Attributes interface.  It returns nil when an action is\n// authorized, otherwise it returns an error.\ntype Authorizer interface {\n\tAuthorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (authorized Decision, reason string, err error)\n}\n\ntype AuthorizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error)\n\nfunc (f AuthorizerFunc) Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error) {\n\treturn f(ctx, a)\n}\n\n// RuleResolver provides a mechanism for resolving the list of rules that apply to a given user within a namespace.\ntype RuleResolver interface {\n\t// RulesFor get the list of cluster wide rules, the list of rules in the specific namespace, incomplete status and errors.\n\tRulesFor(ctx context.Context, user user.Info, namespace string) ([]ResourceRuleInfo, []NonResourceRuleInfo, bool, error)\n}\n\n// RequestAttributesGetter provides a function that extracts Attributes from an http.Request\ntype RequestAttributesGetter interface {"
39. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/types.go | head -100",
  "description": "Read audit types from git"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage audit\n\nimport (\n\tauditinternal \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\"\n)\n\ntype Sink interface {\n\t// ProcessEvents handles events. Per audit ID it might be that ProcessEvents is called up to three times.\n\t// Errors might be logged by the sink itself. If an error should be fatal, leading to an internal\n\t// error, ProcessEvents is supposed to panic. The event must not be mutated and is reused by the caller\n\t// after the call returns, i.e. the sink has to make a deepcopy to keep a copy around if necessary.\n\t// Returns true on success, may return false on error.\n\tProcessEvents(events ...*auditinternal.Event) bool\n}\n\ntype Backend interface {\n\tSink\n\n\t// Run will initialize the backend. It must not block, but may run go routines in the background. If\n\t// stopCh is closed, it is supposed to stop them. Run will be called before the first call to ProcessEvents.\n\tRun(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error\n\n\t// Shutdown will synchronously shut down the backend while making sure that all pending\n\t// events are delivered. It can be assumed that this method is called after\n\t// the stopCh channel passed to the Run method has been closed.\n\tShutdown()\n\n\t// Returns the backend PluginName.\n\tString() string\n}"
40. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go | head -300"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/base32\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"os\"\n\tgoruntime \"runtime\"\n\t\"runtime/debug\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync/atomic\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/google/uuid\"\n\t\"golang.org/x/crypto/cryptobyte\"\n\tjsonpatch \"gopkg.in/evanphx/json-patch.v4\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/version\"\n\tutilwaitgroup \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory\"\n\tauthenticatorunion \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/union\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n\tdiscoveryendpoint \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filterlatency\"\n\tgenericapifilters \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/impersonation\"\n\tapiopenapi \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/openapi\"\n\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n\tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n\tgenericregistry \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/dynamiccertificates\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/egressselector\"\n\tgenericfilters \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/flagz\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routine\"\n\tserverstore \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage\"\n\tstoragevalue \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/value\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\tutilflowcontrol \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol\"\n\tflowcontrolrequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/informers\"\n\trestclient \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n\tbasecompatibility \"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/logs\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/features\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/slis\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\topenapicommon \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/spec3\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n\t\"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n\tutilsnet \"k8s.io/utils/net\"\n\n\t// install apis\n\t_ \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/install\"\n)\n\n// hostnameFunc is a function to set the hostnameFunc of this apiserver.\n// To be used for testing purpose only, to simulate scenarios where multiple apiservers\n// exist. In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\nvar (\n\thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\n)\n\nconst (\n\t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\n\tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \"/api\"\n\n\t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\n\tAPIGroupPrefix = \"/apis\"\n)\n\n// Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\n// Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\ntype Config struct {\n\t// SecureServing is required to serve https\n\tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\n\n\t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\n\tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\n\n\t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\n\tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\n\n\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n\t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\n\t// TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\n\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n\n\t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\n\t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\n\tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\n\n\t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\n\t// in a given namespace\n\tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\n\t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\n\t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\n\tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\n\tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\n\tHSTSDirectives        []string\n\t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\n\tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\n\n\tEnableIndex     bool\n\tEnableProfiling bool\n\tDebugSocketPath string\n\tEnableDiscovery bool\n\n\t// Requires generic profiling enabled\n\tEnableContentionProfiling bool\n\tEnableMetrics             bool\n\n\tDisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n\t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\n\tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\n\n\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n\t// AuditBackend is where audit events are sent to.\n\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\n\t// AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator makes the decision of whether and how to audit log a request.\n\tAuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\n\t// ExternalAddress is the host name to use for external (public internet) facing URLs (e.g. Swagger)\n\t// Will default to a value based on secure serving info and available ipv4 IPs.\n\tExternalAddress string\n\n\t// TracerProvider can provide a tracer, which records spans for distributed tracing.\n\tTracerProvider tracing.TracerProvider\n\n\t//===========================================================================\n\t// Fields you probably don't care about changing\n\t//===========================================================================\n\n\t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\n\tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\n\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n\t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\n\t// always reported\n\tDiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n\t// The default set of healthz checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n\tHealthzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n\t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n\tLivezChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n\t// The default set of readyz-only checks. There might be more added via AddReadyzChecks dynamically.\n\tReadyzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n\tFlagz        flagz.Reader\n\t// LegacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup. New API servers don't generally have legacy groups at all.\n\tLegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n\t// RequestInfoResolver is used to assign attributes (used by admission and authorization) based on a request URL.\n\t// Use-cases that are like kubelets may need to customize this.\n\tRequestInfoResolver apirequest.RequestInfoResolver\n\t// Serializer is required and provides the interface for serializing and converting objects to and from the wire\n\t// The default (api.Codecs) usually works fine.\n\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n\t// OpenAPIConfig will be used in generating OpenAPI spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIConfig for \"working\" defaults.\n\tOpenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n\t// OpenAPIV3Config will be used in generating OpenAPI V3 spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIV3Config for \"working\" defaults.\n\tOpenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation avoids installing the OpenAPI handler if set to true.\n\tSkipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n\n\t// ResourceTransformers are used to transform resources from and to etcd, e.g. encryption.\n\tResourceTransformers storagevalue.ResourceTransformers\n\t// RESTOptionsGetter is used to construct RESTStorage types via the generic registry.\n\tRESTOptionsGetter genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter\n\n\t// If specified, all requests except those which match the LongRunningFunc predicate will timeout\n\t// after this duration.\n\tRequestTimeout time.Duration\n\t// If specified, long running requests such as watch will be allocated a random timeout between this value, and\n\t// twice this value.  Note that it is up to the request handlers to ignore or honor this timeout. In seconds.\n\tMinRequestTimeout int\n\n\t// StorageInitializationTimeout defines the maximum amount of time to wait for storage initialization\n\t// before declaring apiserver ready.\n\tStorageInitializationTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// This represents the maximum amount of time it should take for apiserver to complete its startup\n\t// sequence and become healthy. From apiserver's start time to when this amount of time has\n\t// elapsed, /livez will assume that unfinished post-start hooks will complete successfully and\n\t// therefore return true.\n\tLivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\n\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n\t// but /readyz will return failure.\n\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// The limit on the total size increase all \"copy\" operations in a json\n\t// patch may cause.\n\t// This affects all places that applies json patch in the binary.\n\tJSONPatchMaxCopyBytes int64\n\t// The limit on the request size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request\n\t// 0 means no limit.\n\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n\t// MaxRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel non-long-running requests. Every further\n\t// request has to wait. Applies only to non-mutating requests.\n\tMaxRequestsInFlight int\n\t// MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel mutating requests. Every further\n\t// request has to wait.\n\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight int\n\t// Predicate which is true for paths of long-running http requests\n\tLongRunningFunc apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck\n\n\t// GoawayChance is the probability that send a GOAWAY to HTTP/2 clients. When client received\n\t// GOAWAY, the in-flight requests will not be affected and new requests will use\n\t// a new TCP connection to triggering re-balancing to another server behind the load balance.\n\t// Default to 0, means never send GOAWAY. Max is 0.02 to prevent break the apiserver.\n\tGoawayChance float64\n\n\t// MergedResourceConfig indicates which groupVersion enabled and its resources enabled/disabled.\n\t// This is composed of genericapiserver defaultAPIResourceConfig and those parsed from flags.\n\t// If not specify any in flags, then genericapiserver will only enable defaultAPIResourceConfig.\n\tMergedResourceConfig *serverstore.ResourceConfig\n\n\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals\n\t// that happen during lifecycle of the apiserver.\n\t// it's intentionally marked private as it should never be overridden.\n\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\n\n\t// StorageObjectCountTracker is used to keep track of the total number of objects\n\t// in the storage per resource, so we can estimate width of incoming requests.\n\tStorageObjectCountTracker flowcontrolrequest.StorageObjectCountTracker\n\n\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n\n\t//===========================================================================\n\t// values below here are targets for removal\n\t//===========================================================================\n\n\t// PublicAddress is the IP address where members of the cluster (kubelet,\n\t// kube-proxy, services, etc.) can reach the GenericAPIServer.\n\t// If nil or 0.0.0.0, the host's default interface will be used.\n\tPublicAddress net.IP"
41. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/get.go | head -150"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage handlers\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math/rand\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/attribute\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n\tmetainternalversion \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\"\n\tmetainternalversionscheme \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/scheme\"\n\tmetainternalversionvalidation \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion/validation\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routine\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n)\n\n// getterFunc performs a get request with the given context and object name. The request\n// may be used to deserialize an options object to pass to the getter.\ntype getterFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string, req *http.Request) (runtime.Object, error)\n\n// getResourceHandler is an HTTP handler function for get requests. It delegates to the\n// passed-in getterFunc to perform the actual get.\nfunc getResourceHandler(scope *RequestScope, getter getterFunc) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\tctx := req.Context()\n\t\tctx, span := tracing.Start(ctx, \"Get\", traceFields(req)...)\n\t\treq = req.WithContext(ctx)\n\t\tdefer span.End(500 * time.Millisecond)\n\n\t\tnamespace, name, err := scope.Namer.Name(req)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tctx = request.WithNamespace(ctx, namespace)\n\n\t\toutputMediaType, _, err := negotiation.NegotiateOutputMediaType(req, scope.Serializer, scope)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresult, err := getter(ctx, name, req)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tscope.err(err, w, req)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"About to write a response\")\n\t\tdefer span.AddEvent(\"Writing http response done\")\n\t\ttransformResponseObject(ctx, scope, req, w, http.StatusOK, outputMediaType, result)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetResource returns a function that handles retrieving a single resource from a rest.Storage object.\nfunc GetResource(r rest.Getter, scope *RequestScope) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn getResourceHandler(scope,\n\t\tfunc(ctx context.Context, name string, req *http.Request) (runtime.Object, error) {\n\t\t\t// check for export\n\t\t\toptions := metav1.GetOptions{}\n\t\t\tif values := req.URL.Query(); len(values) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tif len(values[\"export\"]) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\texportBool := true\n\t\t\t\t\texportStrings := values[\"export\"]\n\t\t\t\t\terr := runtime.Convert_Slice_string_To_bool(&exportStrings, &exportBool, nil)\n\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, errors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"the export parameter cannot be parsed: %v\", err))\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tif exportBool {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, errors.NewBadRequest(\"the export parameter, deprecated since v1.14, is no longer supported\")\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tif err := metainternalversionscheme.ParameterCodec.DecodeParameters(values, scope.MetaGroupVersion, &options); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\ttracing.SpanFromContext(ctx).AddEvent(\"About to Get from storage\")\n\t\t\treturn r.Get(ctx, name, &options)\n\t\t})\n}\n\n// GetResourceWithOptions returns a function that handles retrieving a single resource from a rest.Storage object.\nfunc GetResourceWithOptions(r rest.GetterWithOptions, scope *RequestScope, isSubresource bool) http.HandlerFunc {\n\treturn getResourceHandler(scope,\n\t\tfunc(ctx context.Context, name string, req *http.Request) (runtime.Object, error) {\n\t\t\topts, subpath, subpathKey := r.NewGetOptions()\n\t\t\tspan := tracing.SpanFromContext(ctx)\n\t\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"About to process Get options\")\n\t\t\tif err := getRequestOptions(req, scope, opts, subpath, subpathKey, isSubresource); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\terr = errors.NewBadRequest(err.Error())\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tspan.AddEvent(\"About to Get from storage\")\n\t\t\treturn r.Get(ctx, name, opts)\n\t\t})\n}\n\n// getRequestOptions parses out options and can include path information.  The path information shouldn't include the subresource.\nfunc getRequestOptions(req *http.Request, scope *RequestScope, into runtime.Object, subpath bool, subpathKey string, isSubresource bool) error {\n\tif into == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tquery := req.URL.Query()\n\tif subpath {\n\t\tnewQuery := make(url.Values)\n\t\tfor k, v := range query {\n\t\t\tnewQuery[k] = v\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx := req.Context()\n\t\trequestInfo, _ := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n\t\tstartingIndex := 2\n\t\tif isSubresource {\n\t\t\tstartingIndex = 3"
42. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go | head -200"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage rest\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\"\n\n\tmetainternalversion \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n)\n\n//TODO:\n// Storage interfaces need to be separated into two groups; those that operate\n// on collections and those that operate on individually named items.\n// Collection interfaces:\n// (Method: Current -> Proposed)\n//    GET: Lister -> CollectionGetter\n//    WATCH: Watcher -> CollectionWatcher\n//    CREATE: Creater -> CollectionCreater\n//    DELETE: (n/a) -> CollectionDeleter\n//    UPDATE: (n/a) -> CollectionUpdater\n//\n// Single item interfaces:\n// (Method: Current -> Proposed)\n//    GET: Getter -> NamedGetter\n//    WATCH: (n/a) -> NamedWatcher\n//    CREATE: (n/a) -> NamedCreater\n//    DELETE: Deleter -> NamedDeleter\n//    UPDATE: Update -> NamedUpdater\n\n// Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.\n// Resources which are exported to the RESTful API of apiserver need to implement this interface. It is expected\n// that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.\n//\n// Consider using StorageWithReadiness whenever possible.\ntype Storage interface {\n\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.\n\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n\tNew() runtime.Object\n\n\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n\t// for being called more than once.\n\tDestroy()\n}\n\n// StorageWithReadiness extends Storage interface with the readiness check.\ntype StorageWithReadiness interface {\n\tStorage\n\n\t// ReadinessCheck allows for checking storage readiness.\n\tReadinessCheck() error\n}\n\n// Scoper indicates what scope the resource is at. It must be specified.\n// It is usually provided automatically based on your strategy.\ntype Scoper interface {\n\t// NamespaceScoped returns true if the storage is namespaced\n\tNamespaceScoped() bool\n}\n\n// KindProvider specifies a different kind for its API than for its internal storage.  This is necessary for external\n// objects that are not compiled into the api server.  For such objects, there is no in-memory representation for\n// the object, so they must be represented as generic objects (e.g. runtime.Unknown), but when we present the object as part of\n// API discovery we want to present the specific kind, not the generic internal representation.\ntype KindProvider interface {\n\tKind() string\n}\n\n// ShortNamesProvider is an interface for RESTful storage services. Delivers a list of short names for a resource. The list is used by kubectl to have short names representation of resources.\ntype ShortNamesProvider interface {\n\tShortNames() []string\n}\n\n// CategoriesProvider allows a resource to specify which groups of resources (categories) it's part of. Categories can\n// be used by API clients to refer to a batch of resources by using a single name (e.g. \"all\" could translate to \"pod,rc,svc,...\").\ntype CategoriesProvider interface {\n\tCategories() []string\n}\n\n// SingularNameProvider returns singular name of resources. This is used by kubectl discovery to have singular\n// name representation of resources. In case of shortcut conflicts(with CRD shortcuts) singular name should always map to this resource.\ntype SingularNameProvider interface {\n\tGetSingularName() string\n}\n\n// GroupVersionKindProvider is used to specify a particular GroupVersionKind to discovery.  This is used for polymorphic endpoints\n// which generally point to foreign versions.  Scale refers to Scale.v1beta1.extensions for instance.\n// This trumps KindProvider since it is capable of providing the information required.\n// TODO KindProvider (only used by federation) should be removed and replaced with this, but that presents greater risk late in 1.8.\ntype GroupVersionKindProvider interface {\n\tGroupVersionKind(containingGV schema.GroupVersion) schema.GroupVersionKind\n}\n\n// GroupVersionAcceptor is used to determine if a particular GroupVersion is acceptable to send to an endpoint.\n// This is used for endpoints which accept multiple versions (which is extremely rare).\n// The only known instance is pods/evictions which accepts policy/v1, but also policy/v1beta1 for backwards compatibility.\ntype GroupVersionAcceptor interface {\n\tAcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool\n}\n\n// Lister is an object that can retrieve resources that match the provided field and label criteria.\ntype Lister interface {\n\t// NewList returns an empty object that can be used with the List call.\n\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n\tNewList() runtime.Object\n\t// List selects resources in the storage which match to the selector. 'options' can be nil.\n\tList(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n\t// TableConvertor ensures all list implementers also implement table conversion\n\tTableConvertor\n}\n\n// Getter is an object that can retrieve a named RESTful resource.\ntype Getter interface {\n\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\n\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options *metav1.GetOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n}\n\n// GetterWithOptions is an object that retrieve a named RESTful resource and takes\n// additional options on the get request. It allows a caller to also receive the\n// subpath of the GET request.\ntype GetterWithOptions interface {\n\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\n\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n\t// The options object passed to it is of the same type returned by the NewGetOptions\n\t// method.\n\t// TODO: Pass metav1.GetOptions.\n\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error)\n\n\t// NewGetOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\n\t// options to the Get method. It may return a bool and a string, if true, the\n\t// value of the request path below the object will be included as the named\n\t// string in the serialization of the runtime object. E.g., returning \"path\"\n\t// will convert the trailing request scheme value to \"path\" in the map[string][]string\n\t// passed to the converter.\n\tNewGetOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\n}\n\ntype TableConvertor interface {\n\tConvertToTable(ctx context.Context, object runtime.Object, tableOptions runtime.Object) (*metav1.Table, error)\n}\n\n// GracefulDeleter knows how to pass deletion options to allow delayed deletion of a\n// RESTful object.\ntype GracefulDeleter interface {\n\t// Delete finds a resource in the storage and deletes it.\n\t// The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\n\t// If options are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an invalid\n\t// request error.\n\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n\t// Delete *may* return the object that was deleted, or a status object indicating additional\n\t// information about deletion.\n\t// It also returns a boolean which is set to true if the resource was instantly\n\t// deleted or false if it will be deleted asynchronously.\n\tDelete(ctx context.Context, name string, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\n}\n\n// MayReturnFullObjectDeleter may return deleted object (instead of a simple status) on deletion.\ntype MayReturnFullObjectDeleter interface {\n\tDeleteReturnsDeletedObject() bool\n}\n\n// CollectionDeleter is an object that can delete a collection\n// of RESTful resources.\ntype CollectionDeleter interface {\n\t// DeleteCollection selects all resources in the storage matching given 'listOptions'\n\t// and deletes them. The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\n\t// If 'options' are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an\n\t// invalid request error.\n\t// DeleteCollection may not be atomic - i.e. it may delete some objects and still\n\t// return an error after it. On success, returns a list of deleted objects.\n\tDeleteCollection(ctx context.Context, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions, listOptions *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n}"
43. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go | head -250"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage admission\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\tauditinternal \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n)\n\n// Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request\n// that is used to make an admission decision.\ntype Attributes interface {\n\t// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client\n\t// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return\n\t// the empty string\n\tGetName() string\n\t// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)\n\tGetNamespace() string\n\t// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods\n\tGetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource\n\t// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.\n\t// For instance, /pods has the resource \"pods\" and the kind \"Pod\", while /pods/foo/status has the resource \"pods\", the sub resource \"status\", and the kind \"Pod\"\n\t// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource \"pods\", subresource \"binding\", and kind \"Binding\".\n\tGetSubresource() string\n\t// GetOperation is the operation being performed\n\tGetOperation() Operation\n\t// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed\n\tGetOperationOptions() runtime.Object\n\t// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent\n\t// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.\n\t// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it\n\t// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.\n\tIsDryRun() bool\n\t// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied\n\tGetObject() runtime.Object\n\t// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.\n\tGetOldObject() runtime.Object\n\t// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod\n\tGetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind\n\t// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user\n\tGetUserInfo() user.Info\n\n\t// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where\n\t// \"podsecuritypolicy\" is the name of the plugin, \"admission.k8s.io\" is the name of the organization, \"admit-policy\" is the key name.\n\t// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.\n\t// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.\n\t// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or\n\t// equal to Metadata.\n\tAddAnnotation(key, value string) error\n\n\t// AddAnnotationWithLevel sets annotation according to key-value pair with additional intended audit level.\n\t// An Annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or equal to the\n\t// intended audit level.\n\tAddAnnotationWithLevel(key, value string, level auditinternal.Level) error\n\n\t// GetReinvocationContext tracks the admission request information relevant to the re-invocation policy.\n\tGetReinvocationContext() ReinvocationContext\n}\n\n// ObjectInterfaces is an interface used by AdmissionController to get object interfaces\n// such as Converter or Defaulter. These interfaces are normally coming from Request Scope\n// to handle special cases like CRDs.\ntype ObjectInterfaces interface {\n\t// GetObjectCreater is the ObjectCreator appropriate for the requested object.\n\tGetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater\n\t// GetObjectTyper is the ObjectTyper appropriate for the requested object.\n\tGetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper\n\t// GetObjectDefaulter is the ObjectDefaulter appropriate for the requested object.\n\tGetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n\t// GetObjectConvertor is the ObjectConvertor appropriate for the requested object.\n\tGetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor\n\t// GetEquivalentResourceMapper is the EquivalentResourceMapper appropriate for finding equivalent resources and expected kind for the requested object.\n\tGetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n}\n\n// privateAnnotationsGetter is a private interface which allows users to get annotations from Attributes.\ntype privateAnnotationsGetter interface {\n\tgetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n}\n\n// AnnotationsGetter allows users to get annotations from Attributes. An alternate Attribute should implement\n// this interface.\ntype AnnotationsGetter interface {\n\tGetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n}\n\n// ReinvocationContext provides access to the admission related state required to implement the re-invocation policy.\ntype ReinvocationContext interface {\n\t// IsReinvoke returns true if the current admission check is a re-invocation.\n\tIsReinvoke() bool\n\t// SetIsReinvoke sets the current admission check as a re-invocation.\n\tSetIsReinvoke()\n\t// ShouldReinvoke returns true if any plugin has requested a re-invocation.\n\tShouldReinvoke() bool\n\t// SetShouldReinvoke signals that a re-invocation is desired.\n\tSetShouldReinvoke()\n\t// AddValue set a value for a plugin name, possibly overriding a previous value.\n\tSetValue(plugin string, v interface{})\n\t// Value reads a value for a webhook.\n\tValue(plugin string) interface{}\n}\n\n// Interface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\ntype Interface interface {\n\t// Handles returns true if this admission controller can handle the given operation\n\t// where operation can be one of CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, or CONNECT\n\tHandles(operation Operation) bool\n}\n\ntype MutationInterface interface {\n\tInterface\n\n\t// Admit makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.\n\t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\n\tAdmit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\n}\n\n// ValidationInterface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\ntype ValidationInterface interface {\n\tInterface\n\n\t// Validate makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.  It is NOT allowed to mutate\n\t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\n\tValidate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\n}\n\n// Operation is the type of resource operation being checked for admission control\ntype Operation string\n\n// Operation constants\nconst (\n\tCreate  Operation = \"CREATE\"\n\tUpdate  Operation = \"UPDATE\"\n\tDelete  Operation = \"DELETE\"\n\tConnect Operation = \"CONNECT\"\n)\n\n// PluginInitializer is used for initialization of shareable resources between admission plugins.\n// After initialization the resources have to be set separately\ntype PluginInitializer interface {\n\tInitialize(plugin Interface)\n}\n\n// InitializationValidator holds ValidateInitialization functions, which are responsible for validation of initialized\n// shared resources and should be implemented on admission plugins\ntype InitializationValidator interface {\n\tValidateInitialization() error\n}\n\n// ConfigProvider provides a way to get configuration for an admission plugin based on its name\ntype ConfigProvider interface {\n\tConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error)\n}"
44. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go | sed -n '200,400p'"
}
Output
"\t// healthz checks\n\thealthzRegistry healthCheckRegistry\n\treadyzRegistry  healthCheckRegistry\n\tlivezRegistry   healthCheckRegistry\n\n\tlivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n\n\t// auditing. The backend is started before the server starts listening.\n\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\n\n\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\n\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\n\t// the create-on-update case\n\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n\n\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\n\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\n\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n\n\t// delegationTarget is the next delegate in the chain. This is never nil.\n\tdelegationTarget DelegationTarget\n\n\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n\n\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\n\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n\t// but /readyz will return failure.\n\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n\n\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\n\t// 0 means no limit.\n\tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n\n\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\n\tAPIServerID string\n\n\t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\n\t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\n\tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\n\n\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\n\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\n\n\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\n\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n\n\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals that happen during the life cycle of the apiserver.\n\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\n\n\t// destroyFns contains a list of functions that should be called on shutdown to clean up resources.\n\tdestroyFns []func()\n\n\t// muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals holds signals that indicate all known HTTP paths have been registered.\n\t// it exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.\n\t// it is exposed for easier composition of the individual servers.\n\t// the primary users of this field are the WithMuxCompleteProtection filter and the NotFoundHandler\n\tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals map[string]<-chan struct{}\n\n\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n\t// rejected
