with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n\n\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\n\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\n\t// watch request(s) to drain.\n\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\n\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\n\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\n\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\n\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\n\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\n\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\n\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\n\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\n\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\n\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\n\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\n\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\n}\n\n// DelegationTarget is an interface which allows for composition of API servers with top level handling that works\n// as expected.\ntype DelegationTarget interface {\n\t// UnprotectedHandler returns a handler that is NOT protected by a normal chain\n\tUnprotectedHandler() http.Handler\n\n\t// PostStartHooks returns the post-start hooks that need to be combined\n\tPostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry\n\n\t// PreShutdownHooks returns the pre-stop hooks that need to be combined\n\tPreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n\n\t// HealthzChecks returns the healthz checks that need to be combined\n\tHealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker\n\n\t// ListedPaths returns the paths for supporting an index\n\tListedPaths() []string\n\n\t// NextDelegate returns the next delegationTarget in the chain of delegations\n\tNextDelegate() DelegationTarget\n\n\t// PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\n\tPrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer\n\n\t// MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals exposes registered signals that indicate if all known HTTP paths have been installed.\n\tMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{}\n\n\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n\t// for being called more than once.\n\tDestroy()\n}\n\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler {\n\t// when we delegate, we need the server we're delegating to choose whether or not to use gorestful\n\treturn s.Handler.Director\n}\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry {\n\treturn s.postStartHooks\n}\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry {\n\treturn s.preShutdownHooks\n}\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) HealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker {\n\treturn s.healthzRegistry.checks\n}\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) ListedPaths() []string {\n\treturn s.listedPathProvider.ListedPaths()\n}\n\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) NextDelegate() DelegationTarget {\n\treturn s.delegationTarget\n}\n\n// RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal registers the given signal that will be used to determine if all known\n// HTTP paths have been registered. It is okay to call this method after instantiating the generic server but before running.\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal(signalName string, signal <-chan struct{}) error {\n\tif _, exists := s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName]; exists {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s already registered\", signalName)\n\t}\n\ts.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName] = signal\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{} {\n\treturn s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals\n}\n\n// RegisterDestroyFunc registers a function that will be called during Destroy().\n// The function have to be idempotent and prepared to be called more than once.\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterDestroyFunc(destroyFn func()) {\n\ts.destroyFns = append(s.destroyFns, destroyFn)\n}\n\n// Destroy cleans up all its and its delegation target resources on shutdown.\n// It starts with destroying its own resources and later proceeds with\n// its delegation target.\nfunc (s *GenericAPIServer) Destroy() {\n\tfor _, destroyFn := range s.destroyFns {\n\t\tdestroyFn()\n\t}\n\tif s.delegationTarget != nil {\n\t\ts.delegationTarget.Destroy()\n\t}\n}\n\ntype emptyDelegate struct {\n\t// handler is called at the end of the delegation chain\n\t// when a request has been made against an unregistered HTTP path the individual servers will simply pass it through until it reaches the handler.\n\thandler http.Handler\n}\n\nfunc NewEmptyDelegate() DelegationTarget {\n\treturn emptyDelegate{}\n}"
45. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go | sed -n '100,200p'"
}
Output
"func (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\n\tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n\t\tfor _, store := range stores {\n\t\t\tstore.Destroy()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\ntype GenericAPIServer struct {\n\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\n\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n\n\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n\n\t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\n\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\n\t// gracefully shutdown returns.\n\tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\n\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n\n\t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\n\tadmissionControl admission.Interface\n\n\t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\n\tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\n\n\t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\n\t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\n\tExternalAddress string\n\n\t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\n\t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\n\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n\n\t// \"Outputs\"\n\t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\n\tHandler *APIServerHandler\n\n\t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\n\t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\n\tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n\n\t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\n\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\n\n\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\n\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\n\n\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n\n\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\n\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\n\n\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\n\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n\n\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\n\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n\n\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\n\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n\n\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\n\t// during PrepareRun.\n\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\n\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\n\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n\n\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\n\n\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\n\n\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\n\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\n\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\n\n\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\n\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\n\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\n\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\n\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\n\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\n\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n\n\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\n\tpreShutdownHooks       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n\tpreShutdownHooksCalled bool\n\n\t// healthz checks"
46. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/rest.go | head -100"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage handlers\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tgrpccodes \"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n\tgrpcstatus \"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n\n\tapiequality \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/equality\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\tmetav1beta1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1beta1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n\trequestmetrics \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/metrics\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/warning\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\t// 34 chose as a number close to 30 that is likely to be unique enough to jump out at me the next time I see a timeout.\n\t// Everyone chooses 30.\n\trequestTimeoutUpperBound = 34 * time.Second\n\t// DuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat is the warning that a client receives when a create/update request contains\n\t// duplicate owner reference entries.\n\tDuplicateOwnerReferencesWarningFormat = \".metadata.ownerReferences contains duplicate entries; API server dedups owner references in 1.20+, and may reject such requests as early as 1.24; please fix your requests; duplicate UID(s) observed: %v\"\n\t// DuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat indicates the duplication was observed\n\t// after mutating admission.\n\t// NOTE: For CREATE and UPDATE requests the API server dedups both before and after mutating admission.\n\t// For PATCH request the API server only dedups after mutating admission.\n\tDuplicateOwnerReferencesAfterMutatingAdmissionWarningFormat = \".metadata.ownerReferences contains duplicate entries after mutating admission happens; API server dedups owner references in 1.20+, and may reject such requests as early as 1.24; please fix your requests; duplicate UID(s) observed: %v\"\n\t// shortPrefix is one possible beginning of yaml unmarshal strict errors.\n\tshortPrefix = \"yaml: unmarshal errors:\\n\"\n\t// longPrefix is the other possible beginning of yaml unmarshal strict errors.\n\tlongPrefix = \"error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: unmarshal errors:\\n\"\n)\n\n// RequestScope encapsulates common fields across all RESTful handler methods.\ntype RequestScope struct {\n\tNamer ScopeNamer\n\n\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n\truntime.ParameterCodec\n\n\t// StandardSerializers, if set, restricts which serializers can be used when\n\t// we aren't transforming the output (into Table or PartialObjectMetadata).\n\t// Used only by CRDs which do not yet support Protobuf.\n\tStandardSerializers []runtime.SerializerInfo\n\n\tCreater         runtime.ObjectCreater\n\tConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor\n\tDefaulter       runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n\tTyper           runtime.ObjectTyper\n\tUnsafeConvertor runtime.ObjectConvertor\n\tAuthorizer      authorizer.Authorizer\n\n\tEquivalentResourceMapper runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n\n\tTableConvertor rest.TableConvertor\n\tFieldManager   *managedfields.FieldManager\n\n\tResource schema.GroupVersionResource\n\tKind     schema.GroupVersionKind\n\n\t// AcceptsGroupVersionDelegate is an optional delegate that can be queried about whether a given GVK\n\t// can be accepted in create or update requests. If nil, only scope.Kind is accepted.\n\t// Note that this does not enable multi-version support for reads from a single endpoint.\n\tAcceptsGroupVersionDelegate rest.GroupVersionAcceptor"
47. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go | head -150"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\trt \"runtime\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\n\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\"\n)\n\n// APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\n// This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\n// the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\n// and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\n// FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\n\t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\n\t// chain and then call the Director.\n\tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\n\t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\n\tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n\t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\n\t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\n\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\n\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\n\n\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\n\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\n\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\n\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\n\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\n\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\n\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\n\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\n\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\n\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\n\t// order to handle \"normal\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\n\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\n\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\n\tDirector http.Handler\n}\n\n// HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\n// It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\ntype HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\n\nfunc NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\n\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\n\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\n\t\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\n\t}\n\n\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\n\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\n\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\n\t\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\n\t})\n\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\n\t\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\n\t})\n\n\tdirector := director{\n\t\tname:               name,\n\t\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n\t\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n\t}\n\n\treturn &APIServerHandler{\n\t\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\n\t\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n\t\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n\t\tDirector:           director,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\n\tvar handledPaths []string\n\t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\n\tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n\t\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\n\t}\n\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\n\tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\n\n\treturn handledPaths\n}\n\ntype director struct {\n\tname               string\n\tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n\tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\n}\n\nfunc (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\tpath := req.URL.Path\n\n\t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\n\tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase ws.RootPath() == \"/apis\":\n\t\t\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\n\t\t\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\n\t\t\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\n\t\t\tif path == \"/apis\" || path == \"/apis/\" {\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\n\t\t\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\n\t\t\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}"
48. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go",
  "description": "Get full storage interfaces file"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage storage\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n)\n\n// Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\ntype Feature = string\n\n// RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\nvar RequestWatchProgress Feature = \"RequestWatchProgress\"\n\n// Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\n// onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\n// object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\n// a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\n// intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\n// type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\ntype Versioner interface {\n\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\n\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\n\t// from database.\n\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\n\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\n\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\n\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\n\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\n\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\n\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\n\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\n\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\n\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\n\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\n\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\n\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\n\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\n\n\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\n\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\n\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\n\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\n\t// \"1\", you will see updates from \"2\" onwards).\n\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\n}\n\n// ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\n// an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\n// database and to improve testability.\ntype ResponseMeta struct {\n\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\n\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\n\t// expiration time due to server lag).\n\tTTL int64\n\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\n\tResourceVersion uint64\n}\n\n// IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\n// `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\ntype IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\n\n// IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\n// for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\ntype IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\n\n// Everything accepts all objects.\nvar Everything = SelectionPredicate{\n\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\n\tField: fields.Everything(),\n}\n\n// MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\ntype MatchValue struct {\n\tIndexName string\n\tValue     string\n}\n\n// Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\n// that is guaranteed to succeed.\n// See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\ntype UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\n\n// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\n// if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\n// object.\n// NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\n// storage, due to transformation or decode error.\ntype ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\n\n// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \"admit everything\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\nfunc ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\ntype Preconditions struct {\n\t// Specifies the target UID.\n\t// +optional\n\tUID *types.UID `json:\"uid,omitempty\"`\n\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\n\t// +optional\n\tResourceVersion *string `json:\"resourceVersion,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\nfunc NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\n\tu := types.UID(uid)\n\treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\n}\n\nfunc (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\n\tif p == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn NewInternalError(\n\t\t\tfmt.Errorf(\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\",\n\t\t\t\t*p,\n\t\t\t\tobj,\n\t\t\t\terr))\n\t}\n\tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {\n\t\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\"Precondition failed: UID in precondition: %v, UID in object meta: %v\",\n\t\t\t*p.UID,\n\t\t\tobjMeta.GetUID())\n\t\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\n\t}\n\tif p.ResourceVersion != nil && *p.ResourceVersion != objMeta.GetResourceVersion() {\n\t\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\"Precondition failed: ResourceVersion in precondition: %v, ResourceVersion in object meta: %v\",\n\t\t\t*p.ResourceVersion,\n\t\t\tobjMeta.GetResourceVersion())\n\t\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and\n// hides all the storage-related operations behind it.\ntype Interface interface {\n\t// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.\n\tVersioner() Versioner\n\n\t// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live\n\t// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be\n\t// set to the read value from database.\n\tCreate(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\n\n\t// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.\n\t// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.\n\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\n\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\n\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\n\tDelete(\n\t\tctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,\n\t\tvalidateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\n\n\t// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,\n\t// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.\n\t// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,\n\t// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1\n\t// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).\n\t// If resource version is \"0\", this interface will get current object at given key\n\t// and send it in an \"ADDED\" event, before watch starts.\n\tWatch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\n\n\t// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either\n\t// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.\n\t// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.\n\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\n\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n\tGet(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\n\n\t// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object\n\t// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).\n\t// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'\n\t// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.\n\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\n\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n\tGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\n\n\t// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')\n\t// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.\n\t// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if\n\t// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account\n\t// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.\n\t// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false\n\t// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.\n\t// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized\n\t// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those\n\t// contents.\n\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\n\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\n\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\n\t//\n\t// Example:\n\t//\n\t// s := /* implementation of Interface */\n\t// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(\n\t//     \"myKey\", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,\n\t//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\n\t//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, \"input\" is reset to\n\t//       // current contents for \"myKey\" in database.\n\t//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.\n\t//\n\t//       // Make the modification\n\t//       curr.Counter++\n\t//\n\t//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return\n\t//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.\n\t//       return cur, nil, nil\n\t//    }, cachedExistingObject\n\t// )\n\tGuaranteedUpdate(\n\t\tctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\n\t\tpreconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\n\n\t// Stats returns storage stats.\n\tStats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)\n\n\t// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\n\tReadinessCheck() error\n\n\t// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the\n\t// watch response stream as soon as possible.\n\t// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.\n\t//\n\t// If watch is lagging, progress status might:\n\t// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.\n\t// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.\n\t//\n\t// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.\n\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042\n\t//\n\t// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.\n\t// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.\n\tRequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error\n\n\t// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.\n\t// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata\n\tGetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)\n\n\t// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.\n\tEnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error\n\n\t// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.\n\t// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.\n\t// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.\n\tCompactRevision() int64\n}\n\n// KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.\ntype KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)\n\n// GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.\ntype GetOptions struct {\n\t// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If\n\t// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.\n\tIgnoreNotFound bool\n\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation\n\t// as a \"not older than\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\n\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\n\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\n\tResourceVersion string\n}\n\n// ListOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage list operations.\ntype ListOptions struct {\n\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the list operation\n\t// as a \"not older than\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\n\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\n\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\n\tResourceVersion string\n\t// ResourceVersionMatch provides the rule for how the resource version constraint applies. If set\n\t// to the default value \"\" the legacy resource version semantic apply.\n\tResourceVersionMatch metav1.ResourceVersionMatch\n\t// Predicate provides the selection rules for the list operation.\n\tPredicate SelectionPredicate\n\t// Recursive determines whether the list or watch is defined for a single object located at the\n\t// given key, or for the whole set of objects with the given key as a prefix.\n\tRecursive bool\n\t// ProgressNotify determines whether storage-originated bookmark (progress notify) events should\n\t// be delivered to the users. The option is ignored for non-watch requests.\n\t//\n\t// Firstly, note that this field is different from the Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks field.\n\t// Secondly, this field is intended for internal clients only such as the watch cache.\n\t//\n\t// This means that external clients do not have the ability to set this field directly.\n\t// For example by setting the allowWatchBookmarks query parameter.\n\t//\n\t// The motivation for this approach is the fact that the frequency\n\t// of bookmark events from a storage like etcd might be very high.\n\t// As the number of watch requests increases, the server load would also increase.\n\t//\n\t// Furthermore, the server is not obligated to provide bookmark events at all,\n\t// as described in https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/956-watch-bookmark#proposal\n\tProgressNotify bool\n\t// SendInitialEvents, when set together with Watch option,\n\t// begin the watch stream with synthetic init events to build the\n\t// whole state of all resources followed by a synthetic \"Bookmark\"\n\t// event containing a ResourceVersion after which the server\n\t// continues streaming events.\n\tSendInitialEvents *bool\n}\n\n// DeleteOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage delete operations.\ntype DeleteOptions struct {\n\t// IgnoreStoreReadError, if enabled, will ignore store read error\n\t// such as transformation or decode failure and go ahead with the\n\t// deletion of the object.\n\t// NOTE: for normal deletion flow it should always be false, it may be\n\t// enabled by the caller only to facilitate unsafe deletion of corrupt\n\t// object which otherwise can not be deleted using the normal flow\n\tIgnoreStoreReadError bool\n}\n\nfunc ValidateListOptions(keyPrefix string, versioner Versioner, opts ListOptions) (withRev int64, continueKey string, err error) {\n\tif opts.Recursive && len(opts.Predicate.Continue) > 0 {\n\t\tcontinueKey, continueRV, err := DecodeContinue(opts.Predicate.Continue, keyPrefix)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn 0, \"\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid continue token: %v\", err))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(opts.ResourceVersion) > 0 && opts.ResourceVersion != \"0\" {\n\t\t\treturn 0, \"\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(\"specifying resource version is not allowed when using continue\")\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If continueRV > 0, the LIST request needs a specific resource version.\n\t\t// continueRV==0 is invalid.\n\t\t// If continueRV < 0, the request is for the latest resource version.\n\t\tif continueRV > 0 {\n\t\t\twithRev = continueRV\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn withRev, continueKey, nil\n\t}\n\tif len(opts.ResourceVersion) == 0 {\n\t\treturn withRev, \"\", nil\n\t}\n\tparsedRV, err := versioner.ParseResourceVersion(opts.ResourceVersion)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn withRev, \"\", apierrors.NewBadRequest(fmt.Sprintf(\"invalid resource version: %v\", err))\n\t}\n\tswitch opts.ResourceVersionMatch {\n\tcase metav1.ResourceVersionMatchNotOlderThan:\n\t\t// The not older than constraint is checked after we get a response from etcd,\n\t\t// and returnedRV is then set to the revision we get from the etcd response.\n\tcase metav1.ResourceVersionMatchExact:\n\t\twithRev = int64(parsedRV)\n\tcase \"\": // legacy case\n\t\tif opts.Recursive && opts.Predicate.Limit > 0 && parsedRV > 0 {\n\t\t\twithRev = int64(parsedRV)\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn withRev, \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"unknown ResourceVersionMatch value: %v\", opts.ResourceVersionMatch)\n\t}\n\treturn withRev, \"\", nil\n}\n\n// Stats provides statistics information about storage.\ntype Stats struct {\n\t// ObjectCount informs about number of objects stored in the storage.\n\tObjectCount int64\n\t// EstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes informs about size of objects stored in the storage, based on size of serialized values.\n\t// Value is an estimate, meaning it doesn't need to provide accurate nor consistent.\n\tEstimatedAverageObjectSizeBytes int64\n}\n\nfunc PrepareKey(resourcePrefix, key string, recursive bool) (string, error) {\n\tif key == \"..\" ||\n\t\tstrings.HasPrefix(key, \"../\") ||\n\t\tstrings.HasSuffix(key, \"/..\") ||\n\t\tstrings.Contains(key, \"/../\") {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid key: %q\", key)\n\t}\n\tif key == \".\" ||\n\t\tstrings.HasPrefix(key, \"./\") ||\n\t\tstrings.HasSuffix(key, \"/.\") ||\n\t\tstrings.Contains(key, \"/./\") {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid key: %q\", key)\n\t}\n\tif key == \"\" || key == \"/\" {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"empty key: %q\", key)\n\t}\n\t// For recursive requests, we need to make sure the key ended with \"/\" so that we only\n\t// get children \"directories\". e.g. if we have key \"/a\", \"/a/b\", \"/ab\", getting keys\n\t// with prefix \"/a\" will return all three, while with prefix \"/a/\" will return only\n\t// \"/a/b\" which is the correct answer.\n\tif recursive && !strings.HasSuffix(key, \"/\") {\n\t\tkey += \"/\"\n\t}\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(key, resourcePrefix) {\n\t\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"invalid key: %q lacks resource prefix: %q\", key, resourcePrefix)\n\t}\n\treturn key, nil\n}"
49. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator/interfaces.go",
  "description": "Get full authentication interfaces file"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage authenticator\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n)\n\n// Token checks a string value against a backing authentication store and\n// returns a Response or an error if the token could not be checked.\ntype Token interface {\n\tAuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error)\n}\n\n// Request attempts to extract authentication information from a request and\n// returns a Response or an error if the request could not be checked.\ntype Request interface {\n\tAuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error)\n}\n\n// TokenFunc is a function that implements the Token interface.\ntype TokenFunc func(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error)\n\n// AuthenticateToken implements authenticator.Token.\nfunc (f TokenFunc) AuthenticateToken(ctx context.Context, token string) (*Response, bool, error) {\n\treturn f(ctx, token)\n}\n\n// RequestFunc is a function that implements the Request interface.\ntype RequestFunc func(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error)\n\n// AuthenticateRequest implements authenticator.Request.\nfunc (f RequestFunc) AuthenticateRequest(req *http.Request) (*Response, bool, error) {\n\treturn f(req)\n}\n\n// Response is the struct returned by authenticator interfaces upon successful\n// authentication. It contains information about whether the authenticator\n// authenticated the request, information about the context of the\n// authentication, and information about the authenticated user.\ntype Response struct {\n\t// Audiences is the set of audiences the authenticator was able to validate\n\t// the token against. If the authenticator is not audience aware, this field\n\t// will be empty.\n\tAudiences Audiences\n\t// User is the UserInfo associated with the authentication context.\n\tUser user.Info\n}"
50. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer/interfaces.go",
  "description": "Get full authorization interfaces file"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage authorizer\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n)\n\n// Attributes is an interface used by an Authorizer to get information about a request\n// that is used to make an authorization decision.\ntype Attributes interface {\n\t// GetUser returns the user.Info object to authorize\n\tGetUser() user.Info\n\n\t// GetVerb returns the kube verb associated with API requests (this includes get, list, watch, create, update, patch, delete, deletecollection, and proxy),\n\t// or the lowercased HTTP verb associated with non-API requests (this includes get, put, post, patch, and delete)\n\tGetVerb() string\n\n\t// When IsReadOnly() == true, the request has no side effects, other than\n\t// caching, logging, and other incidentals.\n\tIsReadOnly() bool\n\n\t// The namespace of the object, if a request is for a REST object.\n\tGetNamespace() string\n\n\t// The kind of object, if a request is for a REST object.\n\tGetResource() string\n\n\t// GetSubresource returns the subresource being requested, if present\n\tGetSubresource() string\n\n\t// GetName returns the name of the object as parsed off the request.  This will not be present for all request types, but\n\t// will be present for: get, update, delete\n\tGetName() string\n\n\t// The group of the resource, if a request is for a REST object.\n\tGetAPIGroup() string\n\n\t// GetAPIVersion returns the version of the group requested, if a request is for a REST object.\n\tGetAPIVersion() string\n\n\t// IsResourceRequest returns true for requests to API resources, like /api/v1/nodes,\n\t// and false for non-resource endpoints like /api, /healthz\n\tIsResourceRequest() bool\n\n\t// GetPath returns the path of the request\n\tGetPath() string\n\n\t// ParseFieldSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.\n\t// It returns an error if the field selector cannot be parsed.\n\t// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.\n\tGetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error)\n\n\t// ParseLabelSelector is lazy, thread-safe, and stores the parsed result and error.\n\t// It returns an error if the label selector cannot be parsed.\n\t// The returned requirements must be treated as readonly and not modified.\n\tGetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error)\n}\n\n// Authorizer makes an authorization decision based on information gained by making\n// zero or more calls to methods of the Attributes interface.  It returns nil when an action is\n// authorized, otherwise it returns an error.\ntype Authorizer interface {\n\tAuthorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (authorized Decision, reason string, err error)\n}\n\ntype AuthorizerFunc func(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error)\n\nfunc (f AuthorizerFunc) Authorize(ctx context.Context, a Attributes) (Decision, string, error) {\n\treturn f(ctx, a)\n}\n\n// RuleResolver provides a mechanism for resolving the list of rules that apply to a given user within a namespace.\ntype RuleResolver interface {\n\t// RulesFor get the list of cluster wide rules, the list of rules in the specific namespace, incomplete status and errors.\n\tRulesFor(ctx context.Context, user user.Info, namespace string) ([]ResourceRuleInfo, []NonResourceRuleInfo, bool, error)\n}\n\n// RequestAttributesGetter provides a function that extracts Attributes from an http.Request\ntype RequestAttributesGetter interface {\n\tGetRequestAttributes(user.Info, *http.Request) Attributes\n}\n\n// AttributesRecord implements Attributes interface.\ntype AttributesRecord struct {\n\tUser            user.Info\n\tVerb            string\n\tNamespace       string\n\tAPIGroup        string\n\tAPIVersion      string\n\tResource        string\n\tSubresource     string\n\tName            string\n\tResourceRequest bool\n\tPath            string\n\n\tFieldSelectorRequirements fields.Requirements\n\tFieldSelectorParsingErr   error\n\tLabelSelectorRequirements labels.Requirements\n\tLabelSelectorParsingErr   error\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetUser() user.Info {\n\treturn a.User\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetVerb() string {\n\treturn a.Verb\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) IsReadOnly() bool {\n\treturn a.Verb == \"get\" || a.Verb == \"list\" || a.Verb == \"watch\"\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetNamespace() string {\n\treturn a.Namespace\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetResource() string {\n\treturn a.Resource\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetSubresource() string {\n\treturn a.Subresource\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetName() string {\n\treturn a.Name\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetAPIGroup() string {\n\treturn a.APIGroup\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetAPIVersion() string {\n\treturn a.APIVersion\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) IsResourceRequest() bool {\n\treturn a.ResourceRequest\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetPath() string {\n\treturn a.Path\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetFieldSelector() (fields.Requirements, error) {\n\treturn a.FieldSelectorRequirements, a.FieldSelectorParsingErr\n}\n\nfunc (a AttributesRecord) GetLabelSelector() (labels.Requirements, error) {\n\treturn a.LabelSelectorRequirements, a.LabelSelectorParsingErr\n}\n\ntype Decision int\n\nconst (\n\t// DecisionDeny means that an authorizer decided to deny the action.\n\tDecisionDeny Decision = iota\n\t// DecisionAllow means that an authorizer decided to allow the action.\n\tDecisionAllow\n\t// DecisionNoOpinion means that an authorizer has no opinion on whether\n\t// to allow or deny an action.\n\tDecisionNoOpinion\n)\n\nfunc (d Decision) String() string {\n\tswitch d {\n\tcase DecisionDeny:\n\t\treturn \"Deny\"\n\tcase DecisionAllow:\n\t\treturn \"Allow\"\n\tcase DecisionNoOpinion:\n\t\treturn \"NoOpinion\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Unknown (%d)\", int(d))\n\t}\n}"
51. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit/types.go",
  "description": "Get full audit types file"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage audit\n\nimport (\n\tauditinternal \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\"\n)\n\ntype Sink interface {\n\t// ProcessEvents handles events. Per audit ID it might be that ProcessEvents is called up to three times.\n\t// Errors might be logged by the sink itself. If an error should be fatal, leading to an internal\n\t// error, ProcessEvents is supposed to panic. The event must not be mutated and is reused by the caller\n\t// after the call returns, i.e. the sink has to make a deepcopy to keep a copy around if necessary.\n\t// Returns true on success, may return false on error.\n\tProcessEvents(events ...*auditinternal.Event) bool\n}\n\ntype Backend interface {\n\tSink\n\n\t// Run will initialize the backend. It must not block, but may run go routines in the background. If\n\t// stopCh is closed, it is supposed to stop them. Run will be called before the first call to ProcessEvents.\n\tRun(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error\n\n\t// Shutdown will synchronously shut down the backend while making sure that all pending\n\t// events are delivered. It can be assumed that this method is called after\n\t// the stopCh channel passed to the Run method has been closed.\n\tShutdown()\n\n\t// Returns the backend PluginName.\n\tString() string\n}"
52. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "ls -la /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/ 2>/dev/null | grep \"\\.go$\" | awk '{print $NF}'"
}
Output
"create.go\ncreate_test.go\ndelete.go\ndelete_test.go\ndoc.go\nget.go\nhelpers.go\nhelpers_test.go\nnamer.go\npatch.go\nresponse.go\nresponse_test.go\nrest.go\nrest_test.go\ntrace_util.go\nupdate.go\nwatch.go\nwatch_test.go"
53. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/groupversion.go | head -100"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage endpoints\n\nimport (\n\t\"path\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\trestful \"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n\n\tapidiscoveryv2 \"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n)\n\n// ConvertabilityChecker indicates what versions a GroupKind is available in.\ntype ConvertabilityChecker interface {\n\t// VersionsForGroupKind indicates what versions are available to convert a group kind. This determines\n\t// what our decoding abilities are.\n\tVersionsForGroupKind(gk schema.GroupKind) []schema.GroupVersion\n}\n\n// APIGroupVersion is a helper for exposing rest.Storage objects as http.Handlers via go-restful\n// It handles URLs of the form:\n// /${storage_key}[/${object_name}]\n// Where 'storage_key' points to a rest.Storage object stored in storage.\n// This object should contain all parameterization necessary for running a particular API version\ntype APIGroupVersion struct {\n\tStorage map[string]rest.Storage\n\n\tRoot string\n\n\t// GroupVersion is the external group version\n\tGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\n\n\t// AllServedVersionsByResource is indexed by resource and maps to a list of versions that resource exists in.\n\t// This was created so that StorageVersion for APIs can include a list of all version that are served for each\n\t// GroupResource tuple.\n\tAllServedVersionsByResource map[string][]string\n\n\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the Kubernetes APIVersion used for common objects in the apiserver\n\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n\t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects. If\n\t// empty, defaults to GroupVersion.\n\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n\n\t// Serializer is used to determine how to convert responses from API methods into bytes to send over\n\t// the wire.\n\tSerializer     runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n\n\tTyper                 runtime.ObjectTyper\n\tCreater               runtime.ObjectCreater\n\tConvertor             runtime.ObjectConvertor\n\tConvertabilityChecker ConvertabilityChecker\n\tDefaulter             runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n\tNamer                 runtime.Namer\n\tUnsafeConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor\n\tTypeConverter         managedfields.TypeConverter\n\n\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n\n\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\n\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\n\t// the create-on-update case\n\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n\n\tAdmit admission.Interface\n\n\tMinRequestTimeout time.Duration\n\n\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\n\t// 0 means no limit.\n\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64"

Output
"/*\nCopyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage endpoints\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"reflect\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\t\"unicode\"\n\n\trestful \"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n\t\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\"\n\n\t\"golang.org/x/text/cases\"\n\t\"golang.org/x/text/language\"\n\tapidiscoveryv2 \"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\"\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/conversion\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/deprecation\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n\tutilwarning \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/warning\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\tversioninfo \"k8s.io/component-base/version\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tRouteMetaGVK              = \"x-kubernetes-group-version-kind\"\n\tRouteMetaSelectableFields = \"x-kubernetes-selectable-fields\"\n\tRouteMetaAction           = \"x-kubernetes-action\"\n)\n\ntype APIInstaller struct {\n\tgroup             *APIGroupVersion\n\tprefix            string // Path prefix where API resources are to be registered.\n\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n}\n\n// Struct capturing information about an action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\ntype action struct {\n\tVerb          string               // Verb identifying the action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n\tPath          string               // The path of the action\n\tParams        []*restful.Parameter // List of parameters associated with the action.\n\tNamer         handlers.ScopeNamer\n\tAllNamespaces bool // true iff the action is namespaced but works on aggregate result for all namespaces\n}\n\nfunc ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(list []metav1.APIResource) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, error) {\n\tvar apiResourceList []apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery\n\tparentResources := make(map[string]int)\n\n\t// Loop through all top-level resources\n\tfor _, r := range list {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(r.Name, \"/\") {\n\t\t\t// Skip subresources for now so we can get the list of resources\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n\t\tif r.Namespaced {\n\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresource := apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n\t\t\tResource: r.Name,\n\t\t\tScope:    scope,\n\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{\n\t\t\t\tGroup:   r.Group,\n\t\t\t\tVersion: r.Version,\n\t\t\t\tKind:    r.Kind,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tVerbs:            r.Verbs,\n\t\t\tShortNames:       r.ShortNames,\n\t\t\tCategories:       r.Categories,\n\t\t\tSingularResource: r.SingularName,\n\t\t}\n\t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, resource)\n\t\tparentResources[r.Name] = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n\t}\n\n\t// Loop through all subresources\n\tfor _, r := range list {\n\t\t// Split resource name and subresource name\n\t\tsplit := strings.SplitN(r.Name, \"/\", 2)\n\n\t\tif len(split) != 2 {\n\t\t\t// Skip parent resources\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n\t\tif r.Namespaced {\n\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tparentidx, exists := parentResources[split[0]]\n\t\tif !exists {\n\t\t\t// If a subresource exists without a parent, create a parent\n\t\t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n\t\t\t\tResource: split[0],\n\t\t\t\tScope:    scope,\n\t\t\t\t// avoid nil panics in v0.26.0-v0.26.3 client-go clients\n\t\t\t\t// see https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/118361\n\t\t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{},\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tparentidx = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n\t\t\tparentResources[split[0]] = parentidx\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif apiResourceList[parentidx].Scope != scope {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Error: Parent %s (scope: %s) and subresource %s (scope: %s) scope do not match\", split[0], apiResourceList[parentidx].Scope, split[1], scope)\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsubresource := apidiscoveryv2.APISubresourceDiscovery{\n\t\t\tSubresource: split[1],\n\t\t\tVerbs:       r.Verbs,"
55. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/wrap.go",
  "description": "Read wrap filter"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage filters\n\nimport (\n\t\"net/http\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/metrics\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/httplog\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n)\n\n// WithPanicRecovery wraps an http Handler to recover and log panics (except in the special case of http.ErrAbortHandler panics, which suppress logging).\nfunc WithPanicRecovery(handler http.Handler, resolver request.RequestInfoResolver) http.Handler {\n\treturn withPanicRecovery(handler, func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request, err interface{}) {\n\t\tif err == http.ErrAbortHandler {\n\t\t\t// Honor the http.ErrAbortHandler sentinel panic value\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// If ServeHTTP panics, the server (the caller of ServeHTTP) assumes\n\t\t\t// that the effect of the panic was isolated to the active request.\n\t\t\t// It recovers the panic, logs a stack trace to the server error log,\n\t\t\t// and either closes the network connection or sends an HTTP/2\n\t\t\t// RST_STREAM, depending on the HTTP protocol. To abort a handler so\n\t\t\t// the client sees an interrupted response but the server doesn't log\n\t\t\t// an error, panic with the value ErrAbortHandler.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// Note that HandleCrash function is actually crashing, after calling the handlers\n\t\t\tif info, err := resolver.NewRequestInfo(req); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tmetrics.RecordRequestAbort(req, nil)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tmetrics.RecordRequestAbort(req, info)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t// This call can have different handlers, but the default chain rate limits. Call it after the metrics are updated\n\t\t\t// in case the rate limit delays it.  If you outrun the rate for this one timed out requests, something has gone\n\t\t\t// seriously wrong with your server, but generally having a logging signal for timeouts is useful.\n\t\t\truntime.HandleErrorWithContext(req.Context(), nil, \"Timeout or abort while handling\", \"method\", req.Method, \"URI\", req.RequestURI, \"auditID\", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(req.Context()))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\thttp.Error(w, \"This request caused apiserver to panic. Look in the logs for details.\", http.StatusInternalServerError)\n\t\tklog.ErrorS(nil, \"apiserver panic'd\", \"method\", req.Method, \"URI\", req.RequestURI, \"auditID\", audit.GetAuditIDTruncated(req.Context()))\n\t})\n}\n\n// WithHTTPLogging enables logging of incoming requests.\nfunc WithHTTPLogging(handler http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\treturn httplog.WithLogging(handler, httplog.DefaultStacktracePred)\n}\n\nfunc withPanicRecovery(handler http.Handler, crashHandler func(http.ResponseWriter, *http.Request, interface{})) http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\tdefer runtime.HandleCrash(func(err interface{}) {\n\t\t\tcrashHandler(w, req, err)\n\t\t})\n\n\t\t// Dispatch to the internal handler\n\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\t})\n}"
56. Bash @ -
