  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters/doc.go",
  "description": "Read server filters documentation"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\n// Package filters contains all the http handler chain filters which\n// are not api related.\npackage filters"
57. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go",
  "description": "Read authorization filter"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage filters\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n\tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\t// Annotation key names set in advanced audit\n\tdecisionAnnotationKey = \"authorization.k8s.io/decision\"\n\treasonAnnotationKey   = \"authorization.k8s.io/reason\"\n\n\t// Annotation values set in advanced audit\n\tdecisionAllow  = \"allow\"\n\tdecisionForbid = \"forbid\"\n\treasonError    = \"internal error\"\n)\n\ntype recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc func(ctx context.Context, authorized authorizer.Decision, err error, authStart time.Time, authFinish time.Time)\n\n// WithAuthorization passes all authorized requests on to handler, and returns a forbidden error otherwise.\nfunc WithAuthorization(hhandler http.Handler, auth authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {\n\treturn withAuthorization(hhandler, auth, s, recordAuthorizationMetrics)\n}\n\nfunc withAuthorization(handler http.Handler, a authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, metrics recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc) http.Handler {\n\tif a == nil {\n\t\tklog.Warning(\"Authorization is disabled\")\n\t\treturn handler\n\t}\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\tctx := req.Context()\n\t\tauthorizationStart := time.Now()\n\n\t\tattributes, err := GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tauthorized, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)\n\n\t\tauthorizationFinish := time.Now()\n\t\trequest.TrackAuthorizationLatency(ctx, authorizationFinish.Sub(authorizationStart))\n\t\tdefer func() {\n\t\t\tmetrics(ctx, authorized, err, authorizationStart, authorizationFinish)\n\t\t}()\n\n\t\t// an authorizer like RBAC could encounter evaluation errors and still allow the request, so authorizer decision is checked before error here.\n\t\tif authorized == authorizer.DecisionAllow {\n\t\t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,\n\t\t\t\tdecisionAnnotationKey, decisionAllow,\n\t\t\t\treasonAnnotationKey, reason)\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotation(ctx, reasonAnnotationKey, reasonError)\n\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tklog.V(4).InfoS(\"Forbidden\", \"URI\", req.RequestURI, \"reason\", reason)\n\t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,\n\t\t\tdecisionAnnotationKey, decisionForbid,\n\t\t\treasonAnnotationKey, reason)\n\t\tresponsewriters.Forbidden(attributes, w, req, reason, s)\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx context.Context) (authorizer.Attributes, error) {\n\tattribs := authorizer.AttributesRecord{}\n\n\tuser, ok := request.UserFrom(ctx)\n\tif ok {\n\t\tattribs.User = user\n\t}\n\n\trequestInfo, found := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n\tif !found {\n\t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"no RequestInfo found in the context\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Start with common attributes that apply to resource and non-resource requests\n\tattribs.ResourceRequest = requestInfo.IsResourceRequest\n\tattribs.Path = requestInfo.Path\n\tattribs.Verb = requestInfo.Verb\n\n\tattribs.APIGroup = requestInfo.APIGroup\n\tattribs.APIVersion = requestInfo.APIVersion\n\tattribs.Resource = requestInfo.Resource\n\tattribs.Subresource = requestInfo.Subresource\n\tattribs.Namespace = requestInfo.Namespace\n\tattribs.Name = requestInfo.Name\n\n\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.AuthorizeWithSelectors) {\n\t\t// parsing here makes it easy to keep the AttributesRecord type value-only and avoids any mutex copies when\n\t\t// doing shallow copies in other steps.\n\t\tif len(requestInfo.FieldSelector) > 0 {\n\t\t\tfieldSelector, err := fields.ParseSelector(requestInfo.FieldSelector)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tattribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = nil, err\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif requirements := fieldSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tattribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = fieldSelector.Requirements(), nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(requestInfo.LabelSelector) > 0 {\n\t\t\tlabelSelector, err := labels.Parse(requestInfo.LabelSelector)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tattribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = nil, err\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tif requirements, _ /*selectable*/ := labelSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t\tattribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = requirements, nil\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &attribs, nil\n}"
58. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/audit.go",
  "description": "Read audit filter"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage filters\n\nimport (\n\t\"bufio\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\tauditinternal \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/responsewriter\"\n)\n\n// WithAudit decorates a http.Handler with audit logging information for all the\n// requests coming to the server. Audit level is decided according to requests'\n// attributes and audit policy. Logs are emitted to the audit sink to\n// process events. If sink or audit policy is nil, no decoration takes place.\nfunc WithAudit(handler http.Handler, sink audit.Sink, policy audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator, longRunningCheck request.LongRunningRequestCheck) http.Handler {\n\tif sink == nil || policy == nil {\n\t\treturn handler\n\t}\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\tac, err := evaluatePolicyAndCreateAuditEvent(req, policy, sink)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tutilruntime.HandleError(fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create audit event: %v\", err))\n\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New(\"failed to create audit event\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !ac.Enabled() {\n\t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tctx := req.Context()\n\n\t\tif processed := ac.ProcessEventStage(ctx, auditinternal.StageRequestReceived); !processed {\n\t\t\taudit.ApiserverAuditDroppedCounter.WithContext(ctx).Inc()\n\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New(\"failed to store audit event\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// intercept the status code\n\t\tisLongRunning := false\n\t\tif longRunningCheck != nil {\n\t\t\tri, _ := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n\t\t\tif longRunningCheck(req, ri) {\n\t\t\t\tisLongRunning = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\trespWriter := decorateResponseWriter(ctx, w, isLongRunning)\n\n\t\t// send audit event when we leave this func, either via a panic or cleanly. In the case of long\n\t\t// running requests, this will be the second audit event.\n\t\tdefer func() {\n\t\t\tif r := recover(); r != nil {\n\t\t\t\tdefer panic(r)\n\t\t\t\tac.SetEventResponseStatus(&metav1.Status{\n\t\t\t\t\tCode:    http.StatusInternalServerError,\n\t\t\t\t\tStatus:  metav1.StatusFailure,\n\t\t\t\t\tReason:  metav1.StatusReasonInternalError,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage: fmt.Sprintf(\"APIServer panic'd: %v\", r),\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\tac.ProcessEventStage(ctx, auditinternal.StagePanic)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// if no StageResponseStarted event was sent b/c neither a status code nor a body was sent, fake it here\n\t\t\t// But Audit-Id http header will only be sent when http.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader is called.\n\t\t\tfakedSuccessStatus := &metav1.Status{\n\t\t\t\tCode:    http.StatusOK,\n\t\t\t\tStatus:  metav1.StatusSuccess,\n\t\t\t\tMessage: \"Connection closed early\",\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif ac.GetEventResponseStatus() == nil {\n\t\t\t\tac.SetEventResponseStatus(fakedSuccessStatus)\n\t\t\t\tif isLongRunning {\n\t\t\t\t\t// A nil ResponseStatus means the writer never processed the ResponseStarted stage, so do that now.\n\t\t\t\t\tac.ProcessEventStage(ctx, auditinternal.StageResponseStarted)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\twriteLatencyToAnnotation(ctx)\n\t\t\tac.ProcessEventStage(ctx, auditinternal.StageResponseComplete)\n\t\t}()\n\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(respWriter, req)\n\t})\n}\n\n// evaluatePolicyAndCreateAuditEvent is responsible for evaluating the audit\n// policy configuration applicable to the request and initializing the audit\n// context with the audit config for the request, the sink to write to, and the request metadata.\n// - error if anything bad happened\nfunc evaluatePolicyAndCreateAuditEvent(req *http.Request, policy audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator, sink audit.Sink) (*audit.AuditContext, error) {\n\tctx := req.Context()\n\tac := audit.AuditContextFrom(ctx)\n\tif ac == nil {\n\t\t// Auditing not configured.\n\t\treturn nil, nil\n\t}\n\n\tattribs, err := GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn ac, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to GetAuthorizerAttributes: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\trac := policy.EvaluatePolicyRule(attribs)\n\taudit.ObservePolicyLevel(ctx, rac.Level)\n\terr = ac.Init(rac, sink)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to initialize audit context: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif rac.Level == auditinternal.LevelNone {\n\t\t// Don't audit.\n\t\treturn ac, nil\n\t}\n\n\trequestReceivedTimestamp, ok := request.ReceivedTimestampFrom(ctx)\n\tif !ok {\n\t\trequestReceivedTimestamp = time.Now()\n\t}\n\taudit.LogRequestMetadata(ctx, req, requestReceivedTimestamp, attribs)\n\n\treturn ac, nil\n}\n\n// writeLatencyToAnnotation writes the latency incurred in different\n// layers of the apiserver to the annotations of the audit object.\nfunc writeLatencyToAnnotation(ctx context.Context) {\n\tac := audit.AuditContextFrom(ctx)\n\t// we will track latency in annotation only when the total latency\n\t// of the given request exceeds 500ms, this is in keeping with the\n\t// traces in rest/handlers for create, delete, update,\n\t// get, list, and deletecollection.\n\tconst threshold = 500 * time.Millisecond\n\tlatency := time.Since(ac.GetEventRequestReceivedTimestamp().Time)\n\tif latency <= threshold {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// if we are tracking latency incurred inside different layers within\n\t// the apiserver, add these as annotation to the audit event object.\n\tlayerLatencies := request.AuditAnnotationsFromLatencyTrackers(ctx)\n\tif len(layerLatencies) == 0 {\n\t\t// latency tracking is not enabled for this request\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// record the total latency for this request, for convenience.\n\tlayerLatencies[\"apiserver.latency.k8s.io/total\"] = latency.String()\n\taudit.AddAuditAnnotationsMap(ctx, layerLatencies)\n}\n\nfunc decorateResponseWriter(ctx context.Context, responseWriter http.ResponseWriter, processResponseStartedStage bool) http.ResponseWriter {\n\tdelegate := &auditResponseWriter{\n\t\tctx:            ctx,\n\t\tResponseWriter: responseWriter,\n\n\t\tprocessResponseStartedStage: processResponseStartedStage,\n\t}\n\n\treturn responsewriter.WrapForHTTP1Or2(delegate)\n}\n\nvar _ http.ResponseWriter = &auditResponseWriter{}\nvar _ responsewriter.UserProvidedDecorator = &auditResponseWriter{}\n\n// auditResponseWriter intercepts WriteHeader, sets it in the event. If the sink is set, it will\n// create immediately an event (for long running requests).\ntype auditResponseWriter struct {\n\thttp.ResponseWriter\n\tctx  context.Context\n\tonce sync.Once\n\n\tprocessResponseStartedStage bool\n}\n\nfunc (a *auditResponseWriter) Unwrap() http.ResponseWriter {\n\treturn a.ResponseWriter\n}\n\nfunc (a *auditResponseWriter) processCode(code int) {\n\ta.once.Do(func() {\n\t\tac := audit.AuditContextFrom(a.ctx)\n\t\tac.SetEventResponseStatusCode(int32(code))\n\t\tif a.processResponseStartedStage {\n\t\t\tac.ProcessEventStage(a.ctx, auditinternal.StageResponseStarted)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc (a *auditResponseWriter) Write(bs []byte) (int, error) {\n\t// the Go library calls WriteHeader internally if no code was written yet. But this will go unnoticed for us\n\ta.processCode(http.StatusOK)\n\treturn a.ResponseWriter.Write(bs)\n}\n\nfunc (a *auditResponseWriter) WriteHeader(code int) {\n\ta.processCode(code)\n\ta.ResponseWriter.WriteHeader(code)\n}\n\nfunc (a *auditResponseWriter) Hijack() (net.Conn, *bufio.ReadWriter, error) {\n\t// fake a response status before protocol switch happens\n\ta.processCode(http.StatusSwitchingProtocols)\n\n\t// the outer ResponseWriter object returned by WrapForHTTP1Or2 implements\n\t// http.Hijacker if the inner object (a.ResponseWriter) implements http.Hijacker.\n\treturn a.ResponseWriter.(http.Hijacker).Hijack()\n}"
59. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go | sed -n '200,350p'"
}
Output
"// Creater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object.\ntype Creater interface {\n\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.\n\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n\tNew() runtime.Object\n\n\t// Create creates a new version of a resource.\n\tCreate(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n}\n\n// NamedCreater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object using a name parameter.\ntype NamedCreater interface {\n\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.\n\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n\tNew() runtime.Object\n\n\t// Create creates a new version of a resource. It expects a name parameter from the path.\n\t// This is needed for create operations on subresources which include the name of the parent\n\t// resource in the path.\n\tCreate(ctx context.Context, name string, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n}\n\n// SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver adds configuration options to a Creater for subresources.\ntype SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver interface {\n\t// PreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate indicates that a\n\t// handler should preserve fields of ObjectMeta that are managed by the system.\n\tPreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate() bool\n}\n\n// UpdatedObjectInfo provides information about an updated object to an Updater.\n// It requires access to the old object in order to return the newly updated object.\ntype UpdatedObjectInfo interface {\n\t// Returns preconditions built from the updated object, if applicable.\n\t// May return nil, or a preconditions object containing nil fields,\n\t// if no preconditions can be determined from the updated object.\n\tPreconditions() *metav1.Preconditions\n\n\t// UpdatedObject returns the updated object, given a context and old object.\n\t// The only time an empty oldObj should be passed in is if a \"create on update\" is occurring (there is no oldObj).\n\tUpdatedObject(ctx context.Context, oldObj runtime.Object) (newObj runtime.Object, err error)\n}\n\n// ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\n// if the hook cannot be completed. A ValidateObjectFunc may NOT transform the provided\n// object.\ntype ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\n\n// ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \"admit everything\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\nfunc ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ValidateObjectUpdateFunc is a function to act on a given object and its predecessor.\n// An error may be returned if the hook cannot be completed. An UpdateObjectFunc\n// may NOT transform the provided object.\ntype ValidateObjectUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error\n\n// ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc is a \"admit everything\" instance of ValidateObjectUpdateFunc.\nfunc ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error {\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Updater is an object that can update an instance of a RESTful object.\ntype Updater interface {\n\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Update after request data has been put into it.\n\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n\tNew() runtime.Object\n\n\t// Update finds a resource in the storage and updates it. Some implementations\n\t// may allow updates creates the object - they should set the created boolean\n\t// to true.\n\tUpdate(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\n}\n\n// CreaterUpdater is a storage object that must support both create and update.\n// Go prevents embedded interfaces that implement the same method.\ntype CreaterUpdater interface {\n\tCreater\n\tUpdate(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\n}\n\n// CreaterUpdater must satisfy the Updater interface.\nvar _ Updater = CreaterUpdater(nil)\n\n// Patcher is a storage object that supports both get and update.\ntype Patcher interface {\n\tGetter\n\tUpdater\n}\n\n// Watcher should be implemented by all Storage objects that\n// want to offer the ability to watch for changes through the watch api.\ntype Watcher interface {\n\t// 'label' selects on labels; 'field' selects on the object's fields. Not all fields\n\t// are supported; an error should be returned if 'field' tries to select on a field that\n\t// isn't supported. 'resourceVersion' allows for continuing/starting a watch at a\n\t// particular version.\n\tWatch(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\n}\n\n// StandardStorage is an interface covering the common verbs. Provided for testing whether a\n// resource satisfies the normal storage methods. Use Storage when passing opaque storage objects.\ntype StandardStorage interface {\n\tGetter\n\tLister\n\tCreaterUpdater\n\tGracefulDeleter\n\tCollectionDeleter\n\tWatcher\n\n\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n\t// for being called more than once.\n\tDestroy()\n}\n\n// Redirector know how to return a remote resource's location.\ntype Redirector interface {\n\t// ResourceLocation should return the remote location of the given resource, and an optional transport to use to request it, or an error.\n\tResourceLocation(ctx context.Context, id string) (remoteLocation *url.URL, transport http.RoundTripper, err error)\n}\n\n// Responder abstracts the normal response behavior for a REST method and is passed to callers that\n// may wish to handle the response directly in some cases, but delegate to the normal error or object\n// behavior in other cases.\ntype Responder interface {\n\t// Object writes the provided object to the response. Invoking this method multiple times is undefined.\n\tObject(statusCode int, obj runtime.Object)\n\t// Error writes the provided error to the response. This method may only be invoked once.\n\tError(err error)\n}\n\n// Connecter is a storage object that responds to a connection request.\ntype Connecter interface {\n\t// Connect returns an http.Handler that will handle the request/response for a given API invocation.\n\t// The provided responder may be used for common API responses. The responder will write both status\n\t// code and body, so the ServeHTTP method should exit after invoking the responder. The Handler will\n\t// be used for a single API request and then discarded. The Responder is guaranteed to write to the\n\t// same http.ResponseWriter passed to ServeHTTP.\n\tConnect(ctx context.Context, id string, options runtime.Object, r Responder) (http.Handler, error)\n\n\t// NewConnectOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\n\t// options to the Connect method. If nil, then a nil options object is passed to\n\t// Connect. It may return a bool and a string. If true, the value of the request\n\t// path below the object will be included as the named string in the serialization\n\t// of the runtime object.\n\tNewConnectOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\n\n\t// ConnectMethods returns the list of HTTP methods handled by Connect\n\tConnectMethods() []string"
60. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go | sed -n '150,300p'"
}
Output
"const (\n\tCreate  Operation = \"CREATE\"\n\tUpdate  Operation = \"UPDATE\"\n\tDelete  Operation = \"DELETE\"\n\tConnect Operation = \"CONNECT\"\n)\n\n// PluginInitializer is used for initialization of shareable resources between admission plugins.\n// After initialization the resources have to be set separately\ntype PluginInitializer interface {\n\tInitialize(plugin Interface)\n}\n\n// InitializationValidator holds ValidateInitialization functions, which are responsible for validation of initialized\n// shared resources and should be implemented on admission plugins\ntype InitializationValidator interface {\n\tValidateInitialization() error\n}\n\n// ConfigProvider provides a way to get configuration for an admission plugin based on its name\ntype ConfigProvider interface {\n\tConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error)\n}"
61. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go | sed -n '50,200p'"
}
Output
"\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\n\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\n\n\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\n\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\n\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\n\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\n\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\n\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\n\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\n\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\n\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\n\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\n\t// order to handle \"normal\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\n\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\n\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\n\tDirector http.Handler\n}\n\n// HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\n// It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\ntype HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\n\nfunc NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\n\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\n\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\n\t\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\n\t}\n\n\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\n\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\n\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\n\t\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\n\t})\n\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\n\t\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\n\t})\n\n\tdirector := director{\n\t\tname:               name,\n\t\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n\t\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n\t}\n\n\treturn &APIServerHandler{\n\t\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\n\t\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n\t\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n\t\tDirector:           director,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\n\tvar handledPaths []string\n\t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\n\tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n\t\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\n\t}\n\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\n\tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\n\n\treturn handledPaths\n}\n\ntype director struct {\n\tname               string\n\tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n\tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\n}\n\nfunc (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\tpath := req.URL.Path\n\n\t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\n\tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase ws.RootPath() == \"/apis\":\n\t\t\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\n\t\t\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\n\t\t\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\n\t\t\tif path == \"/apis\" || path == \"/apis/\" {\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\n\t\t\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\n\t\t\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether\n\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful\", d.name, req.Method, path)\n\td.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n}\n\n// TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?\nfunc logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {\n\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"recover from panic situation: - %v\\r\\n\", panicReason))\n\tfor i := 2; ; i++ {\n\t\t_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %s:%d\\r\\n\", file, line))\n\t}\n\tklog.Errorln(buffer.String())\n\n\theaders := http.Header{}\n\tif ct := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\"); len(ct) > 0 {\n\t\theaders.Set(\"Accept\", ct)\n\t}\n\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", \"\", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})\n}\n\nfunc serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {\n\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(\n\t\tapierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),\n\t\ts,\n\t\tschema.GroupVersion{},\n\t\tresp,\n\t\trequest.Request,\n\t)\n}\n\n// ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\ta.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n}"
62. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go | head -200",
  "description": "Read server config for filter chain setup"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/base32\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"os\"\n\tgoruntime \"runtime\"\n\t\"runtime/debug\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"sync/atomic\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/google/uuid\"\n\t\"golang.org/x/crypto/cryptobyte\"\n\tjsonpatch \"gopkg.in/evanphx/json-patch.v4\"\n\n\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/version\"\n\tutilwaitgroup \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory\"\n\tauthenticatorunion \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/union\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n\tdiscoveryendpoint \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filterlatency\"\n\tgenericapifilters \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/impersonation\"\n\tapiopenapi \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/openapi\"\n\tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n\tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n\tgenericregistry \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/dynamiccertificates\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/egressselector\"\n\tgenericfilters \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/flagz\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routine\"\n\tserverstore \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage\"\n\tstoragevalue \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/value\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\tutilflowcontrol \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol\"\n\tflowcontrolrequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/informers\"\n\trestclient \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n\tbasecompatibility \"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/logs\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/features\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/slis\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\topenapicommon \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/spec3\"\n\t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n\t\"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n\tutilsnet \"k8s.io/utils/net\"\n\n\t// install apis\n\t_ \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/install\"\n)\n\n// hostnameFunc is a function to set the hostnameFunc of this apiserver.\n// To be used for testing purpose only, to simulate scenarios where multiple apiservers\n// exist. In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\nvar (\n\thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\n)\n\nconst (\n\t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\n\tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \"/api\"\n\n\t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\n\tAPIGroupPrefix = \"/apis\"\n)\n\n// Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\n// Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\ntype Config struct {\n\t// SecureServing is required to serve https\n\tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\n\n\t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\n\tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\n\n\t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\n\tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\n\n\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n\t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\n\t// TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\n\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n\n\t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\n\t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\n\tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\n\n\t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\n\t// in a given namespace\n\tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\n\t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\n\t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\n\tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\n\tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\n\tHSTSDirectives        []string\n\t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\n\tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\n\n\tEnableIndex     bool\n\tEnableProfiling bool\n\tDebugSocketPath string\n\tEnableDiscovery bool\n\n\t// Requires generic profiling enabled\n\tEnableContentionProfiling bool\n\tEnableMetrics             bool\n\n\tDisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n\t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\n\tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\n\n\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n\t// AuditBackend is where audit events are sent to.\n\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\n\t// AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator makes the decision of whether and how to audit log a request.\n\tAuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\n\t// ExternalAddress is the host name to use for external (public internet) facing URLs (e.g. Swagger)\n\t// Will default to a value based on secure serving info and available ipv4 IPs.\n\tExternalAddress string\n\n\t// TracerProvider can provide a tracer, which records spans for distributed tracing.\n\tTracerProvider tracing.TracerProvider\n\n\t//===========================================================================\n\t// Fields you probably don't care about changing\n\t//===========================================================================\n\n\t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\n\tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\n\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n\t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\n\t// always reported\n\tDiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n\t// The default set of healthz checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n\tHealthzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n\t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically."
63. Bash @ -

  "command": "find /workspace/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server -name \"*handler*.go\" ! -name \"*test*\" | head -5",

Output

64. Bash @ -
Input

  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/ | head -50",

Output
"tree HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/\n\nOWNERS\naudit.go\naudit_init.go\naudit_init_test.go\naudit_test.go\nauthentication.go\nauthentication_test.go\nauthn_audit.go\nauthn_audit_test.go\nauthorization.go\nauthorization_test.go\ncachecontrol.go\ncachecontrol_test.go\ndoc.go\nimpersonation/\nmetrics.go\nmetrics_test.go\nmux_discovery_complete.go\nmux_discovery_complete_test.go\nread_write_deadline_test.go\nrequest_deadline.go\nrequest_deadline_test.go\nrequest_received_time.go\nrequest_received_time_test.go\nrequestinfo.go\nrequestinfo_test.go\nstorageversion.go\ntraces.go\nwarning.go\nwarning_test.go\nwebhook_duration.go"
65. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go | head -300",
  "description": "Read handler setup code"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\trt \"runtime\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n\n\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\"\n)\n\n// APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\n// This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\n// the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\n// and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\n// FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\n\t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\n\t// chain and then call the Director.\n\tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\n\t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\n\tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n\t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\n\t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\n\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\n\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\n\n\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\n\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\n\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\n\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\n\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\n\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\n\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\n\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\n\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\n\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\n\t// order to handle \"normal\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\n\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\n\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\n\tDirector http.Handler\n}\n\n// HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\n// It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\ntype HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\n\nfunc NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\n\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\n\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\n\t\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\n\t}\n\n\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\n\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\n\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\n\t\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\n\t})\n\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\n\t\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\n\t})\n\n\tdirector := director{\n\t\tname:               name,\n\t\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n\t\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n\t}\n\n\treturn &APIServerHandler{\n\t\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\n\t\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n\t\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n\t\tDirector:           director,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\n\tvar handledPaths []string\n\t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\n\tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n\t\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\n\t}\n\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\n\tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\n\n\treturn handledPaths\n}\n\ntype director struct {\n\tname               string\n\tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n\tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\n}\n\nfunc (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\tpath := req.URL.Path\n\n\t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\n\tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase ws.RootPath() == \"/apis\":\n\t\t\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\n\t\t\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\n\t\t\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\n\t\t\tif path == \"/apis\" || path == \"/apis/\" {\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\n\t\t\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\n\t\t\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\n\t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n\t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n\t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n\t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether\n\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful\", d.name, req.Method, path)\n\td.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n}\n\n// TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?\nfunc logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {\n\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"recover from panic situation: - %v\\r\\n\", panicReason))\n\tfor i := 2; ; i++ {\n\t\t_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %s:%d\\r\\n\", file, line))\n\t}\n\tklog.Errorln(buffer.String())\n\n\theaders := http.Header{}\n\tif ct := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\"); len(ct) > 0 {\n\t\theaders.Set(\"Accept\", ct)\n\t}\n\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", \"\", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})\n}\n\nfunc serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {\n\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(\n\t\tapierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),\n\t\ts,\n\t\tschema.GroupVersion{},\n\t\tresp,\n\t\trequest.Request,\n\t)\n}\n\n// ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler\nfunc (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\ta.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n}"
66. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go",
  "description": "Read authentication filter"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage filters\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/headerrequest\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n\tgenericapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n\tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n)\n\ntype authenticationRecordMetricsFunc func(context.Context, *authenticator.Response, bool, error, authenticator.Audiences, time.Time, time.Time)\n\n// WithAuthentication creates an http handler that tries to authenticate the given request as a user, and then\n// stores any such user found onto the provided context for the request. If authentication fails or returns an error\n// the failed handler is used. On success, \"Authorization\" header is removed from the request and handler\n// is invoked to serve the request.\nfunc WithAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig) http.Handler {\n\treturn withAuthentication(handler, auth, failed, apiAuds, requestHeaderConfig, recordAuthenticationMetrics)\n}\n\nfunc withAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig, metrics authenticationRecordMetricsFunc) http.Handler {\n\tif auth == nil {\n\t\tklog.Warning(\"Authentication is disabled\")\n\t\treturn handler\n\t}\n\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig := &authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig{\n\t\tUsernameHeaders:     headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-User\"},\n\t\tUIDHeaders:          headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Uid\"},\n\t\tGroupHeaders:        headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Group\"},\n\t\tExtraHeaderPrefixes: headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Extra-\"},\n\t}\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\tauthenticationStart := time.Now()\n\n\t\tif len(apiAuds) > 0 {\n\t\t\treq = req.WithContext(authenticator.WithAudiences(req.Context(), apiAuds))\n\t\t}\n\t\tresp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)\n\t\tauthenticationFinish := time.Now()\n\t\tgenericapirequest.TrackAuthenticationLatency(req.Context(), authenticationFinish.Sub(authenticationStart))\n\t\tdefer func() {\n\t\t\tmetrics(req.Context(), resp, ok, err, apiAuds, authenticationStart, authenticationFinish)\n\t\t}()\n\t\tif err != nil || !ok {\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tklog.ErrorS(err, \"Unable to authenticate the request\")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfailed.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, resp.Audiences) {\n\t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"unable to match the audience: %v , accepted: %v\", resp.Audiences, apiAuds)\n\t\t\tklog.Error(err)\n\t\t\tfailed.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// authorization header is not required anymore in case of a successful authentication.\n\t\treq.Header.Del(\"Authorization\")\n\n\t\t// delete standard front proxy headers\n\t\theaderrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(\n\t\t\treq.Header,\n\t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\n\t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\n\t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\n\t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\n\t\t)\n\n\t\t// also delete any custom front proxy headers\n\t\tif requestHeaderConfig != nil {\n\t\t\theaderrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(\n\t\t\t\treq.Header,\n\t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\n\t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\n\t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\n\t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,\n\t\t// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.\n\t\t// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these\n\t\t// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the\n\t\t// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).\n\t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 && isAnonymousUser(resp.User) {\n\t\t\t// limit this connection to just this request,\n\t\t\t// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection\n\t\t\t// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"close\")\n\t\t}\n\t\treq = req.WithContext(genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User))\n\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc Unauthorized(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\t// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,\n\t\t// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.\n\t\t// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these\n\t\t// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the\n\t\t// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).\n\t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 {\n\t\t\t// limit this connection to just this request,\n\t\t\t// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection\n\t\t\t// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c\n\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"close\")\n\t\t}\n\t\tctx := req.Context()\n\t\trequestInfo, found := genericapirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New(\"no RequestInfo found in the context\"))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tgv := schema.GroupVersion{Group: requestInfo.APIGroup, Version: requestInfo.APIVersion}\n\t\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewUnauthorized(\"Unauthorized\"), s, gv, w, req)\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, responseAudiences authenticator.Audiences) bool {\n\tif len(apiAuds) == 0 || len(responseAudiences) == 0 {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\treturn len(apiAuds.Intersect(responseAudiences)) > 0\n}\n\nfunc isAnonymousUser(u user.Info) bool {\n\tif u.GetName() == user.Anonymous {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\tfor _, group := range u.GetGroups() {\n\t\tif group == user.AllUnauthenticated {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}"
67. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/requestinfo.go | head -150",
  "description": "Read request info filter"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage filters\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n)\n\n// WithRequestInfo attaches a RequestInfo to the context.\nfunc WithRequestInfo(handler http.Handler, resolver request.RequestInfoResolver) http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n\t\tctx := req.Context()\n\t\tinfo, err := resolver.NewRequestInfo(req)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to create RequestInfo: %v\", err))\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treq = req.WithContext(request.WithRequestInfo(ctx, info))\n\n\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\t})\n}"

"<persisted-output>\nOutput too large (34.9KB). Full output saved to: /logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/752ac532-984b-4a0a-a7cc-d440e8e44976/tool-results/toolu_01WE1FkQ7guxmEtaNoJuxm67.txt\n\nPreview (first 2KB):\n\t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\n\tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\n\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n\t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\n\t// always reported\n\tDiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n\t// The default set of healthz checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n\tHealthzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n\t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n\tLivezChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n\t// The default set of readyz-only checks. There might be more added via AddReadyzChecks dynamically.\n\tReadyzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n\tFlagz        flagz.Reader\n\t// LegacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup. New API servers don't generally have legacy groups at all.\n\tLegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n\t// RequestInfoResolver is used to assign attributes (used by admission and authorization) based on a request URL.\n\t// Use-cases that are like kubelets may need to customize this.\n\tRequestInfoResolver apirequest.RequestInfoResolver\n\t// Serializer is required and provides the interface for serializing and converting objects to and from the wire\n\t// The default (api.Codecs) usually works fine.\n\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n...\n</persisted-output>"

77. Bash @ -

{

Output
"\t}\n\n\t// if there is no port, and we listen on one securely, use that one\n\tif _, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.ExternalAddress); err != nil {\n\t\tif c.SecureServing == nil {\n\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"cannot derive external address port without listening on a secure port.\")\n\t\t}\n\t\t_, port, err := c.SecureServing.HostPort()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"cannot derive external address from the secure port: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tc.ExternalAddress = net.JoinHostPort(c.ExternalAddress, strconv.Itoa(port))\n\t}\n\tcompleteOpenAPI(c.OpenAPIConfig, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\n\tcompleteOpenAPIV3(c.OpenAPIV3Config, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\n\n\tif c.DiscoveryAddresses == nil {\n\t\tc.DiscoveryAddresses = discovery.DefaultAddresses{DefaultAddress: c.ExternalAddress}\n\t}\n\n\tAuthorizeClientBearerToken(c.LoopbackClientConfig, &c.Authentication, &c.Authorization)\n\n\tif c.RequestInfoResolver == nil {\n\t\tc.RequestInfoResolver = NewRequestInfoResolver(c)\n\t}\n\n\tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\n\t\tif c.RESTOptionsGetter == nil {\n\t\t\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistry()\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistryWithIdentity(func(groupResource schema.GroupResource) string {\n\t\t\t\t// use the storage prefix as the key if possible\n\t\t\t\tif opts, err := c.RESTOptionsGetter.GetRESTOptions(groupResource, nil); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn opts.ResourcePrefix\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// otherwise return \"\" to use the default key (parent GV name)\n\t\t\t\treturn \"\"\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn CompletedConfig{&completedConfig{c, informers}}\n}\n\n// Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\n// from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\nfunc (c *RecommendedConfig) Complete() CompletedConfig {\n\treturn c.Config.Complete(c.SharedInformerFactory)\n}\n\nvar defaultAllowedMediaTypes = []string{\n\truntime.ContentTypeJSON,\n\truntime.ContentTypeYAML,\n\truntime.ContentTypeProtobuf,\n}\n\n// New creates a new server which logically combines the handling chain with the passed server.\n// name is used to differentiate for logging. The handler chain in particular can be difficult as it starts delegating.\n// delegationTarget may not be nil.\nfunc (c completedConfig) New(name string, delegationTarget DelegationTarget) (*GenericAPIServer, error) {\n\tif c.Serializer == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.Serializer == nil\")\n\t}\n\tallowedMediaTypes := defaultAllowedMediaTypes\n\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n\t\tallowedMediaTypes = append(allowedMediaTypes, runtime.ContentTypeCBOR)\n\t}\n\tfor _, info := range c.Serializer.SupportedMediaTypes() {\n\t\tvar ok bool\n\t\tfor _, mt := range allowedMediaTypes {\n\t\t\tif info.MediaType == mt {\n\t\t\t\tok = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"refusing to create new apiserver %q with support for media type %q (allowed media types are: %s)\", name, info.MediaType, strings.Join(allowedMediaTypes, \", \"))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif c.LoopbackClientConfig == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.LoopbackClientConfig == nil\")\n\t}\n\tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil\")\n\t}\n\n\thandlerChainBuilder := func(handler http.Handler) http.Handler {\n\t\treturn c.BuildHandlerChainFunc(handler, c.Config)\n\t}\n\n\tvar debugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n\tif c.DebugSocketPath != \"\" {\n\t\tdebugSocket = routes.NewDebugSocket(c.DebugSocketPath)\n\t}\n\n\tapiServerHandler := NewAPIServerHandler(name, c.Serializer, handlerChainBuilder, delegationTarget.UnprotectedHandler())\n\n\ts := &GenericAPIServer{\n\t\tdiscoveryAddresses:             c.DiscoveryAddresses,\n\t\tLoopbackClientConfig:           c.LoopbackClientConfig,\n\t\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes:         c.LegacyAPIGroupPrefixes,\n\t\tadmissionControl:               c.AdmissionControl,\n\t\tSerializer:                     c.Serializer,\n\t\tAuditBackend:                   c.AuditBackend,\n\t\tAuthorizer:                     c.Authorization.Authorizer,\n\t\tdelegationTarget:               delegationTarget,\n\t\tEquivalentResourceRegistry:     c.EquivalentResourceRegistry,\n\t\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup: c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup,\n\t\tWatchRequestWaitGroup:          c.WatchRequestWaitGroup,\n\t\tHandler:                        apiServerHandler,\n\t\tUnprotectedDebugSocket:         debugSocket,\n\n\t\tlistedPathProvider: apiServerHandler,\n\n\t\tminRequestTimeout:                   time.Duration(c.MinRequestTimeout) * time.Second,\n\t\tShutdownTimeout:                     c.RequestTimeout,\n\t\tShutdownDelayDuration:               c.ShutdownDelayDuration,\n\t\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod: c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod,\n\t\tSecureServingInfo:                   c.SecureServing,\n\t\tExternalAddress:                     c.ExternalAddress,\n\n\t\topenAPIConfig:           c.OpenAPIConfig,\n\t\topenAPIV3Config:         c.OpenAPIV3Config,\n\t\tskipOpenAPIInstallation: c.SkipOpenAPIInstallation,\n\n\t\tpostStartHooks:         map[string]postStartHookEntry{},\n\t\tpreShutdownHooks:       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry{},\n\t\tdisabledPostStartHooks: c.DisabledPostStartHooks,\n\n\t\thealthzRegistry:  healthCheckRegistry{path: \"/healthz\", checks: c.HealthzChecks},\n\t\tlivezRegistry:    healthCheckRegistry{path: \"/livez\", checks: c.LivezChecks, clock: clock.RealClock{}},\n\t\treadyzRegistry:   healthCheckRegistry{path: \"/readyz\", checks: c.ReadyzChecks},\n\t\tlivezGracePeriod: c.LivezGracePeriod,\n\n\t\tDiscoveryGroupManager: discovery.NewRootAPIsHandler(c.DiscoveryAddresses, c.Serializer),\n\n\t\tmaxRequestBodyBytes: c.MaxRequestBodyBytes,\n\n\t\tlifecycleSignals:       c.lifecycleSignals,\n\t\tShutdownSendRetryAfter: c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter,\n\n\t\tAPIServerID:           c.APIServerID,\n\t\tStorageReadinessHook:  NewStorageReadinessHook(c.StorageInitializationTimeout),\n\t\tStorageVersionManager: c.StorageVersionManager,\n\n\t\tEffectiveVersion:                        c.EffectiveVersion,\n\t\tEmulationForwardCompatible:              c.EmulationForwardCompatible,\n\t\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible: c.RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible,\n\t\tFeatureGate:                             c.FeatureGate,\n\n\t\tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals: map[string]<-chan struct{}{},\n\t}\n\n\tmanager := c.AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager\n\tif manager == nil {\n\t\tmanager = discoveryendpoint.NewResourceManager(\"apis\")\n\t}\n\ts.AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager = manager\n\ts.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager = discoveryendpoint.NewResourceManager(\"api\")\n\tfor {\n\t\tif c.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes <= 0 {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\texisting := atomic.LoadInt64(&jsonpatch.AccumulatedCopySizeLimit)\n\t\tif existing > 0 && existing < c.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t\tif atomic.CompareAndSwapInt64(&jsonpatch.AccumulatedCopySizeLimit, existing, c.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes) {\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// first add poststarthooks from delegated targets\n\tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.PostStartHooks() {\n\t\ts.postStartHooks[k] = v\n\t}\n\n\tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.PreShutdownHooks() {\n\t\ts.preShutdownHooks[k] = v\n\t}\n\n\t// add poststarthooks that were preconfigured.  Using the add method will give us an error if the same name has already been registered.\n\tfor name, preconfiguredPostStartHook := range c.PostStartHooks {\n\t\tif err := s.AddPostStartHook(name, preconfiguredPostStartHook.hook); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// register mux signals from the delegated server\n\tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\n\t\tif err := s.RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal(k, v); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tgenericApiServerHookName := \"generic-apiserver-start-informers\"\n\tif c.SharedInformerFactory != nil {\n\t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(genericApiServerHookName) {\n\t\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(genericApiServerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n\t\t\t\tc.SharedInformerFactory.Start(hookContext.Done())\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// TODO: Once we get rid of /healthz consider changing this to post-start-hook.\n\t\terr := s.AddReadyzChecks(healthz.NewInformerSyncHealthz(c.SharedInformerFactory))\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tconst priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName = \"priority-and-fairness-config-consumer\"\n\tif s.isPostStartHookRegistered(priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName) {\n\t} else if c.FlowControl != nil {\n\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n\t\t\tgo c.FlowControl.Run(hookContext.Done())\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t})\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t}\n\t\t// TODO(yue9944882): plumb pre-shutdown-hook for request-management system?\n\t} else {\n\t\tklog.V(3).Infof(\"Not requested to run hook %s\", priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName)\n\t}\n\n\t// Add PostStartHooks for maintaining the watermarks for the Priority-and-Fairness and the Max-in-Flight filters.\n\tif c.FlowControl != nil {\n\t\tconst priorityAndFairnessFilterHookName = \"priority-and-fairness-filter\"\n\t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(priorityAndFairnessFilterHookName) {\n\t\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(priorityAndFairnessFilterHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n\t\t\t\tgenericfilters.StartPriorityAndFairnessWatermarkMaintenance(hookContext.Done())\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tconst maxInFlightFilterHookName = \"max-in-flight-filter\"\n\t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(maxInFlightFilterHookName) {\n\t\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(maxInFlightFilterHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n\t\t\t\tgenericfilters.StartMaxInFlightWatermarkMaintenance(hookContext.Done())\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add PostStartHook for maintenaing the object count tracker.\n\tif c.StorageObjectCountTracker != nil {\n\t\tconst storageObjectCountTrackerHookName = \"storage-object-count-tracker-hook\"\n\t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(storageObjectCountTrackerHookName) {\n\t\t\tif err := s.AddPostStartHook(storageObjectCountTrackerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n\t\t\t\tgo c.StorageObjectCountTracker.RunUntil(hookContext.Done())\n\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t}); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, delegateCheck := range delegationTarget.HealthzChecks() {\n\t\tskip := false\n\t\tfor _, existingCheck := range c.HealthzChecks {\n\t\t\tif existingCheck.Name() == delegateCheck.Name() {\n\t\t\t\tskip = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif skip {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.AddHealthChecks(delegateCheck)\n\t}\n\ts.RegisterDestroyFunc(func() {\n\t\tif err := c.Config.TracerProvider.Shutdown(context.Background()); err != nil {\n\t\t\tklog.Errorf(\"failed to shut down tracer provider: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\ts.listedPathProvider = routes.ListedPathProviders{s.listedPathProvider, delegationTarget}\n\n\tinstallAPI(name, s, c.Config)\n\n\t// use the UnprotectedHandler from the delegation target to ensure that we don't attempt to double authenticator, authorize,\n\t// or some other part of the filter chain in delegation cases.\n\tif delegationTarget.UnprotectedHandler() == nil && c.EnableIndex {\n\t\ts.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(routes.IndexLister{\n\t\t\tStatusCode:   http.StatusNotFound,\n\t\t\tPathProvider: s.listedPathProvider,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn s, nil\n}"
