81. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/server_run_options.go | head -200",
  "description": "Read server options"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage options\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n\tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/compatibility\"\n\tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\tbasecompatibility \"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\"\n\n\t\"github.com/spf13/pflag\"\n)\n\nconst (\n\tcorsAllowedOriginsHelpText = \"List of allowed origins for CORS, comma separated. \" +\n\t\t\"An allowed origin can be a regular expression to support subdomain matching. \" +\n\t\t\"If this list is empty CORS will not be enabled. \" +\n\t\t\"Please ensure each expression matches the entire hostname by anchoring \" +\n\t\t\"to the start with '^' or including the '//' prefix, and by anchoring to the \" +\n\t\t\"end with '$' or including the ':' port separator suffix. \" +\n\t\t\"Examples of valid expressions are '//example\\\\.com(:|$)' and '^https://example\\\\.com(:|$)'\"\n)\n\n// ServerRunOptions contains the options while running a generic api server.\ntype ServerRunOptions struct {\n\tAdvertiseAddress net.IP\n\n\tCorsAllowedOriginList        []string\n\tHSTSDirectives               []string\n\tExternalHost                 string\n\tMaxRequestsInFlight          int\n\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight  int\n\tRequestTimeout               time.Duration\n\tGoawayChance                 float64\n\tLivezGracePeriod             time.Duration\n\tMinRequestTimeout            int\n\tStorageInitializationTimeout time.Duration\n\tShutdownDelayDuration        time.Duration\n\t// We intentionally did not add a flag for this option. Users of the\n\t// apiserver library can wire it to a flag.\n\tJSONPatchMaxCopyBytes int64\n\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and\n\t// decoded in a write request. 0 means no limit.\n\t// We intentionally did not add a flag for this option. Users of the\n\t// apiserver library can wire it to a flag.\n\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n\n\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n\n\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\n\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\n\t// watch request(s) to drain.\n\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\n\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\n\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\n\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\n\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\n\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\n\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\n\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\n\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\n\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\n\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\n\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\n\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\n\n\t// ComponentGlobalsRegistry is the registry where the effective versions and feature gates for all components are stored.\n\tComponentGlobalsRegistry basecompatibility.ComponentGlobalsRegistry\n\t// ComponentName is name under which the server's global variabled are registered in the ComponentGlobalsRegistry.\n\tComponentName string\n\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n}\n\nfunc NewServerRunOptions() *ServerRunOptions {\n\tif compatibility.DefaultComponentGlobalsRegistry.EffectiveVersionFor(basecompatibility.DefaultKubeComponent) == nil {\n\t\tfeatureGate := utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate\n\t\teffectiveVersion := compatibility.DefaultBuildEffectiveVersion()\n\t\tutilruntime.Must(compatibility.DefaultComponentGlobalsRegistry.Register(basecompatibility.DefaultKubeComponent, effectiveVersion, featureGate))\n\t}\n\n\treturn NewServerRunOptionsForComponent(basecompatibility.DefaultKubeComponent, compatibility.DefaultComponentGlobalsRegistry)\n}\n\nfunc NewServerRunOptionsForComponent(componentName string, componentGlobalsRegistry basecompatibility.ComponentGlobalsRegistry) *ServerRunOptions {\n\tdefaults := server.NewConfig(serializer.CodecFactory{})\n\treturn &ServerRunOptions{\n\t\tMaxRequestsInFlight:                 defaults.MaxRequestsInFlight,\n\t\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight:         defaults.MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight,\n\t\tRequestTimeout:                      defaults.RequestTimeout,\n\t\tLivezGracePeriod:                    defaults.LivezGracePeriod,\n\t\tMinRequestTimeout:                   defaults.MinRequestTimeout,\n\t\tStorageInitializationTimeout:        defaults.StorageInitializationTimeout,\n\t\tShutdownDelayDuration:               defaults.ShutdownDelayDuration,\n\t\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod: defaults.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod,\n\t\tJSONPatchMaxCopyBytes:               defaults.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes,\n\t\tMaxRequestBodyBytes:                 defaults.MaxRequestBodyBytes,\n\t\tShutdownSendRetryAfter:              false,\n\t\tComponentName:                       componentName,\n\t\tComponentGlobalsRegistry:            componentGlobalsRegistry,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ApplyTo applies the run options to the method receiver and returns self\nfunc (s *ServerRunOptions) ApplyTo(c *server.Config) error {\n\tif err := s.ComponentGlobalsRegistry.SetFallback(); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tc.CorsAllowedOriginList = s.CorsAllowedOriginList\n\tc.HSTSDirectives = s.HSTSDirectives\n\tc.ExternalAddress = s.ExternalHost\n\tc.MaxRequestsInFlight = s.MaxRequestsInFlight\n\tc.MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight = s.MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight\n\tc.LivezGracePeriod = s.LivezGracePeriod\n\tc.RequestTimeout = s.RequestTimeout\n\tc.GoawayChance = s.GoawayChance\n\tc.MinRequestTimeout = s.MinRequestTimeout\n\tc.StorageInitializationTimeout = s.StorageInitializationTimeout\n\tc.ShutdownDelayDuration = s.ShutdownDelayDuration\n\tc.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes = s.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes\n\tc.MaxRequestBodyBytes = s.MaxRequestBodyBytes\n\tc.PublicAddress = s.AdvertiseAddress\n\tc.ShutdownSendRetryAfter = s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter\n\tc.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod = s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod\n\tc.EffectiveVersion = s.ComponentGlobalsRegistry.EffectiveVersionFor(s.ComponentName)\n\tc.FeatureGate = s.ComponentGlobalsRegistry.FeatureGateFor(s.ComponentName)\n\tc.EmulationForwardCompatible = s.EmulationForwardCompatible\n\tc.RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible = s.RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// DefaultAdvertiseAddress sets the field AdvertiseAddress if unset. The field will be set based on the SecureServingOptions.\nfunc (s *ServerRunOptions) DefaultAdvertiseAddress(secure *SecureServingOptions) error {\n\tif secure == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tif s.AdvertiseAddress == nil || s.AdvertiseAddress.IsUnspecified() {\n\t\thostIP, err := secure.DefaultExternalAddress()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"Unable to find suitable network address.error='%v'. \"+\n\t\t\t\t\"Try to set the AdvertiseAddress directly or provide a valid BindAddress to fix this.\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.AdvertiseAddress = hostIP\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// Validate checks validation of ServerRunOptions\nfunc (s *ServerRunOptions) Validate() []error {\n\terrors := []error{}\n\n\tif s.LivezGracePeriod < 0 {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Errorf(\"--livez-grace-period can not be a negative value\"))\n\t}\n\n\tif s.MaxRequestsInFlight < 0 {\n\t\terrors = append(errors, fmt.Errorf(\"--max-requests-inflight can not be negative value\"))\n\t}\n\tif s.MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight < 0 {"
82. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "git -C /workspace show HEAD:staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/options/recommended.go | head -250",
  "description": "Read recommended config setup"
}
Output
"/*\nCopyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage options\n\nimport (\n\t\"github.com/spf13/pflag\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/storagebackend\"\n\t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/dynamic\"\n\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n\t\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\"\n)\n\n// RecommendedOptions contains the recommended options for running an API server.\n// If you add something to this list, it should be in a logical grouping.\n// Each of them can be nil to leave the feature unconfigured on ApplyTo.\ntype RecommendedOptions struct {\n\tEtcd           *EtcdOptions\n\tSecureServing  *SecureServingOptionsWithLoopback\n\tAuthentication *DelegatingAuthenticationOptions\n\tAuthorization  *DelegatingAuthorizationOptions\n\tAudit          *AuditOptions\n\tFeatures       *FeatureOptions\n\tCoreAPI        *CoreAPIOptions\n\n\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n\t// ExtraAdmissionInitializers is called once after all ApplyTo from the options above, to pass the returned\n\t// admission plugin initializers to Admission.ApplyTo.\n\tExtraAdmissionInitializers func(c *server.RecommendedConfig) ([]admission.PluginInitializer, error)\n\tAdmission                  *AdmissionOptions\n\t// API Server Egress Selector is used to control outbound traffic from the API Server\n\tEgressSelector *EgressSelectorOptions\n\t// Traces contains options to control distributed request tracing.\n\tTraces *TracingOptions\n}\n\nfunc NewRecommendedOptions(prefix string, codec runtime.Codec) *RecommendedOptions {\n\tsso := NewSecureServingOptions()\n\n\t// We are composing recommended options for an aggregated api-server,\n\t// whose client is typically a proxy multiplexing many operations ---\n\t// notably including long-running ones --- into one HTTP/2 connection\n\t// into this server.  So allow many concurrent operations.\n\tsso.HTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection = 1000\n\n\treturn &RecommendedOptions{\n\t\tEtcd:           NewEtcdOptions(storagebackend.NewDefaultConfig(prefix, codec)),\n\t\tSecureServing:  sso.WithLoopback(),\n\t\tAuthentication: NewDelegatingAuthenticationOptions(),\n\t\tAuthorization:  NewDelegatingAuthorizationOptions(),\n\t\tAudit:          NewAuditOptions(),\n\t\tFeatures:       NewFeatureOptions(),\n\t\tCoreAPI:        NewCoreAPIOptions(),\n\t\t// Wired a global by default that sadly people will abuse to have different meanings in different repos.\n\t\t// Please consider creating your own FeatureGate so you can have a consistent meaning for what a variable contains\n\t\t// across different repos.  Future you will thank you.\n\t\tFeatureGate:                feature.DefaultFeatureGate,\n\t\tExtraAdmissionInitializers: func(c *server.RecommendedConfig) ([]admission.PluginInitializer, error) { return nil, nil },\n\t\tAdmission:                  NewAdmissionOptions(),\n\t\tEgressSelector:             NewEgressSelectorOptions(),\n\t\tTraces:                     NewTracingOptions(),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (o *RecommendedOptions) AddFlags(fs *pflag.FlagSet) {\n\to.Etcd.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.SecureServing.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.Authentication.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.Authorization.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.Audit.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.Features.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.CoreAPI.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.Admission.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.EgressSelector.AddFlags(fs)\n\to.Traces.AddFlags(fs)\n}\n\n// ApplyTo adds RecommendedOptions to the server configuration.\n// pluginInitializers can be empty, it is only need for additional initializers.\nfunc (o *RecommendedOptions) ApplyTo(config *server.RecommendedConfig) error {\n\tif err := o.Etcd.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif err := o.EgressSelector.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif err := o.Traces.ApplyTo(config.Config.EgressSelector, &config.Config); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif err := o.SecureServing.ApplyTo(&config.Config.SecureServing, &config.Config.LoopbackClientConfig); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif err := o.Authentication.ApplyTo(&config.Config.Authentication, config.SecureServing, config.OpenAPIConfig); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif err := o.Authorization.ApplyTo(&config.Config.Authorization); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif err := o.Audit.ApplyTo(&config.Config); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif err := o.CoreAPI.ApplyTo(config); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tvar kubeClient kubernetes.Interface\n\tvar dynamicClient dynamic.Interface\n\tif config.ClientConfig != nil {\n\t\tvar err error\n\t\tkubeClient, err = kubernetes.NewForConfig(config.ClientConfig)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t\tdynamicClient, err = dynamic.NewForConfig(config.ClientConfig)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif err := o.Features.ApplyTo(&config.Config, kubeClient, config.SharedInformerFactory); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tinitializers, err := o.ExtraAdmissionInitializers(config)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tif err := o.Admission.ApplyTo(&config.Config, config.SharedInformerFactory, kubeClient, dynamicClient, o.FeatureGate,\n\t\tconfig.EffectiveVersion,\n\t\tinitializers...); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (o *RecommendedOptions) Validate() []error {\n\terrors := []error{}\n\terrors = append(errors, o.Etcd.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.SecureServing.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.Authentication.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.Authorization.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.Audit.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.Features.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.CoreAPI.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.Admission.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.EgressSelector.Validate()...)\n\terrors = append(errors, o.Traces.Validate()...)\n\n\treturn errors\n}"

the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package server\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n22: \\\"github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22/daemon\\\"\\n30: \\n31: \\t\\\"golang.org/x/time/rate\\\"\\n32: \\tapidiscoveryv2 \\\"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n34: \\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer/cbor\\\"\\n39: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\\\"\\n40: \\tutilruntime \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\\\"\\n41: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n42: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\\\"\\n43: \\tutilwaitgroup \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\\\"\\n44: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n45: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\\\"\\n46: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\\\"\\n47: \\tgenericapi \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints\\\"\\n48: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\\\"\\n49: \\tdiscoveryendpoint \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\\\"\\n50: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n51: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\\\"\\n52: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/flagz\\\"\\n53: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\\\"\\n54: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\\\"\\n55: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/statusz\\\"\\n56: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\\\"\\n57: \\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n58: \\trestclient \\\"k8s.io/client-go/rest\\\"\\n59: \\tbasecompatibility \\\"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\\\"\\n60: \\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\\\"\\n61: \\tzpagesfeatures \\\"k8s.io/component-base/zpages/features\\\"\\n62: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n63: \\topenapibuilder3 \\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/builder3\\\"\\n64: \\topenapicommon \\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\\\"\\n65: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler\\\"\\n66: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler3\\\"\\n67: \\topenapiutil \\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/util\\\"\\n68: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\\\"\\n69: )\\n70: \\n71: // Info about an API group.\\n72: type APIGroupInfo struct {\\n73: \\tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\\n74: \\t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\\n75: \\tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\\n76: \\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\\n77: \\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\\n78: \\t// define a version \\\"v1beta1\\\" but want to use the Kubernetes \\\"v1\\\" internal objects.\\n79: \\t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\\n80: \\t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\\n81: \\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n82: \\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \\\"meta.k8s.io/v1\\\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\\n83: \\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\\n84: \\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\\n85: \\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n86: \\n87: \\t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\\n88: \\t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\\n89: \\t// TODO: replace with interfaces\\n90: \\tScheme *runtime.Scheme\\n91: \\t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\\n92: \\tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n93: \\t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\\n94: \\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\\n95: \\n96: \\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\\n97: \\t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\\n98: \\tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\\n99: }\\n100: \\n101: func (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\\n102: \\tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\\n103: \\t\\tfor _, store := range stores {\\n104: \\t\\t\\tstore.Destroy()\\n105: \\t\\t}\\n106: \\t}\\n107: }\\n108: \\n109: // GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\\n110: type GenericAPIServer struct {\\n111: \\t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\\n112: \\tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\\n113: \\n114: \\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\\n115: \\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\\n116: \\n117: \\t// Flagz is used to set up flagz endpoint.\\n118: \\tFlagz flagz.Reader\\n119: \\n120: \\t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\\n121: \\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\\n122: \\n123: \\t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\\n124: \\t// gracefully shutdown returns.\\n125: \\tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\\n126: \\n127: \\t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\\n128: \\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\\n129: \\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\\n130: \\n131: \\t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\\n132: \\tadmissionControl admission.Interface\\n133: \\n134: \\t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\\n135: \\tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\\n136: \\n137: \\t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\\n138: \\t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\\n139: \\tExternalAddress string\\n140: \\n141: \\t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\\n142: \\t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\\n143: \\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n144: \\n145: \\t// \\\"Outputs\\\"\\n146: \\t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\\n147: \\tHandler *APIServerHandler\\n148: \\n149: \\t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\\n150: \\t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\\n151: \\tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\\n152: \\n153: \\t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\\n154: \\tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\\n155: \\n156: \\t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\\n157: \\tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\\n158: \\n159: \\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\\n160: \\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n161: \\n162: \\t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\\n163: \\tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\\n164: \\n165: \\t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\\n166: \\tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n167: \\n168: \\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\\n169: \\topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\\n170: \\n171: \\t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\\n172: \\topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\\n173: \\n174: \\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\\n175: \\t// during PrepareRun.\\n176: \\t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\\n177: \\t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\\n178: \\tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\\n179: \\n180: \\t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\\n181: \\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\\n182: \\tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\\n183: \\n184: \\t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\\n185: \\t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\\n186: \\tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\\n187: \\n188: \\t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\\n189: \\t// It is set during PrepareRun.\\n190: \\tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\\n191: \\n192: \\t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\\n193: \\t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\\n194: \\t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\\n195: \\tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\\n196: \\tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\\n197: \\tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\\n198: \\tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\\n199: \\n200: \\tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL1-200\"}",

    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package server\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"errors\"\n22: \t\"fmt\"\n23: \t\"net/http\"\n24: \tgpath \"path\"\n25: \t\"strings\"\n26: \t\"sync\"\n27: \t\"time\"\n28: \n29: \tsystemd \"github.com/coreos/go-systemd/v22/daemon\"\n30: \n31: \t\"golang.org/x/time/rate\"\n32: \tapidiscoveryv2 \"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\"\n33: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n34: \tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n35: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n36: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n37: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n38: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer/cbor\"\n39: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n40: \tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n41: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n42: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/wait\"\n43: \tutilwaitgroup \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\"\n44: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n45: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n46: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n47: \tgenericapi \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints\"\n48: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n49: \tdiscoveryendpoint \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\"\n50: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n51: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n52: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/flagz\"\n53: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\"\n54: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\"\n55: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/statusz\"\n56: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n57: \tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n58: \trestclient \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n59: \tbasecompatibility \"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\"\n60: \t\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\"\n61: \tzpagesfeatures \"k8s.io/component-base/zpages/features\"\n62: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n63: \topenapibuilder3 \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/builder3\"\n64: \topenapicommon \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\"\n65: \t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler\"\n66: \t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/handler3\"\n67: \topenapiutil \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/util\"\n68: \t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n69: )\n70: \n71: // Info about an API group.\n72: type APIGroupInfo struct {\n73: \tPrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion\n74: \t// Info about the resources in this group. It's a map from version to resource to the storage.\n75: \tVersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage\n76: \t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the APIVersion used for common objects in the\n77: \t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n78: \t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects.\n79: \t// If nil, defaults to groupMeta.GroupVersion.\n80: \t// TODO: Remove this when https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/19018 is fixed.\n81: \tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n82: \t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n83: \t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n84: \t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n85: \tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n86: \n87: \t// Scheme includes all of the types used by this group and how to convert between them (or\n88: \t// to convert objects from outside of this group that are accepted in this API).\n89: \t// TODO: replace with interfaces\n90: \tScheme *runtime.Scheme\n91: \t// NegotiatedSerializer controls how this group encodes and decodes data\n92: \tNegotiatedSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n93: \t// ParameterCodec performs conversions for query parameters passed to API calls\n94: \tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n95: \n96: \t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the definitions of all resources installed together.\n97: \t// It is set during InstallAPIGroups, InstallAPIGroup, and InstallLegacyAPIGroup.\n98: \tStaticOpenAPISpec map[string]*spec.Schema\n99: }\n100: \n101: func (a *APIGroupInfo) destroyStorage() {\n102: \tfor _, stores := range a.VersionedResourcesStorageMap {\n103: \t\tfor _, store := range stores {\n104: \t\t\tstore.Destroy()\n105: \t\t}\n106: \t}\n107: }\n108: \n109: // GenericAPIServer contains state for a Kubernetes cluster api server.\n110: type GenericAPIServer struct {\n111: \t// discoveryAddresses is used to build cluster IPs for discovery.\n112: \tdiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n113: \n114: \t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n115: \tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n116: \n117: \t// Flagz is used to set up flagz endpoint.\n118: \tFlagz flagz.Reader\n119: \n120: \t// minRequestTimeout is how short the request timeout can be.  This is used to build the RESTHandler\n121: \tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n122: \n123: \t// ShutdownTimeout is the timeout used for server shutdown. This specifies the timeout before server\n124: \t// gracefully shutdown returns.\n125: \tShutdownTimeout time.Duration\n126: \n127: \t// legacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\n128: \t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup\n129: \tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n130: \n131: \t// admissionControl is used to build the RESTStorage that backs an API Group.\n132: \tadmissionControl admission.Interface\n133: \n134: \t// SecureServingInfo holds configuration of the TLS server.\n135: \tSecureServingInfo *SecureServingInfo\n136: \n137: \t// ExternalAddress is the address (hostname or IP and port) that should be used in\n138: \t// external (public internet) URLs for this GenericAPIServer.\n139: \tExternalAddress string\n140: \n141: \t// Serializer controls how common API objects not in a group/version prefix are serialized for this server.\n142: \t// Individual APIGroups may define their own serializers.\n143: \tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n144: \n145: \t// \"Outputs\"\n146: \t// Handler holds the handlers being used by this API server\n147: \tHandler *APIServerHandler\n148: \n149: \t// UnprotectedDebugSocket is used to serve pprof information in a unix-domain socket. This socket is\n150: \t// not protected by authentication/authorization.\n151: \tUnprotectedDebugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n152: \n153: \t// listedPathProvider is a lister which provides the set of paths to show at /\n154: \tlistedPathProvider routes.ListedPathProvider\n155: \n156: \t// DiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an unaggregated form.\n157: \tDiscoveryGroupManager discovery.GroupManager\n158: \n159: \t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n160: \tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n161: \n162: \t// PeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager serves /apis aggregated from all peer apiservers.\n163: \tPeerAggregatedDiscoveryManager discoveryendpoint.PeerAggregatedResourceManager\n164: \n165: \t// AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager serves /api in an aggregated form.\n166: \tAggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n167: \n168: \t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI if these configs are non-nil.\n169: \topenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n170: \n171: \t// Enable swagger and/or OpenAPI V3 if these configs are non-nil.\n172: \topenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n173: \n174: \t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation indicates not to install the OpenAPI handler\n175: \t// during PrepareRun.\n176: \t// Set this to true when the specific API Server has its own OpenAPI handler\n177: \t// (e.g. kube-aggregator)\n178: \tskipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n179: \n180: \t// OpenAPIVersionedService controls the /openapi/v2 endpoint, and can be used to update the served spec.\n181: \t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n182: \tOpenAPIVersionedService *handler.OpenAPIService\n183: \n184: \t// OpenAPIV3VersionedService controls the /openapi/v3 endpoint and can be used to update the served spec.\n185: \t// It is set during PrepareRun if `openAPIConfig` is non-nil unless `skipOpenAPIInstallation` is true.\n186: \tOpenAPIV3VersionedService *handler3.OpenAPIService\n187: \n188: \t// StaticOpenAPISpec is the spec derived from the restful container endpoints.\n189: \t// It is set during PrepareRun.\n190: \tStaticOpenAPISpec *spec.Swagger\n191: \n192: \t// PostStartHooks are each called after the server has started listening, in a separate go func for each\n193: \t// with no guarantee of ordering between them.  The map key is a name used for error reporting.\n194: \t// It may kill the process with a panic if it wishes to by returning an error.\n195: \tpostStartHookLock      sync.Mutex\n196: \tpostStartHooks         map[string]postStartHookEntry\n197: \tpostStartHooksCalled   bool\n198: \tdisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n199: \n200: \tpreShutdownHookLock    sync.Mutex\n201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL1-200"
  }
}

\\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/slis\\\"\\n83: \\t\\\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\\\"\\n84: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n85: \\topenapicommon \\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\\\"\\n86: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/spec3\\\"\\n87: \\t\\\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\\\"\\n88: \\t\\\"k8s.io/utils/clock\\\"\\n89: \\tutilsnet \\\"k8s.io/utils/net\\\"\\n90: \\n91: \\t// install apis\\n92: \\t_ \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/install\\\"\\n93: )\\n94: \\n95: // hostnameFunc is a function to set the hostnameFunc of this apiserver.\\n96: // To be used for testing purpose only, to simulate scenarios where multiple apiservers\\n97: // exist. In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\\n98: var (\\n99: \\thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\\n100: )\\n101: \\n102: const (\\n103: \\t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\\n104: \\tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \\\"/api\\\"\\n105: \\n106: \\t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\\n107: \\tAPIGroupPrefix = \\\"/apis\\\"\\n108: )\\n109: \\n110: // Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\\n111: // Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\\n112: type Config struct {\\n113: \\t// SecureServing is required to serve https\\n114: \\tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\\n115: \\n116: \\t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\\n117: \\tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\\n118: \\n119: \\t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\\n120: \\tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\\n121: \\n122: \\t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\\n123: \\t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\\n124: \\t// TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\\n125: \\tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\\n126: \\n127: \\t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\\n128: \\t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\\n129: \\tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\\n130: \\n131: \\t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\\n132: \\t// in a given namespace\\n133: \\tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\\n134: \\t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\\n135: \\t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\\n136: \\tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\\n137: \\tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\\n138: \\tHSTSDirectives        []string\\n139: \\t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\\n140: \\tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\\n141: \\n142: \\tEnableIndex     bool\\n143: \\tEnableProfiling bool\\n144: \\tDebugSocketPath string\\n145: \\tEnableDiscovery bool\\n146: \\n147: \\t// Requires generic profiling enabled\\n148: \\tEnableContentionProfiling bool\\n149: \\tEnableMetrics             bool\\n150: \\n151: \\tDisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\\n152: \\t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\\n153: \\tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\\n154: \\n155: \\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\\n156: \\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\\n157: \\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\\n158: \\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\\n159: \\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\\n160: \\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\\n161: \\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\\n162: \\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\\n163: \\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n164: \\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\\n165: \\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\\n166: \\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\\n167: \\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n168: \\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\\n169: \\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\\n170: \\t// AuditBackend is where audit events are sent to.\\n171: \\tAuditBackend audit.Backend\\n172: \\t// AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator makes the decision of whether and how to audit log a request.\\n173: \\tAuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\\n174: \\t// ExternalAddress is the host name to use for external (public internet) facing URLs (e.g. Swagger)\\n175: \\t// Will default to a value based on secure serving info and available ipv4 IPs.\\n176: \\tExternalAddress string\\n177: \\n178: \\t// TracerProvider can provide a tracer, which records spans for distributed tracing.\\n179: \\tTracerProvider tracing.TracerProvider\\n180: \\n181: \\t//===========================================================================\\n182: \\t// Fields you probably don't care about changing\\n183: \\t//===========================================================================\\n184: \\n185: \\t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\\n186: \\tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\\n187: \\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\\n188: \\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\\n189: \\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\\n190: \\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\\n191: \\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\\n192: \\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\\n193: \\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\\n194: \\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\\n195: \\t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\\n196: \\t// always reported\\n197: \\tDiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\\n198: \\t// The default set of healthz checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\\n199: \\tHealthzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\\n200: \\t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL1-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package server\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"crypto/sha256\"\n22: \t\"encoding/base32\"\n23: \t\"fmt\"\n24: \t\"net\"\n25: \t\"net/http\"\n26: \t\"os\"\n27: \tgoruntime \"runtime\"\n28: \t\"runtime/debug\"\n29: \t\"sort\"\n30: \t\"strconv\"\n31: \t\"strings\"\n32: \t\"sync/atomic\"\n33: \t\"time\"\n34: \n35: \t\"github.com/google/uuid\"\n36: \t\"golang.org/x/crypto/cryptobyte\"\n37: \tjsonpatch \"gopkg.in/evanphx/json-patch.v4\"\n38: \n39: \tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n40: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n41: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n42: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/serializer\"\n43: \tutilruntime \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/runtime\"\n44: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/sets\"\n45: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/version\"\n46: \tutilwaitgroup \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/waitgroup\"\n47: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n48: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n49: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n50: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory\"\n51: \tauthenticatorunion \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/union\"\n52: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n53: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n54: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n55: \tdiscoveryendpoint \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery/aggregated\"\n56: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filterlatency\"\n57: \tgenericapifilters \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters\"\n58: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/impersonation\"\n59: \tapiopenapi \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/openapi\"\n60: \tapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n61: \tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n62: \tgenericregistry \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic\"\n63: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/dynamiccertificates\"\n64: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/egressselector\"\n65: \tgenericfilters \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/filters\"\n66: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/flagz\"\n67: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/healthz\"\n68: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routes\"\n69: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/routine\"\n70: \tserverstore \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/storage\"\n71: \tstoragevalue \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/value\"\n72: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n73: \tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n74: \tutilflowcontrol \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol\"\n75: \tflowcontrolrequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/flowcontrol/request\"\n76: \t\"k8s.io/client-go/informers\"\n77: \trestclient \"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n78: \tbasecompatibility \"k8s.io/component-base/compatibility\"\n79: \t\"k8s.io/component-base/featuregate\"\n80: \t\"k8s.io/component-base/logs\"\n81: \t\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/features\"\n82: \t\"k8s.io/component-base/metrics/prometheus/slis\"\n83: \t\"k8s.io/component-base/tracing\"\n84: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n85: \topenapicommon \"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/common\"\n86: \t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/spec3\"\n87: \t\"k8s.io/kube-openapi/pkg/validation/spec\"\n88: \t\"k8s.io/utils/clock\"\n89: \tutilsnet \"k8s.io/utils/net\"\n90: \n91: \t// install apis\n92: \t_ \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/apiserver/install\"\n93: )\n94: \n95: // hostnameFunc is a function to set the hostnameFunc of this apiserver.\n96: // To be used for testing purpose only, to simulate scenarios where multiple apiservers\n97: // exist. In such cases we want to ensure unique apiserver IDs which are a hash of hostnameFunc.\n98: var (\n99: \thostnameFunc = os.Hostname\n100: )\n101: \n102: const (\n103: \t// DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix is where the legacy APIs will be located.\n104: \tDefaultLegacyAPIPrefix = \"/api\"\n105: \n106: \t// APIGroupPrefix is where non-legacy API group will be located.\n107: \tAPIGroupPrefix = \"/apis\"\n108: )\n109: \n110: // Config is a structure used to configure a GenericAPIServer.\n111: // Its members are sorted roughly in order of importance for composers.\n112: type Config struct {\n113: \t// SecureServing is required to serve https\n114: \tSecureServing *SecureServingInfo\n115: \n116: \t// Authentication is the configuration for authentication\n117: \tAuthentication AuthenticationInfo\n118: \n119: \t// Authorization is the configuration for authorization\n120: \tAuthorization AuthorizationInfo\n121: \n122: \t// LoopbackClientConfig is a config for a privileged loopback connection to the API server\n123: \t// This is required for proper functioning of the PostStartHooks on a GenericAPIServer\n124: \t// TODO: move into SecureServing(WithLoopback) as soon as insecure serving is gone\n125: \tLoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config\n126: \n127: \t// EgressSelector provides a lookup mechanism for dialing outbound connections.\n128: \t// It does so based on a EgressSelectorConfiguration which was read at startup.\n129: \tEgressSelector *egressselector.EgressSelector\n130: \n131: \t// RuleResolver is required to get the list of rules that apply to a given user\n132: \t// in a given namespace\n133: \tRuleResolver authorizer.RuleResolver\n134: \t// AdmissionControl performs deep inspection of a given request (including content)\n135: \t// to set values and determine whether its allowed\n136: \tAdmissionControl      admission.Interface\n137: \tCorsAllowedOriginList []string\n138: \tHSTSDirectives        []string\n139: \t// FlowControl, if not nil, gives priority and fairness to request handling\n140: \tFlowControl utilflowcontrol.Interface\n141: \n142: \tEnableIndex     bool\n143: \tEnableProfiling bool\n144: \tDebugSocketPath string\n145: \tEnableDiscovery bool\n146: \n147: \t// Requires generic profiling enabled\n148: \tEnableContentionProfiling bool\n149: \tEnableMetrics             bool\n150: \n151: \tDisabledPostStartHooks sets.String\n152: \t// done values in this values for this map are ignored.\n153: \tPostStartHooks map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry\n154: \n155: \t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n156: \t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n157: \tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n158: \t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n159: \t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n160: \t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n161: \t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n162: \t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n163: \tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n164: \t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n165: \t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n166: \t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n167: \tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n168: \t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n169: \tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n170: \t// AuditBackend is where audit events are sent to.\n171: \tAuditBackend audit.Backend\n172: \t// AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator makes the decision of whether and how to audit log a request.\n173: \tAuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\n174: \t// ExternalAddress is the host name to use for external (public internet) facing URLs (e.g. Swagger)\n175: \t// Will default to a value based on secure serving info and available ipv4 IPs.\n176: \tExternalAddress string\n177: \n178: \t// TracerProvider can provide a tracer, which records spans for distributed tracing.\n179: \tTracerProvider tracing.TracerProvider\n180: \n181: \t//===========================================================================\n182: \t// Fields you probably don't care about changing\n183: \t//===========================================================================\n184: \n185: \t// BuildHandlerChainFunc allows you to build custom handler chains by decorating the apiHandler.\n186: \tBuildHandlerChainFunc func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) (secure http.Handler)\n187: \t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\n188: \t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\n189: \t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\n190: \tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\n191: \t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\n192: \t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\n193: \t// complete while the server is shuting down.\n194: \tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\n195: \t// DiscoveryAddresses is used to build the IPs pass to discovery. If nil, the ExternalAddress is\n196: \t// always reported\n197: \tDiscoveryAddresses discovery.Addresses\n198: \t// The default set of healthz checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n199: \tHealthzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\n200: \t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\n201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL1-200"
  }
}
89. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go",
  "repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package server\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n23: \\trt \\\"runtime\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"sort\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n26: \\n27: \\t\\\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n29: \\n30: \\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\\\"\\n34: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\\\"\\n35: )\\n36: \\n37: // APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\\n38: // This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\\n39: // the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\\n40: // and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\\n41: // FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\\n42: type APIServerHandler struct {\\n43: \\t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\\n44: \\t// chain and then call the Director.\\n45: \\tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\\n46: \\t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\\n47: \\tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\\n48: \\t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\\n49: \\t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\\n50: \\t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\\n51: \\tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\\n52: \\n53: \\t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\\n54: \\t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\\n55: \\t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\\n56: \\t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\\n57: \\t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\\n58: \\t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\\n59: \\t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\\n60: \\t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\\n61: \\t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\\n62: \\t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\\n63: \\t// order to handle \\\"normal\\\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\\n64: \\t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\\n65: \\t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\\n66: \\tDirector http.Handler\\n67: }\\n68: \\n69: // HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\\n70: // It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\\n71: type HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\\n72: \\n73: func NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\\n74: \\tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\\n75: \\tif notFoundHandler != nil {\\n76: \\t\\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\\n77: \\t}\\n78: \\n79: \\tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\\n80: \\tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\\n81: \\tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\\n82: \\t\\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\\n83: \\t})\\n84: \\tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\\n85: \\t\\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\\n86: \\t})\\n87: \\n88: \\tdirector := director{\\n89: \\t\\tname:               name,\\n90: \\t\\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\\n91: \\t\\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\\n92: \\t}\\n93: \\n94: \\treturn &APIServerHandler{\\n95: \\t\\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\\n96: \\t\\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\\n97: \\t\\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\\n98: \\t\\tDirector:           director,\\n99: \\t}\\n100: }\\n101: \\n102: // ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\\n103: func (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\\n104: \\tvar handledPaths []string\\n105: \\t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\\n106: \\tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\\n107: \\t\\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\\n108: \\t}\\n109: \\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\\n110: \\tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\\n111: \\n112: \\treturn handledPaths\\n113: }\\n114: \\n115: type director struct {\\n116: \\tname               string\\n117: \\tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\\n118: \\tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\\n119: }\\n120: \\n121: func (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n122: \\tpath := req.URL.Path\\n123: \\n124: \\t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\\n125: \\tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\\n126: \\t\\tswitch {\\n127: \\t\\tcase ws.RootPath() == \\\"/apis\\\":\\n128: \\t\\t\\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\\n129: \\t\\t\\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\\n130: \\t\\t\\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\\n131: \\t\\t\\tif path == \\\"/apis\\\" || path == \\\"/apis/\\\" {\\n132: \\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\\\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\\n133: \\t\\t\\t\\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\\n134: \\t\\t\\t\\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\\n135: \\t\\t\\t\\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\\n136: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n137: \\t\\t\\t}\\n138: \\n139: \\t\\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\\n140: \\t\\t\\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\\n141: \\t\\t\\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\\n142: \\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\\\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\\n143: \\t\\t\\t\\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\\n144: \\t\\t\\t\\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\\n145: \\t\\t\\t\\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\\n146: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n147: \\t\\t\\t}\\n148: \\t\\t}\\n149: \\t}\\n150: \\n151: \\t// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether\\n152: \\tklog.V(5).Infof(\\\"%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful\\\", d.name, req.Method, path)\\n153: \\td.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n154: }\\n155: \\n156: // TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?\\n157: func logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {\\n158: \\tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\\n159: \\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"recover from panic situation: - %v\\\\r\\\\n\\\", panicReason))\\n160: \\tfor i := 2; ; i++ {\\n161: \\t\\t_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)\\n162: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n163: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n164: \\t\\t}\\n165: \\t\\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"    %s:%d\\\\r\\\\n\\\", file, line))\\n166: \\t}\\n167: \\tklog.Errorln(buffer.String())\\n168: \\n169: \\theaders := http.Header{}\\n170: \\tif ct := w.Header().Get(\\\"Content-Type\\\"); len(ct) > 0 {\\n171: \\t\\theaders.Set(\\\"Accept\\\", ct)\\n172: \\t}\\n173: \\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", \\\"\\\", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})\\n174: }\\n175: \\n176: func serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {\\n177: \\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(\\n178: \\t\\tapierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, \\\"\\\", schema.GroupResource{}, \\\"\\\", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),\\n179: \\t\\ts,\\n180: \\t\\tschema.GroupVersion{},\\n181: \\t\\tresp,\\n182: \\t\\trequest.Request,\\n183: \\t)\\n184: }\\n185: \\n186: // ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler\\n187: func (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n188: \\ta.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)\\n189: }\\n190: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2017 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package server\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"bytes\"\n21: \t\"fmt\"\n22: \t\"net/http\"\n23: \trt \"runtime\"\n24: \t\"sort\"\n25: \t\"strings\"\n26: \n27: \t\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n28: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n29: \n30: \tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n32: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n33: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n34: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/mux\"\n35: )\n36: \n37: // APIServerHandlers holds the different http.Handlers used by the API server.\n38: // This includes the full handler chain, the director (which chooses between gorestful and nonGoRestful,\n39: // the gorestful handler (used for the API) which falls through to the nonGoRestful handler on unregistered paths,\n40: // and the nonGoRestful handler (which can contain a fallthrough of its own)\n41: // FullHandlerChain -> Director -> {GoRestfulContainer,NonGoRestfulMux} based on inspection of registered web services\n42: type APIServerHandler struct {\n43: \t// FullHandlerChain is the one that is eventually served with.  It should include the full filter\n44: \t// chain and then call the Director.\n45: \tFullHandlerChain http.Handler\n46: \t// The registered APIs.  InstallAPIs uses this.  Other servers probably shouldn't access this directly.\n47: \tGoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n48: \t// NonGoRestfulMux is the final HTTP handler in the chain.\n49: \t// It comes after all filters and the API handling\n50: \t// This is where other servers can attach handler to various parts of the chain.\n51: \tNonGoRestfulMux *mux.PathRecorderMux\n52: \n53: \t// Director is here so that we can properly handle fall through and proxy cases.\n54: \t// This looks a bit bonkers, but here's what's happening.  We need to have /apis handling registered in gorestful in order to have\n55: \t// swagger generated for compatibility.  Doing that with `/apis` as a webservice, means that it forcibly 404s (no defaulting allowed)\n56: \t// all requests which are not /apis or /apis/.  We need those calls to fall through behind gorestful for proper delegation.  Trying to\n57: \t// register for a pattern which includes everything behind it doesn't work because gorestful negotiates for verbs and content encoding\n58: \t// and all those things go crazy when gorestful really just needs to pass through.  In addition, openapi enforces unique verb constraints\n59: \t// which we don't fit into and it still muddies up swagger.  Trying to switch the webservices into a route doesn't work because the\n60: \t//  containing webservice faces all the same problems listed above.\n61: \t// This leads to the crazy thing done here.  Our mux does what we need, so we'll place it in front of gorestful.  It will introspect to\n62: \t// decide if the route is likely to be handled by gorestful and route there if needed.  Otherwise, it goes to NonGoRestfulMux mux in\n63: \t// order to handle \"normal\" paths and delegation. Hopefully no API consumers will ever have to deal with this level of detail.  I think\n64: \t// we should consider completely removing gorestful.\n65: \t// Other servers should only use this opaquely to delegate to an API server.\n66: \tDirector http.Handler\n67: }\n68: \n69: // HandlerChainBuilderFn is used to wrap the GoRestfulContainer handler using the provided handler chain.\n70: // It is normally used to apply filtering like authentication and authorization\n71: type HandlerChainBuilderFn func(apiHandler http.Handler) http.Handler\n72: \n73: func NewAPIServerHandler(name string, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, handlerChainBuilder HandlerChainBuilderFn, notFoundHandler http.Handler) *APIServerHandler {\n74: \tnonGoRestfulMux := mux.NewPathRecorderMux(name)\n75: \tif notFoundHandler != nil {\n76: \t\tnonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(notFoundHandler)\n77: \t}\n78: \n79: \tgorestfulContainer := restful.NewContainer()\n80: \tgorestfulContainer.Router(restful.CurlyRouter{}) // e.g. for proxy/{kind}/{name}/{*}\n81: \tgorestfulContainer.RecoverHandler(func(panicReason interface{}, httpWriter http.ResponseWriter) {\n82: \t\tlogStackOnRecover(s, panicReason, httpWriter)\n83: \t})\n84: \tgorestfulContainer.ServiceErrorHandler(func(serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, response *restful.Response) {\n85: \t\tserviceErrorHandler(s, serviceErr, request, response)\n86: \t})\n87: \n88: \tdirector := director{\n89: \t\tname:               name,\n90: \t\tgoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n91: \t\tnonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n92: \t}\n93: \n94: \treturn &APIServerHandler{\n95: \t\tFullHandlerChain:   handlerChainBuilder(director),\n96: \t\tGoRestfulContainer: gorestfulContainer,\n97: \t\tNonGoRestfulMux:    nonGoRestfulMux,\n98: \t\tDirector:           director,\n99: \t}\n100: }\n101: \n102: // ListedPaths returns the paths that should be shown under /\n103: func (a *APIServerHandler) ListedPaths() []string {\n104: \tvar handledPaths []string\n105: \t// Extract the paths handled using restful.WebService\n106: \tfor _, ws := range a.GoRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n107: \t\thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, ws.RootPath())\n108: \t}\n109: \thandledPaths = append(handledPaths, a.NonGoRestfulMux.ListedPaths()...)\n110: \tsort.Strings(handledPaths)\n111: \n112: \treturn handledPaths\n113: }\n114: \n115: type director struct {\n116: \tname               string\n117: \tgoRestfulContainer *restful.Container\n118: \tnonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux\n119: }\n120: \n121: func (d director) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n122: \tpath := req.URL.Path\n123: \n124: \t// check to see if our webservices want to claim this path\n125: \tfor _, ws := range d.goRestfulContainer.RegisteredWebServices() {\n126: \t\tswitch {\n127: \t\tcase ws.RootPath() == \"/apis\":\n128: \t\t\t// if we are exactly /apis or /apis/, then we need special handling in loop.\n129: \t\t\t// normally these are passed to the nonGoRestfulMux, but if discovery is enabled, it will go directly.\n130: \t\t\t// We can't rely on a prefix match since /apis matches everything (see the big comment on Director above)\n131: \t\t\tif path == \"/apis\" || path == \"/apis/\" {\n132: \t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n133: \t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n134: \t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n135: \t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n136: \t\t\t\treturn\n137: \t\t\t}\n138: \n139: \t\tcase strings.HasPrefix(path, ws.RootPath()):\n140: \t\t\t// ensure an exact match or a path boundary match\n141: \t\t\tif len(path) == len(ws.RootPath()) || path[len(ws.RootPath())] == '/' {\n142: \t\t\t\tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by gorestful with webservice %v\", d.name, req.Method, path, ws.RootPath())\n143: \t\t\t\t// don't use servemux here because gorestful servemuxes get messed up when removing webservices\n144: \t\t\t\t// TODO fix gorestful, remove TPRs, or stop using gorestful\n145: \t\t\t\td.goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)\n146: \t\t\t\treturn\n147: \t\t\t}\n148: \t\t}\n149: \t}\n150: \n151: \t// if we didn't find a match, then we just skip gorestful altogether\n152: \tklog.V(5).Infof(\"%v: %v %q satisfied by nonGoRestful\", d.name, req.Method, path)\n153: \td.nonGoRestfulMux.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n154: }\n155: \n156: // TODO: Unify with RecoverPanics?\n157: func logStackOnRecover(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, panicReason interface{}, w http.ResponseWriter) {\n158: \tvar buffer bytes.Buffer\n159: \tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"recover from panic situation: - %v\\r\\n\", panicReason))\n160: \tfor i := 2; ; i++ {\n161: \t\t_, file, line, ok := rt.Caller(i)\n162: \t\tif !ok {\n163: \t\t\tbreak\n164: \t\t}\n165: \t\tbuffer.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"    %s:%d\\r\\n\", file, line))\n166: \t}\n167: \tklog.Errorln(buffer.String())\n168: \n169: \theaders := http.Header{}\n170: \tif ct := w.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\"); len(ct) > 0 {\n171: \t\theaders.Set(\"Accept\", ct)\n172: \t}\n173: \tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(http.StatusInternalServerError, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", \"\", 0, false), s, schema.GroupVersion{}, w, &http.Request{Header: headers})\n174: }\n175: \n176: func serviceErrorHandler(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, serviceErr restful.ServiceError, request *restful.Request, resp *restful.Response) {\n177: \tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(\n178: \t\tapierrors.NewGenericServerResponse(serviceErr.Code, \"\", schema.GroupResource{}, \"\", serviceErr.Message, 0, false),\n179: \t\ts,\n180: \t\tschema.GroupVersion{},\n181: \t\tresp,\n182: \t\trequest.Request,\n183: \t)\n184: }\n185: \n186: // ServeHTTP makes it an http.Handler\n187: func (a *APIServerHandler) ServeHTTP(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n188: \ta.FullHandlerChain.ServeHTTP(w, r)\n189: }\n190: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/handler.go"
  }
}
