{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"200: \\t// The default set of livez checks. There might be more added via AddHealthChecks dynamically.\\n201: \\tLivezChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\\n202: \\t// The default set of readyz-only checks. There might be more added via AddReadyzChecks dynamically.\\n203: \\tReadyzChecks []healthz.HealthChecker\\n204: \\tFlagz        flagz.Reader\\n205: \\t// LegacyAPIGroupPrefixes is used to set up URL parsing for authorization and for validating requests\\n206: \\t// to InstallLegacyAPIGroup. New API servers don't generally have legacy groups at all.\\n207: \\tLegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\\n208: \\t// RequestInfoResolver is used to assign attributes (used by admission and authorization) based on a request URL.\\n209: \\t// Use-cases that are like kubelets may need to customize this.\\n210: \\tRequestInfoResolver apirequest.RequestInfoResolver\\n211: \\t// Serializer is required and provides the interface for serializing and converting objects to and from the wire\\n212: \\t// The default (api.Codecs) usually works fine.\\n213: \\tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n214: \\t// OpenAPIConfig will be used in generating OpenAPI spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIConfig for \\\"working\\\" defaults.\\n215: \\tOpenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\\n216: \\t// OpenAPIV3Config will be used in generating OpenAPI V3 spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIV3Config for \\\"working\\\" defaults.\\n217: \\tOpenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\\n218: \\t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation avoids installing the OpenAPI handler if set to true.\\n219: \\tSkipOpenAPIInstallation bool\\n220: \\n221: \\t// ResourceTransformers are used to transform resources from and to etcd, e.g. encryption.\\n222: \\tResourceTransformers storagevalue.ResourceTransformers\\n223: \\t// RESTOptionsGetter is used to construct RESTStorage types via the generic registry.\\n224: \\tRESTOptionsGetter genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter\\n225: \\n226: \\t// If specified, all requests except those which match the LongRunningFunc predicate will timeout\\n227: \\t// after this duration.\\n228: \\tRequestTimeout time.Duration\\n229: \\t// If specified, long running requests such as watch will be allocated a random timeout between this value, and\\n230: \\t// twice this value.  Note that it is up to the request handlers to ignore or honor this timeout. In seconds.\\n231: \\tMinRequestTimeout int\\n232: \\n233: \\t// StorageInitializationTimeout defines the maximum amount of time to wait for storage initialization\\n234: \\t// before declaring apiserver ready.\\n235: \\tStorageInitializationTimeout time.Duration\\n236: \\n237: \\t// This represents the maximum amount of time it should take for apiserver to complete its startup\\n238: \\t// sequence and become healthy. From apiserver's start time to when this amount of time has\\n239: \\t// elapsed, /livez will assume that unfinished post-start hooks will complete successfully and\\n240: \\t// therefore return true.\\n241: \\tLivezGracePeriod time.Duration\\n242: \\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\\n243: \\t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\\n244: \\t// but /readyz will return failure.\\n245: \\tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\\n246: \\n247: \\t// The limit on the total size increase all \\\"copy\\\" operations in a json\\n248: \\t// patch may cause.\\n249: \\t// This affects all places that applies json patch in the binary.\\n250: \\tJSONPatchMaxCopyBytes int64\\n251: \\t// The limit on the request size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request\\n252: \\t// 0 means no limit.\\n253: \\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\\n254: \\t// MaxRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel non-long-running requests. Every further\\n255: \\t// request has to wait. Applies only to non-mutating requests.\\n256: \\tMaxRequestsInFlight int\\n257: \\t// MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel mutating requests. Every further\\n258: \\t// request has to wait.\\n259: \\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight int\\n260: \\t// Predicate which is true for paths of long-running http requests\\n261: \\tLongRunningFunc apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck\\n262: \\n263: \\t// GoawayChance is the probability that send a GOAWAY to HTTP/2 clients. When client received\\n264: \\t// GOAWAY, the in-flight requests will not be affected and new requests will use\\n265: \\t// a new TCP connection to triggering re-balancing to another server behind the load balance.\\n266: \\t// Default to 0, means never send GOAWAY. Max is 0.02 to prevent break the apiserver.\\n267: \\tGoawayChance float64\\n268: \\n269: \\t// MergedResourceConfig indicates which groupVersion enabled and its resources enabled/disabled.\\n270: \\t// This is composed of genericapiserver defaultAPIResourceConfig and those parsed from flags.\\n271: \\t// If not specify any in flags, then genericapiserver will only enable defaultAPIResourceConfig.\\n272: \\tMergedResourceConfig *serverstore.ResourceConfig\\n273: \\n274: \\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals\\n275: \\t// that happen during lifecycle of the apiserver.\\n276: \\t// it's intentionally marked private as it should never be overridden.\\n277: \\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\\n278: \\n279: \\t// StorageObjectCountTracker is used to keep track of the total number of objects\\n280: \\t// in the storage per resource, so we can estimate width of incoming requests.\\n281: \\tStorageObjectCountTracker flowcontrolrequest.StorageObjectCountTracker\\n282: \\n283: \\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\\n284: \\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\\n285: \\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\\n286: \\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\\n287: \\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\\n288: \\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\\n289: \\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\\n290: \\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\\n291: \\n292: \\t//===========================================================================\\n293: \\t// values below here are targets for removal\\n294: \\t//===========================================================================\\n295: \\n296: \\t// PublicAddress is the IP address where members of the cluster (kubelet,\\n297: \\t// kube-proxy, services, etc.) can reach the GenericAPIServer.\\n298: \\t// If nil or 0.0.0.0, the host's default interface will be used.\\n299: \\tPublicAddress net.IP\\n300: \\n301: \\t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\\n302: \\t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\\n303: \\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\\n304: \\n305: \\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\\n306: \\tAPIServerID string\\n307: \\n308: \\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\\n309: \\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\\n310: \\n311: \\t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\\n312: \\tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\\n313: \\n314: \\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\\n315: \\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\\n316: \\t// watch request(s) to drain.\\n317: \\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\\n318: \\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\\n319: \\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\\n320: \\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\\n321: \\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\\n322: \\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\\n323: \\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\\n324: \\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\\n325: \\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\\n326: \\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\\n327: \\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\\n328: \\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\\n329: \\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\\n330: }\\n331: \\n332: type RecommendedConfig struct {\\n333: \\tConfig\\n334: \\n335: \\t// SharedInformerFactory provides shared informers for Kubernetes resources. This value is set by\\n336: \\t// RecommendedOptions.CoreAPI.ApplyTo called by RecommendedOptions.ApplyTo. It uses an in-cluster client config\\n337: \\t// by default, or the kubeconfig given with kubeconfig command line flag.\\n338: \\tSharedInformerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory\\n339: \\n340: \\t// ClientConfig holds the kubernetes client configuration.\\n341: \\t// This value is set by RecommendedOptions.CoreAPI.ApplyTo called by RecommendedOptions.ApplyTo.\\n342: \\t// By default in-cluster client config is used.\\n343: \\tClientConfig *restclient.Config\\n344: }\\n345: \\n346: type SecureServingInfo struct {\\n347: \\t// Listener is the secure server network listener.\\n348: \\tListener net.Listener\\n349: \\n350: \\t// Cert is the main server cert which is used if SNI does not match. Cert must be non-nil and is\\n351: \\t// allowed to be in SNICerts.\\n352: \\tCert dynamiccertificates.CertKeyContentProvider\\n353: \\n354: \\t// SNICerts are the TLS certificates used for SNI.\\n355: \\tSNICerts []dynamiccertificates.SNICertKeyContentProvider\\n356: \\n357: \\t// ClientCA is the certificate bundle for all the signers that you'll recognize for incoming client certificates\\n358: \\tClientCA dynamiccertificates.CAContentProvider\\n359: \\n360: \\t// MinTLSVersion optionally overrides the minimum TLS version supported.\\n361: \\t// Values are from tls package constants (https://golang.org/pkg/crypto/tls/#pkg-constants).\\n362: \\tMinTLSVersion uint16\\n363: \\n364: \\t// CipherSuites optionally overrides the list of allowed cipher suites for the server.\\n365: \\t// Values are from tls package constants (https://golang.org/pkg/crypto/tls/#pkg-constants).\\n366: \\tCipherSuites []uint16\\n367: \\n368: \\t// HTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection is the limit that the api server imposes on each client.\\n369: \\t// A value of zero means to use the default provided by golang's HTTP/2 support.\\n370: \\tHTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection int\\n371: \\n372: \\t// DisableHTTP2 indicates that http2 should not be enabled.\\n373: \\tDisableHTTP2 bool\\n374: }\\n375: \\n376: type AuthenticationInfo struct {\\n377: \\t// APIAudiences is a list of identifier that the API identifies as. This is\\n378: \\t// used by some authenticators to validate audience bound credentials.\\n379: \\tAPIAudiences authenticator.Audiences\\n380: \\t// Authenticator determines which subject is making the request\\n381: \\tAuthenticator authenticator.Request\\n382: \\n383: \\tRequestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig\\n384: }\\n385: \\n386: type AuthorizationInfo struct {\\n387: \\t// Authorizer determines whether the subject is allowed to make the request based only\\n388: \\t// on the RequestURI\\n389: \\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\\n390: }\\n391: \\n392: func init() {\\n393: \\tutilruntime.Must(features.AddFeatureGates(utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate))\\n394: }\\n395: \\n396: // NewConfig returns a Config struct with the default values\\n397: func NewConfig(codecs serializer.CodecFactory) *Config {\\n398: \\tdefaultHealthChecks := []healthz.HealthChecker{healthz.PingHealthz, healthz.LogHealthz}\\n399: \\tvar id string\\n400: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerIdentity) {\\n401: \\t\\thostname, err := hostnameFunc()\\n402: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n403: \\t\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"error getting hostname for apiserver identity: %v\\\", err)\\n404: \\t\\t}\\n405: \\n406: \\t\\t// Since the hash needs to be unique across each kube-apiserver and aggregated apiservers,\\n407: \\t\\t// the hash used for the identity should include both the hostname and the identity value.\\n408: \\t\\t// TODO: receive the identity value as a parameter once the apiserver identity lease controller\\n409: \\t\\t// post start hook is moved to generic apiserver.\\n410: \\t\\tb := cryptobyte.NewBuilder(nil)\\n411: \\t\\tb.AddUint16LengthPrefixed(func(b *cryptobyte.Builder) {\\n412: \\t\\t\\tb.AddBytes([]byte(hostname))\\n413: \\t\\t})\\n414: \\t\\tb.AddUint16LengthPrefixed(func(b *cryptobyte.Builder) {\\n415: \\t\\t\\tb.AddBytes([]byte(\\\"kube-apiserver\\\"))\\n416: \\t\\t})\\n417: \\t\\thashData, err := b.Bytes()\\n418: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n419: \\t\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"error building hash data for apiserver identity: %v\\\", err)\\n420: \\t\\t}\\n421: \\n422: \\t\\thash := sha256.Sum256(hashData)\\n423: \\t\\tid = \\\"apiserver-\\\" + strings.ToLower(base32.StdEncoding.WithPadding(base32.NoPadding).EncodeToString(hash[:16]))\\n424: \\t}\\n425: \\tlifecycleSignals := newLifecycleSignals()\\n426: \\n427: \\treturn &Config{\\n428: \\t\\tSerializer:                     codecs,\\n429: \\t\\tBuildHandlerChainFunc:          DefaultBuildHandlerChain,\\n430: \\t\\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup: new(utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup),\\n431: \\t\\tWatchRequestWaitGroup:          &utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup{},\\n432: \\t\\tLegacyAPIGroupPrefixes:         sets.NewString(DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix),\\n433: \\t\\tDisabledPostStartHooks:         sets.NewString(),\\n434: \\t\\tPostStartHooks:                 map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry{},\\n435: \\t\\tHealthzChecks:                  append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\\n436: \\t\\tReadyzChecks:                   append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\\n437: \\t\\tLivezChecks:                    append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\\n438: \\t\\tEnableIndex:                    true,\\n439: \\t\\tEnableDiscovery:                true,\\n440: \\t\\tEnableProfiling:                true,\\n441: \\t\\tDebugSocketPath:                \\\"\\\",\\n442: \\t\\tEnableMetrics:                  true,\\n443: \\t\\tMaxRequestsInFlight:            400,\\n444: \\t\\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight:    200,\\n445: \\t\\tRequestTimeout:                 time.Duration(60) * time.Second,\\n446: \\t\\tMinRequestTimeout:              1800,\\n447: \\t\\tStorageInitializationTimeout:   time.Minute,\\n448: \\t\\tLivezGracePeriod:               time.Duration(0),\\n449: \\t\\tShutdownDelayDuration:          time.Duration(0),\\n450: \\t\\t// 1.5MB is the default client request size in bytes\\n451: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL200-450\"}",

    "content": "200: \t// The default set of livez checks. New API servers don't generally have legacy groups at all.\n207: \tLegacyAPIGroupPrefixes sets.String\n208: \t// RequestInfoResolver is used to assign attributes (used by admission and authorization) based on a request URL.\n209: \t// Use-cases that are like kubelets may need to customize this.\n210: \tRequestInfoResolver apirequest.RequestInfoResolver\n211: \t// Serializer is required and provides the interface for serializing and converting objects to and from the wire\n212: \t// The default (api.Codecs) usually works fine.\n213: \tSerializer runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n214: \t// OpenAPIConfig will be used in generating OpenAPI spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIConfig for \"working\" defaults.\n215: \tOpenAPIConfig *openapicommon.Config\n216: \t// OpenAPIV3Config will be used in generating OpenAPI V3 spec. This is nil by default. Use DefaultOpenAPIV3Config for \"working\" defaults.\n217: \tOpenAPIV3Config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config\n218: \t// SkipOpenAPIInstallation avoids installing the OpenAPI handler if set to true.\n219: \tSkipOpenAPIInstallation bool\n220: \n221: \t// ResourceTransformers are used to transform resources from and to etcd, e.g. encryption.\n222: \tResourceTransformers storagevalue.ResourceTransformers\n223: \t// RESTOptionsGetter is used to construct RESTStorage types via the generic registry.\n224: \tRESTOptionsGetter genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter\n225: \n226: \t// If specified, all requests except those which match the LongRunningFunc predicate will timeout\n227: \t// after this duration.\n228: \tRequestTimeout time.Duration\n229: \t// If specified, long running requests such as watch will be allocated a random timeout between this value, and\n230: \t// twice this value.  Note that it is up to the request handlers to ignore or honor this timeout. In seconds.\n231: \tMinRequestTimeout int\n232: \n233: \t// StorageInitializationTimeout defines the maximum amount of time to wait for storage initialization\n234: \t// before declaring apiserver ready.\n235: \tStorageInitializationTimeout time.Duration\n236: \n237: \t// This represents the maximum amount of time it should take for apiserver to complete its startup\n238: \t// sequence and become healthy. From apiserver's start time to when this amount of time has\n239: \t// elapsed, /livez will assume that unfinished post-start hooks will complete successfully and\n240: \t// therefore return true.\n241: \tLivezGracePeriod time.Duration\n242: \t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\n243: \t// have converged on all node. During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n244: \t// but /readyz will return failure.\n245: \tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n246: \n247: \t// The limit on the total size increase all \"copy\" operations in a json\n248: \t// patch may cause.\n249: \t// This affects all places that applies json patch in the binary.\n250: \tJSONPatchMaxCopyBytes int64\n251: \t// The limit on the request size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request\n252: \t// 0 means no limit.\n253: \tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n254: \t// MaxRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel non-long-running requests. Every further\n255: \t// request has to wait. Applies only to non-mutating requests.\n256: \tMaxRequestsInFlight int\n257: \t// MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight is the maximum number of parallel mutating requests. Every further\n258: \t// request has to wait.\n259: \tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight int\n260: \t// Predicate which is true for paths of long-running http requests\n261: \tLongRunningFunc apirequest.LongRunningRequestCheck\n262: \n263: \t// GoawayChance is the probability that send a GOAWAY to HTTP/2 clients. When client received\n264: \t// GOAWAY, the in-flight requests will not be affected and new requests will use\n265: \t// a new TCP connection to triggering re-balancing to another server behind the load balance.\n266: \t// Default to 0, means never send GOAWAY. Max is 0.02 to prevent break the apiserver.\n267: \tGoawayChance float64\n268: \n269: \t// MergedResourceConfig indicates which groupVersion enabled and its resources enabled/disabled.\n270: \t// This is composed of genericapiserver defaultAPIResourceConfig and those parsed from flags.\n271: \t// If not specify any in flags, then genericapiserver will only enable defaultAPIResourceConfig.\n272: \tMergedResourceConfig *serverstore.ResourceConfig\n273: \n274: \t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals\n275: \t// that happen during lifecycle of the apiserver.\n276: \t// it's intentionally marked private as it should never be overridden.\n277: \tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\n278: \n279: \t// StorageObjectCountTracker is used to keep track of the total number of objects\n280: \t// in the storage per resource, so we can estimate width of incoming requests.\n281: \tStorageObjectCountTracker flowcontrolrequest.StorageObjectCountTracker\n282: \n283: \t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n284: \t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n285: \t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n286: \t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n287: \t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n288: \t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n289: \t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n290: \tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n291: \n292: \t//===========================================================================\n293: \t// values below here are targets for removal\n294: \t//===========================================================================\n295: \n296: \t// PublicAddress is the IP address where members of the cluster (kubelet,\n297: \t// kube-proxy, services, etc.) can reach the GenericAPIServer.\n298: \t// If nil or 0.0.0.0, the host's default interface will be used.\n299: \tPublicAddress net.IP\n300: \n301: \t// EquivalentResourceRegistry provides information about resources equivalent to a given resource,\n302: \t// and the kind associated with a given resource. As resources are installed, they are registered here.\n303: \tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n304: \n305: \t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\n306: \tAPIServerID string\n307: \n308: \t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\n309: \tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\n310: \n311: \t// AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager serves /apis in an aggregated form.\n312: \tAggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager discoveryendpoint.ResourceManager\n313: \n314: \t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\n315: \t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\n316: \t// watch request(s) to drain.\n317: \t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\n318: \t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\n319: \t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\n320: \t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\n321: \t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\n322: \t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\n323: \t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\n324: \t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\n325: \t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\n326: \t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\n327: \t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\n328: \t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\n329: \tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\n330: }\n331: \n332: type RecommendedConfig struct {\n333: \tConfig\n334: \n335: \t// SharedInformerFactory provides shared informers for Kubernetes resources. This value is set by\n336: \t// RecommendedOptions.CoreAPI.ApplyTo called by RecommendedOptions.ApplyTo. It uses an in-cluster client config\n337: \t// by default, or the kubeconfig given with kubeconfig command line flag.\n338: \tSharedInformerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory\n339: \n340: \t// ClientConfig holds the kubernetes client configuration.\n341: \t// This value is set by RecommendedOptions.CoreAPI.ApplyTo called by RecommendedOptions.ApplyTo.\n342: \t// By default in-cluster client config is used.\n343: \tClientConfig *restclient.Config\n344: }\n345: \n346: type SecureServingInfo struct {\n347: \t// Listener is the secure server network listener.\n348: \tListener net.Listener\n349: \n350: \t// Cert is the main server cert which is used if SNI does not match. Cert must be non-nil and is\n351: \t// allowed to be in SNICerts.\n352: \tCert dynamiccertificates.CertKeyContentProvider\n353: \n354: \t// SNICerts are the TLS certificates used for SNI.\n355: \tSNICerts []dynamiccertificates.SNICertKeyContentProvider\n356: \n357: \t// ClientCA is the certificate bundle for all the signers that you'll recognize for incoming client certificates\n358: \tClientCA dynamiccertificates.CAContentProvider\n359: \n360: \t// MinTLSVersion optionally overrides the minimum TLS version supported.\n361: \t// Values are from tls package constants (https://golang.org/pkg/crypto/tls/#pkg-constants).\n362: \tMinTLSVersion uint16\n363: \n364: \t// CipherSuites optionally overrides the list of allowed cipher suites for the server.\n365: \t// Values are from tls package constants (https://golang.org/pkg/crypto/tls/#pkg-constants).\n366: \tCipherSuites []uint16\n367: \n368: \t// HTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection is the limit that the api server imposes on each client.\n369: \t// A value of zero means to use the default provided by golang's HTTP/2 support.\n370: \tHTTP2MaxStreamsPerConnection int\n371: \n372: \t// DisableHTTP2 indicates that http2 should not be enabled.\n373: \tDisableHTTP2 bool\n374: }\n375: \n376: type AuthenticationInfo struct {\n377: \t// APIAudiences is a list of identifier that the API identifies as. This is\n378: \t// used by some authenticators to validate audience bound credentials.\n379: \tAPIAudiences authenticator.Audiences\n380: \t// Authenticator determines which subject is making the request\n381: \tAuthenticator authenticator.Request\n382: \n383: \tRequestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig\n384: }\n385: \n386: type AuthorizationInfo struct {\n387: \t// Authorizer determines whether the subject is allowed to make the request based only\n388: \t// on the RequestURI\n389: \tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n390: }\n391: \n392: func init() {\n393: \tutilruntime.Must(features.AddFeatureGates(utilfeature.DefaultMutableFeatureGate))\n394: }\n395: \n396: // NewConfig returns a Config struct with the default values\n397: func NewConfig(codecs serializer.CodecFactory) *Config {\n398: \tdefaultHealthChecks := []healthz.HealthChecker{healthz.PingHealthz, healthz.LogHealthz}\n399: \tvar id string\n400: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerIdentity) {\n401: \t\thostname, err := hostnameFunc()\n402: \t\tif err != nil {\n403: \t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"error getting hostname for apiserver identity: %v\", err)\n404: \t\t}\n405: \n406: \t\t// Since the hash needs to be unique across each kube-apiserver and aggregated apiservers,\n407: \t\t// the hash used for the identity should include both the hostname and the identity value.\n408: \t\t// TODO: receive the identity value as a parameter once the apiserver identity lease controller\n409: \t\t// post start hook is moved to generic apiserver.\n410: \t\tb := cryptobyte.NewBuilder(nil)\n411: \t\tb.AddUint16LengthPrefixed(func(b *cryptobyte.Builder) {\n412: \t\t\tb.AddBytes([]byte(hostname))\n413: \t\t})\n414: \t\tb.AddUint16LengthPrefixed(func(b *cryptobyte.Builder) {\n415: \t\t\tb.AddBytes([]byte(\"kube-apiserver\"))\n416: \t\t})\n417: \t\thashData, err := b.Bytes()\n418: \t\tif err != nil {\n419: \t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"error building hash data for apiserver identity: %v\", err)\n420: \t\t}\n421: \n422: \t\thash := sha256.Sum256(hashData)\n423: \t\tid = \"apiserver-\" + strings.ToLower(base32.StdEncoding.WithPadding(base32.NoPadding).EncodeToString(hash[:16]))\n424: \t}\n425: \tlifecycleSignals := newLifecycleSignals()\n426: \n427: \treturn &Config{\n428: \t\tSerializer:                     codecs,\n429: \t\tBuildHandlerChainFunc:          DefaultBuildHandlerChain,\n430: \t\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup: new(utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup),\n431: \t\tWatchRequestWaitGroup:          &utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup{},\n432: \t\tLegacyAPIGroupPrefixes:         sets.NewString(DefaultLegacyAPIPrefix),\n433: \t\tDisabledPostStartHooks:         sets.NewString(),\n434: \t\tPostStartHooks:                 map[string]PostStartHookConfigEntry{},\n435: \t\tHealthzChecks:                  append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\n436: \t\tReadyzChecks:                   append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\n437: \t\tLivezChecks:                    append([]healthz.HealthChecker{}, defaultHealthChecks...),\n438: \t\tEnableIndex:                    true,\n439: \t\tEnableDiscovery:                true,\n440: \t\tEnableProfiling:                true,\n441: \t\tDebugSocketPath:                \"\",\n442: \t\tEnableMetrics:                  true,\n443: \t\tMaxRequestsInFlight:            400,\n444: \t\tMaxMutatingRequestsInFlight:    200,\n445: \t\tRequestTimeout:                 time.Duration(60) * time.Second,\n446: \t\tMinRequestTimeout:              1800,\n447: \t\tStorageInitializationTimeout:   time.Minute,\n448: \t\tLivezGracePeriod:               time.Duration(0),\n449: \t\tShutdownDelayDuration:          time.Duration(0),\n450: \t\t// 1.5MB is the default client request size in bytes\n451: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL200-450"
  }
}

As resources are installed, they are registered here.\\n221: \\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\\n222: \\n223: \\t// delegationTarget is the next delegate in the chain. This is never nil.\\n224: \\tdelegationTarget DelegationTarget\\n225: \\n226: \\t// NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup allows you to wait for all chain\\n227: \\t// handlers associated with non long-running requests\\n228: \\t// to complete while the server is shuting down.\\n229: \\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.SafeWaitGroup\\n230: \\t// WatchRequestWaitGroup allows us to wait for all chain\\n231: \\t// handlers associated with active watch requests to\\n232: \\t// complete while the server is shuting down.\\n233: \\tWatchRequestWaitGroup *utilwaitgroup.RateLimitedSafeWaitGroup\\n234: \\n235: \\t// ShutdownDelayDuration allows to block shutdown for some time, e.g. until endpoints pointing to this API server\\n236: \\t// have converged on all node. the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\\n241: \\t// 0 means no limit.\\n242: \\tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\\n243: \\n244: \\t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\\n245: \\tAPIServerID string\\n246: \\n247: \\t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\\n248: \\t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\\n249: \\tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\\n250: \\n251: \\t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\\n252: \\tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\\n253: \\n254: \\t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\\n255: \\t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\\n256: \\tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\\n257: \\t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\\n258: \\t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\\n259: \\t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\\n260: \\t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\\n261: \\t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\\n262: \\tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n263: \\t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\\n264: \\t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\\n265: \\t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\\n266: \\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\\n267: \\n268: \\t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\\n269: \\tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\\n270: \\n271: \\t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals that happen during the life cycle of the apiserver.\\n272: \\tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\\n273: \\n274: \\t// destroyFns contains a list of functions that should be called on shutdown to clean up resources.\\n275: \\tdestroyFns []func()\\n276: \\n277: \\t// muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals holds signals that indicate all known HTTP paths have been registered.\\n278: \\t// it exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.\\n279: \\t// it is exposed for easier composition of the individual servers.\\n280: \\t// the primary users of this field are the WithMuxCompleteProtection filter and the NotFoundHandler\\n281: \\tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals map[string]<-chan struct{}\\n282: \\n283: \\t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\\n284: \\t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\\n285: \\t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\\n286: \\t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\\n287: \\t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\\n288: \\t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\\n289: \\t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\\n290: \\tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\\n291: \\n292: \\t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\\n293: \\t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\\n294: \\t// watch request(s) to drain.\\n295: \\t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\\n296: \\t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\\n297: \\t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\\n298: \\t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\\n299: \\t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\\n300: \\t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\\n301: \\t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\\n302: \\t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\\n303: \\t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\\n304: \\t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\\n305: \\t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\\n306: \\t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\\n307: \\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\\n308: }\\n309: \\n310: // DelegationTarget is an interface which allows for composition of API servers with top level handling that works\\n311: // as expected.\\n312: type DelegationTarget interface {\\n313: \\t// UnprotectedHandler returns a handler that is NOT protected by a normal chain\\n314: \\tUnprotectedHandler() http.Handler\\n315: \\n316: \\t// PostStartHooks returns the post-start hooks that need to be combined\\n317: \\tPostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry\\n318: \\n319: \\t// PreShutdownHooks returns the pre-stop hooks that need to be combined\\n320: \\tPreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\\n321: \\n322: \\t// HealthzChecks returns the healthz checks that need to be combined\\n323: \\tHealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker\\n324: \\n325: \\t// ListedPaths returns the paths for supporting an index\\n326: \\tListedPaths() []string\\n327: \\n328: \\t// NextDelegate returns the next delegationTarget in the chain of delegations\\n329: \\tNextDelegate() DelegationTarget\\n330: \\n331: \\t// PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\\n332: \\tPrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer\\n333: \\n334: \\t// MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals exposes registered signals that indicate if all known HTTP paths have been installed.\\n335: \\tMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{}\\n336: \\n337: \\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\n338: \\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\\n339: \\t// for being called more than once.\\n340: \\tDestroy()\\n341: }\\n342: \\n343: func (s *GenericAPIServer) UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler {\\n344: \\t// when we delegate, we need the server we're delegating to choose whether or not to use gorestful\\n345: \\treturn s.Handler.Director\\n346: }\\n347: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry {\\n348: \\treturn s.postStartHooks\\n349: }\\n350: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry {\\n351: \\treturn s.preShutdownHooks\\n352: }\\n353: func (s *GenericAPIServer) HealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker {\\n354: \\treturn s.healthzRegistry.checks\\n355: }\\n356: func (s *GenericAPIServer) ListedPaths() []string {\\n357: \\treturn s.listedPathProvider.ListedPaths()\\n358: }\\n359: \\n360: func (s *GenericAPIServer) NextDelegate() DelegationTarget {\\n361: \\treturn s.delegationTarget\\n362: }\\n363: \\n364: // RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal registers the given signal that will be used to determine if all known\\n365: // HTTP paths have been registered. It is okay to call this method after instantiating the generic server but before running.\\n366: func (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal(signalName string, signal <-chan struct{}) error {\\n367: \\tif _, exists := s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName]; exists {\\n368: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"%s already registered\\\", signalName)\\n369: \\t}\\n370: \\ts.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName] = signal\\n371: \\treturn nil\\n372: }\\n373: \\n374: func (s *GenericAPIServer) MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{} {\\n375: \\treturn s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals\\n376: }\\n377: \\n378: // RegisterDestroyFunc registers a function that will be called during Destroy().\\n379: // The function have to be idempotent and prepared to be called more than once.\\n380: func (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterDestroyFunc(destroyFn func()) {\\n381: \\ts.destroyFns = append(s.destroyFns, destroyFn)\\n382: }\\n383: \\n384: // Destroy cleans up all its and its delegation target resources on shutdown.\\n385: // It starts with destroying its own resources and later proceeds with\\n386: // its delegation target.\\n387: func (s *GenericAPIServer) Destroy() {\\n388: \\tfor _, destroyFn := range s.destroyFns {\\n389: \\t\\tdestroyFn()\\n390: \\t}\\n391: \\tif s.delegationTarget != nil {\\n392: \\t\\ts.delegationTarget.Destroy()\\n393: \\t}\\n394: }\\n395: \\n396: type emptyDelegate struct {\\n397: \\t// handler is called at the end of the delegation chain\\n398: \\t// when a request has been made against an unregistered HTTP path the individual servers will simply pass it through until it reaches the handler.\\n399: \\thandler http.Handler\\n400: }\\n401: \\n402: func NewEmptyDelegate() DelegationTarget {\\n403: \\treturn emptyDelegate{}\\n404: }\\n405: \\n406: // NewEmptyDelegateWithCustomHandler allows for registering a custom handler usually for special handling of 404 requests\\n407: func NewEmptyDelegateWithCustomHandler(handler http.Handler) DelegationTarget {\\n408: \\treturn emptyDelegate{handler}\\n409: }\\n410: \\n411: func (s emptyDelegate) UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler {\\n412: \\treturn s.handler\\n413: }\\n414: func (s emptyDelegate) PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry {\\n415: \\treturn map[string]postStartHookEntry{}\\n416: }\\n417: func (s emptyDelegate) PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry {\\n418: \\treturn map[string]preShutdownHookEntry{}\\n419: }\\n420: func (s emptyDelegate) HealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker {\\n421: \\treturn []healthz.HealthChecker{}\\n422: }\\n423: func (s emptyDelegate) ListedPaths() []string {\\n424: \\treturn []string{}\\n425: }\\n426: func (s emptyDelegate) NextDelegate() DelegationTarget {\\n427: \\treturn nil\\n428: }\\n429: func (s emptyDelegate) PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer {\\n430: \\treturn preparedGenericAPIServer{nil}\\n431: }\\n432: func (s emptyDelegate) MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{} {\\n433: \\treturn map[string]<-chan struct{}{}\\n434: }\\n435: func (s emptyDelegate) Destroy() {\\n436: }\\n437: \\n438: // preparedGenericAPIServer is a private wrapper that enforces a call of PrepareRun() before Run can be invoked.\\n439: type preparedGenericAPIServer struct {\\n440: \\t*GenericAPIServer\\n441: }\\n442: \\n443: // PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\\n444: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer {\\n445: \\ts.delegationTarget.PrepareRun()\\n446: \\n447: \\tif s.openAPIConfig != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\\n448: \\t\\ts.OpenAPIVersionedService, s.StaticOpenAPISpec = routes.OpenAPI{\\n449: \\t\\t\\tConfig: s.openAPIConfig,\\n450: \\t\\t}.InstallV2(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n451: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL200-450\"}",

During this time, the API server keeps serving, /healthz will return 200,\n237: \t// but /readyz will return failure.\n238: \tShutdownDelayDuration time.Duration\n239: \n240: \t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\n241: \t// 0 means no limit.\n242: \tmaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n243: \n244: \t// APIServerID is the ID of this API server\n245: \tAPIServerID string\n246: \n247: \t// StorageReadinessHook implements post-start-hook functionality for checking readiness\n248: \t// of underlying storage for registered resources.\n249: \tStorageReadinessHook *StorageReadinessHook\n250: \n251: \t// StorageVersionManager holds the storage versions of the API resources installed by this server.\n252: \tStorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager\n253: \n254: \t// EffectiveVersion determines which apis and features are available\n255: \t// based on when the api/feature lifecyle.\n256: \tEffectiveVersion basecompatibility.EffectiveVersion\n257: \t// EmulationForwardCompatible is an option to implicitly enable all APIs which are introduced after the emulation version and\n258: \t// have higher priority than APIs of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version.\n259: \t// If true, all APIs that have higher priority than the APIs(beta+) of the same group resource enabled at the emulation version will be installed.\n260: \t// This is needed when a controller implementation migrates to newer API versions, for the binary version, and also uses the newer API versions even when emulation version is set.\n261: \t// Not applicable to alpha APIs.\n262: \tEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n263: \t// RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible is an option to explicitly enable specific APIs introduced after the emulation version through the runtime-config.\n264: \t// If true, APIs identified by group/version that are enabled in the --runtime-config flag will be installed even if it is introduced after the emulation version. --runtime-config flag values that identify multiple APIs, such as api/all,api/ga,api/beta, are not influenced by this flag and will only enable APIs available at the current emulation version.\n265: \t// If false, error would be thrown if any GroupVersion or GroupVersionResource explicitly enabled in the --runtime-config flag is introduced after the emulation version.\n266: \tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible bool\n267: \n268: \t// FeatureGate is a way to plumb feature gate through if you have them.\n269: \tFeatureGate featuregate.FeatureGate\n270: \n271: \t// lifecycleSignals provides access to the various signals that happen during the life cycle of the apiserver.\n272: \tlifecycleSignals lifecycleSignals\n273: \n274: \t// destroyFns contains a list of functions that should be called on shutdown to clean up resources.\n275: \tdestroyFns []func()\n276: \n277: \t// muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals holds signals that indicate all known HTTP paths have been registered.\n278: \t// it exists primarily to avoid returning a 404 response when a resource actually exists but we haven't installed the path to a handler.\n279: \t// it is exposed for easier composition of the individual servers.\n280: \t// the primary users of this field are the WithMuxCompleteProtection filter and the NotFoundHandler\n281: \tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals map[string]<-chan struct{}\n282: \n283: \t// ShutdownSendRetryAfter dictates when to initiate shutdown of the HTTP\n284: \t// Server during the graceful termination of the apiserver. If true, we wait\n285: \t// for non longrunning requests in flight to be drained and then initiate a\n286: \t// shutdown of the HTTP Server. If false, we initiate a shutdown of the HTTP\n287: \t// Server as soon as ShutdownDelayDuration has elapsed.\n288: \t// If enabled, after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses, any incoming request is\n289: \t// rejected with a 429 status code and a 'Retry-After' response.\n290: \tShutdownSendRetryAfter bool\n291: \n292: \t// ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod, if set to a positive value,\n293: \t// is the maximum duration the apiserver will wait for all active\n294: \t// watch request(s) to drain.\n295: \t// Once this grace period elapses, the apiserver will no longer\n296: \t// wait for any active watch request(s) in flight to drain, it will\n297: \t// proceed to the next step in the graceful server shutdown process.\n298: \t// If set to a positive value, the apiserver will keep track of the\n299: \t// number of active watch request(s) in flight and during shutdown\n300: \t// it will wait, at most, for the specified duration and allow these\n301: \t// active watch requests to drain with some rate limiting in effect.\n302: \t// The default is zero, which implies the apiserver will not keep\n303: \t// track of active watch request(s) in flight and will not wait\n304: \t// for them to drain, this maintains backward compatibility.\n305: \t// This grace period is orthogonal to other grace periods, and\n306: \t// it is not overridden by any other grace period.\n307: \tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod time.Duration\n308: }\n309: \n310: // DelegationTarget is an interface which allows for composition of API servers with top level handling that works\n311: // as expected.\n312: type DelegationTarget interface {\n313: \t// UnprotectedHandler returns a handler that is NOT protected by a normal chain\n314: \tUnprotectedHandler() http.Handler\n315: \n316: \t// PostStartHooks returns the post-start hooks that need to be combined\n317: \tPostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry\n318: \n319: \t// PreShutdownHooks returns the pre-stop hooks that need to be combined\n320: \tPreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n321: \n322: \t// HealthzChecks returns the healthz checks that need to be combined\n323: \tHealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker\n324: \n325: \t// ListedPaths returns the paths for supporting an index\n326: \tListedPaths() []string\n327: \n328: \t// NextDelegate returns the next delegationTarget in the chain of delegations\n329: \tNextDelegate() DelegationTarget\n330: \n331: \t// PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\n332: \tPrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer\n333: \n334: \t// MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals exposes registered signals that indicate if all known HTTP paths have been installed.\n335: \tMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{}\n336: \n337: \t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n338: \t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n339: \t// for being called more than once.\n340: \tDestroy()\n341: }\n342: \n343: func (s *GenericAPIServer) UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler {\n344: \t// when we delegate, we need the server we're delegating to choose whether or not to use gorestful\n345: \treturn s.Handler.Director\n346: }\n347: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry {\n348: \treturn s.postStartHooks\n349: }\n350: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry {\n351: \treturn s.preShutdownHooks\n352: }\n353: func (s *GenericAPIServer) HealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker {\n354: \treturn s.healthzRegistry.checks\n355: }\n356: func (s *GenericAPIServer) ListedPaths() []string {\n357: \treturn s.listedPathProvider.ListedPaths()\n358: }\n359: \n360: func (s *GenericAPIServer) NextDelegate() DelegationTarget {\n361: \treturn s.delegationTarget\n362: }\n363: \n364: // RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal registers the given signal that will be used to determine if all known\n365: // HTTP paths have been registered. It is okay to call this method after instantiating the generic server but before running.\n366: func (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal(signalName string, signal <-chan struct{}) error {\n367: \tif _, exists := s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName]; exists {\n368: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%s already registered\", signalName)\n369: \t}\n370: \ts.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals[signalName] = signal\n371: \treturn nil\n372: }\n373: \n374: func (s *GenericAPIServer) MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{} {\n375: \treturn s.muxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals\n376: }\n377: \n378: // RegisterDestroyFunc registers a function that will be called during Destroy().\n379: // The function have to be idempotent and prepared to be called more than once.\n380: func (s *GenericAPIServer) RegisterDestroyFunc(destroyFn func()) {\n381: \ts.destroyFns = append(s.destroyFns, destroyFn)\n382: }\n383: \n384: // Destroy cleans up all its and its delegation target resources on shutdown.\n385: // It starts with destroying its own resources and later proceeds with\n386: // its delegation target.\n387: func (s *GenericAPIServer) Destroy() {\n388: \tfor _, destroyFn := range s.destroyFns {\n389: \t\tdestroyFn()\n390: \t}\n391: \tif s.delegationTarget != nil {\n392: \t\ts.delegationTarget.Destroy()\n393: \t}\n394: }\n395: \n396: type emptyDelegate struct {\n397: \t// handler is called at the end of the delegation chain\n398: \t// when a request has been made against an unregistered HTTP path the individual servers will simply pass it through until it reaches the handler.\n399: \thandler http.Handler\n400: }\n401: \n402: func NewEmptyDelegate() DelegationTarget {\n403: \treturn emptyDelegate{}\n404: }\n405: \n406: // NewEmptyDelegateWithCustomHandler allows for registering a custom handler usually for special handling of 404 requests\n407: func NewEmptyDelegateWithCustomHandler(handler http.Handler) DelegationTarget {\n408: \treturn emptyDelegate{handler}\n409: }\n410: \n411: func (s emptyDelegate) UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler {\n412: \treturn s.handler\n413: }\n414: func (s emptyDelegate) PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry {\n415: \treturn map[string]postStartHookEntry{}\n416: }\n417: func (s emptyDelegate) PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry {\n418: \treturn map[string]preShutdownHookEntry{}\n419: }\n420: func (s emptyDelegate) HealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker {\n421: \treturn []healthz.HealthChecker{}\n422: }\n423: func (s emptyDelegate) ListedPaths() []string {\n424: \treturn []string{}\n425: }\n426: func (s emptyDelegate) NextDelegate() DelegationTarget {\n427: \treturn nil\n428: }\n429: func (s emptyDelegate) PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer {\n430: \treturn preparedGenericAPIServer{nil}\n431: }\n432: func (s emptyDelegate) MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{} {\n433: \treturn map[string]<-chan struct{}{}\n434: }\n435: func (s emptyDelegate) Destroy() {\n436: }\n437: \n438: // preparedGenericAPIServer is a private wrapper that enforces a call of PrepareRun() before Run can be invoked.\n439: type preparedGenericAPIServer struct {\n440: \t*GenericAPIServer\n441: }\n442: \n443: // PrepareRun does post API installation setup steps. It calls recursively the same function of the delegates.\n444: func (s *GenericAPIServer) PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer {\n445: \ts.delegationTarget.PrepareRun()\n446: \n447: \tif s.openAPIConfig != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\n448: \t\ts.OpenAPIVersionedService, s.StaticOpenAPISpec = routes.OpenAPI{\n449: \t\t\tConfig: s.openAPIConfig,\n450: \t\t}.InstallV2(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n451: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL200-450"
  }
}

\t\t\t\tTitle: \"Generic API Server\",\n516: \t\t\t},\n517: \t\t},\n518: \t\tDefaultResponse: &spec3.Response{\n519: \t\t\tResponseProps: spec3.ResponseProps{\n520: \t\t\t\tDescription: \"Default Response.\",\n521: \t\t\t},\n522: \t\t},\n523: \t\tGetOperationIDAndTags: apiopenapi.GetOperationIDAndTags,\n524: \t\tGetDefinitionName:     defNamer.GetDefinitionName,\n525: \t\tGetDefinitions:        getDefinitions,\n526: \t}\n527: \tdefaultConfig.Definitions = getDefinitions(func(name string) spec.Ref {\n528: \t\tdefName, _ := defaultConfig.GetDefinitionName(name)\n529: \t\treturn spec.MustCreateRef(\"#/components/schemas/\" + openapicommon.EscapeJsonPointer(defName))\n530: \t})\n531: \n532: \treturn defaultConfig\n533: }\n534: \n535: func (c *AuthenticationInfo) ApplyClientCert(clientCA dynamiccertificates.CAContentProvider, servingInfo *SecureServingInfo) error {\n536: \tif servingInfo == nil {\n537: \t\treturn nil\n538: \t}\n539: \tif clientCA == nil {\n540: \t\treturn nil\n541: \t}\n542: \tif servingInfo.ClientCA == nil {\n543: \t\tservingInfo.ClientCA = clientCA\n544: \t\treturn nil\n545: \t}\n546: \n547: \tservingInfo.ClientCA = dynamiccertificates.NewUnionCAContentProvider(servingInfo.ClientCA, clientCA)\n548: \treturn nil\n549: }\n550: \n551: type completedConfig struct {\n552: \t*Config\n553: \n554: \t//===========================================================================\n555: \t// values below here are filled in during completion\n556: \t//===========================================================================\n557: \n558: \t// SharedInformerFactory provides shared informers for resources\n559: \tSharedInformerFactory informers.SharedInformerFactory\n560: }\n561: \n562: type CompletedConfig struct {\n563: \t// Embed a private pointer that cannot be instantiated outside of this package.\n564: \t*completedConfig\n565: }\n566: \n567: // AddHealthChecks adds a health check to our config to be exposed by the health endpoints\n568: // of our configured apiserver. We should prefer this to adding healthChecks directly to\n569: // the config unless we explicitly want to add a healthcheck only to a specific health endpoint.\n570: func (c *Config) AddHealthChecks(healthChecks ...healthz.HealthChecker) {\n571: \tc.HealthzChecks = append(c.HealthzChecks, healthChecks...)\n572: \tc.LivezChecks = append(c.LivezChecks, healthChecks...)\n573: \tc.ReadyzChecks = append(c.ReadyzChecks, healthChecks...)\n574: }\n575: \n576: // AddHealthzChecks adds the provided health checks to our config to be exposed by the\n577: // healthz endpoint of our configured apiserver.\n578: func (c *Config) AddHealthzChecks(healthChecks ...healthz.HealthChecker) {\n579: \tc.HealthzChecks = append(c.HealthzChecks, healthChecks...)\n580: }\n581: \n582: // AddLivezChecks adds the provided health checks to our config to be exposed by the\n583: // livez endpoint of our configured apiserver.\n584: func (c *Config) AddLivezChecks(healthChecks ...healthz.HealthChecker) {\n585: \tc.LivezChecks = append(c.LivezChecks, healthChecks...)\n586: }\n587: \n588: // AddReadyzChecks adds a health check to our config to be exposed by the readyz endpoint\n589: // of our configured apiserver.\n590: func (c *Config) AddReadyzChecks(healthChecks ...healthz.HealthChecker) {\n591: \tc.ReadyzChecks = append(c.ReadyzChecks, healthChecks...)\n592: }\n593: \n594: // AddPostStartHook allows you to add a PostStartHook that will later be added to the server itself in a New call.\n595: // Name conflicts will cause an error.\n596: func (c *Config) AddPostStartHook(name string, hook PostStartHookFunc) error {\n597: \tif len(name) == 0 {\n598: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"missing name\")\n599: \t}\n600: \tif hook == nil {\n601: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL450-600"
  }
}
94. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 200,
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go",
