}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package rest\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"io\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"net/url\\\"\\n24: \\n25: \\t\\\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\\\"\\n26: \\n27: \\tmetainternalversion \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\\\"\\n28: \\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n32: )\\n33: \\n34: //TODO:\\n35: // Storage interfaces need to be separated into two groups; those that operate\\n36: // on collections and those that operate on individually named items.\\n37: // Collection interfaces:\\n38: // (Method: Current -> Proposed)\\n39: //    GET: Lister -> CollectionGetter\\n40: //    WATCH: Watcher -> CollectionWatcher\\n41: //    CREATE: Creater -> CollectionCreater\\n42: //    DELETE: (n/a) -> CollectionDeleter\\n43: //    UPDATE: (n/a) -> CollectionUpdater\\n44: //\\n45: // Single item interfaces:\\n46: // (Method: Current -> Proposed)\\n47: //    GET: Getter -> NamedGetter\\n48: //    WATCH: (n/a) -> NamedWatcher\\n49: //    CREATE: (n/a) -> NamedCreater\\n50: //    DELETE: Deleter -> NamedDeleter\\n51: //    UPDATE: Update -> NamedUpdater\\n52: \\n53: // Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.\\n54: // Resources which are exported to the RESTful API of apiserver need to implement this interface. It is expected\\n55: // that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.\\n56: //\\n57: // Consider using StorageWithReadiness whenever possible.\\n58: type Storage interface {\\n59: \\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.\\n60: \\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n61: \\tNew() runtime.Object\\n62: \\n63: \\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\n64: \\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\\n65: \\t// for being called more than once.\\n66: \\tDestroy()\\n67: }\\n68: \\n69: // StorageWithReadiness extends Storage interface with the readiness check.\\n70: type StorageWithReadiness interface {\\n71: \\tStorage\\n72: \\n73: \\t// ReadinessCheck allows for checking storage readiness.\\n74: \\tReadinessCheck() error\\n75: }\\n76: \\n77: // Scoper indicates what scope the resource is at. It must be specified.\\n78: // It is usually provided automatically based on your strategy.\\n79: type Scoper interface {\\n80: \\t// NamespaceScoped returns true if the storage is namespaced\\n81: \\tNamespaceScoped() bool\\n82: }\\n83: \\n84: // KindProvider specifies a different kind for its API than for its internal storage.  This is necessary for external\\n85: // objects that are not compiled into the api server.  For such objects, there is no in-memory representation for\\n86: // the object, so they must be represented as generic objects (e.g. runtime.Unknown), but when we present the object as part of\\n87: // API discovery we want to present the specific kind, not the generic internal representation.\\n88: type KindProvider interface {\\n89: \\tKind() string\\n90: }\\n91: \\n92: // ShortNamesProvider is an interface for RESTful storage services. Delivers a list of short names for a resource. The list is used by kubectl to have short names representation of resources.\\n93: type ShortNamesProvider interface {\\n94: \\tShortNames() []string\\n95: }\\n96: \\n97: // CategoriesProvider allows a resource to specify which groups of resources (categories) it's part of. Categories can\\n98: // be used by API clients to refer to a batch of resources by using a single name (e.g. \\\"all\\\" could translate to \\\"pod,rc,svc,...\\\").\\n99: type CategoriesProvider interface {\\n100: \\tCategories() []string\\n101: }\\n102: \\n103: // SingularNameProvider returns singular name of resources. This is used by kubectl discovery to have singular\\n104: // name representation of resources. In case of shortcut conflicts(with CRD shortcuts) singular name should always map to this resource.\\n105: type SingularNameProvider interface {\\n106: \\tGetSingularName() string\\n107: }\\n108: \\n109: // GroupVersionKindProvider is used to specify a particular GroupVersionKind to discovery.  This is used for polymorphic endpoints\\n110: // which generally point to foreign versions.  Scale refers to Scale.v1beta1.extensions for instance.\\n111: // This trumps KindProvider since it is capable of providing the information required.\\n112: // TODO KindProvider (only used by federation) should be removed and replaced with this, but that presents greater risk late in 1.8.\\n113: type GroupVersionKindProvider interface {\\n114: \\tGroupVersionKind(containingGV schema.GroupVersion) schema.GroupVersionKind\\n115: }\\n116: \\n117: // GroupVersionAcceptor is used to determine if a particular GroupVersion is acceptable to send to an endpoint.\\n118: // This is used for endpoints which accept multiple versions (which is extremely rare).\\n119: // The only known instance is pods/evictions which accepts policy/v1, but also policy/v1beta1 for backwards compatibility.\\n120: type GroupVersionAcceptor interface {\\n121: \\tAcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool\\n122: }\\n123: \\n124: // Lister is an object that can retrieve resources that match the provided field and label criteria.\\n125: type Lister interface {\\n126: \\t// NewList returns an empty object that can be used with the List call.\\n127: \\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n128: \\tNewList() runtime.Object\\n129: \\t// List selects resources in the storage which match to the selector. 'options' can be nil.\\n130: \\tList(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n131: \\t// TableConvertor ensures all list implementers also implement table conversion\\n132: \\tTableConvertor\\n133: }\\n134: \\n135: // Getter is an object that can retrieve a named RESTful resource.\\n136: type Getter interface {\\n137: \\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\\n138: \\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\\n139: \\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\\n140: \\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options *metav1.GetOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n141: }\\n142: \\n143: // GetterWithOptions is an object that retrieve a named RESTful resource and takes\\n144: // additional options on the get request. It allows a caller to also receive the\\n145: // subpath of the GET request.\\n146: type GetterWithOptions interface {\\n147: \\t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\\n148: \\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\\n149: \\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\\n150: \\t// The options object passed to it is of the same type returned by the NewGetOptions\\n151: \\t// method.\\n152: \\t// TODO: Pass metav1.GetOptions.\\n153: \\tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error)\\n154: \\n155: \\t// NewGetOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\\n156: \\t// options to the Get method. It may return a bool and a string, if true, the\\n157: \\t// value of the request path below the object will be included as the named\\n158: \\t// string in the serialization of the runtime object. E.g., returning \\\"path\\\"\\n159: \\t// will convert the trailing request scheme value to \\\"path\\\" in the map[string][]string\\n160: \\t// passed to the converter.\\n161: \\tNewGetOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\\n162: }\\n163: \\n164: type TableConvertor interface {\\n165: \\tConvertToTable(ctx context.Context, object runtime.Object, tableOptions runtime.Object) (*metav1.Table, error)\\n166: }\\n167: \\n168: // GracefulDeleter knows how to pass deletion options to allow delayed deletion of a\\n169: // RESTful object.\\n170: type GracefulDeleter interface {\\n171: \\t// Delete finds a resource in the storage and deletes it.\\n172: \\t// The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\\n173: \\t// If options are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an invalid\\n174: \\t// request error.\\n175: \\t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\\n176: \\t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\\n177: \\t// Delete *may* return the object that was deleted, or a status object indicating additional\\n178: \\t// information about deletion.\\n179: \\t// It also returns a boolean which is set to true if the resource was instantly\\n180: \\t// deleted or false if it will be deleted asynchronously.\\n181: \\tDelete(ctx context.Context, name string, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\\n182: }\\n183: \\n184: // MayReturnFullObjectDeleter may return deleted object (instead of a simple status) on deletion.\\n185: type MayReturnFullObjectDeleter interface {\\n186: \\tDeleteReturnsDeletedObject() bool\\n187: }\\n188: \\n189: // CollectionDeleter is an object that can delete a collection\\n190: // of RESTful resources.\\n191: type CollectionDeleter interface {\\n192: \\t// DeleteCollection selects all resources in the storage matching given 'listOptions'\\n193: \\t// and deletes them. The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\\n194: \\t// If 'options' are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an\\n195: \\t// invalid request error.\\n196: \\t// DeleteCollection may not be atomic - i.e. it may delete some objects and still\\n197: \\t// return an error after it. On success, returns a list of deleted objects.\\n198: \\tDeleteCollection(ctx context.Context, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions, listOptions *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n199: }\\n200: \\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go%3FL1-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package rest\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"io\"\n22: \t\"net/http\"\n23: \t\"net/url\"\n24: \n25: \t\"sigs.k8s.io/structured-merge-diff/v6/fieldpath\"\n26: \n27: \tmetainternalversion \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/internalversion\"\n28: \tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n29: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n30: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n32: )\n33: \n34: //TODO:\n35: // Storage interfaces need to be separated into two groups; those that operate\n36: // on collections and those that operate on individually named items.\n37: // Collection interfaces:\n38: // (Method: Current -> Proposed)\n39: //    GET: Lister -> CollectionGetter\n40: //    WATCH: Watcher -> CollectionWatcher\n41: //    CREATE: Creater -> CollectionCreater\n42: //    DELETE: (n/a) -> CollectionDeleter\n43: //    UPDATE: (n/a) -> CollectionUpdater\n44: //\n45: // Single item interfaces:\n46: // (Method: Current -> Proposed)\n47: //    GET: Getter -> NamedGetter\n48: //    WATCH: (n/a) -> NamedWatcher\n49: //    CREATE: (n/a) -> NamedCreater\n50: //    DELETE: Deleter -> NamedDeleter\n51: //    UPDATE: Update -> NamedUpdater\n52: \n53: // Storage is a generic interface for RESTful storage services.\n54: // Resources which are exported to the RESTful API of apiserver need to implement this interface. It is expected\n55: // that objects may implement any of the below interfaces.\n56: //\n57: // Consider using StorageWithReadiness whenever possible.\n58: type Storage interface {\n59: \t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create and Update after request data has been put into it.\n60: \t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n61: \tNew() runtime.Object\n62: \n63: \t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n64: \t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n65: \t// for being called more than once.\n66: \tDestroy()\n67: }\n68: \n69: // StorageWithReadiness extends Storage interface with the readiness check.\n70: type StorageWithReadiness interface {\n71: \tStorage\n72: \n73: \t// ReadinessCheck allows for checking storage readiness.\n74: \tReadinessCheck() error\n75: }\n76: \n77: // Scoper indicates what scope the resource is at. It must be specified.\n78: // It is usually provided automatically based on your strategy.\n79: type Scoper interface {\n80: \t// NamespaceScoped returns true if the storage is namespaced\n81: \tNamespaceScoped() bool\n82: }\n83: \n84: // KindProvider specifies a different kind for its API than for its internal storage.  This is necessary for external\n85: // objects that are not compiled into the api server.  For such objects, there is no in-memory representation for\n86: // the object, so they must be represented as generic objects (e.g. runtime.Unknown), but when we present the object as part of\n87: // API discovery we want to present the specific kind, not the generic internal representation.\n88: type KindProvider interface {\n89: \tKind() string\n90: }\n91: \n92: // ShortNamesProvider is an interface for RESTful storage services. Delivers a list of short names for a resource. The list is used by kubectl to have short names representation of resources.\n93: type ShortNamesProvider interface {\n94: \tShortNames() []string\n95: }\n96: \n97: // CategoriesProvider allows a resource to specify which groups of resources (categories) it's part of. Categories can\n98: // be used by API clients to refer to a batch of resources by using a single name (e.g. \"all\" could translate to \"pod,rc,svc,...\").\n99: type CategoriesProvider interface {\n100: \tCategories() []string\n101: }\n102: \n103: // SingularNameProvider returns singular name of resources. This is used by kubectl discovery to have singular\n104: // name representation of resources. In case of shortcut conflicts(with CRD shortcuts) singular name should always map to this resource.\n105: type SingularNameProvider interface {\n106: \tGetSingularName() string\n107: }\n108: \n109: // GroupVersionKindProvider is used to specify a particular GroupVersionKind to discovery.  This is used for polymorphic endpoints\n110: // which generally point to foreign versions.  Scale refers to Scale.v1beta1.extensions for instance.\n111: // This trumps KindProvider since it is capable of providing the information required.\n112: // TODO KindProvider (only used by federation) should be removed and replaced with this, but that presents greater risk late in 1.8.\n113: type GroupVersionKindProvider interface {\n114: \tGroupVersionKind(containingGV schema.GroupVersion) schema.GroupVersionKind\n115: }\n116: \n117: // GroupVersionAcceptor is used to determine if a particular GroupVersion is acceptable to send to an endpoint.\n118: // This is used for endpoints which accept multiple versions (which is extremely rare).\n119: // The only known instance is pods/evictions which accepts policy/v1, but also policy/v1beta1 for backwards compatibility.\n120: type GroupVersionAcceptor interface {\n121: \tAcceptsGroupVersion(gv schema.GroupVersion) bool\n122: }\n123: \n124: // Lister is an object that can retrieve resources that match the provided field and label criteria.\n125: type Lister interface {\n126: \t// NewList returns an empty object that can be used with the List call.\n127: \t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n128: \tNewList() runtime.Object\n129: \t// List selects resources in the storage which match to the selector. 'options' can be nil.\n130: \tList(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n131: \t// TableConvertor ensures all list implementers also implement table conversion\n132: \tTableConvertor\n133: }\n134: \n135: // Getter is an object that can retrieve a named RESTful resource.\n136: type Getter interface {\n137: \t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\n138: \t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n139: \t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n140: \tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options *metav1.GetOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n141: }\n142: \n143: // GetterWithOptions is an object that retrieve a named RESTful resource and takes\n144: // additional options on the get request. It allows a caller to also receive the\n145: // subpath of the GET request.\n146: type GetterWithOptions interface {\n147: \t// Get finds a resource in the storage by name and returns it.\n148: \t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n149: \t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n150: \t// The options object passed to it is of the same type returned by the NewGetOptions\n151: \t// method.\n152: \t// TODO: Pass metav1.GetOptions.\n153: \tGet(ctx context.Context, name string, options runtime.Object) (runtime.Object, error)\n154: \n155: \t// NewGetOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\n156: \t// options to the Get method. It may return a bool and a string, if true, the\n157: \t// value of the request path below the object will be included as the named\n158: \t// string in the serialization of the runtime object. E.g., returning \"path\"\n159: \t// will convert the trailing request scheme value to \"path\" in the map[string][]string\n160: \t// passed to the converter.\n161: \tNewGetOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\n162: }\n163: \n164: type TableConvertor interface {\n165: \tConvertToTable(ctx context.Context, object runtime.Object, tableOptions runtime.Object) (*metav1.Table, error)\n166: }\n167: \n168: // GracefulDeleter knows how to pass deletion options to allow delayed deletion of a\n169: // RESTful object.\n170: type GracefulDeleter interface {\n171: \t// Delete finds a resource in the storage and deletes it.\n172: \t// The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\n173: \t// If options are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an invalid\n174: \t// request error.\n175: \t// Although it can return an arbitrary error value, IsNotFound(err) is true for the\n176: \t// returned error value err when the specified resource is not found.\n177: \t// Delete *may* return the object that was deleted, or a status object indicating additional\n178: \t// information about deletion.\n179: \t// It also returns a boolean which is set to true if the resource was instantly\n180: \t// deleted or false if it will be deleted asynchronously.\n181: \tDelete(ctx context.Context, name string, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\n182: }\n183: \n184: // MayReturnFullObjectDeleter may return deleted object (instead of a simple status) on deletion.\n185: type MayReturnFullObjectDeleter interface {\n186: \tDeleteReturnsDeletedObject() bool\n187: }\n188: \n189: // CollectionDeleter is an object that can delete a collection\n190: // of RESTful resources.\n191: type CollectionDeleter interface {\n192: \t// DeleteCollection selects all resources in the storage matching given 'listOptions'\n193: \t// and deletes them. The delete attempt is validated by the deleteValidation first.\n194: \t// If 'options' are provided, the resource will attempt to honor them or return an\n195: \t// invalid request error.\n196: \t// DeleteCollection may not be atomic - i.e. it may delete some objects and still\n197: \t// return an error after it. On success, returns a list of deleted objects.\n198: \tDeleteCollection(ctx context.Context, deleteValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.DeleteOptions, listOptions *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n199: }\n200: \n201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go%3FL1-200"
  }
}
95. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go",
  "repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package admission\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"io\\\"\\n22: \\n23: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n25: \\tauditinternal \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\\\"\\n27: )\\n28: \\n29: // Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request\\n30: // that is used to make an admission decision.\\n31: type Attributes interface {\\n32: \\t// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client\\n33: \\t// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return\\n34: \\t// the empty string\\n35: \\tGetName() string\\n36: \\t// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)\\n37: \\tGetNamespace() string\\n38: \\t// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods\\n39: \\tGetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource\\n40: \\t// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.\\n41: \\t// For instance, /pods has the resource \\\"pods\\\" and the kind \\\"Pod\\\", while /pods/foo/status has the resource \\\"pods\\\", the sub resource \\\"status\\\", and the kind \\\"Pod\\\"\\n42: \\t// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource \\\"pods\\\", subresource \\\"binding\\\", and kind \\\"Binding\\\".\\n43: \\tGetSubresource() string\\n44: \\t// GetOperation is the operation being performed\\n45: \\tGetOperation() Operation\\n46: \\t// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed\\n47: \\tGetOperationOptions() runtime.Object\\n48: \\t// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent\\n49: \\t// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.\\n50: \\t// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it\\n51: \\t// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.\\n52: \\tIsDryRun() bool\\n53: \\t// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied\\n54: \\tGetObject() runtime.Object\\n55: \\t// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.\\n56: \\tGetOldObject() runtime.Object\\n57: \\t// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod\\n58: \\tGetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind\\n59: \\t// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user\\n60: \\tGetUserInfo() user.Info\\n61: \\n62: \\t// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where\\n63: \\t// \\\"podsecuritypolicy\\\" is the name of the plugin, \\\"admission.k8s.io\\\" is the name of the organization, \\\"admit-policy\\\" is the key name.\\n64: \\t// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.\\n65: \\t// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.\\n66: \\t// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or\\n67: \\t// equal to Metadata.\\n68: \\tAddAnnotation(key, value string) error\\n69: \\n70: \\t// AddAnnotationWithLevel sets annotation according to key-value pair with additional intended audit level.\\n71: \\t// An Annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or equal to the\\n72: \\t// intended audit level.\\n73: \\tAddAnnotationWithLevel(key, value string, level auditinternal.Level) error\\n74: \\n75: \\t// GetReinvocationContext tracks the admission request information relevant to the re-invocation policy.\\n76: \\tGetReinvocationContext() ReinvocationContext\\n77: }\\n78: \\n79: // ObjectInterfaces is an interface used by AdmissionController to get object interfaces\\n80: // such as Converter or Defaulter. These interfaces are normally coming from Request Scope\\n81: // to handle special cases like CRDs.\\n82: type ObjectInterfaces interface {\\n83: \\t// GetObjectCreater is the ObjectCreator appropriate for the requested object.\\n84: \\tGetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater\\n85: \\t// GetObjectTyper is the ObjectTyper appropriate for the requested object.\\n86: \\tGetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper\\n87: \\t// GetObjectDefaulter is the ObjectDefaulter appropriate for the requested object.\\n88: \\tGetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n89: \\t// GetObjectConvertor is the ObjectConvertor appropriate for the requested object.\\n90: \\tGetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n91: \\t// GetEquivalentResourceMapper is the EquivalentResourceMapper appropriate for finding equivalent resources and expected kind for the requested object.\\n92: \\tGetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\\n93: }\\n94: \\n95: // privateAnnotationsGetter is a private interface which allows users to get annotations from Attributes.\\n96: type privateAnnotationsGetter interface {\\n97: \\tgetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\\n98: }\\n99: \\n100: // AnnotationsGetter allows users to get annotations from Attributes. An alternate Attribute should implement\\n101: // this interface.\\n102: type AnnotationsGetter interface {\\n103: \\tGetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\\n104: }\\n105: \\n106: // ReinvocationContext provides access to the admission related state required to implement the re-invocation policy.\\n107: type ReinvocationContext interface {\\n108: \\t// IsReinvoke returns true if the current admission check is a re-invocation.\\n109: \\tIsReinvoke() bool\\n110: \\t// SetIsReinvoke sets the current admission check as a re-invocation.\\n111: \\tSetIsReinvoke()\\n112: \\t// ShouldReinvoke returns true if any plugin has requested a re-invocation.\\n113: \\tShouldReinvoke() bool\\n114: \\t// SetShouldReinvoke signals that a re-invocation is desired.\\n115: \\tSetShouldReinvoke()\\n116: \\t// AddValue set a value for a plugin name, possibly overriding a previous value.\\n117: \\tSetValue(plugin string, v interface{})\\n118: \\t// Value reads a value for a webhook.\\n119: \\tValue(plugin string) interface{}\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // Interface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\\n123: type Interface interface {\\n124: \\t// Handles returns true if this admission controller can handle the given operation\\n125: \\t// where operation can be one of CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, or CONNECT\\n126: \\tHandles(operation Operation) bool\\n127: }\\n128: \\n129: type MutationInterface interface {\\n130: \\tInterface\\n131: \\n132: \\t// Admit makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.\\n133: \\t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\\n134: \\tAdmit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\\n135: }\\n136: \\n137: // ValidationInterface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\\n138: type ValidationInterface interface {\\n139: \\tInterface\\n140: \\n141: \\t// Validate makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.  It is NOT allowed to mutate\\n142: \\t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\\n143: \\tValidate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\\n144: }\\n145: \\n146: // Operation is the type of resource operation being checked for admission control\\n147: type Operation string\\n148: \\n149: // Operation constants\\n150: const (\\n151: \\tCreate  Operation = \\\"CREATE\\\"\\n152: \\tUpdate  Operation = \\\"UPDATE\\\"\\n153: \\tDelete  Operation = \\\"DELETE\\\"\\n154: \\tConnect Operation = \\\"CONNECT\\\"\\n155: )\\n156: \\n157: // PluginInitializer is used for initialization of shareable resources between admission plugins.\\n158: // After initialization the resources have to be set separately\\n159: type PluginInitializer interface {\\n160: \\tInitialize(plugin Interface)\\n161: }\\n162: \\n163: // InitializationValidator holds ValidateInitialization functions, which are responsible for validation of initialized\\n164: // shared resources and should be implemented on admission plugins\\n165: type InitializationValidator interface {\\n166: \\tValidateInitialization() error\\n167: }\\n168: \\n169: // ConfigProvider provides a way to get configuration for an admission plugin based on its name\\n170: type ConfigProvider interface {\\n171: \\tConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error)\\n172: }\\n173: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package admission\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"io\"\n22: \n23: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n24: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n25: \tauditinternal \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/apis/audit\"\n26: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n27: )\n28: \n29: // Attributes is an interface used by AdmissionController to get information about a request\n30: // that is used to make an admission decision.\n31: type Attributes interface {\n32: \t// GetName returns the name of the object as presented in the request.  On a CREATE operation, the client\n33: \t// may omit name and rely on the server to generate the name.  If that is the case, this method will return\n34: \t// the empty string\n35: \tGetName() string\n36: \t// GetNamespace is the namespace associated with the request (if any)\n37: \tGetNamespace() string\n38: \t// GetResource is the name of the resource being requested.  This is not the kind.  For example: pods\n39: \tGetResource() schema.GroupVersionResource\n40: \t// GetSubresource is the name of the subresource being requested.  This is a different resource, scoped to the parent resource, but it may have a different kind.\n41: \t// For instance, /pods has the resource \"pods\" and the kind \"Pod\", while /pods/foo/status has the resource \"pods\", the sub resource \"status\", and the kind \"Pod\"\n42: \t// (because status operates on pods). The binding resource for a pod though may be /pods/foo/binding, which has resource \"pods\", subresource \"binding\", and kind \"Binding\".\n43: \tGetSubresource() string\n44: \t// GetOperation is the operation being performed\n45: \tGetOperation() Operation\n46: \t// GetOperationOptions is the options for the operation being performed\n47: \tGetOperationOptions() runtime.Object\n48: \t// IsDryRun indicates that modifications will definitely not be persisted for this request. This is to prevent\n49: \t// admission controllers with side effects and a method of reconciliation from being overwhelmed.\n50: \t// However, a value of false for this does not mean that the modification will be persisted, because it\n51: \t// could still be rejected by a subsequent validation step.\n52: \tIsDryRun() bool\n53: \t// GetObject is the object from the incoming request prior to default values being applied\n54: \tGetObject() runtime.Object\n55: \t// GetOldObject is the existing object. Only populated for UPDATE and DELETE requests.\n56: \tGetOldObject() runtime.Object\n57: \t// GetKind is the type of object being manipulated.  For example: Pod\n58: \tGetKind() schema.GroupVersionKind\n59: \t// GetUserInfo is information about the requesting user\n60: \tGetUserInfo() user.Info\n61: \n62: \t// AddAnnotation sets annotation according to key-value pair. The key should be qualified, e.g., podsecuritypolicy.admission.k8s.io/admit-policy, where\n63: \t// \"podsecuritypolicy\" is the name of the plugin, \"admission.k8s.io\" is the name of the organization, \"admit-policy\" is the key name.\n64: \t// An error is returned if the format of key is invalid. When trying to overwrite annotation with a new value, an error is returned.\n65: \t// Both ValidationInterface and MutationInterface are allowed to add Annotations.\n66: \t// By default, an annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or\n67: \t// equal to Metadata.\n68: \tAddAnnotation(key, value string) error\n69: \n70: \t// AddAnnotationWithLevel sets annotation according to key-value pair with additional intended audit level.\n71: \t// An Annotation gets logged into audit event if the request's audit level is greater or equal to the\n72: \t// intended audit level.\n73: \tAddAnnotationWithLevel(key, value string, level auditinternal.Level) error\n74: \n75: \t// GetReinvocationContext tracks the admission request information relevant to the re-invocation policy.\n76: \tGetReinvocationContext() ReinvocationContext\n77: }\n78: \n79: // ObjectInterfaces is an interface used by AdmissionController to get object interfaces\n80: // such as Converter or Defaulter. These interfaces are normally coming from Request Scope\n81: // to handle special cases like CRDs.\n82: type ObjectInterfaces interface {\n83: \t// GetObjectCreater is the ObjectCreator appropriate for the requested object.\n84: \tGetObjectCreater() runtime.ObjectCreater\n85: \t// GetObjectTyper is the ObjectTyper appropriate for the requested object.\n86: \tGetObjectTyper() runtime.ObjectTyper\n87: \t// GetObjectDefaulter is the ObjectDefaulter appropriate for the requested object.\n88: \tGetObjectDefaulter() runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n89: \t// GetObjectConvertor is the ObjectConvertor appropriate for the requested object.\n90: \tGetObjectConvertor() runtime.ObjectConvertor\n91: \t// GetEquivalentResourceMapper is the EquivalentResourceMapper appropriate for finding equivalent resources and expected kind for the requested object.\n92: \tGetEquivalentResourceMapper() runtime.EquivalentResourceMapper\n93: }\n94: \n95: // privateAnnotationsGetter is a private interface which allows users to get annotations from Attributes.\n96: type privateAnnotationsGetter interface {\n97: \tgetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n98: }\n99: \n100: // AnnotationsGetter allows users to get annotations from Attributes. An alternate Attribute should implement\n101: // this interface.\n102: type AnnotationsGetter interface {\n103: \tGetAnnotations(maxLevel auditinternal.Level) map[string]string\n104: }\n105: \n106: // ReinvocationContext provides access to the admission related state required to implement the re-invocation policy.\n107: type ReinvocationContext interface {\n108: \t// IsReinvoke returns true if the current admission check is a re-invocation.\n109: \tIsReinvoke() bool\n110: \t// SetIsReinvoke sets the current admission check as a re-invocation.\n111: \tSetIsReinvoke()\n112: \t// ShouldReinvoke returns true if any plugin has requested a re-invocation.\n113: \tShouldReinvoke() bool\n114: \t// SetShouldReinvoke signals that a re-invocation is desired.\n115: \tSetShouldReinvoke()\n116: \t// AddValue set a value for a plugin name, possibly overriding a previous value.\n117: \tSetValue(plugin string, v interface{})\n118: \t// Value reads a value for a webhook.\n119: \tValue(plugin string) interface{}\n120: }\n121: \n122: // Interface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\n123: type Interface interface {\n124: \t// Handles returns true if this admission controller can handle the given operation\n125: \t// where operation can be one of CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, or CONNECT\n126: \tHandles(operation Operation) bool\n127: }\n128: \n129: type MutationInterface interface {\n130: \tInterface\n131: \n132: \t// Admit makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.\n133: \t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\n134: \tAdmit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\n135: }\n136: \n137: // ValidationInterface is an abstract, pluggable interface for Admission Control decisions.\n138: type ValidationInterface interface {\n139: \tInterface\n140: \n141: \t// Validate makes an admission decision based on the request attributes.  It is NOT allowed to mutate\n142: \t// Context is used only for timeout/deadline/cancellation and tracing information.\n143: \tValidate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) (err error)\n144: }\n145: \n146: // Operation is the type of resource operation being checked for admission control\n147: type Operation string\n148: \n149: // Operation constants\n150: const (\n151: \tCreate  Operation = \"CREATE\"\n152: \tUpdate  Operation = \"UPDATE\"\n153: \tDelete  Operation = \"DELETE\"\n154: \tConnect Operation = \"CONNECT\"\n155: )\n156: \n157: // PluginInitializer is used for initialization of shareable resources between admission plugins.\n158: // After initialization the resources have to be set separately\n159: type PluginInitializer interface {\n160: \tInitialize(plugin Interface)\n161: }\n162: \n163: // InitializationValidator holds ValidateInitialization functions, which are responsible for validation of initialized\n164: // shared resources and should be implemented on admission plugins\n165: type InitializationValidator interface {\n166: \tValidateInitialization() error\n167: }\n168: \n169: // ConfigProvider provides a way to get configuration for an admission plugin based on its name\n170: type ConfigProvider interface {\n171: \tConfigFor(pluginName string) (io.Reader, error)\n172: }\n173: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go"
  }
}
96. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 400,
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go",
  "repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes",

}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"200: \\n201: // Creater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object.\\n202: type Creater interface {\\n203: \\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.\\n204: \\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n205: \\tNew() runtime.Object\\n206: \\n207: \\t// Create creates a new version of a resource.\\n208: \\tCreate(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n209: }\\n210: \\n211: // NamedCreater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object using a name parameter.\\n212: type NamedCreater interface {\\n213: \\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.\\n214: \\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n215: \\tNew() runtime.Object\\n216: \\n217: \\t// Create creates a new version of a resource. It expects a name parameter from the path.\\n218: \\t// This is needed for create operations on subresources which include the name of the parent\\n219: \\t// resource in the path.\\n220: \\tCreate(ctx context.Context, name string, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\\n221: }\\n222: \\n223: // SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver adds configuration options to a Creater for subresources.\\n224: type SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver interface {\\n225: \\t// PreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate indicates that a\\n226: \\t// handler should preserve fields of ObjectMeta that are managed by the system.\\n227: \\tPreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate() bool\\n228: }\\n229: \\n230: // UpdatedObjectInfo provides information about an updated object to an Updater.\\n231: // It requires access to the old object in order to return the newly updated object.\\n232: type UpdatedObjectInfo interface {\\n233: \\t// Returns preconditions built from the updated object, if applicable.\\n234: \\t// May return nil, or a preconditions object containing nil fields,\\n235: \\t// if no preconditions can be determined from the updated object.\\n236: \\tPreconditions() *metav1.Preconditions\\n237: \\n238: \\t// UpdatedObject returns the updated object, given a context and old object.\\n239: \\t// The only time an empty oldObj should be passed in is if a \\\"create on update\\\" is occurring (there is no oldObj).\\n240: \\tUpdatedObject(ctx context.Context, oldObj runtime.Object) (newObj runtime.Object, err error)\\n241: }\\n242: \\n243: // ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\\n244: // if the hook cannot be completed. A ValidateObjectFunc may NOT transform the provided\\n245: // object.\\n246: type ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\\n247: \\n248: // ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\\n249: func ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n250: \\treturn nil\\n251: }\\n252: \\n253: // ValidateObjectUpdateFunc is a function to act on a given object and its predecessor.\\n254: // An error may be returned if the hook cannot be completed. An UpdateObjectFunc\\n255: // may NOT transform the provided object.\\n256: type ValidateObjectUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error\\n257: \\n258: // ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectUpdateFunc.\\n259: func ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error {\\n260: \\treturn nil\\n261: }\\n262: \\n263: // Updater is an object that can update an instance of a RESTful object.\\n264: type Updater interface {\\n265: \\t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Update after request data has been put into it.\\n266: \\t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\\n267: \\tNew() runtime.Object\\n268: \\n269: \\t// Update finds a resource in the storage and updates it. Some implementations\\n270: \\t// may allow updates creates the object - they should set the created boolean\\n271: \\t// to true.\\n272: \\tUpdate(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\\n273: }\\n274: \\n275: // CreaterUpdater is a storage object that must support both create and update.\\n276: // Go prevents embedded interfaces that implement the same method.\\n277: type CreaterUpdater interface {\\n278: \\tCreater\\n279: \\tUpdate(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\\n280: }\\n281: \\n282: // CreaterUpdater must satisfy the Updater interface.\\n283: var _ Updater = CreaterUpdater(nil)\\n284: \\n285: // Patcher is a storage object that supports both get and update.\\n286: type Patcher interface {\\n287: \\tGetter\\n288: \\tUpdater\\n289: }\\n290: \\n291: // Watcher should be implemented by all Storage objects that\\n292: // want to offer the ability to watch for changes through the watch api.\\n293: type Watcher interface {\\n294: \\t// 'label' selects on labels; 'field' selects on the object's fields. Not all fields\\n295: \\t// are supported; an error should be returned if 'field' tries to select on a field that\\n296: \\t// isn't supported. 'resourceVersion' allows for continuing/starting a watch at a\\n297: \\t// particular version.\\n298: \\tWatch(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\\n299: }\\n300: \\n301: // StandardStorage is an interface covering the common verbs. Provided for testing whether a\\n302: // resource satisfies the normal storage methods. Use Storage when passing opaque storage objects.\\n303: type StandardStorage interface {\\n304: \\tGetter\\n305: \\tLister\\n306: \\tCreaterUpdater\\n307: \\tGracefulDeleter\\n308: \\tCollectionDeleter\\n309: \\tWatcher\\n310: \\n311: \\t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\\n312: \\t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\\n313: \\t// for being called more than once.\\n314: \\tDestroy()\\n315: }\\n316: \\n317: // Redirector know how to return a remote resource's location.\\n318: type Redirector interface {\\n319: \\t// ResourceLocation should return the remote location of the given resource, and an optional transport to use to request it, or an error.\\n320: \\tResourceLocation(ctx context.Context, id string) (remoteLocation *url.URL, transport http.RoundTripper, err error)\\n321: }\\n322: \\n323: // Responder abstracts the normal response behavior for a REST method and is passed to callers that\\n324: // may wish to handle the response directly in some cases, but delegate to the normal error or object\\n325: // behavior in other cases.\\n326: type Responder interface {\\n327: \\t// Object writes the provided object to the response. Invoking this method multiple times is undefined.\\n328: \\tObject(statusCode int, obj runtime.Object)\\n329: \\t// Error writes the provided error to the response. This method may only be invoked once.\\n330: \\tError(err error)\\n331: }\\n332: \\n333: // Connecter is a storage object that responds to a connection request.\\n334: type Connecter interface {\\n335: \\t// Connect returns an http.Handler that will handle the request/response for a given API invocation.\\n336: \\t// The provided responder may be used for common API responses. The responder will write both status\\n337: \\t// code and body, so the ServeHTTP method should exit after invoking the responder. The Handler will\\n338: \\t// be used for a single API request and then discarded. The Responder is guaranteed to write to the\\n339: \\t// same http.ResponseWriter passed to ServeHTTP.\\n340: \\tConnect(ctx context.Context, id string, options runtime.Object, r Responder) (http.Handler, error)\\n341: \\n342: \\t// NewConnectOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\\n343: \\t// options to the Connect method. If nil, then a nil options object is passed to\\n344: \\t// Connect. It may return a bool and a string. If true, the value of the request\\n345: \\t// path below the object will be included as the named string in the serialization\\n346: \\t// of the runtime object.\\n347: \\tNewConnectOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\\n348: \\n349: \\t// ConnectMethods returns the list of HTTP methods handled by Connect\\n350: \\tConnectMethods() []string\\n351: }\\n352: \\n353: // ResourceStreamer is an interface implemented by objects that prefer to be streamed from the server\\n354: // instead of decoded directly.\\n355: type ResourceStreamer interface {\\n356: \\t// InputStream should return an io.ReadCloser if the provided object supports streaming. The desired\\n357: \\t// api version and an accept header (may be empty) are passed to the call. If no error occurs,\\n358: \\t// the caller may return a flag indicating whether the result should be flushed as writes occur\\n359: \\t// and a content type string that indicates the type of the stream.\\n360: \\t// If a null stream is returned, a StatusNoContent response wil be generated.\\n361: \\tInputStream(ctx context.Context, apiVersion, acceptHeader string) (stream io.ReadCloser, flush bool, mimeType string, err error)\\n362: }\\n363: \\n364: // StorageMetadata is an optional interface that callers can implement to provide additional\\n365: // information about their Storage objects.\\n366: type StorageMetadata interface {\\n367: \\t// ProducesMIMETypes returns a list of the MIME types the specified HTTP verb (GET, POST, DELETE,\\n368: \\t// PATCH) can respond with.\\n369: \\tProducesMIMETypes(verb string) []string\\n370: \\n371: \\t// ProducesObject returns an object the specified HTTP verb respond with. It will overwrite storage object if\\n372: \\t// it is not nil. Only the type of the return object matters, the value will be ignored.\\n373: \\tProducesObject(verb string) interface{}\\n374: }\\n375: \\n376: // StorageVersionProvider is an optional interface that a storage object can\\n377: // implement if it wishes to disclose its storage version.\\n378: type StorageVersionProvider interface {\\n379: \\t// StorageVersion returns a group versioner, which will outputs the gvk\\n380: \\t// an object will be converted to before persisted in etcd, given a\\n381: \\t// list of kinds the object might belong to.\\n382: \\tStorageVersion() runtime.GroupVersioner\\n383: }\\n384: \\n385: // ResetFieldsStrategy is an optional interface that a storage object can\\n386: // implement if it wishes to provide the fields reset by its strategies.\\n387: type ResetFieldsStrategy interface {\\n388: \\tGetResetFields() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\\n389: }\\n390: \\n391: // ResetFieldsFilterStrategy is an optional interface that a storage object can\\n392: // implement if it wishes to provide a fields filter reset by its strategies.\\n393: type ResetFieldsFilterStrategy interface {\\n394: \\tGetResetFieldsFilter() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\\n395: }\\n396: \\n397: // CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy is a union of RESTCreateUpdateStrategy\\n398: // and ResetFieldsStrategy.\\n399: type CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy interface {\\n400: \\tRESTCreateUpdateStrategy\\n401: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go%3FL200-400\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "200: \n201: // Creater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object.\n202: type Creater interface {\n203: \t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.\n204: \t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n205: \tNew() runtime.Object\n206: \n207: \t// Create creates a new version of a resource.\n208: \tCreate(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n209: }\n210: \n211: // NamedCreater is an object that can create an instance of a RESTful object using a name parameter.\n212: type NamedCreater interface {\n213: \t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Create after request data has been put into it.\n214: \t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n215: \tNew() runtime.Object\n216: \n217: \t// Create creates a new version of a resource. It expects a name parameter from the path.\n218: \t// This is needed for create operations on subresources which include the name of the parent\n219: \t// resource in the path.\n220: \tCreate(ctx context.Context, name string, obj runtime.Object, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (runtime.Object, error)\n221: }\n222: \n223: // SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver adds configuration options to a Creater for subresources.\n224: type SubresourceObjectMetaPreserver interface {\n225: \t// PreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate indicates that a\n226: \t// handler should preserve fields of ObjectMeta that are managed by the system.\n227: \tPreserveRequestObjectMetaSystemFieldsOnSubresourceCreate() bool\n228: }\n229: \n230: // UpdatedObjectInfo provides information about an updated object to an Updater.\n231: // It requires access to the old object in order to return the newly updated object.\n232: type UpdatedObjectInfo interface {\n233: \t// Returns preconditions built from the updated object, if applicable.\n234: \t// May return nil, or a preconditions object containing nil fields,\n235: \t// if no preconditions can be determined from the updated object.\n236: \tPreconditions() *metav1.Preconditions\n237: \n238: \t// UpdatedObject returns the updated object, given a context and old object.\n239: \t// The only time an empty oldObj should be passed in is if a \"create on update\" is occurring (there is no oldObj).\n240: \tUpdatedObject(ctx context.Context, oldObj runtime.Object) (newObj runtime.Object, err error)\n241: }\n242: \n243: // ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\n244: // if the hook cannot be completed. A ValidateObjectFunc may NOT transform the provided\n245: // object.\n246: type ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\n247: \n248: // ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \"admit everything\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\n249: func ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\n250: \treturn nil\n251: }\n252: \n253: // ValidateObjectUpdateFunc is a function to act on a given object and its predecessor.\n254: // An error may be returned if the hook cannot be completed. An UpdateObjectFunc\n255: // may NOT transform the provided object.\n256: type ValidateObjectUpdateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error\n257: \n258: // ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc is a \"admit everything\" instance of ValidateObjectUpdateFunc.\n259: func ValidateAllObjectUpdateFunc(ctx context.Context, obj, old runtime.Object) error {\n260: \treturn nil\n261: }\n262: \n263: // Updater is an object that can update an instance of a RESTful object.\n264: type Updater interface {\n265: \t// New returns an empty object that can be used with Update after request data has been put into it.\n266: \t// This object must be a pointer type for use with Codec.DecodeInto([]byte, runtime.Object)\n267: \tNew() runtime.Object\n268: \n269: \t// Update finds a resource in the storage and updates it. Some implementations\n270: \t// may allow updates creates the object - they should set the created boolean\n271: \t// to true.\n272: \tUpdate(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\n273: }\n274: \n275: // CreaterUpdater is a storage object that must support both create and update.\n276: // Go prevents embedded interfaces that implement the same method.\n277: type CreaterUpdater interface {\n278: \tCreater\n279: \tUpdate(ctx context.Context, name string, objInfo UpdatedObjectInfo, createValidation ValidateObjectFunc, updateValidation ValidateObjectUpdateFunc, forceAllowCreate bool, options *metav1.UpdateOptions) (runtime.Object, bool, error)\n280: }\n281: \n282: // CreaterUpdater must satisfy the Updater interface.\n283: var _ Updater = CreaterUpdater(nil)\n284: \n285: // Patcher is a storage object that supports both get and update.\n286: type Patcher interface {\n287: \tGetter\n288: \tUpdater\n289: }\n290: \n291: // Watcher should be implemented by all Storage objects that\n292: // want to offer the ability to watch for changes through the watch api.\n293: type Watcher interface {\n294: \t// 'label' selects on labels; 'field' selects on the object's fields. Not all fields\n295: \t// are supported; an error should be returned if 'field' tries to select on a field that\n296: \t// isn't supported. 'resourceVersion' allows for continuing/starting a watch at a\n297: \t// particular version.\n298: \tWatch(ctx context.Context, options *metainternalversion.ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\n299: }\n300: \n301: // StandardStorage is an interface covering the common verbs. Provided for testing whether a\n302: // resource satisfies the normal storage methods. Use Storage when passing opaque storage objects.\n303: type StandardStorage interface {\n304: \tGetter\n305: \tLister\n306: \tCreaterUpdater\n307: \tGracefulDeleter\n308: \tCollectionDeleter\n309: \tWatcher\n310: \n311: \t// Destroy cleans up its resources on shutdown.\n312: \t// Destroy has to be implemented in thread-safe way and be prepared\n313: \t// for being called more than once.\n314: \tDestroy()\n315: }\n316: \n317: // Redirector know how to return a remote resource's location.\n318: type Redirector interface {\n319: \t// ResourceLocation should return the remote location of the given resource, and an optional transport to use to request it, or an error.\n320: \tResourceLocation(ctx context.Context, id string) (remoteLocation *url.URL, transport http.RoundTripper, err error)\n321: }\n322: \n323: // Responder abstracts the normal response behavior for a REST method and is passed to callers that\n324: // may wish to handle the response directly in some cases, but delegate to the normal error or object\n325: // behavior in other cases.\n326: type Responder interface {\n327: \t// Object writes the provided object to the response. Invoking this method multiple times is undefined.\n328: \tObject(statusCode int, obj runtime.Object)\n329: \t// Error writes the provided error to the response. This method may only be invoked once.\n330: \tError(err error)\n331: }\n332: \n333: // Connecter is a storage object that responds to a connection request.\n334: type Connecter interface {\n335: \t// Connect returns an http.Handler that will handle the request/response for a given API invocation.\n336: \t// The provided responder may be used for common API responses. The responder will write both status\n337: \t// code and body, so the ServeHTTP method should exit after invoking the responder. The Handler will\n338: \t// be used for a single API request and then discarded. The Responder is guaranteed to write to the\n339: \t// same http.ResponseWriter passed to ServeHTTP.\n340: \tConnect(ctx context.Context, id string, options runtime.Object, r Responder) (http.Handler, error)\n341: \n342: \t// NewConnectOptions returns an empty options object that will be used to pass\n343: \t// options to the Connect method. If nil, then a nil options object is passed to\n344: \t// Connect. It may return a bool and a string. If true, the value of the request\n345: \t// path below the object will be included as the named string in the serialization\n346: \t// of the runtime object.\n347: \tNewConnectOptions() (runtime.Object, bool, string)\n348: \n349: \t// ConnectMethods returns the list of HTTP methods handled by Connect\n350: \tConnectMethods() []string\n351: }\n352: \n353: // ResourceStreamer is an interface implemented by objects that prefer to be streamed from the server\n354: // instead of decoded directly.\n355: type ResourceStreamer interface {\n356: \t// InputStream should return an io.ReadCloser if the provided object supports streaming. The desired\n357: \t// api version and an accept header (may be empty) are passed to the call. If no error occurs,\n358: \t// the caller may return a flag indicating whether the result should be flushed as writes occur\n359: \t// and a content type string that indicates the type of the stream.\n360: \t// If a null stream is returned, a StatusNoContent response wil be generated.\n361: \tInputStream(ctx context.Context, apiVersion, acceptHeader string) (stream io.ReadCloser, flush bool, mimeType string, err error)\n362: }\n363: \n364: // StorageMetadata is an optional interface that callers can implement to provide additional\n365: // information about their Storage objects.\n366: type StorageMetadata interface {\n367: \t// ProducesMIMETypes returns a list of the MIME types the specified HTTP verb (GET, POST, DELETE,\n368: \t// PATCH) can respond with.\n369: \tProducesMIMETypes(verb string) []string\n370: \n371: \t// ProducesObject returns an object the specified HTTP verb respond with. It will overwrite storage object if\n372: \t// it is not nil. Only the type of the return object matters, the value will be ignored.\n373: \tProducesObject(verb string) interface{}\n374: }\n375: \n376: // StorageVersionProvider is an optional interface that a storage object can\n377: // implement if it wishes to disclose its storage version.\n378: type StorageVersionProvider interface {\n379: \t// StorageVersion returns a group versioner, which will outputs the gvk\n380: \t// an object will be converted to before persisted in etcd, given a\n381: \t// list of kinds the object might belong to.\n382: \tStorageVersion() runtime.GroupVersioner\n383: }\n384: \n385: // ResetFieldsStrategy is an optional interface that a storage object can\n386: // implement if it wishes to provide the fields reset by its strategies.\n387: type ResetFieldsStrategy interface {\n388: \tGetResetFields() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]*fieldpath.Set\n389: }\n390: \n391: // ResetFieldsFilterStrategy is an optional interface that a storage object can\n392: // implement if it wishes to provide a fields filter reset by its strategies.\n393: type ResetFieldsFilterStrategy interface {\n394: \tGetResetFieldsFilter() map[fieldpath.APIVersion]fieldpath.Filter\n395: }\n396: \n397: // CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy is a union of RESTCreateUpdateStrategy\n398: // and ResetFieldsStrategy.\n399: type CreateUpdateResetFieldsStrategy interface {\n400: \tRESTCreateUpdateStrategy\n401: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go%3FL200-400"
  }
}
97. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
