Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package storage\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n23: \\n24: \\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n25: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\\\"\\n26: \\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\\\"\\n32: )\\n33: \\n34: // Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\\n35: type Feature = string\\n36: \\n37: // RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\\n38: var RequestWatchProgress Feature = \\\"RequestWatchProgress\\\"\\n39: \\n40: // Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\\n41: // onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\\n42: // object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\\n43: // a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\\n44: // intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\\n45: // type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\\n46: type Versioner interface {\\n47: \\t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\\n48: \\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\\n49: \\t// from database.\\n50: \\tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\\n51: \\t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\\n52: \\t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\\n53: \\t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\\n54: \\t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\\n55: \\t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\\n56: \\t// serialization if it is set to nil.\\n57: \\tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\\n58: \\t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\\n59: \\t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\\n60: \\tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\\n61: \\t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\\n62: \\t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\\n63: \\tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\\n64: \\n65: \\t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\\n66: \\t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\\n67: \\t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\\n68: \\t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\\n69: \\t// \\\"1\\\", you will see updates from \\\"2\\\" onwards).\\n70: \\tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\\n71: }\\n72: \\n73: // ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\\n74: // an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\\n75: // database and to improve testability.\\n76: type ResponseMeta struct {\\n77: \\t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\\n78: \\t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\\n79: \\t// expiration time due to server lag).\\n80: \\tTTL int64\\n81: \\t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\\n82: \\tResourceVersion uint64\\n83: }\\n84: \\n85: // IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\\n86: // `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\\n87: type IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\\n88: \\n89: // IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\\n90: // for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\\n91: type IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\\n92: \\n93: // Everything accepts all objects.\\n94: var Everything = SelectionPredicate{\\n95: \\tLabel: labels.Everything(),\\n96: \\tField: fields.Everything(),\\n97: }\\n98: \\n99: // MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\\n100: type MatchValue struct {\\n101: \\tIndexName string\\n102: \\tValue     string\\n103: }\\n104: \\n105: // Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\\n106: // that is guaranteed to succeed.\\n107: // See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\\n108: type UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\\n109: \\n110: // ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\\n111: // if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\\n112: // object.\\n113: // NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\\n114: // storage, due to transformation or decode error.\\n115: type ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\\n116: \\n117: // ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \\\"admit everything\\\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\\n118: func ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n119: \\treturn nil\\n120: }\\n121: \\n122: // Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\\n123: type Preconditions struct {\\n124: \\t// Specifies the target UID.\\n125: \\t// +optional\\n126: \\tUID *types.UID `json:\\\"uid,omitempty\\\"`\\n127: \\t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\\n128: \\t// +optional\\n129: \\tResourceVersion *string `json:\\\"resourceVersion,omitempty\\\"`\\n130: }\\n131: \\n132: // NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\\n133: func NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\\n134: \\tu := types.UID(uid)\\n135: \\treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\\n136: }\\n137: \\n138: func (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\\n139: \\tif p == nil {\\n140: \\t\\treturn nil\\n141: \\t}\\n142: \\tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\\n143: \\tif err != nil {\\n144: \\t\\treturn NewInternalError(\\n145: \\t\\t\\tfmt.Errorf(\\\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\\\",\\n146: \\t\\t\\t\\t*p,\\n147: \\t\\t\\t\\tobj,\\n148: \\t\\t\\t\\terr))\\n149: \\t}\\n150: \\tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {\\n151: \\t\\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\\n152: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Precondition failed: UID in precondition: %v, UID in object meta: %v\\\",\\n153: \\t\\t\\t*p.UID,\\n154: \\t\\t\\tobjMeta.GetUID())\\n155: \\t\\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\\n156: \\t}\\n157: \\tif p.ResourceVersion != nil && *p.ResourceVersion != objMeta.GetResourceVersion() {\\n158: \\t\\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\\n159: \\t\\t\\t\\\"Precondition failed: ResourceVersion in precondition: %v, ResourceVersion in object meta: %v\\\",\\n160: \\t\\t\\t*p.ResourceVersion,\\n161: \\t\\t\\tobjMeta.GetResourceVersion())\\n162: \\t\\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\\n163: \\t}\\n164: \\treturn nil\\n165: }\\n166: \\n167: // Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and\\n168: // hides all the storage-related operations behind it.\\n169: type Interface interface {\\n170: \\t// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.\\n171: \\tVersioner() Versioner\\n172: \\n173: \\t// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live\\n174: \\t// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be\\n175: \\t// set to the read value from database.\\n176: \\tCreate(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\\n177: \\n178: \\t// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.\\n179: \\t// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.\\n180: \\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\\n181: \\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\\n182: \\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\\n183: \\tDelete(\\n184: \\t\\tctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,\\n185: \\t\\tvalidateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\\n186: \\n187: \\t// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,\\n188: \\t// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.\\n189: \\t// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,\\n190: \\t// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1\\n191: \\t// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).\\n192: \\t// If resource version is \\\"0\\\", this interface will get current object at given key\\n193: \\t// and send it in an \\\"ADDED\\\" event, before watch starts.\\n194: \\tWatch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\\n195: \\n196: \\t// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either\\n197: \\t// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.\\n198: \\t// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.\\n199: \\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\\n200: \\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go%3FL1-200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2015 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package storage\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"fmt\"\n22: \t\"strings\"\n23: \n24: \tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n25: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta\"\n26: \tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n27: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n28: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n29: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n30: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/types\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/watch\"\n32: )\n33: \n34: // Feature is the name of each feature in storage that we check in feature_support_checker.\n35: type Feature = string\n36: \n37: // RequestWatchProgress is an etcd feature that may use to check if it supported or not.\n38: var RequestWatchProgress Feature = \"RequestWatchProgress\"\n39: \n40: // Versioner abstracts setting and retrieving metadata fields from database response\n41: // onto the object ot list. It is required to maintain storage invariants - updating an\n42: // object twice with the same data except for the ResourceVersion and SelfLink must be\n43: // a no-op. A resourceVersion of type uint64 is a 'raw' resourceVersion,\n44: // intended to be sent directly to or from the backend. A resourceVersion of\n45: // type string is a 'safe' resourceVersion, intended for consumption by users.\n46: type Versioner interface {\n47: \t// UpdateObject sets storage metadata into an API object. Returns an error if the object\n48: \t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata\n49: \t// from database.\n50: \tUpdateObject(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64) error\n51: \t// UpdateList sets the resource version into an API list object. Returns an error if the object\n52: \t// cannot be updated correctly. May return nil if the requested object does not need metadata from\n53: \t// database. continueValue is optional and indicates that more results are available if the client\n54: \t// passes that value to the server in a subsequent call. remainingItemCount indicates the number\n55: \t// of remaining objects if the list is partial. The remainingItemCount field is omitted during\n56: \t// serialization if it is set to nil.\n57: \tUpdateList(obj runtime.Object, resourceVersion uint64, continueValue string, remainingItemCount *int64) error\n58: \t// PrepareObjectForStorage should set SelfLink and ResourceVersion to the empty value. Should\n59: \t// return an error if the specified object cannot be updated.\n60: \tPrepareObjectForStorage(obj runtime.Object) error\n61: \t// ObjectResourceVersion returns the resource version (for persistence) of the specified object.\n62: \t// Should return an error if the specified object does not have a persistable version.\n63: \tObjectResourceVersion(obj runtime.Object) (uint64, error)\n64: \n65: \t// ParseResourceVersion takes a resource version argument and\n66: \t// converts it to the storage backend. For watch we should pass to helper.Watch().\n67: \t// Because resourceVersion is an opaque value, the default watch\n68: \t// behavior for non-zero watch is to watch the next value (if you pass\n69: \t// \"1\", you will see updates from \"2\" onwards).\n70: \tParseResourceVersion(resourceVersion string) (uint64, error)\n71: }\n72: \n73: // ResponseMeta contains information about the database metadata that is associated with\n74: // an object. It abstracts the actual underlying objects to prevent coupling with concrete\n75: // database and to improve testability.\n76: type ResponseMeta struct {\n77: \t// TTL is the time to live of the node that contained the returned object. It may be\n78: \t// zero or negative in some cases (objects may be expired after the requested\n79: \t// expiration time due to server lag).\n80: \tTTL int64\n81: \t// The resource version of the node that contained the returned object.\n82: \tResourceVersion uint64\n83: }\n84: \n85: // IndexerFunc is a function that for a given object computes\n86: // `<value of an index>` for a particular `<index>`.\n87: type IndexerFunc func(obj runtime.Object) string\n88: \n89: // IndexerFuncs is a mapping from `<index name>` to function that\n90: // for a given object computes `<value for that index>`.\n91: type IndexerFuncs map[string]IndexerFunc\n92: \n93: // Everything accepts all objects.\n94: var Everything = SelectionPredicate{\n95: \tLabel: labels.Everything(),\n96: \tField: fields.Everything(),\n97: }\n98: \n99: // MatchValue defines a pair (`<index name>`, `<value for that index>`).\n100: type MatchValue struct {\n101: \tIndexName string\n102: \tValue     string\n103: }\n104: \n105: // Pass an UpdateFunc to Interface.GuaranteedUpdate to make an update\n106: // that is guaranteed to succeed.\n107: // See the comment for GuaranteedUpdate for more details.\n108: type UpdateFunc func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (output runtime.Object, ttl *uint64, err error)\n109: \n110: // ValidateObjectFunc is a function to act on a given object. An error may be returned\n111: // if the hook cannot be completed. The function may NOT transform the provided\n112: // object.\n113: // NOTE: the object in obj may be nil if it cannot be read from the\n114: // storage, due to transformation or decode error.\n115: type ValidateObjectFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error\n116: \n117: // ValidateAllObjectFunc is a \"admit everything\" instance of ValidateObjectFunc.\n118: func ValidateAllObjectFunc(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object) error {\n119: \treturn nil\n120: }\n121: \n122: // Preconditions must be fulfilled before an operation (update, delete, etc.) is carried out.\n123: type Preconditions struct {\n124: \t// Specifies the target UID.\n125: \t// +optional\n126: \tUID *types.UID `json:\"uid,omitempty\"`\n127: \t// Specifies the target ResourceVersion\n128: \t// +optional\n129: \tResourceVersion *string `json:\"resourceVersion,omitempty\"`\n130: }\n131: \n132: // NewUIDPreconditions returns a Preconditions with UID set.\n133: func NewUIDPreconditions(uid string) *Preconditions {\n134: \tu := types.UID(uid)\n135: \treturn &Preconditions{UID: &u}\n136: }\n137: \n138: func (p *Preconditions) Check(key string, obj runtime.Object) error {\n139: \tif p == nil {\n140: \t\treturn nil\n141: \t}\n142: \tobjMeta, err := meta.Accessor(obj)\n143: \tif err != nil {\n144: \t\treturn NewInternalError(\n145: \t\t\tfmt.Errorf(\"can't enforce preconditions %v on un-introspectable object %v, got error: %w\",\n146: \t\t\t\t*p,\n147: \t\t\t\tobj,\n148: \t\t\t\terr))\n149: \t}\n150: \tif p.UID != nil && *p.UID != objMeta.GetUID() {\n151: \t\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\n152: \t\t\t\"Precondition failed: UID in precondition: %v, UID in object meta: %v\",\n153: \t\t\t*p.UID,\n154: \t\t\tobjMeta.GetUID())\n155: \t\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\n156: \t}\n157: \tif p.ResourceVersion != nil && *p.ResourceVersion != objMeta.GetResourceVersion() {\n158: \t\terr := fmt.Sprintf(\n159: \t\t\t\"Precondition failed: ResourceVersion in precondition: %v, ResourceVersion in object meta: %v\",\n160: \t\t\t*p.ResourceVersion,\n161: \t\t\tobjMeta.GetResourceVersion())\n162: \t\treturn NewInvalidObjError(key, err)\n163: \t}\n164: \treturn nil\n165: }\n166: \n167: // Interface offers a common interface for object marshaling/unmarshaling operations and\n168: // hides all the storage-related operations behind it.\n169: type Interface interface {\n170: \t// Returns Versioner associated with this interface.\n171: \tVersioner() Versioner\n172: \n173: \t// Create adds a new object at a key unless it already exists. 'ttl' is time-to-live\n174: \t// in seconds (0 means forever). If no error is returned and out is not nil, out will be\n175: \t// set to the read value from database.\n176: \tCreate(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\n177: \n178: \t// Delete removes the specified key and returns the value that existed at that spot.\n179: \t// If key didn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error.\n180: \t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\n181: \t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\n182: \t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\n183: \tDelete(\n184: \t\tctx context.Context, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions,\n185: \t\tvalidateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\n186: \n187: \t// Watch begins watching the specified key. Events are decoded into API objects,\n188: \t// and any items selected by 'p' are sent down to returned watch.Interface.\n189: \t// resourceVersion may be used to specify what version to begin watching,\n190: \t// which should be the current resourceVersion, and no longer rv+1\n191: \t// (e.g. reconnecting without missing any updates).\n192: \t// If resource version is \"0\", this interface will get current object at given key\n193: \t// and send it in an \"ADDED\" event, before watch starts.\n194: \tWatch(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\n195: \n196: \t// Get unmarshals object found at key into objPtr. On a not found error, will either\n197: \t// return a zero object of the requested type, or an error, depending on 'opts.ignoreNotFound'.\n198: \t// Treats empty responses and nil response nodes exactly like a not found error.\n199: \t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\n200: \t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\n201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go%3FL1-200"
  }
}
98. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

{

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"200: \\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n201: \\tGet(ctx context.Context, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\\n202: \\n203: \\t// GetList unmarshalls objects found at key into a *List api object (an object\\n204: \\t// that satisfies runtime.IsList definition).\\n205: \\t// If 'opts.Recursive' is false, 'key' is used as an exact match. If `opts.Recursive'\\n206: \\t// is true, 'key' is used as a prefix.\\n207: \\t// The returned contents may be delayed, but it is guaranteed that they will\\n208: \\t// match 'opts.ResourceVersion' according 'opts.ResourceVersionMatch'.\\n209: \\tGetList(ctx context.Context, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\\n210: \\n211: \\t// GuaranteedUpdate keeps calling 'tryUpdate()' to update key 'key' (of type 'destination')\\n212: \\t// retrying the update until success if there is index conflict.\\n213: \\t// Note that object passed to tryUpdate may change across invocations of tryUpdate() if\\n214: \\t// other writers are simultaneously updating it, so tryUpdate() needs to take into account\\n215: \\t// the current contents of the object when deciding how the update object should look.\\n216: \\t// If the key doesn't exist, it will return NotFound storage error if ignoreNotFound=false\\n217: \\t// else `destination` will be set to the zero value of it's type.\\n218: \\t// If the eventual successful invocation of `tryUpdate` returns an output with the same serialized\\n219: \\t// contents as the input, it won't perform any update, but instead set `destination` to an object with those\\n220: \\t// contents.\\n221: \\t// If 'cachedExistingObject' is non-nil, it can be used as a suggestion about the\\n222: \\t// current version of the object to avoid read operation from storage to get it.\\n223: \\t// However, the implementations have to retry in case suggestion is stale.\\n224: \\t//\\n225: \\t// Example:\\n226: \\t//\\n227: \\t// s := /* implementation of Interface */\\n228: \\t// err := s.GuaranteedUpdate(\\n229: \\t//     \\\"myKey\\\", &MyType{}, true, preconditions,\\n230: \\t//     func(input runtime.Object, res ResponseMeta) (runtime.Object, *uint64, error) {\\n231: \\t//       // Before each invocation of the user defined function, \\\"input\\\" is reset to\\n232: \\t//       // current contents for \\\"myKey\\\" in database.\\n233: \\t//       curr := input.(*MyType)  // Guaranteed to succeed.\\n234: \\t//\\n235: \\t//       // Make the modification\\n236: \\t//       curr.Counter++\\n237: \\t//\\n238: \\t//       // Return the modified object - return an error to stop iterating. Return\\n239: \\t//       // a uint64 to alter the TTL on the object, or nil to keep it the same value.\\n240: \\t//       return cur, nil, nil\\n241: \\t//    }, cachedExistingObject\\n242: \\t// )\\n243: \\tGuaranteedUpdate(\\n244: \\t\\tctx context.Context, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\\n245: \\t\\tpreconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\\n246: \\n247: \\t// Stats returns storage stats.\\n248: \\tStats(ctx context.Context) (Stats, error)\\n249: \\n250: \\t// ReadinessCheck checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\\n251: \\tReadinessCheck() error\\n252: \\n253: \\t// RequestWatchProgress requests the a watch stream progress status be sent in the\\n254: \\t// watch response stream as soon as possible.\\n255: \\t// Used for monitor watch progress even if watching resources with no changes.\\n256: \\t//\\n257: \\t// If watch is lagging, progress status might:\\n258: \\t// * be pointing to stale resource version. Use etcd KV request to get linearizable resource version.\\n259: \\t// * not be delivered at all. It's recommended to poll request progress periodically.\\n260: \\t//\\n261: \\t// Note: Only watches with matching context grpc metadata will be notified.\\n262: \\t// https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/9325a57125e8502941d1b0c7379c4bb80a678d5c/vendor/go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3/watch.go#L1037-L1042\\n263: \\t//\\n264: \\t// TODO: Remove when storage.Interface will be separate from etc3.store.\\n265: \\t// Deprecated: Added temporarily to simplify exposing RequestProgress for watch cache.\\n266: \\tRequestWatchProgress(ctx context.Context) error\\n267: \\n268: \\t// GetCurrentResourceVersion gets the current resource version from etcd.\\n269: \\t// This method issues an empty list request and reads only the ResourceVersion from the object metadata\\n270: \\tGetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx context.Context) (uint64, error)\\n271: \\n272: \\t// EnableResourceSizeEstimation enables estimating resource size by providing function get keys from storage.\\n273: \\tEnableResourceSizeEstimation(KeysFunc) error\\n274: \\n275: \\t// CompactRevision returns latest observed revision that was compacted.\\n276: \\t// Without ListFromCacheSnapshot enabled only locally executed compaction will be observed.\\n277: \\t// Returns 0 if no compaction was yet observed.\\n278: \\tCompactRevision() int64\\n279: }\\n280: \\n281: // KeysFunc is a function prototype to fetch keys from storage.\\n282: type KeysFunc func(context.Context) ([]string, error)\\n283: \\n284: // GetOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage get operations.\\n285: type GetOptions struct {\\n286: \\t// IgnoreNotFound determines what is returned if the requested object is not found. If\\n287: \\t// true, a zero object is returned. If false, an error is returned.\\n288: \\tIgnoreNotFound bool\\n289: \\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the get operation\\n290: \\t// as a \\\"not older than\\\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\\n291: \\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\\n292: \\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\\n293: \\tResourceVersion string\\n294: }\\n295: \\n296: // ListOptions provides the options that may be provided for storage list operations.\\n297: type ListOptions struct {\\n298: \\t// ResourceVersion provides a resource version constraint to apply to the list operation\\n299: \\t// as a \\\"not older than\\\" constraint: the result contains data at least as new as the provided\\n300: \\t// ResourceVersion. The newest available data is preferred, but any data not older than this\\n301: \\t// ResourceVersion may be served.\\n302: \\tResourceVersion string\\n303: \\t// ResourceVersionMatch provides the rule for how the resource version constraint applies. If set\\n304: \\t// to the default value \\\"\\\" the legacy resource version semantic apply.\\n305: \\tResourceVersionMatch metav1.ResourceVersionMatch\\n306: \\t// Predicate provides the selection rules for the list operation.\\n307: \\tPredicate SelectionPredicate\\n308: \\t// Recursive determines whether the list or watch is defined for a single object located at the\\n309: \\t// given key, or for the whole set of objects with the given key as a prefix.\\n310: \\tRecursive bool\\n311: \\t// ProgressNotify determines whether storage-originated bookmark (progress notify) events should\\n312: \\t// be delivered to the users. The option is ignored for non-watch requests.\\n313: \\t//\\n314: \\t// Firstly, note that this field is different from the Predicate.AllowWatchBookmarks field.\\n315: \\t// Secondly, this field is intended for internal clients only such as the watch cache.\\n316: \\t//\\n317: \\t// This means that external clients do not have the ability to set this field directly.\\n318: \\t// For example by setting the allowWatchBookmarks query parameter.\\n319: \\t//\\n320: \\t// The motivation for this approach is the fact that the frequency\\n321: \\t// of bookmark events from a storage like etcd might be very high.\\n322: \\t// As the number of watch requests increases, the server load would also increase.\\n323: \\t//\\n324: \\t// Furthermore, the server is not obligated to provide bookmark events at all,\\n325: \\t// as described in https://github.com/kubernetes/enhancements/tree/master/keps/sig-api-machinery/956-watch-bookmark#proposal\\n326: \\tProgressNotify bool\\n327: \\t// SendInitialEvents, when set together with Watch option,\\n328: \\t// begin the watch stream with synthetic init events to build the\\n329: \\t// whole state of all resources followed by a synthetic \\\"Bookmark\\\"\\n330: \\t// event containing a ResourceVersion after which the server\\n331: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go%3FL200-330\"}",

{
  "endLine": 120,

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package endpoints\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"path\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n22: \\n23: \\trestful \\\"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\\\"\\n24: \\n25: \\tapidiscoveryv2 \\\"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\\\"\\n26: \\tmetav1 \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n29: \\tutilerrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\\\"\\n34: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\\\"\\n36: )\\n37: \\n38: // ConvertabilityChecker indicates what versions a GroupKind is available in.\\n39: type ConvertabilityChecker interface {\\n40: \\t// VersionsForGroupKind indicates what versions are available to convert a group kind. This determines\\n41: \\t// what our decoding abilities are.\\n42: \\tVersionsForGroupKind(gk schema.GroupKind) []schema.GroupVersion\\n43: }\\n44: \\n45: // APIGroupVersion is a helper for exposing rest.Storage objects as http.Handlers via go-restful\\n46: // It handles URLs of the form:\\n47: // /${storage_key}[/${object_name}]\\n48: // Where 'storage_key' points to a rest.Storage object stored in storage.\\n49: // This object should contain all parameterization necessary for running a particular API version\\n50: type APIGroupVersion struct {\\n51: \\tStorage map[string]rest.Storage\\n52: \\n53: \\tRoot string\\n54: \\n55: \\t// GroupVersion is the external group version\\n56: \\tGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\\n57: \\n58: \\t// AllServedVersionsByResource is indexed by resource and maps to a list of versions that resource exists in.\\n59: \\t// This was created so that StorageVersion for APIs can include a list of all version that are served for each\\n60: \\t// GroupResource tuple.\\n61: \\tAllServedVersionsByResource map[string][]string\\n62: \\n63: \\t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the Kubernetes APIVersion used for common objects in the apiserver\\n64: \\t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\\n65: \\t// define a version \\\"v1beta1\\\" but want to use the Kubernetes \\\"v1\\\" internal objects. If\\n66: \\t// empty, defaults to GroupVersion.\\n67: \\tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n68: \\t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \\\"meta.k8s.io/v1\\\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\\n69: \\t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\\n70: \\t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\\n71: \\tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\\n72: \\n73: \\t// Serializer is used to determine how to convert responses from API methods into bytes to send over\\n74: \\t// the wire.\\n75: \\tSerializer     runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\\n76: \\tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\\n77: \\n78: \\tTyper                 runtime.ObjectTyper\\n79: \\tCreater               runtime.ObjectCreater\\n80: \\tConvertor             runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n81: \\tConvertabilityChecker ConvertabilityChecker\\n82: \\tDefaulter             runtime.ObjectDefaulter\\n83: \\tNamer                 runtime.Namer\\n84: \\tUnsafeConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor\\n85: \\tTypeConverter         managedfields.TypeConverter\\n86: \\n87: \\tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\\n88: \\n89: \\t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\\n90: \\t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\\n91: \\t// the create-on-update case\\n92: \\tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\\n93: \\n94: \\tAdmit admission.Interface\\n95: \\n96: \\tMinRequestTimeout time.Duration\\n97: \\n98: \\t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\\n99: \\t// 0 means no limit.\\n100: \\tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\\n101: }\\n102: \\n103: // InstallREST registers the REST handlers (storage, watch, proxy and redirect) into a restful Container.\\n104: // It is expected that the provided path root prefix will serve all operations. Root MUST NOT end\\n105: // in a slash.\\n106: func (g *APIGroupVersion) InstallREST(container *restful.Container) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, []*storageversion.ResourceInfo, error) {\\n107: \\tprefix := path.Join(g.Root, g.GroupVersion.Group, g.GroupVersion.Version)\\n108: \\tinstaller := &APIInstaller{\\n109: \\t\\tgroup:             g,\\n110: \\t\\tprefix:            prefix,\\n111: \\t\\tminRequestTimeout: g.MinRequestTimeout,\\n112: \\t}\\n113: \\n114: \\tapiResources, resourceInfos, ws, registrationErrors := installer.Install()\\n115: \\tversionDiscoveryHandler := discovery.NewAPIVersionHandler(g.Serializer, g.GroupVersion, staticLister{apiResources})\\n116: \\tversionDiscoveryHandler.AddToWebService(ws)\\n117: \\tcontainer.Add(ws)\\n118: \\taggregatedDiscoveryResources, err := ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(apiResources)\\n119: \\tif err != nil {\\n120: \\t\\tregistrationErrors = append(registrationErrors, err)\\n121: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/groupversion.go%3FL1-120\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package endpoints\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"path\"\n21: \t\"time\"\n22: \n23: \trestful \"github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3\"\n24: \n25: \tapidiscoveryv2 \"k8s.io/api/apidiscovery/v2\"\n26: \tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n27: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n28: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n29: \tutilerrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors\"\n30: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission\"\n32: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n33: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n34: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest\"\n35: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storageversion\"\n36: )\n37: \n38: // ConvertabilityChecker indicates what versions a GroupKind is available in.\n39: type ConvertabilityChecker interface {\n40: \t// VersionsForGroupKind indicates what versions are available to convert a group kind. This determines\n41: \t// what our decoding abilities are.\n42: \tVersionsForGroupKind(gk schema.GroupKind) []schema.GroupVersion\n43: }\n44: \n45: // APIGroupVersion is a helper for exposing rest.Storage objects as http.Handlers via go-restful\n46: // It handles URLs of the form:\n47: // /${storage_key}[/${object_name}]\n48: // Where 'storage_key' points to a rest.Storage object stored in storage.\n49: // This object should contain all parameterization necessary for running a particular API version\n50: type APIGroupVersion struct {\n51: \tStorage map[string]rest.Storage\n52: \n53: \tRoot string\n54: \n55: \t// GroupVersion is the external group version\n56: \tGroupVersion schema.GroupVersion\n57: \n58: \t// AllServedVersionsByResource is indexed by resource and maps to a list of versions that resource exists in.\n59: \t// This was created so that StorageVersion for APIs can include a list of all version that are served for each\n60: \t// GroupResource tuple.\n61: \tAllServedVersionsByResource map[string][]string\n62: \n63: \t// OptionsExternalVersion controls the Kubernetes APIVersion used for common objects in the apiserver\n64: \t// schema like api.Status, api.DeleteOptions, and metav1.ListOptions. Other implementors may\n65: \t// define a version \"v1beta1\" but want to use the Kubernetes \"v1\" internal objects. If\n66: \t// empty, defaults to GroupVersion.\n67: \tOptionsExternalVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n68: \t// MetaGroupVersion defaults to \"meta.k8s.io/v1\" and is the scheme group version used to decode\n69: \t// common API implementations like ListOptions. Future changes will allow this to vary by group\n70: \t// version (for when the inevitable meta/v2 group emerges).\n71: \tMetaGroupVersion *schema.GroupVersion\n72: \n73: \t// Serializer is used to determine how to convert responses from API methods into bytes to send over\n74: \t// the wire.\n75: \tSerializer     runtime.NegotiatedSerializer\n76: \tParameterCodec runtime.ParameterCodec\n77: \n78: \tTyper                 runtime.ObjectTyper\n79: \tCreater               runtime.ObjectCreater\n80: \tConvertor             runtime.ObjectConvertor\n81: \tConvertabilityChecker ConvertabilityChecker\n82: \tDefaulter             runtime.ObjectDefaulter\n83: \tNamer                 runtime.Namer\n84: \tUnsafeConvertor       runtime.ObjectConvertor\n85: \tTypeConverter         managedfields.TypeConverter\n86: \n87: \tEquivalentResourceRegistry runtime.EquivalentResourceRegistry\n88: \n89: \t// Authorizer determines whether a user is allowed to make a certain request. The Handler does a preliminary\n90: \t// authorization check using the request URI but it may be necessary to make additional checks, such as in\n91: \t// the create-on-update case\n92: \tAuthorizer authorizer.Authorizer\n93: \n94: \tAdmit admission.Interface\n95: \n96: \tMinRequestTimeout time.Duration\n97: \n98: \t// The limit on the request body size that would be accepted and decoded in a write request.\n99: \t// 0 means no limit.\n100: \tMaxRequestBodyBytes int64\n101: }\n102: \n103: // InstallREST registers the REST handlers (storage, watch, proxy and redirect) into a restful Container.\n104: // It is expected that the provided path root prefix will serve all operations. Root MUST NOT end\n105: // in a slash.\n106: func (g *APIGroupVersion) InstallREST(container *restful.Container) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, []*storageversion.ResourceInfo, error) {\n107: \tprefix := path.Join(g.Root, g.GroupVersion.Group, g.GroupVersion.Version)\n108: \tinstaller := &APIInstaller{\n109: \t\tgroup:             g,\n110: \t\tprefix:            prefix,\n111: \t\tminRequestTimeout: g.MinRequestTimeout,\n112: \t}\n113: \n114: \tapiResources, resourceInfos, ws, registrationErrors := installer.Install()\n115: \tversionDiscoveryHandler := discovery.NewAPIVersionHandler(g.Serializer, g.GroupVersion, staticLister{apiResources})\n116: \tversionDiscoveryHandler.AddToWebService(ws)\n117: \tcontainer.Add(ws)\n118: \taggregatedDiscoveryResources, err := ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(apiResources)\n119: \tif err != nil {\n120: \t\tregistrationErrors = append(registrationErrors, err)\n121: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/groupversion.go%3FL1-120"
  }
}

\\\"x-kubernetes-action\\\"\\n60: )\\n61: \\n62: type APIInstaller struct {\\n63: \\tgroup             *APIGroupVersion\\n64: \\tprefix            string // Path prefix where API resources are to be registered.\\n65: \\tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\\n66: }\\n67: \\n68: // Struct capturing information about an action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\\n69: type action struct {\\n70: \\tVerb          string               // Verb identifying the action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\\n71: \\tPath          string               // The path of the action\\n72: \\tParams        []*restful.Parameter // List of parameters associated with the action.\\n73: \\tNamer         handlers.ScopeNamer\\n74: \\tAllNamespaces bool // true iff the action is namespaced but works on aggregate result for all namespaces\\n75: }\\n76: \\n77: func ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(list []metav1.APIResource) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, error) {\\n78: \\tvar apiResourceList []apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery\\n79: \\tparentResources := make(map[string]int)\\n80: \\n81: \\t// Loop through all top-level resources\\n82: \\tfor _, r := range list {\\n83: \\t\\tif strings.Contains(r.Name, \\\"/\\\") {\\n84: \\t\\t\\t// Skip subresources for now so we can get the list of resources\\n85: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n86: \\t\\t}\\n87: \\n88: \\t\\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\\n89: \\t\\tif r.Namespaced {\\n90: \\t\\t\\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\\n91: \\t\\t} else {\\n92: \\t\\t\\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\\n93: \\t\\t}\\n94: \\n95: \\t\\tresource := apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\\n96: \\t\\t\\tResource: r.Name,\\n97: \\t\\t\\tScope:    scope,\\n98: \\t\\t\\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{\\n99: \\t\\t\\t\\tGroup:   r.Group,\\n100: \\t\\t\\t\\tVersion: r.Version,\\n101: \\t\\t\\t\\tKind:    r.Kind,\\n102: \\t\\t\\t},\\n103: \\t\\t\\tVerbs:            r.Verbs,\\n104: \\t\\t\\tShortNames:       r.ShortNames,\\n105: \\t\\t\\tCategories:       r.Categories,\\n106: \\t\\t\\tSingularResource: r.SingularName,\\n107: \\t\\t}\\n108: \\t\\tapiResourceList = \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/discovery\"\n44: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers\"\n45: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/negotiation\"\n46: \tversioninfo \"k8s.io/component-base/version\"\n54: )\n55: \n56: const (\n57: \tRouteMetaGVK              = \"x-kubernetes-group-version-kind\"\n58: \tRouteMetaSelectableFields = \"x-kubernetes-selectable-fields\"\n59: \tRouteMetaAction           = \"x-kubernetes-action\"\n60: )\n61: \n62: type APIInstaller struct {\n63: \tgroup             *APIGroupVersion\n64: \tprefix            string // Path prefix where API resources are to be registered.\n65: \tminRequestTimeout time.Duration\n66: }\n67: \n68: // Struct capturing information about an action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n69: type action struct {\n70: \tVerb          string               // Verb identifying the action (MethodGet, MethodPost, MethodWatch, MethodProxy, etc).\n71: \tPath          string               // The path of the action\n72: \tParams        []*restful.Parameter // List of parameters associated with the action.\n73: \tNamer         handlers.ScopeNamer\n74: \tAllNamespaces bool // true iff the action is namespaced but works on aggregate result for all namespaces\n75: }\n76: \n77: func ConvertGroupVersionIntoToDiscovery(list []metav1.APIResource) ([]apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery, error) {\n78: \tvar apiResourceList []apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery\n79: \tparentResources := make(map[string]int)\n80: \n81: \t// Loop through all top-level resources\n82: \tfor _, r := range list {\n83: \t\tif strings.Contains(r.Name, \"/\") {\n84: \t\t\t// Skip subresources for now so we can get the list of resources\n85: \t\t\tcontinue\n86: \t\t}\n87: \n88: \t\tvar scope apidiscoveryv2.ResourceScope\n89: \t\tif r.Namespaced {\n90: \t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeNamespace\n91: \t\t} else {\n92: \t\t\tscope = apidiscoveryv2.ScopeCluster\n93: \t\t}\n94: \n95: \t\tresource := apidiscoveryv2.APIResourceDiscovery{\n96: \t\t\tResource: r.Name,\n97: \t\t\tScope:    scope,\n98: \t\t\tResponseKind: &metav1.GroupVersionKind{\n99: \t\t\t\tGroup:   r.Group,\n100: \t\t\t\tVersion: r.Version,\n101: \t\t\t\tKind:    r.Kind,\n102: \t\t\t},\n103: \t\t\tVerbs:            r.Verbs,\n104: \t\t\tShortNames:       r.ShortNames,\n105: \t\t\tCategories:       r.Categories,\n106: \t\t\tSingularResource: r.SingularName,\n107: \t\t}\n108: \t\tapiResourceList = append(apiResourceList, resource)\n109: \t\tparentResources[r.Name] = len(apiResourceList) - 1\n110: \t}\n111: \n112: \t// Loop through all subresources\n113: \tfor _, r := range list {\n114: \t\t// Split resource name and subresource name\n115: \t\tsplit := strings.SplitN(r.Name, \"/\", 2)\n116: \n117: \t\tif len(split) != 2 {\n118: \t\t\t// Skip parent resources\n119: \t\t\tcontinue\n120: \t\t}\n121: ",

104. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go",
  "repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package admission\\n18: \\n19: import \\\"context\\\"\\n20: \\n21: // chainAdmissionHandler is an instance of admission.NamedHandler that performs admission control using\\n22: // a chain of admission handlers\\n23: type chainAdmissionHandler []Interface\\n24: \\n25: // NewChainHandler creates a new chain handler from an array of handlers. Used for testing.\\n26: func NewChainHandler(handlers ...Interface) chainAdmissionHandler {\\n27: \\treturn chainAdmissionHandler(handlers)\\n28: }\\n29: \\n30: // Admit performs an admission control check using a chain of handlers, and returns immediately on first error\\n31: func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {\\n32: \\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\\n33: \\t\\tif !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) {\\n34: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n35: \\t\\t}\\n36: \\t\\tif mutator, ok := handler.(MutationInterface); ok {\\n37: \\t\\t\\terr := mutator.Admit(ctx, a, o)\\n38: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n39: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n40: \\t\\t\\t}\\n41: \\t\\t}\\n42: \\t}\\n43: \\treturn nil\\n44: }\\n45: \\n46: // Validate performs an admission control check using a chain of handlers, and returns immediately on first error\\n47: func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {\\n48: \\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\\n49: \\t\\tif !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) {\\n50: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n51: \\t\\t}\\n52: \\t\\tif validator, ok := handler.(ValidationInterface); ok {\\n53: \\t\\t\\terr := validator.Validate(ctx, a, o)\\n54: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n55: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n56: \\t\\t\\t}\\n57: \\t\\t}\\n58: \\t}\\n59: \\treturn nil\\n60: }\\n61: \\n62: // Handles will return true if any of the handlers handles the given operation\\n63: func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Handles(operation Operation) bool {\\n64: \\tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\\n65: \\t\\tif handler.Handles(operation) {\\n66: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n67: \\t\\t}\\n68: \\t}\\n69: \\treturn false\\n70: }\\n71: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package admission\n18: \n19: import \"context\"\n20: \n21: // chainAdmissionHandler is an instance of admission.NamedHandler that performs admission control using\n22: // a chain of admission handlers\n23: type chainAdmissionHandler []Interface\n24: \n25: // NewChainHandler creates a new chain handler from an array of handlers. Used for testing.\n26: func NewChainHandler(handlers ...Interface) chainAdmissionHandler {\n27: \treturn chainAdmissionHandler(handlers)\n28: }\n29: \n30: // Admit performs an admission control check using a chain of handlers, and returns immediately on first error\n31: func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {\n32: \tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n33: \t\tif !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) {\n34: \t\t\tcontinue\n35: \t\t}\n36: \t\tif mutator, ok := handler.(MutationInterface); ok {\n37: \t\t\terr := mutator.Admit(ctx, a, o)\n38: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n39: \t\t\t\treturn err\n40: \t\t\t}\n41: \t\t}\n42: \t}\n43: \treturn nil\n44: }\n45: \n46: // Validate performs an admission control check using a chain of handlers, and returns immediately on first error\n47: func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error {\n48: \tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n49: \t\tif !handler.Handles(a.GetOperation()) {\n50: \t\t\tcontinue\n51: \t\t}\n52: \t\tif validator, ok := handler.(ValidationInterface); ok {\n53: \t\t\terr := validator.Validate(ctx, a, o)\n54: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n55: \t\t\t\treturn err\n56: \t\t\t}\n57: \t\t}\n58: \t}\n59: \treturn nil\n60: }\n61: \n62: // Handles will return true if any of the handlers handles the given operation\n63: func (admissionHandler chainAdmissionHandler) Handles(operation Operation) bool {\n64: \tfor _, handler := range admissionHandler {\n65: \t\tif handler.Handles(operation) {\n66: \t\t\treturn true\n67: \t\t}\n68: \t}\n69: \treturn false\n70: }\n71: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/chain.go"
  }
}
105. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 800,
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go",
  "repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes",
  "startLine": 600
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"600: \\tif hook == nil {\\n601: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"hook func may not be nil: %q\\\", name)\\n602: \\t}\\n603: \\tif c.DisabledPostStartHooks.Has(name) {\\n604: \\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"skipping %q because it was explicitly disabled\\\", name)\\n605: \\t\\treturn nil\\n606: \\t}\\n607: \\n608: \\tif postStartHook, exists := c.PostStartHooks[name]; exists {\\n609: \\t\\t// this is programmer error, but it can be hard to debug\\n610: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to add %q because it was already registered by: %s\\\", name, postStartHook.originatingStack)\\n611: \\t}\\n612: \\tc.PostStartHooks[name] = PostStartHookConfigEntry{hook: hook, originatingStack: string(debug.Stack())}\\n613: \\n614: \\treturn nil\\n615: }\\n616: \\n617: // AddPostStartHookOrDie allows you to add a PostStartHook, but dies on failure.\\n618: func (c *Config) AddPostStartHookOrDie(name string, hook PostStartHookFunc) {\\n619: \\tif err := c.AddPostStartHook(name, hook); err != nil {\\n620: \\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"Error registering PostStartHook %q: %v\\\", name, err)\\n621: \\t}\\n622: }\\n623: \\n624: func completeOpenAPI(config *openapicommon.Config, version *version.Version) {\\n625: \\tif config == nil {\\n626: \\t\\treturn\\n627: \\t}\\n628: \\tif config.SecurityDefinitions != nil {\\n629: \\t\\t// Setup OpenAPI security: all APIs will have the same authentication for now.\\n630: \\t\\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = []map[string][]string{}\\n631: \\t\\tkeys := []string{}\\n632: \\t\\tfor k := range *config.SecurityDefinitions {\\n633: \\t\\t\\tkeys = append(keys, k)\\n634: \\t\\t}\\n635: \\t\\tsort.Strings(keys)\\n636: \\t\\tfor _, k := range keys {\\n637: \\t\\t\\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = append(config.DefaultSecurity, map[string][]string{k: {}})\\n638: \\t\\t}\\n639: \\t\\tif config.CommonResponses == nil {\\n640: \\t\\t\\tconfig.CommonResponses = map[int]spec.Response{}\\n641: \\t\\t}\\n642: \\t\\tif _, exists := config.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized]; !exists {\\n643: \\t\\t\\tconfig.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized] = spec.Response{\\n644: \\t\\t\\t\\tResponseProps: spec.ResponseProps{\\n645: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDescription: \\\"Unauthorized\\\",\\n646: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n647: \\t\\t\\t}\\n648: \\t\\t}\\n649: \\t}\\n650: \\t// make sure we populate info, and info.version, if not manually set\\n651: \\tif config.Info == nil {\\n652: \\t\\tconfig.Info = &spec.Info{}\\n653: \\t}\\n654: \\tif config.Info.Version == \\\"\\\" {\\n655: \\t\\tif version != nil {\\n656: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Info.Version = strings.Split(version.String(), \\\"-\\\")[0]\\n657: \\t\\t} else {\\n658: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Info.Version = \\\"unversioned\\\"\\n659: \\t\\t}\\n660: \\t}\\n661: }\\n662: \\n663: func completeOpenAPIV3(config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config, version *version.Version) {\\n664: \\tif config == nil {\\n665: \\t\\treturn\\n666: \\t}\\n667: \\tif config.SecuritySchemes != nil {\\n668: \\t\\t// Setup OpenAPI security: all APIs will have the same authentication for now.\\n669: \\t\\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = []map[string][]string{}\\n670: \\t\\tkeys := []string{}\\n671: \\t\\tfor k := range config.SecuritySchemes {\\n672: \\t\\t\\tkeys = append(keys, k)\\n673: \\t\\t}\\n674: \\t\\tsort.Strings(keys)\\n675: \\t\\tfor _, k := range keys {\\n676: \\t\\t\\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = append(config.DefaultSecurity, map[string][]string{k: {}})\\n677: \\t\\t}\\n678: \\t\\tif config.CommonResponses == nil {\\n679: \\t\\t\\tconfig.CommonResponses = map[int]*spec3.Response{}\\n680: \\t\\t}\\n681: \\t\\tif _, exists := config.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized]; !exists {\\n682: \\t\\t\\tconfig.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized] = &spec3.Response{\\n683: \\t\\t\\t\\tResponseProps: spec3.ResponseProps{\\n684: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDescription: \\\"Unauthorized\\\",\\n685: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n686: \\t\\t\\t}\\n687: \\t\\t}\\n688: \\t}\\n689: \\t// make sure we populate info, and info.version, if not manually set\\n690: \\tif config.Info == nil {\\n691: \\t\\tconfig.Info = &spec.Info{}\\n692: \\t}\\n693: \\tif config.Info.Version == \\\"\\\" {\\n694: \\t\\tif version != nil {\\n695: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Info.Version = strings.Split(version.String(), \\\"-\\\")[0]\\n696: \\t\\t} else {\\n697: \\t\\t\\tconfig.Info.Version = \\\"unversioned\\\"\\n698: \\t\\t}\\n699: \\t}\\n700: }\\n701: \\n702: // DrainedNotify returns a lifecycle signal of genericapiserver already drained while shutting down.\\n703: func (c *Config) DrainedNotify() <-chan struct{} {\\n704: \\treturn c.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained.Signaled()\\n705: }\\n706: \\n707: // ShutdownInitiated returns a lifecycle signal of apiserver shutdown having been initiated.\\n708: func (c *Config) ShutdownInitiatedNotify() <-chan struct{} {\\n709: \\treturn c.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\\n710: }\\n711: \\n712: // Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\\n713: // from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\\n714: func (c *Config) Complete(informers informers.SharedInformerFactory) CompletedConfig {\\n715: \\tif c.FeatureGate == nil {\\n716: \\t\\tc.FeatureGate = utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate\\n717: \\t}\\n718: \\tif len(c.ExternalAddress) == 0 && c.PublicAddress != nil {\\n719: \\t\\tc.ExternalAddress = c.PublicAddress.String()\\n720: \\t}\\n721: \\n722: \\t// if there is no port, and we listen on one securely, use that one\\n723: \\tif _, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.ExternalAddress); err != nil {\\n724: \\t\\tif c.SecureServing == nil {\\n725: \\t\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"cannot derive external address port without listening on a secure port.\\\")\\n726: \\t\\t}\\n727: \\t\\t_, port, err := c.SecureServing.HostPort()\\n728: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n729: \\t\\t\\tklog.Fatalf(\\\"cannot derive external address from the secure port: %v\\\", err)\\n730: \\t\\t}\\n731: \\t\\tc.ExternalAddress = net.JoinHostPort(c.ExternalAddress, strconv.Itoa(port))\\n732: \\t}\\n733: \\tcompleteOpenAPI(c.OpenAPIConfig, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\\n734: \\tcompleteOpenAPIV3(c.OpenAPIV3Config, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\\n735: \\n736: \\tif c.DiscoveryAddresses == nil {\\n737: \\t\\tc.DiscoveryAddresses = discovery.DefaultAddresses{DefaultAddress: c.ExternalAddress}\\n738: \\t}\\n739: \\n740: \\tAuthorizeClientBearerToken(c.LoopbackClientConfig, &c.Authentication, &c.Authorization)\\n741: \\n742: \\tif c.RequestInfoResolver == nil {\\n743: \\t\\tc.RequestInfoResolver = NewRequestInfoResolver(c)\\n744: \\t}\\n745: \\n746: \\tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\\n747: \\t\\tif c.RESTOptionsGetter == nil {\\n748: \\t\\t\\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistry()\\n749: \\t\\t} else {\\n750: \\t\\t\\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistryWithIdentity(func(groupResource schema.GroupResource) string {\\n751: \\t\\t\\t\\t// use the storage prefix as the key if possible\\n752: \\t\\t\\t\\tif opts, err := c.RESTOptionsGetter.GetRESTOptions(groupResource, nil); err == nil {\\n753: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn opts.ResourcePrefix\\n754: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n755: \\t\\t\\t\\t// otherwise return \\\"\\\" to use the default key (parent GV name)\\n756: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n757: \\t\\t\\t})\\n758: \\t\\t}\\n759: \\t}\\n760: \\n761: \\treturn CompletedConfig{&completedConfig{c, informers}}\\n762: }\\n763: \\n764: // Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\\n765: // from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\\n766: func (c *RecommendedConfig) Complete() CompletedConfig {\\n767: \\treturn c.Config.Complete(c.SharedInformerFactory)\\n768: }\\n769: \\n770: var defaultAllowedMediaTypes = []string{\\n771: \\truntime.ContentTypeJSON,\\n772: \\truntime.ContentTypeYAML,\\n773: \\truntime.ContentTypeProtobuf,\\n774: }\\n775: \\n776: // New creates a new server which logically combines the handling chain with the passed server.\\n777: // name is used to differentiate for logging. The handler chain in particular can be difficult as it starts delegating.\\n778: // delegationTarget may not be nil.\\n779: func (c completedConfig) New(name string, delegationTarget DelegationTarget) (*GenericAPIServer, error) {\\n780: \\tif c.Serializer == nil {\\n781: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.Serializer == nil\\\")\\n782: \\t}\\n783: \\tallowedMediaTypes := defaultAllowedMediaTypes\\n784: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.CBORServingAndStorage) {\\n785: \\t\\tallowedMediaTypes = append(allowedMediaTypes, runtime.ContentTypeCBOR)\\n786: \\t}\\n787: \\tfor _, info := range c.Serializer.SupportedMediaTypes() {\\n788: \\t\\tvar ok bool\\n789: \\t\\tfor _, mt := range allowedMediaTypes {\\n790: \\t\\t\\tif info.MediaType == mt {\\n791: \\t\\t\\t\\tok = true\\n792: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n793: \\t\\t\\t}\\n794: \\t\\t}\\n795: \\t\\tif !ok {\\n796: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"refusing to create new apiserver %q with support for media type %q (allowed media types are: %s)\\\", name, info.MediaType, strings.Join(allowedMediaTypes, \\\", \\\"))\\n797: \\t\\t}\\n798: \\t}\\n799: \\tif c.LoopbackClientConfig == nil {\\n800: \\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.LoopbackClientConfig == nil\\\")\\n801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL600-800\"}",
