    "content": "600: \tif hook == nil {\n601: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"hook func may not be nil: %q\", name)\n602: \t}\n603: \tif c.DisabledPostStartHooks.Has(name) {\n604: \t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"skipping %q because it was explicitly disabled\", name)\n605: \t\treturn nil\n606: \t}\n607: \n608: \tif postStartHook, exists := c.PostStartHooks[name]; exists {\n609: \t\t// this is programmer error, but it can be hard to debug\n610: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to add %q because it was already registered by: %s\", name, postStartHook.originatingStack)\n611: \t}\n612: \tc.PostStartHooks[name] = PostStartHookConfigEntry{hook: hook, originatingStack: string(debug.Stack())}\n613: \n614: \treturn nil\n615: }\n616: \n617: // AddPostStartHookOrDie allows you to add a PostStartHook, but dies on failure.\n618: func (c *Config) AddPostStartHookOrDie(name string, hook PostStartHookFunc) {\n619: \tif err := c.AddPostStartHook(name, hook); err != nil {\n620: \t\tklog.Fatalf(\"Error registering PostStartHook %q: %v\", name, err)\n621: \t}\n622: }\n623: \n624: func completeOpenAPI(config *openapicommon.Config, version *version.Version) {\n625: \tif config == nil {\n626: \t\treturn\n627: \t}\n628: \tif config.SecurityDefinitions != nil {\n629: \t\t// Setup OpenAPI security: all APIs will have the same authentication for now.\n630: \t\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = []map[string][]string{}\n631: \t\tkeys := []string{}\n632: \t\tfor k := range *config.SecurityDefinitions {\n633: \t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n634: \t\t}\n635: \t\tsort.Strings(keys)\n636: \t\tfor _, k := range keys {\n637: \t\t\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = append(config.DefaultSecurity, map[string][]string{k: {}})\n638: \t\t}\n639: \t\tif config.CommonResponses == nil {\n640: \t\t\tconfig.CommonResponses = map[int]spec.Response{}\n641: \t\t}\n642: \t\tif _, exists := config.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized]; !exists {\n643: \t\t\tconfig.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized] = spec.Response{\n644: \t\t\t\tResponseProps: spec.ResponseProps{\n645: \t\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Unauthorized\",\n646: \t\t\t\t},\n647: \t\t\t}\n648: \t\t}\n649: \t}\n650: \t// make sure we populate info, and info.version, if not manually set\n651: \tif config.Info == nil {\n652: \t\tconfig.Info = &spec.Info{}\n653: \t}\n654: \tif config.Info.Version == \"\" {\n655: \t\tif version != nil {\n656: \t\t\tconfig.Info.Version = strings.Split(version.String(), \"-\")[0]\n657: \t\t} else {\n658: \t\t\tconfig.Info.Version = \"unversioned\"\n659: \t\t}\n660: \t}\n661: }\n662: \n663: func completeOpenAPIV3(config *openapicommon.OpenAPIV3Config, version *version.Version) {\n664: \tif config == nil {\n665: \t\treturn\n666: \t}\n667: \tif config.SecuritySchemes != nil {\n668: \t\t// Setup OpenAPI security: all APIs will have the same authentication for now.\n669: \t\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = []map[string][]string{}\n670: \t\tkeys := []string{}\n671: \t\tfor k := range config.SecuritySchemes {\n672: \t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n673: \t\t}\n674: \t\tsort.Strings(keys)\n675: \t\tfor _, k := range keys {\n676: \t\t\tconfig.DefaultSecurity = append(config.DefaultSecurity, map[string][]string{k: {}})\n677: \t\t}\n678: \t\tif config.CommonResponses == nil {\n679: \t\t\tconfig.CommonResponses = map[int]*spec3.Response{}\n680: \t\t}\n681: \t\tif _, exists := config.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized]; !exists {\n682: \t\t\tconfig.CommonResponses[http.StatusUnauthorized] = &spec3.Response{\n683: \t\t\t\tResponseProps: spec3.ResponseProps{\n684: \t\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Unauthorized\",\n685: \t\t\t\t},\n686: \t\t\t}\n687: \t\t}\n688: \t}\n689: \t// make sure we populate info, and info.version, if not manually set\n690: \tif config.Info == nil {\n691: \t\tconfig.Info = &spec.Info{}\n692: \t}\n693: \tif config.Info.Version == \"\" {\n694: \t\tif version != nil {\n695: \t\t\tconfig.Info.Version = strings.Split(version.String(), \"-\")[0]\n696: \t\t} else {\n697: \t\t\tconfig.Info.Version = \"unversioned\"\n698: \t\t}\n699: \t}\n700: }\n701: \n702: // DrainedNotify returns a lifecycle signal of genericapiserver already drained while shutting down.\n703: func (c *Config) DrainedNotify() <-chan struct{} {\n704: \treturn c.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained.Signaled()\n705: }\n706: \n707: // ShutdownInitiated returns a lifecycle signal of apiserver shutdown having been initiated.\n708: func (c *Config) ShutdownInitiatedNotify() <-chan struct{} {\n709: \treturn c.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\n710: }\n711: \n712: // Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\n713: // from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\n714: func (c *Config) Complete(informers informers.SharedInformerFactory) CompletedConfig {\n715: \tif c.FeatureGate == nil {\n716: \t\tc.FeatureGate = utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate\n717: \t}\n718: \tif len(c.ExternalAddress) == 0 && c.PublicAddress != nil {\n719: \t\tc.ExternalAddress = c.PublicAddress.String()\n720: \t}\n721: \n722: \t// if there is no port, and we listen on one securely, use that one\n723: \tif _, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(c.ExternalAddress); err != nil {\n724: \t\tif c.SecureServing == nil {\n725: \t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"cannot derive external address port without listening on a secure port.\")\n726: \t\t}\n727: \t\t_, port, err := c.SecureServing.HostPort()\n728: \t\tif err != nil {\n729: \t\t\tklog.Fatalf(\"cannot derive external address from the secure port: %v\", err)\n730: \t\t}\n731: \t\tc.ExternalAddress = net.JoinHostPort(c.ExternalAddress, strconv.Itoa(port))\n732: \t}\n733: \tcompleteOpenAPI(c.OpenAPIConfig, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\n734: \tcompleteOpenAPIV3(c.OpenAPIV3Config, c.EffectiveVersion.EmulationVersion())\n735: \n736: \tif c.DiscoveryAddresses == nil {\n737: \t\tc.DiscoveryAddresses = discovery.DefaultAddresses{DefaultAddress: c.ExternalAddress}\n738: \t}\n739: \n740: \tAuthorizeClientBearerToken(c.LoopbackClientConfig, &c.Authentication, &c.Authorization)\n741: \n742: \tif c.RequestInfoResolver == nil {\n743: \t\tc.RequestInfoResolver = NewRequestInfoResolver(c)\n744: \t}\n745: \n746: \tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\n747: \t\tif c.RESTOptionsGetter == nil {\n748: \t\t\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistry()\n749: \t\t} else {\n750: \t\t\tc.EquivalentResourceRegistry = runtime.NewEquivalentResourceRegistryWithIdentity(func(groupResource schema.GroupResource) string {\n751: \t\t\t\t// use the storage prefix as the key if possible\n752: \t\t\t\tif opts, err := c.RESTOptionsGetter.GetRESTOptions(groupResource, nil); err == nil {\n753: \t\t\t\t\treturn opts.ResourcePrefix\n754: \t\t\t\t}\n755: \t\t\t\t// otherwise return \"\" to use the default key (parent GV name)\n756: \t\t\t\treturn \"\"\n757: \t\t\t})\n758: \t\t}\n759: \t}\n760: \n761: \treturn CompletedConfig{&completedConfig{c, informers}}\n762: }\n763: \n764: // Complete fills in any fields not set that are required to have valid data and can be derived\n765: // from other fields. If you're going to `ApplyOptions`, do that first. It's mutating the receiver.\n766: func (c *RecommendedConfig) Complete() CompletedConfig {\n767: \treturn c.Config.Complete(c.SharedInformerFactory)\n768: }\n769: \n770: var defaultAllowedMediaTypes = []string{\n771: \truntime.ContentTypeJSON,\n772: \truntime.ContentTypeYAML,\n773: \truntime.ContentTypeProtobuf,\n774: }\n775: \n776: // New creates a new server which logically combines the handling chain with the passed server.\n777: // name is used to differentiate for logging. The handler chain in particular can be difficult as it starts delegating.\n778: // delegationTarget may not be nil.\n779: func (c completedConfig) New(name string, delegationTarget DelegationTarget) (*GenericAPIServer, error) {\n780: \tif c.Serializer == nil {\n781: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.Serializer == nil\")\n782: \t}\n783: \tallowedMediaTypes := defaultAllowedMediaTypes\n784: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.CBORServingAndStorage) {\n785: \t\tallowedMediaTypes = append(allowedMediaTypes, runtime.ContentTypeCBOR)\n786: \t}\n787: \tfor _, info := range c.Serializer.SupportedMediaTypes() {\n788: \t\tvar ok bool\n789: \t\tfor _, mt := range allowedMediaTypes {\n790: \t\t\tif info.MediaType == mt {\n791: \t\t\t\tok = true\n792: \t\t\t\tbreak\n793: \t\t\t}\n794: \t\t}\n795: \t\tif !ok {\n796: \t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"refusing to create new apiserver %q with support for media type %q (allowed media types are: %s)\", name, info.MediaType, strings.Join(allowedMediaTypes, \", \"))\n797: \t\t}\n798: \t}\n799: \tif c.LoopbackClientConfig == nil {\n800: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.LoopbackClientConfig == nil\")\n801: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL600-800"
  }
}
106. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go",
  "repo": "github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package filters\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n24: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n25: \\n26: \\tapierrors \\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\\\"\\n27: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n28: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\\\"\\n29: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\\\"\\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/headerrequest\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\\\"\\n34: \\tgenericapirequest \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n35: \\tgenericfeatures \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n36: \\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n37: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n38: )\\n39: \\n40: type authenticationRecordMetricsFunc func(context.Context, *authenticator.Response, bool, error, authenticator.Audiences, time.Time, time.Time)\\n41: \\n42: // WithAuthentication creates an http handler that tries to authenticate the given request as a user, and then\\n43: // stores any such user found onto the provided context for the request. If authentication fails or returns an error\\n44: // the failed handler is used. On success, \\\"Authorization\\\" header is removed from the request and handler\\n45: // is invoked to serve the request.\\n46: func WithAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig) http.Handler {\\n47: \\treturn withAuthentication(handler, auth, failed, apiAuds, requestHeaderConfig, recordAuthenticationMetrics)\\n48: }\\n49: \\n50: func withAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig, metrics authenticationRecordMetricsFunc) http.Handler {\\n51: \\tif auth == nil {\\n52: \\t\\tklog.Warning(\\\"Authentication is disabled\\\")\\n53: \\t\\treturn handler\\n54: \\t}\\n55: \\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig := &authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig{\\n56: \\t\\tUsernameHeaders:     headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\\\"X-Remote-User\\\"},\\n57: \\t\\tUIDHeaders:          headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\\\"X-Remote-Uid\\\"},\\n58: \\t\\tGroupHeaders:        headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\\\"X-Remote-Group\\\"},\\n59: \\t\\tExtraHeaderPrefixes: headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\\\"X-Remote-Extra-\\\"},\\n60: \\t}\\n61: \\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n62: \\t\\tauthenticationStart := time.Now()\\n63: \\n64: \\t\\tif len(apiAuds) > 0 {\\n65: \\t\\t\\treq = req.WithContext(authenticator.WithAudiences(req.Context(), apiAuds))\\n66: \\t\\t}\\n67: \\t\\tresp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)\\n68: \\t\\tauthenticationFinish := time.Now()\\n69: \\t\\tgenericapirequest.TrackAuthenticationLatency(req.Context(), authenticationFinish.Sub(authenticationStart))\\n70: \\t\\tdefer func() {\\n71: \\t\\t\\tmetrics(req.Context(), resp, ok, err, apiAuds, authenticationStart, authenticationFinish)\\n72: \\t\\t}()\\n73: \\t\\tif err != nil || !ok {\\n74: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n75: \\t\\t\\t\\tklog.ErrorS(err, \\\"Unable to authenticate the request\\\")\\n76: \\t\\t\\t}\\n77: \\t\\t\\tfailed.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n78: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n79: \\t\\t}\\n80: \\n81: \\t\\tif !audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, resp.Audiences) {\\n82: \\t\\t\\terr = fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to match the audience: %v , accepted: %v\\\", resp.Audiences, apiAuds)\\n83: \\t\\t\\tklog.Error(err)\\n84: \\t\\t\\tfailed.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n85: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n86: \\t\\t}\\n87: \\n88: \\t\\t// authorization header is not required anymore in case of a successful authentication.\\n89: \\t\\treq.Header.Del(\\\"Authorization\\\")\\n90: \\n91: \\t\\t// delete standard front proxy headers\\n92: \\t\\theaderrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(\\n93: \\t\\t\\treq.Header,\\n94: \\t\\t\\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\\n95: \\t\\t\\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\\n96: \\t\\t\\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\\n97: \\t\\t\\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\\n98: \\t\\t)\\n99: \\n100: \\t\\t// also delete any custom front proxy headers\\n101: \\t\\tif requestHeaderConfig != nil {\\n102: \\t\\t\\theaderrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(\\n103: \\t\\t\\t\\treq.Header,\\n104: \\t\\t\\t\\trequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\\n105: \\t\\t\\t\\trequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\\n106: \\t\\t\\t\\trequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\\n107: \\t\\t\\t\\trequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\\n108: \\t\\t\\t)\\n109: \\t\\t}\\n110: \\n111: \\t\\t// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,\\n112: \\t\\t// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.\\n113: \\t\\t// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these\\n114: \\t\\t// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the\\n115: \\t\\t// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).\\n116: \\t\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 && isAnonymousUser(resp.User) {\\n117: \\t\\t\\t// limit this connection to just this request,\\n118: \\t\\t\\t// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection\\n119: \\t\\t\\t// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c\\n120: \\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Connection\\\", \\\"close\\\")\\n121: \\t\\t}\\n122: \\t\\treq = req.WithContext(genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User))\\n123: \\t\\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n124: \\t})\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: func Unauthorized(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {\\n128: \\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n129: \\t\\t// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,\\n130: \\t\\t// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.\\n131: \\t\\t// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these\\n132: \\t\\t// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the\\n133: \\t\\t// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).\\n134: \\t\\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 {\\n135: \\t\\t\\t// limit this connection to just this request,\\n136: \\t\\t\\t// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection\\n137: \\t\\t\\t// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c\\n138: \\t\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Connection\\\", \\\"close\\\")\\n139: \\t\\t}\\n140: \\t\\tctx := req.Context()\\n141: \\t\\trequestInfo, found := genericapirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\\n142: \\t\\tif !found {\\n143: \\t\\t\\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New(\\\"no RequestInfo found in the context\\\"))\\n144: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n145: \\t\\t}\\n146: \\n147: \\t\\tgv := schema.GroupVersion{Group: requestInfo.APIGroup, Version: requestInfo.APIVersion}\\n148: \\t\\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewUnauthorized(\\\"Unauthorized\\\"), s, gv, w, req)\\n149: \\t})\\n150: }\\n151: \\n152: func audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, responseAudiences authenticator.Audiences) bool {\\n153: \\tif len(apiAuds) == 0 || len(responseAudiences) == 0 {\\n154: \\t\\treturn true\\n155: \\t}\\n156: \\n157: \\treturn len(apiAuds.Intersect(responseAudiences)) > 0\\n158: }\\n159: \\n160: func isAnonymousUser(u user.Info) bool {\\n161: \\tif u.GetName() == user.Anonymous {\\n162: \\t\\treturn true\\n163: \\t}\\n164: \\tfor _, group := range u.GetGroups() {\\n165: \\t\\tif group == user.AllUnauthenticated {\\n166: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n167: \\t\\t}\\n168: \\t}\\n169: \\treturn false\\n170: }\\n171: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2014 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package filters\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"errors\"\n22: \t\"fmt\"\n23: \t\"net/http\"\n24: \t\"time\"\n25: \n26: \tapierrors \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n27: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n28: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n29: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticator\"\n30: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/authenticatorfactory\"\n31: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/request/headerrequest\"\n32: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authentication/user\"\n33: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n34: \tgenericapirequest \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n35: \tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n36: \tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n37: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n38: )\n39: \n40: type authenticationRecordMetricsFunc func(context.Context, *authenticator.Response, bool, error, authenticator.Audiences, time.Time, time.Time)\n41: \n42: // WithAuthentication creates an http handler that tries to authenticate the given request as a user, and then\n43: // stores any such user found onto the provided context for the request. If authentication fails or returns an error\n44: // the failed handler is used. On success, \"Authorization\" header is removed from the request and handler\n45: // is invoked to serve the request.\n46: func WithAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig) http.Handler {\n47: \treturn withAuthentication(handler, auth, failed, apiAuds, requestHeaderConfig, recordAuthenticationMetrics)\n48: }\n49: \n50: func withAuthentication(handler http.Handler, auth authenticator.Request, failed http.Handler, apiAuds authenticator.Audiences, requestHeaderConfig *authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig, metrics authenticationRecordMetricsFunc) http.Handler {\n51: \tif auth == nil {\n52: \t\tklog.Warning(\"Authentication is disabled\")\n53: \t\treturn handler\n54: \t}\n55: \tstandardRequestHeaderConfig := &authenticatorfactory.RequestHeaderConfig{\n56: \t\tUsernameHeaders:     headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-User\"},\n57: \t\tUIDHeaders:          headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Uid\"},\n58: \t\tGroupHeaders:        headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Group\"},\n59: \t\tExtraHeaderPrefixes: headerrequest.StaticStringSlice{\"X-Remote-Extra-\"},\n60: \t}\n61: \treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n62: \t\tauthenticationStart := time.Now()\n63: \n64: \t\tif len(apiAuds) > 0 {\n65: \t\t\treq = req.WithContext(authenticator.WithAudiences(req.Context(), apiAuds))\n66: \t\t}\n67: \t\tresp, ok, err := auth.AuthenticateRequest(req)\n68: \t\tauthenticationFinish := time.Now()\n69: \t\tgenericapirequest.TrackAuthenticationLatency(req.Context(), authenticationFinish.Sub(authenticationStart))\n70: \t\tdefer func() {\n71: \t\t\tmetrics(req.Context(), resp, ok, err, apiAuds, authenticationStart, authenticationFinish)\n72: \t\t}()\n73: \t\tif err != nil || !ok {\n74: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n75: \t\t\t\tklog.ErrorS(err, \"Unable to authenticate the request\")\n76: \t\t\t}\n77: \t\t\tfailed.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n78: \t\t\treturn\n79: \t\t}\n80: \n81: \t\tif !audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, resp.Audiences) {\n82: \t\t\terr = fmt.Errorf(\"unable to match the audience: %v , accepted: %v\", resp.Audiences, apiAuds)\n83: \t\t\tklog.Error(err)\n84: \t\t\tfailed.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n85: \t\t\treturn\n86: \t\t}\n87: \n88: \t\t// authorization header is not required anymore in case of a successful authentication.\n89: \t\treq.Header.Del(\"Authorization\")\n90: \n91: \t\t// delete standard front proxy headers\n92: \t\theaderrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(\n93: \t\t\treq.Header,\n94: \t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\n95: \t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\n96: \t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\n97: \t\t\tstandardRequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\n98: \t\t)\n99: \n100: \t\t// also delete any custom front proxy headers\n101: \t\tif requestHeaderConfig != nil {\n102: \t\t\theaderrequest.ClearAuthenticationHeaders(\n103: \t\t\t\treq.Header,\n104: \t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.UsernameHeaders,\n105: \t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.UIDHeaders,\n106: \t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.GroupHeaders,\n107: \t\t\t\trequestHeaderConfig.ExtraHeaderPrefixes,\n108: \t\t\t)\n109: \t\t}\n110: \n111: \t\t// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,\n112: \t\t// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.\n113: \t\t// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these\n114: \t\t// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the\n115: \t\t// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).\n116: \t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 && isAnonymousUser(resp.User) {\n117: \t\t\t// limit this connection to just this request,\n118: \t\t\t// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection\n119: \t\t\t// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c\n120: \t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"close\")\n121: \t\t}\n122: \t\treq = req.WithContext(genericapirequest.WithUser(req.Context(), resp.User))\n123: \t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n124: \t})\n125: }\n126: \n127: func Unauthorized(s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {\n128: \treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n129: \t\t// http2 is an expensive protocol that is prone to abuse,\n130: \t\t// see CVE-2023-44487 and CVE-2023-39325 for an example.\n131: \t\t// Do not allow unauthenticated clients to keep these\n132: \t\t// connections open (i.e. basically degrade them to the\n133: \t\t// performance of http1 with keep-alive disabled).\n134: \t\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.UnauthenticatedHTTP2DOSMitigation) && req.ProtoMajor == 2 {\n135: \t\t\t// limit this connection to just this request,\n136: \t\t\t// and then send a GOAWAY and tear down the TCP connection\n137: \t\t\t// https://github.com/golang/net/commit/97aa3a539ec716117a9d15a4659a911f50d13c3c\n138: \t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Connection\", \"close\")\n139: \t\t}\n140: \t\tctx := req.Context()\n141: \t\trequestInfo, found := genericapirequest.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n142: \t\tif !found {\n143: \t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, errors.New(\"no RequestInfo found in the context\"))\n144: \t\t\treturn\n145: \t\t}\n146: \n147: \t\tgv := schema.GroupVersion{Group: requestInfo.APIGroup, Version: requestInfo.APIVersion}\n148: \t\tresponsewriters.ErrorNegotiated(apierrors.NewUnauthorized(\"Unauthorized\"), s, gv, w, req)\n149: \t})\n150: }\n151: \n152: func audiencesAreAcceptable(apiAuds, responseAudiences authenticator.Audiences) bool {\n153: \tif len(apiAuds) == 0 || len(responseAudiences) == 0 {\n154: \t\treturn true\n155: \t}\n156: \n157: \treturn len(apiAuds.Intersect(responseAudiences)) > 0\n158: }\n159: \n160: func isAnonymousUser(u user.Info) bool {\n161: \tif u.GetName() == user.Anonymous {\n162: \t\treturn true\n163: \t}\n164: \tfor _, group := range u.GetGroups() {\n165: \t\tif group == user.AllUnauthenticated {\n166: \t\t\treturn true\n167: \t\t}\n168: \t}\n169: \treturn false\n170: }\n171: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authentication.go"
  }
}

c.DisabledPostStartHooks,\\n849: \\n850: \\t\\thealthzRegistry:  healthCheckRegistry{path: \\\"/healthz\\\", checks: c.HealthzChecks},\\n851: \\t\\tlivezRegistry:    healthCheckRegistry{path: atomic.CompareAndSwapInt64(&jsonpatch.AccumulatedCopySizeLimit, existing, c.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes) {\\n889: \\t\\t\\tbreak\\n890: \\t\\t}\\n891: \\t}\\n892: \\n893: \\t// first add poststarthooks from delegated targets\\n894: \\tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.PostStartHooks() {\\n895: \\t\\ts.postStartHooks[k] = v\\n896: \\t}\\n897: \\n898: \\tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.PreShutdownHooks() {\\n899: \\t\\ts.preShutdownHooks[k] = v\\n900: \\t}\\n901: \\n902: \\t// add poststarthooks that were preconfigured.  Using the add method will give us an error if the same name has already been registered.\\n903: \\tfor name, preconfiguredPostStartHook := range c.PostStartHooks {\\n904: \\t\\tif err := s.AddPostStartHook(name, preconfiguredPostStartHook.hook); err != nil {\\n905: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n906: \\t\\t}\\n907: \\t}\\n908: \\n909: \\t// register mux signals from the delegated server\\n910: \\tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\\n911: \\t\\tif err := s.RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal(k, v); err != nil {\\n912: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n913: \\t\\t}\\n914: \\t}\\n915: \\n916: \\tgenericApiServerHookName := \\\"generic-apiserver-start-informers\\\"\\n917: \\tif c.SharedInformerFactory != nil {\\n918: \\t\\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(genericApiServerHookName) {\\n919: \\t\\t\\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(genericApiServerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\\n920: \\t\\t\\t\\tc.SharedInformerFactory.Start(hookContext.Done())\\n921: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n922: \\t\\t\\t})\\n923: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n924: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n925: \\t\\t\\t}\\n926: \\t\\t}\\n927: \\t\\t// TODO: Once we get rid of /healthz consider changing this to post-start-hook.\\n928: \\t\\terr := c.FlowControl != nil {\\n937: \\t\\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\\n938: \\t\\t\\tgo c.FlowControl.Run(hookContext.Done())\\n939: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n940: \\t\\t})\\n941: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n942: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n943: \\t\\t}\\n944: \\t\\t// TODO(yue9944882): plumb err\\n970: \\t\\t\\t}\\n971: \\t\\t}\\n972: \\t}\\n973: \\n974: \\t// Add PostStartHook for maintenaing the object count tracker.\\n975: \\tif c.StorageObjectCountTracker != nil {\\n976: \\t\\tconst storageObjectCountTrackerHookName = \\\"storage-object-count-tracker-hook\\\"\\n977: \\t\\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(storageObjectCountTrackerHookName) {\\n978: \\t\\t\\tif err := s.AddPostStartHook(storageObjectCountTrackerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\\n979: \\t\\t\\t\\tgo c.StorageObjectCountTracker.RunUntil(hookContext.Done())\\n980: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n981: \\t\\t\\t}); err != nil {\\n982: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn nil, err\\n983: \\t\\t\\t}\\n984: \\t\\t}\\n985: \\t}\\n986: \\n987: \\tfor _, delegateCheck := range delegationTarget.HealthzChecks() {\\n988: \\t\\tskip := false\\n989: \\t\\tfor _, existingCheck := range c.HealthzChecks {\\n990: \\t\\t\\tif existingCheck.Name() == delegateCheck.Name() {\\n991: \\t\\t\\t\\tskip = true\\n992: \\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n993: \\t\\t\\t}\\n994: \\t\\t}\\n995: \\t\\tif skip {\\n996: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n997: \\t\\t}\\n998: \\t\\ts.AddHealthChecks(delegateCheck)\\n999: \\t}\\n1000: \\ts.RegisterDestroyFunc(func() {\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL800-1000\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "800: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.LoopbackClientConfig == nil\")\n801: \t}\n802: \tif c.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil {\n803: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"Genericapiserver.New() called with config.EquivalentResourceRegistry == nil\")\n804: \t}\n805: \n806: \thandlerChainBuilder := func(handler http.Handler) http.Handler {\n807: \t\treturn c.BuildHandlerChainFunc(handler, c.Config)\n808: \t}\n809: \n810: \tvar debugSocket *routes.DebugSocket\n811: \tif c.DebugSocketPath != \"\" {\n812: \t\tdebugSocket = routes.NewDebugSocket(c.DebugSocketPath)\n813: \t}\n814: \n815: \tapiServerHandler := NewAPIServerHandler(name, c.Serializer, handlerChainBuilder, delegationTarget.UnprotectedHandler())\n816: \n817: \ts := &GenericAPIServer{\n818: \t\tdiscoveryAddresses:             c.DiscoveryAddresses,\n819: \t\tLoopbackClientConfig:           c.LoopbackClientConfig,\n820: \t\tlegacyAPIGroupPrefixes:         c.LegacyAPIGroupPrefixes,\n821: \t\tadmissionControl:               c.AdmissionControl,\n822: \t\tSerializer:                     c.Serializer,\n823: \t\tAuditBackend:                   c.AuditBackend,\n824: \t\tAuthorizer:                     c.Authorization.Authorizer,\n825: \t\tdelegationTarget:               delegationTarget,\n826: \t\tEquivalentResourceRegistry:     c.EquivalentResourceRegistry,\n827: \t\tNonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup: c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup,\n828: \t\tWatchRequestWaitGroup:          c.WatchRequestWaitGroup,\n829: \t\tHandler:                        apiServerHandler,\n830: \t\tUnprotectedDebugSocket:         debugSocket,\n831: \n832: \t\tlistedPathProvider: apiServerHandler,\n833: \t\tFlagz:              c.Flagz,\n834: \n835: \t\tminRequestTimeout:                   time.Duration(c.MinRequestTimeout) * time.Second,\n836: \t\tShutdownTimeout:                     c.RequestTimeout,\n837: \t\tShutdownDelayDuration:               c.ShutdownDelayDuration,\n838: \t\tShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod: c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod,\n839: \t\tSecureServingInfo:                   c.SecureServing,\n840: \t\tExternalAddress:                     c.ExternalAddress,\n841: \n842: \t\topenAPIConfig:           c.OpenAPIConfig,\n843: \t\topenAPIV3Config:         c.OpenAPIV3Config,\n844: \t\tskipOpenAPIInstallation: c.SkipOpenAPIInstallation,\n845: \n846: \t\tpostStartHooks:         map[string]postStartHookEntry{},\n847: \t\tpreShutdownHooks:       map[string]preShutdownHookEntry{},\n848: \t\tdisabledPostStartHooks: c.DisabledPostStartHooks,\n849: \n850: \t\thealthzRegistry:  healthCheckRegistry{path: \"/healthz\", checks: c.HealthzChecks},\n851: \t\tlivezRegistry:    healthCheckRegistry{path: \"/livez\", checks: c.LivezChecks, clock: clock.RealClock{}},\n852: \t\treadyzRegistry:   healthCheckRegistry{path: \"/readyz\", checks: c.ReadyzChecks},\n853: \t\tlivezGracePeriod: c.LivezGracePeriod,\n854: \n855: \t\tDiscoveryGroupManager: discovery.NewRootAPIsHandler(c.DiscoveryAddresses, c.Serializer),\n856: \n857: \t\tmaxRequestBodyBytes: c.MaxRequestBodyBytes,\n858: \n859: \t\tlifecycleSignals:       c.lifecycleSignals,\n860: \t\tShutdownSendRetryAfter: c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter,\n861: \n862: \t\tAPIServerID:           c.APIServerID,\n863: \t\tStorageReadinessHook:  NewStorageReadinessHook(c.StorageInitializationTimeout),\n864: \t\tStorageVersionManager: c.StorageVersionManager,\n865: \n866: \t\tEffectiveVersion:                        c.EffectiveVersion,\n867: \t\tEmulationForwardCompatible:              c.EmulationForwardCompatible,\n868: \t\tRuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible: c.RuntimeConfigEmulationForwardCompatible,\n869: \t\tFeatureGate:                             c.FeatureGate,\n870: \n871: \t\tmuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals: map[string]<-chan struct{}{},\n872: \t}\n873: \n874: \tmanager := c.AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager\n875: \tif manager == nil {\n876: \t\tmanager = discoveryendpoint.NewResourceManager(\"apis\")\n877: \t}\n878: \ts.AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager = manager\n879: \ts.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager = discoveryendpoint.NewResourceManager(\"api\")\n880: \tfor {\n881: \t\tif c.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes <= 0 {\n882: \t\t\tbreak\n883: \t\t}\n884: \t\texisting := atomic.LoadInt64(&jsonpatch.AccumulatedCopySizeLimit)\n885: \t\tif existing > 0 && existing < c.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes {\n886: \t\t\tbreak\n887: \t\t}\n888: \t\tif atomic.CompareAndSwapInt64(&jsonpatch.AccumulatedCopySizeLimit, existing, c.JSONPatchMaxCopyBytes) {\n889: \t\t\tbreak\n890: \t\t}\n891: \t}\n892: \n893: \t// first add poststarthooks from delegated targets\n894: \tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.PostStartHooks() {\n895: \t\ts.postStartHooks[k] = v\n896: \t}\n897: \n898: \tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.PreShutdownHooks() {\n899: \t\ts.preShutdownHooks[k] = v\n900: \t}\n901: \n902: \t// add poststarthooks that were preconfigured.  Using the add method will give us an error if the same name has already been registered.\n903: \tfor name, preconfiguredPostStartHook := range c.PostStartHooks {\n904: \t\tif err := s.AddPostStartHook(name, preconfiguredPostStartHook.hook); err != nil {\n905: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n906: \t\t}\n907: \t}\n908: \n909: \t// register mux signals from the delegated server\n910: \tfor k, v := range delegationTarget.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\n911: \t\tif err := s.RegisterMuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignal(k, v); err != nil {\n912: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n913: \t\t}\n914: \t}\n915: \n916: \tgenericApiServerHookName := \"generic-apiserver-start-informers\"\n917: \tif c.SharedInformerFactory != nil {\n918: \t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(genericApiServerHookName) {\n919: \t\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(genericApiServerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n920: \t\t\t\tc.SharedInformerFactory.Start(hookContext.Done())\n921: \t\t\t\treturn nil\n922: \t\t\t})\n923: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n924: \t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n925: \t\t\t}\n926: \t\t}\n927: \t\t// TODO: Once we get rid of /healthz consider changing this to post-start-hook.\n928: \t\terr := s.AddReadyzChecks(healthz.NewInformerSyncHealthz(c.SharedInformerFactory))\n929: \t\tif err != nil {\n930: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n931: \t\t}\n932: \t}\n933: \n934: \tconst priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName = \"priority-and-fairness-config-consumer\"\n935: \tif s.isPostStartHookRegistered(priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName) {\n936: \t} else if c.FlowControl != nil {\n937: \t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n938: \t\t\tgo c.FlowControl.Run(hookContext.Done())\n939: \t\t\treturn nil\n940: \t\t})\n941: \t\tif err != nil {\n942: \t\t\treturn nil, err\n943: \t\t}\n944: \t\t// TODO(yue9944882): plumb pre-shutdown-hook for request-management system?\n945: \t} else {\n946: \t\tklog.V(3).Infof(\"Not requested to run hook %s\", priorityAndFairnessConfigConsumerHookName)\n947: \t}\n948: \n949: \t// Add PostStartHooks for maintaining the watermarks for the Priority-and-Fairness and the Max-in-Flight filters.\n950: \tif c.FlowControl != nil {\n951: \t\tconst priorityAndFairnessFilterHookName = \"priority-and-fairness-filter\"\n952: \t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(priorityAndFairnessFilterHookName) {\n953: \t\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(priorityAndFairnessFilterHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n954: \t\t\t\tgenericfilters.StartPriorityAndFairnessWatermarkMaintenance(hookContext.Done())\n955: \t\t\t\treturn nil\n956: \t\t\t})\n957: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n958: \t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n959: \t\t\t}\n960: \t\t}\n961: \t} else {\n962: \t\tconst maxInFlightFilterHookName = \"max-in-flight-filter\"\n963: \t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(maxInFlightFilterHookName) {\n964: \t\t\terr := s.AddPostStartHook(maxInFlightFilterHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n965: \t\t\t\tgenericfilters.StartMaxInFlightWatermarkMaintenance(hookContext.Done())\n966: \t\t\t\treturn nil\n967: \t\t\t})\n968: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n969: \t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n970: \t\t\t}\n971: \t\t}\n972: \t}\n973: \n974: \t// Add PostStartHook for maintenaing the object count tracker.\n975: \tif c.StorageObjectCountTracker != nil {\n976: \t\tconst storageObjectCountTrackerHookName = \"storage-object-count-tracker-hook\"\n977: \t\tif !s.isPostStartHookRegistered(storageObjectCountTrackerHookName) {\n978: \t\t\tif err := s.AddPostStartHook(storageObjectCountTrackerHookName, func(hookContext PostStartHookContext) error {\n979: \t\t\t\tgo c.StorageObjectCountTracker.RunUntil(hookContext.Done())\n980: \t\t\t\treturn nil\n981: \t\t\t}); err != nil {\n982: \t\t\t\treturn nil, err\n983: \t\t\t}\n984: \t\t}\n985: \t}\n986: \n987: \tfor _, delegateCheck := range delegationTarget.HealthzChecks() {\n988: \t\tskip := false\n989: \t\tfor _, existingCheck := range c.HealthzChecks {\n990: \t\t\tif existingCheck.Name() == delegateCheck.Name() {\n991: \t\t\t\tskip = true\n992: \t\t\t\tbreak\n993: \t\t\t}\n994: \t\t}\n995: \t\tif skip {\n996: \t\t\tcontinue\n997: \t\t}\n998: \t\ts.AddHealthChecks(delegateCheck)\n999: \t}\n1000: \ts.RegisterDestroyFunc(func() {\n1001: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL800-1000"
  }

the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: package filters\\n18: \\n19: import (\\n20: \\t\\\"context\\\"\\n21: \\t\\\"errors\\\"\\n22: \\t\\\"net/http\\\"\\n23: \\t\\\"time\\\"\\n24: \\n25: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\\\"\\n26: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\\\"\\n27: \\tgenericfeatures \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\\\"\\n28: \\tutilfeature \\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\\\"\\n29: \\n30: \\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n31: \\n32: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\\\"\\n33: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\\\"\\n34: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\\\"\\n36: \\t\\\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\\\"\\n37: )\\n38: \\n39: const (\\n40: \\t// Annotation key names set in advanced audit\\n41: \\tdecisionAnnotationKey = \\\"authorization.k8s.io/decision\\\"\\n42: \\treasonAnnotationKey   = \\\"authorization.k8s.io/reason\\\"\\n43: \\n44: \\t// Annotation values set in advanced audit\\n45: \\tdecisionAllow  = \\\"allow\\\"\\n46: \\tdecisionForbid = \\\"forbid\\\"\\n47: \\treasonError    = \\\"internal error\\\"\\n48: )\\n49: \\n50: type recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc func(ctx context.Context, authorized authorizer.Decision, err error, authStart time.Time, authFinish time.Time)\\n51: \\n52: // WithAuthorization passes all authorized requests on to handler, and returns a forbidden error otherwise.\\n53: func WithAuthorization(hhandler http.Handler, auth authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {\\n54: \\treturn withAuthorization(hhandler, auth, s, recordAuthorizationMetrics)\\n55: }\\n56: \\n57: func withAuthorization(handler http.Handler, a authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, metrics recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc) http.Handler {\\n58: \\tif a == nil {\\n59: \\t\\tklog.Warning(\\\"Authorization is disabled\\\")\\n60: \\t\\treturn handler\\n61: \\t}\\n62: \\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\\n63: \\t\\tctx := req.Context()\\n64: \\t\\tauthorizationStart := time.Now()\\n65: \\n66: \\t\\tattributes, err := GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)\\n67: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n68: \\t\\t\\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)\\n69: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n70: \\t\\t}\\n71: \\t\\tauthorized, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)\\n72: \\n73: \\t\\tauthorizationFinish := time.Now()\\n74: \\t\\trequest.TrackAuthorizationLatency(ctx, authorizationFinish.Sub(authorizationStart))\\n75: \\t\\tdefer func() {\\n76: \\t\\t\\tmetrics(ctx, authorized, err, authorizationStart, authorizationFinish)\\n77: \\t\\t}()\\n78: \\n79: \\t\\t// an authorizer like RBAC could encounter evaluation errors and still allow the request, so authorizer decision is checked before error here.\\n80: \\t\\tif authorized == authorizer.DecisionAllow {\\n81: \\t\\t\\taudit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,\\n82: \\t\\t\\t\\tdecisionAnnotationKey, decisionAllow,\\n83: \\t\\t\\t\\treasonAnnotationKey, reason)\\n84: \\t\\t\\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\\n85: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n86: \\t\\t}\\n87: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n88: \\t\\t\\taudit.AddAuditAnnotation(ctx, reasonAnnotationKey, reasonError)\\n89: \\t\\t\\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)\\n90: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n91: \\t\\t}\\n92: \\n93: \\t\\tklog.V(4).InfoS(\\\"Forbidden\\\", \\\"URI\\\", req.RequestURI, \\\"reason\\\", reason)\\n94: \\t\\taudit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,\\n95: \\t\\t\\tdecisionAnnotationKey, decisionForbid,\\n96: \\t\\t\\treasonAnnotationKey, reason)\\n97: \\t\\tresponsewriters.Forbidden(attributes, w, req, reason, s)\\n98: \\t})\\n99: }\\n100: \\n101: func GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx context.Context) (authorizer.Attributes, error) {\\n102: \\tattribs := authorizer.AttributesRecord{}\\n103: \\n104: \\tuser, ok := request.UserFrom(ctx)\\n105: \\tif ok {\\n106: \\t\\tattribs.User = user\\n107: \\t}\\n108: \\n109: \\trequestInfo, found := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\\n110: \\tif !found {\\n111: \\t\\treturn nil, errors.New(\\\"no RequestInfo found in the context\\\")\\n112: \\t}\\n113: \\n114: \\t// Start with common attributes that apply to resource and non-resource requests\\n115: \\tattribs.ResourceRequest = requestInfo.IsResourceRequest\\n116: \\tattribs.Path = requestInfo.Path\\n117: \\tattribs.Verb = requestInfo.Verb\\n118: \\n119: \\tattribs.APIGroup = requestInfo.APIGroup\\n120: \\tattribs.APIVersion = requestInfo.APIVersion\\n121: \\tattribs.Resource = requestInfo.Resource\\n122: \\tattribs.Subresource = requestInfo.Subresource\\n123: \\tattribs.Namespace = requestInfo.Namespace\\n124: \\tattribs.Name = requestInfo.Name\\n125: \\n126: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.AuthorizeWithSelectors) {\\n127: \\t\\t// parsing here makes it easy to keep the AttributesRecord type value-only and avoids any mutex copies when\\n128: \\t\\t// doing shallow copies in other steps.\\n129: \\t\\tif len(requestInfo.FieldSelector) > 0 {\\n130: \\t\\t\\tfieldSelector, err := fields.ParseSelector(requestInfo.FieldSelector)\\n131: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n132: \\t\\t\\t\\tattribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = nil, err\\n133: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n134: \\t\\t\\t\\tif requirements := fieldSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {\\n135: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tattribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = fieldSelector.Requirements(), nil\\n136: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n137: \\t\\t\\t}\\n138: \\t\\t}\\n139: \\n140: \\t\\tif len(requestInfo.LabelSelector) > 0 {\\n141: \\t\\t\\tlabelSelector, err := labels.Parse(requestInfo.LabelSelector)\\n142: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n143: \\t\\t\\t\\tattribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = nil, err\\n144: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n145: \\t\\t\\t\\tif requirements, _ /*selectable*/ := labelSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {\\n146: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tattribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = requirements, nil\\n147: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n148: \\t\\t\\t}\\n149: \\t\\t}\\n150: \\t}\\n151: \\n152: \\treturn &attribs, nil\\n153: }\\n154: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go\"}",

    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2016 The Kubernetes Authors.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: package filters\n18: \n19: import (\n20: \t\"context\"\n21: \t\"errors\"\n22: \t\"net/http\"\n23: \t\"time\"\n24: \n25: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n26: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/labels\"\n27: \tgenericfeatures \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/features\"\n28: \tutilfeature \"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/util/feature\"\n29: \n30: \t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n31: \n32: \t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime\"\n33: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/audit\"\n34: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/authorization/authorizer\"\n35: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/handlers/responsewriters\"\n36: \t\"k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/request\"\n37: )\n38: \n39: const (\n40: \t// Annotation key names set in advanced audit\n41: \tdecisionAnnotationKey = \"authorization.k8s.io/decision\"\n42: \treasonAnnotationKey   = \"authorization.k8s.io/reason\"\n43: \n44: \t// Annotation values set in advanced audit\n45: \tdecisionAllow  = \"allow\"\n46: \tdecisionForbid = \"forbid\"\n47: \treasonError    = \"internal error\"\n48: )\n49: \n50: type recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc func(ctx context.Context, authorized authorizer.Decision, err error, authStart time.Time, authFinish time.Time)\n51: \n52: // WithAuthorization passes all authorized requests on to handler, and returns a forbidden error otherwise.\n53: func WithAuthorization(hhandler http.Handler, auth authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer) http.Handler {\n54: \treturn withAuthorization(hhandler, auth, s, recordAuthorizationMetrics)\n55: }\n56: \n57: func withAuthorization(handler http.Handler, a authorizer.Authorizer, s runtime.NegotiatedSerializer, metrics recordAuthorizationMetricsFunc) http.Handler {\n58: \tif a == nil {\n59: \t\tklog.Warning(\"Authorization is disabled\")\n60: \t\treturn handler\n61: \t}\n62: \treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {\n63: \t\tctx := req.Context()\n64: \t\tauthorizationStart := time.Now()\n65: \n66: \t\tattributes, err := GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)\n67: \t\tif err != nil {\n68: \t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)\n69: \t\t\treturn\n70: \t\t}\n71: \t\tauthorized, reason, err := a.Authorize(ctx, attributes)\n72: \n73: \t\tauthorizationFinish := time.Now()\n74: \t\trequest.TrackAuthorizationLatency(ctx, authorizationFinish.Sub(authorizationStart))\n75: \t\tdefer func() {\n76: \t\t\tmetrics(ctx, authorized, err, authorizationStart, authorizationFinish)\n77: \t\t}()\n78: \n79: \t\t// an authorizer like RBAC could encounter evaluation errors and still allow the request, so authorizer decision is checked before error here.\n80: \t\tif authorized == authorizer.DecisionAllow {\n81: \t\t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,\n82: \t\t\t\tdecisionAnnotationKey, decisionAllow,\n83: \t\t\t\treasonAnnotationKey, reason)\n84: \t\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n85: \t\t\treturn\n86: \t\t}\n87: \t\tif err != nil {\n88: \t\t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotation(ctx, reasonAnnotationKey, reasonError)\n89: \t\t\tresponsewriters.InternalError(w, req, err)\n90: \t\t\treturn\n91: \t\t}\n92: \n93: \t\tklog.V(4).InfoS(\"Forbidden\", \"URI\", req.RequestURI, \"reason\", reason)\n94: \t\taudit.AddAuditAnnotations(ctx,\n95: \t\t\tdecisionAnnotationKey, decisionForbid,\n96: \t\t\treasonAnnotationKey, reason)\n97: \t\tresponsewriters.Forbidden(attributes, w, req, reason, s)\n98: \t})\n99: }\n100: \n101: func GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx context.Context) (authorizer.Attributes, error) {\n102: \tattribs := authorizer.AttributesRecord{}\n103: \n104: \tuser, ok := request.UserFrom(ctx)\n105: \tif ok {\n106: \t\tattribs.User = user\n107: \t}\n108: \n109: \trequestInfo, found := request.RequestInfoFrom(ctx)\n110: \tif !found {\n111: \t\treturn nil, errors.New(\"no RequestInfo found in the context\")\n112: \t}\n113: \n114: \t// Start with common attributes that apply to resource and non-resource requests\n115: \tattribs.ResourceRequest = requestInfo.IsResourceRequest\n116: \tattribs.Path = requestInfo.Path\n117: \tattribs.Verb = requestInfo.Verb\n118: \n119: \tattribs.APIGroup = requestInfo.APIGroup\n120: \tattribs.APIVersion = requestInfo.APIVersion\n121: \tattribs.Resource = requestInfo.Resource\n122: \tattribs.Subresource = requestInfo.Subresource\n123: \tattribs.Namespace = requestInfo.Namespace\n124: \tattribs.Name = requestInfo.Name\n125: \n126: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.AuthorizeWithSelectors) {\n127: \t\t// parsing here makes it easy to keep the AttributesRecord type value-only and avoids any mutex copies when\n128: \t\t// doing shallow copies in other steps.\n129: \t\tif len(requestInfo.FieldSelector) > 0 {\n130: \t\t\tfieldSelector, err := fields.ParseSelector(requestInfo.FieldSelector)\n131: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n132: \t\t\t\tattribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = nil, err\n133: \t\t\t} else {\n134: \t\t\t\tif requirements := fieldSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {\n135: \t\t\t\t\tattribs.FieldSelectorRequirements, attribs.FieldSelectorParsingErr = fieldSelector.Requirements(), nil\n136: \t\t\t\t}\n137: \t\t\t}\n138: \t\t}\n139: \n140: \t\tif len(requestInfo.LabelSelector) > 0 {\n141: \t\t\tlabelSelector, err := labels.Parse(requestInfo.LabelSelector)\n142: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n143: \t\t\t\tattribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = nil, err\n144: \t\t\t} else {\n145: \t\t\t\tif requirements, _ /*selectable*/ := labelSelector.Requirements(); len(requirements) > 0 {\n146: \t\t\t\t\tattribs.LabelSelectorRequirements, attribs.LabelSelectorParsingErr = requirements, nil\n147: \t\t\t\t}\n148: \t\t\t}\n149: \t\t}\n150: \t}\n151: \n152: \treturn &attribs, nil\n153: }\n154: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/filters/authorization.go"
  }
}

\\\"constrainedimpersonation\\\")\\n1050: \\t} else {\\n1051: \\t\\thandler = impersonation.WithImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\\n1052: \\t\\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \\\"impersonation\\\")\\n1053: \\t}\\n1054: \\n1055: \\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\\n1056: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAudit(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator, c.LongRunningFunc)\\n1057: \\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \\\"audit\\\")\\n1058: c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator)\\n1061: \\n1062: \\t// WithTracing comes after authentication so we can allow authenticated\\n1063: \\t// clients to influence sampling.\\n1064: \\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerTracing) {\\n1065: \\t\\thandler = genericapifilters.WithTracing(handler, c.TracerProvider)\\n1066: \\t}\\n1067: \\tfailedHandler = by the timeout handler\\n1075: \\t// to make the addition of warning headers threadsafe\\n1076: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithWarningRecorder(handler)\\n1077: \\n1078: \\t// WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests will call the rest of the request handling in a go-routine with the\\n1079: \\t// context with deadline. The go-routine can keep running, while the timeout logic will return a timeout to the client.\\n1080: \\thandler = genericfilters.WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\\n1081: \\n1082: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestDeadline(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator,\\n1083: \\t\\tc.LongRunningFunc, c.Serializer, c.RequestTimeout)\\n1084: \\thandler = genericfilters.WithWaitGroup(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup)\\n1085: \\tif c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod > 0 {\\n1086: \\t\\thandler = genericfilters.WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown(handler, c.lifecycleSignals, c.WatchRequestWaitGroup)\\n1087: \\t}\\n1088: \\tif c.SecureServing != nil && !c.SecureServing.DisableHTTP2 && c.GoawayChance > 0 {\\n1089: \\t\\thandler = genericfilters.WithProbabilisticGoaway(handler, c.GoawayChance)\\n1090: \\t}\\n1091: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithCacheControl(handler)\\n1092: \\thandler = genericfilters.WithHSTS(handler, c.HSTSDirectives)\\n1093: \\tif c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\\n1094: \\t\\thandler = genericfilters.WithRetryAfter(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest.Signaled())\\n1095: \\t}\\n1096: \\thandler = genericfilters.WithHTTPLogging(handler)\\n1097: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithLatencyTrackers(handler)\\n1098: \\t// WithRoutine will execute future handlers in a separate goroutine and serving\\n1099: \\t// handler in current goroutine to minimize the stack memory usage. It must be\\n1100: \\t// after WithPanicRecover() to be protected from panics.\\n1101: \\tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServingWithRoutine) {\\n1102: \\t\\thandler = routine.WithRoutine(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\\n1103: \\t}\\n1104: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestInfo(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\\n1105: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestReceivedTimestamp(handler)\\n1106: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signaled())\\n1107: \\thandler = genericfilters.WithPanicRecovery(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\\n1108: \\thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuditInit(handler)\\n1109: \\treturn handler\\n1110: }\\n1111: \\n1112: func installAPI(name string, s *GenericAPIServer, c *Config) {\\n1113: \\tif c.EnableIndex {\\n1114: \\t\\troutes.Index{}.Install(s.listedPathProvider, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n1115: \\t}\\n1116: \\tif c.EnableProfiling {\\n1117: \\t\\troutes.Profiling{}.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n1118: \\t\\tif c.EnableContentionProfiling {\\n1119: \\t\\t\\tgoruntime.SetBlockProfileRate(1)\\n1120: \\t\\t}\\n1121: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go%3FL1000-1120\"}",

c.Config)\n1009: \n1010: \t// use the UnprotectedHandler from the delegation target to ensure that we don't attempt to double authenticator, authorize,\n1011: \t// or some other part of the filter chain in delegation cases.\n1012: \tif delegationTarget.UnprotectedHandler() == nil && c.EnableIndex {\n1013: \t\ts.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux.NotFoundHandler(routes.IndexLister{\n1014: \t\t\tStatusCode:   http.StatusNotFound,\n1015: \t\t\tPathProvider: s.listedPathProvider,\n1016: \t\t})\n1017: \t}\n1018: \n1019: \treturn s, nil\n1020: }\n1021: \n1022: func genericapifilters.WithStorageVersionPrecondition(apiHandler, c.StorageVersionManager, c.Serializer)\n1025: \treturn DefaultBuildHandlerChain(handler, c)\n1026: }\n1027: \n1028: func DefaultBuildHandlerChain(apiHandler http.Handler, c *Config) http.Handler {\n1029: \thandler := apiHandler\n1030: \n1031: \thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n1032: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuthorization(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n1033: \thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"authorization\")\n1034: \n1035: \tif \t\t\tc.StorageObjectCountTracker.Get, c.FlowControl.GetInterestedWatchCount, workEstimatorCfg, c.FlowControl.GetMaxSeats)\n1039: \t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n1040: \t\thandler = genericfilters.WithPriorityAndFairness(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.FlowControl, requestWorkEstimator, c.RequestTimeout/4)\n1041: \t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"priorityandfairness\")\n1042: \t} else {\n1043: \t\thandler = genericfilters.WithMaxInFlightLimit(handler, c.MaxRequestsInFlight, c.MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight, c.LongRunningFunc)\n1044: \t}\n1045: \n1046: \thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n1047: \tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.ConstrainedImpersonation) {\n1048: \t\thandler = impersonation.WithConstrainedImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n1049: \t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"constrainedimpersonation\")\n1050: \t} else {\n1051: \t\thandler = impersonation.WithImpersonation(handler, c.Authorization.Authorizer, c.Serializer)\n1052: \t\thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"impersonation\")\n1053: \t}\n1054: \n1055: \thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n1056: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithAudit(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator, c.LongRunningFunc)\n1057: \thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"audit\")\n1058: \n1059: \tfailedHandler := genericapifilters.Unauthorized(c.Serializer)\n1060: \tfailedHandler = genericapifilters.WithFailedAuthenticationAudit(failedHandler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator)\n1061: \n1062: \t// WithTracing comes after authentication so we can allow authenticated\n1063: \t// clients to influence sampling.\n1064: \tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServerTracing) {\n1065: \t\thandler = genericapifilters.WithTracing(handler, c.TracerProvider)\n1066: \t}\n1067: \tfailedHandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(failedHandler)\n1068: \thandler = filterlatency.TrackCompleted(handler)\n1069: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuthentication(handler, c.Authentication.Authenticator, failedHandler, c.Authentication.APIAudiences, c.Authentication.RequestHeaderConfig)\n1070: \thandler = filterlatency.TrackStarted(handler, c.TracerProvider, \"authentication\")\n1071: \n1072: \thandler = genericfilters.WithCORS(handler, c.CorsAllowedOriginList, nil, nil, nil, \"true\")\n1073: \n1074: \t// WithWarningRecorder must be wrapped by the timeout handler\n1075: \t// to make the addition of warning headers threadsafe\n1076: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithWarningRecorder(handler)\n1077: \n1078: \t// WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests will call the rest of the request handling in a go-routine with the\n1079: \t// context with deadline. The go-routine can keep running, while the timeout logic will return a timeout to the client.\n1080: \thandler = genericfilters.WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n1081: \n1082: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestDeadline(handler, c.AuditBackend, c.AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator,\n1083: \t\tc.LongRunningFunc, c.Serializer, c.RequestTimeout)\n1084: \thandler = genericfilters.WithWaitGroup(handler, c.LongRunningFunc, c.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup)\n1085: \tif c.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod > 0 {\n1086: \t\thandler = genericfilters.WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown(handler, c.lifecycleSignals, c.WatchRequestWaitGroup)\n1087: \t}\n1088: \tif c.SecureServing != nil && !c.SecureServing.DisableHTTP2 && c.GoawayChance > 0 {\n1089: \t\thandler = genericfilters.WithProbabilisticGoaway(handler, c.GoawayChance)\n1090: \t}\n1091: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithCacheControl(handler)\n1092: \thandler = genericfilters.WithHSTS(handler, c.HSTSDirectives)\n1093: \tif c.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n1094: \t\thandler = genericfilters.WithRetryAfter(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest.Signaled())\n1095: \t}\n1096: \thandler = genericfilters.WithHTTPLogging(handler)\n1097: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithLatencyTrackers(handler)\n1098: \t// WithRoutine will execute future handlers in a separate goroutine and serving\n1099: \t// handler in current goroutine to minimize the stack memory usage. It must be\n1100: \t// after WithPanicRecover() to be protected from panics.\n1101: \tif c.FeatureGate.Enabled(genericfeatures.APIServingWithRoutine) {\n1102: \t\thandler = routine.WithRoutine(handler, c.LongRunningFunc)\n1103: \t}\n1104: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestInfo(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\n1105: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithRequestReceivedTimestamp(handler)\n1106: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete(handler, c.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signaled())\n1107: \thandler = genericfilters.WithPanicRecovery(handler, c.RequestInfoResolver)\n1108: \thandler = genericapifilters.WithAuditInit(handler)\n1109: \treturn handler\n1110: }\n1111: \n1112: func installAPI(name string, s *GenericAPIServer, c *Config) {\n1113: \tif c.EnableIndex {\n1114: \t\troutes.Index{}.Install(s.listedPathProvider, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n1115: \t}\n1116: \tif c.EnableProfiling {\n1117: \t\troutes.Profiling{}.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n1118: \t\tif c.EnableContentionProfiling {\n1119: \t\t\tgoruntime.SetBlockProfileRate(1)\n1120: \t\t}\n1121: ",

\\tDecorator     StorageDecorator\\n34: \\n35: \\tEnableGarbageCollection   bool\\n36: \\tDeleteCollectionWorkers   int\\n37: \\tResourcePrefix            string\\n38: \\tCountMetricPollPeriod     time.Duration\\n39: \\tStorageObjectCountTracker flowcontrolrequest.StorageObjectCountTracker\\n40: }\\n41: \\n42: // Implement RESTOptionsGetter so that RESTOptions can directly be used when available (i.e. tests)\\n43: func (opts RESTOptions) GetRESTOptions(schema.GroupResource, runtime.Object) (RESTOptions, error) {\\n44: \\treturn opts, nil\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: type RESTOptionsGetter resource.\\n50: \\t// If the example object is nil, the storage version will be determined by the resource's default storage version.\\n51: \\tGetRESTOptions(resource schema.GroupResource, example runtime.Object) (RESTOptions, error)\\n52: }\\n53: \\n54: // StoreOptions is set of configuration options used to complete generic registries.\\n55: type StoreOptions struct {\\n56: \\tRESTOptions // AfterDeleteFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterDelete hook.\\n63: type AfterDeleteFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.DeleteOptions)\\n64: \\n65: // BeginCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginCreate hook.\\n66: type BeginCreateFunc func(ctx context.Context, obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions) (FinishFunc, error)\\n67: \\n68: // AfterCreateFunc is the type used for the Store.AfterCreate hook.\\n69: type AfterCreateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.CreateOptions)\\n70: \\n71: // BeginUpdateFunc is the type used for the Store.BeginUpdate Store.AfterUpdate hook.\\n75: type AfterUpdateFunc func(obj runtime.Object, options *metav1.UpdateOptions)\\n76: \\n77: // GenericStore interface can be used for type assertions when we need to access the underlying strategies.\\n78: type GenericStore interface {\\n79: \\tGetCreateStrategy() rest.RESTCreateStrategy\\n80: \\tGetUpdateStrategy() rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\\n81: \\tGetDeleteStrategy() rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\\n82: }\\n83: \\n84: // Store implements k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest.StandardStorage. It's\\n85: // intended to be embeddable and allows the consumer to implement any\\n86: // non-generic functions that are required. This object is intended to be\\n87: // copyable so that it can be used in different ways but share the same\\n88: // underlying behavior.\\n89: //\\n90: // All fields are required unless specified.\\n91: //\\n92: // The intended use of this type is embedding within a Kind specific\\n93: // RESTStorage implementation. This type provides CRUD semantics on a Kubelike\\n94: // resource, handling details like conflict detection with ResourceVersion and\\n95: // semantics. The RESTCreateStrategy, RESTUpdateStrategy, and\\n96: // RESTDeleteStrategy are generic across all backends, and encapsulate logic\\n97: // specific to the API.\\n98: //\\n99: // TODO: make the default exposed methods exactly match a generic RESTStorage\\n100: type Store struct {\\n101: \\t// NewFunc returns a new instance of the type this registry returns for a\\n102: \\t// GET of a single object, e.g.:\\n103: \\t//\\n104: \\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource/name-of-object\\n105: \\tNewFunc func() runtime.Object\\n106: \\n107: \\t// NewListFunc returns a new list of the type this registry; it is the\\n108: \\t// type returned when the resource is listed, e.g.:\\n109: \\t//\\n110: \\t// curl GET /apis/group/version/namespaces/my-ns/myresource\\n111: \\tNewListFunc func() runtime.Object\\n112: \\n113: \\t// DefaultQualifiedResource is the pluralized name of the resource.\\n114: \\t// This field is used if there is no request info present in the context.\\n115: \\t// See qualifiedResourceFromContext for details.\\n116: \\tDefaultQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\\n117: \\n118: \\t// SingularQualifiedResource is the singular name of the resource.\\n119: \\tSingularQualifiedResource schema.GroupResource\\n120: \\n121: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go%3FL1-120\"}",

{
  "endLine": 700,
