Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"450: \\t\\t}.InstallV2(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n451: \\t}\\n452: \\n453: \\tif s.openAPIV3Config != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\\n454: \\t\\ts.OpenAPIV3VersionedService = routes.OpenAPI{\\n455: \\t\\t\\tV3Config: s.openAPIV3Config,\\n456: \\t\\t}.InstallV3(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\\n457: \\t}\\n458: \\n459: \\ts.installHealthz()\\n460: \\ts.installLivez()\\n461: \\n462: \\t// as soon as shutdown is initiated, readiness should start failing\\n463: \\treadinessStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\\n464: \\terr := s.addReadyzShutdownCheck(readinessStopCh)\\n465: \\tif err != nil {\\n466: \\t\\tklog.Errorf(\\\"Failed to install readyz shutdown check %s\\\", err)\\n467: \\t}\\n468: \\ts.installReadyz()\\n469: \\n470: \\tcomponentName := \\\"apiserver\\\"\\n471: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentStatusz) {\\n472: \\t\\tstatusz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, statusz.NewRegistry(s.EffectiveVersion, statusz.WithListedPaths(s.ListedPaths())))\\n473: \\t}\\n474: \\n475: \\tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentFlagz) {\\n476: \\t\\tif s.Flagz != nil {\\n477: \\t\\t\\tflagz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, s.Flagz)\\n478: \\t\\t}\\n479: \\t}\\n480: \\n481: \\treturn preparedGenericAPIServer{s}\\n482: }\\n483: \\n484: // Run spawns the secure http server. It only returns if stopCh is closed\\n485: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\\n486: //\\n487: // Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid\\n488: // breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.\\n489: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error {\\n490: \\tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\\n491: \\treturn s.RunWithContext(ctx)\\n492: }\\n493: \\n494: // RunWithContext spawns the secure http server. It only returns if ctx is canceled\\n495: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\\n496: // This is the diagram of what contexts/channels/signals are dependent on each other:\\n497: //\\n498: // |                                   ctx\\n499: // |                                    |\\n500: // |           ---------------------------------------------------------\\n501: // |           |                                                       |\\n502: // |    ShutdownInitiated (shutdownInitiatedCh)                        |\\n503: // |           |                                                       |\\n504: // | (ShutdownDelayDuration)                                    (PreShutdownHooks)\\n505: // |           |                                                       |\\n506: // |  AfterShutdownDelayDuration (delayedStopCh)   PreShutdownHooksStopped (preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh)\\n507: // |           |                                                       |\\n508: // |           |-------------------------------------------------------|\\n509: // |                                    |\\n510: // |                                    |\\n511: // |               NotAcceptingNewRequest (notAcceptingNewRequestCh)\\n512: // |                                    |\\n513: // |                                    |\\n514: // |           |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\\n515: // |           |                        |              |                                          |\\n516: // |        [without                 [with             |                                          |\\n517: // | ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  |                                          |\\n518: // |           |                        |              |                                          |\\n519: // |           |                        ---------------|                                          |\\n520: // |           |                                       |                                          |\\n521: // |           |                      |----------------|-----------------------|                  |\\n522: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |\\n523: // |           |         (NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   (WatchRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   |\\n524: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |\\n525: // |           |                      |------------------|---------------------|                  |\\n526: // |           |                                         |                                        |\\n527: // |           |                         InFlightRequestsDrained (drainedCh)                      |\\n528: // |           |                                         |                                        |\\n529: // |           |-------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------|\\n530: // |                               |                     |\\n531: // |                       stopHttpServerCtx     (AuditBackend::Shutdown())\\n532: // |                               |\\n533: // |                       listenerStoppedCh\\n534: // |                               |\\n535: // |      HTTPServerStoppedListening (httpServerStoppedListeningCh)\\n536: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) error {\\n537: \\tstopCh := ctx.Done()\\n538: \\tdelayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration\\n539: \\tshutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated\\n540: \\n541: \\t// Clean up resources on shutdown.\\n542: \\tdefer s.Destroy()\\n543: \\n544: \\t// If UDS profiling is enabled, start a local http server listening on that socket\\n545: \\tif s.UnprotectedDebugSocket != nil {\\n546: \\t\\tgo func() {\\n547: \\t\\t\\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(ctx)\\n548: \\t\\t\\tklog.Error(s.UnprotectedDebugSocket.RunWithContext(ctx))\\n549: \\t\\t}()\\n550: \\t}\\n551: \\n552: \\t// spawn a new goroutine for closing the MuxAndDiscoveryComplete signal\\n553: \\t// registration happens during construction of the generic api server\\n554: \\t// the last server in the chain aggregates signals from the previous instances\\n555: \\tgo func() {\\n556: \\t\\tfor _, muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal := range s.GenericAPIServer.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\\n557: \\t\\t\\tselect {\\n558: \\t\\t\\tcase <-muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal:\\n559: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n560: \\t\\t\\tcase <-stopCh:\\n561: \\t\\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"haven't completed %s, stop requested\\\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\\n562: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n563: \\t\\t\\t}\\n564: \\t\\t}\\n565: \\t\\ts.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signal()\\n566: \\t\\tklog.V(1).Infof(\\\"%s has all endpoints registered and discovery information is complete\\\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\\n567: \\t}()\\n568: \\n569: \\tgo func() {\\n570: \\t\\tdefer delayedStopCh.Signal()\\n571: \\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", delayedStopCh.Name())\\n572: \\n573: \\t\\t<-stopCh\\n574: \\n575: \\t\\t// As soon as shutdown is initiated, /readyz should start returning failure.\\n576: \\t\\t// This gives the load balancer a window defined by ShutdownDelayDuration to detect that /readyz is red\\n577: \\t\\t// and stop sending traffic to this server.\\n578: \\t\\tshutdownInitiatedCh.Signal()\\n579: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", shutdownInitiatedCh.Name())\\n580: \\n581: \\t\\ttime.Sleep(s.ShutdownDelayDuration)\\n582: \\t}()\\n583: \\n584: \\t// close socket after delayed stopCh\\n585: \\tshutdownTimeout := s.ShutdownTimeout\\n586: \\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\\n587: \\t\\t// when this mode is enabled, we do the following:\\n588: \\t\\t// - the server will continue to listen until all existing requests in flight\\n589: \\t\\t//   (not including active long running requests) have been drained.\\n590: \\t\\t// - once drained, http Server Shutdown is invoked with a timeout of 2s,\\n591: \\t\\t//   net/http waits for 1s for the peer to respond to a GO_AWAY frame, so\\n592: \\t\\t//   we should wait for a minimum of 2s\\n593: \\t\\tshutdownTimeout = 2 * time.Second\\n594: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] using HTTP Server shutdown timeout\\\", \\\"shutdownTimeout\\\", shutdownTimeout)\\n595: \\t}\\n596: \\n597: \\tnotAcceptingNewRequestCh := s.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest\\n598: \\tdrainedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained\\n599: \\t// Canceling the parent context does not immediately cancel the HTTP server.\\n600: \\t// We only inherit context values here and deal with cancellation ourselves.\\n601: \\tstopHTTPServerCtx, stopHTTPServer := context.WithCancelCause(context.WithoutCancel(ctx))\\n602: \\tgo func() {\\n603: \\t\\tdefer stopHTTPServer(errors.New(\\\"time to stop HTTP server\\\"))\\n604: \\n605: \\t\\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh := notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n606: \\t\\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\\n607: \\t\\t\\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh = drainedCh.Signaled()\\n608: \\t\\t}\\n609: \\n610: \\t\\t<-timeToStopHttpServerCh\\n611: \\t}()\\n612: \\n613: \\t// Start the audit backend before any request comes in. This means we must call Backend.Run\\n614: \\t// before http server start serving. Otherwise the Backend.ProcessEvents call might block.\\n615: \\t// AuditBackend.Run will stop as soon as all in-flight requests are drained.\\n616: \\tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\\n617: \\t\\tif err := s.AuditBackend.Run(drainedCh.Signaled()); err != nil {\\n618: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to run the audit backend: %v\\\", err)\\n619: \\t\\t}\\n620: \\t}\\n621: \\n622: \\tstoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err := s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(stopHTTPServerCtx, shutdownTimeout)\\n623: \\tif err != nil {\\n624: \\t\\treturn err\\n625: \\t}\\n626: \\n627: \\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh := s.lifecycleSignals.HTTPServerStoppedListening\\n628: \\tgo func() {\\n629: \\t\\t<-listenerStoppedCh\\n630: \\t\\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh.Signal()\\n631: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Name())\\n632: \\t}()\\n633: \\n634: \\t// we don't accept new request as soon as both ShutdownDelayDuration has\\n635: \\t// elapsed and preshutdown hooks have completed.\\n636: \\tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.PreShutdownHooksStopped\\n637: \\tgo func() {\\n638: \\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Name())\\n639: \\t\\tdefer notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signal()\\n640: \\n641: \\t\\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain\\n642: \\t\\t<-delayedStopCh.Signaled()\\n643: \\n644: \\t\\t// Additionally wait for preshutdown hooks to also be finished, as some of them need\\n645: \\t\\t// to send API calls to clean up after themselves (e.g. lease reconcilers removing\\n646: \\t\\t// itself from the active servers).\\n647: \\t\\t<-preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signaled()\\n648: \\t}()\\n649: \\n650: \\t// wait for all in-flight non-long running requests to finish\\n651: \\tnonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\\n652: \\tgo func() {\\n653: \\t\\tdefer close(nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh)\\n654: \\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).Info(\\\"[graceful-termination] in-flight non long-running request(s) have drained\\\")\\n655: \\n656: \\t\\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain (it rejects everything after Wait has been called).\\n657: \\t\\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n658: \\n659: \\t\\t// Wait for all requests to finish, which are bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\\n660: \\t\\t// once NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait is invoked, the apiserver is\\n661: \\t\\t// expected to reject any incoming request with a {503, Retry-After}\\n662: \\t\\t// response via the WithWaitGroup filter. On the contrary, we observe\\n663: \\t\\t// that incoming request(s) get a 'connection refused' error, this is\\n664: \\t\\t// because, at this point, we have called 'Server.Shutdown' and\\n665: \\t\\t// net/http server has stopped listening. This causes incoming\\n666: \\t\\t// request to get a 'connection refused' error.\\n667: \\t\\t// On the other hand, if 'ShutdownSendRetryAfter' is enabled incoming\\n668: \\t\\t// requests will be rejected with a {429, Retry-After} since\\n669: \\t\\t// 'Server.Shutdown' will be invoked only after in-flight requests\\n670: \\t\\t// have been drained.\\n671: \\t\\t// TODO: can we consolidate these two modes of graceful termination?\\n672: \\t\\ts.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait()\\n673: \\t}()\\n674: \\n675: \\t// wait for all in-flight watches to finish\\n676: \\tactiveWatchesDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\\n677: \\tgo func() {\\n678: \\t\\tdefer close(activeWatchesDrainedCh)\\n679: \\n680: \\t\\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\\n681: \\t\\tif s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod <= time.Duration(0) {\\n682: \\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] not going to wait for active watch request(s) to drain\\\")\\n683: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n684: \\t\\t}\\n685: \\n686: \\t\\t// Wait for all active watches to finish\\n687: \\t\\tgrace := s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod\\n688: \\t\\tactiveBefore, activeAfter, err := s.WatchRequestWaitGroup.Wait(func(count int) (utilwaitgroup.RateLimiter, context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\\n689: \\t\\t\\tqps := float64(count) / grace.Seconds()\\n690: \\t\\t\\t// TODO: we don't want the QPS (max requests drained per second) to\\n691: \\t\\t\\t//  get below a certain floor value, since we want the server to\\n692: \\t\\t\\t//  drain the active watch requests as soon as possible.\\n693: \\t\\t\\t//  For now, it's hard coded to 200, and it is subject to change\\n694: \\t\\t\\t//  based on the result from the scale testing.\\n695: \\t\\t\\tif qps < 200 {\\n696: \\t\\t\\t\\tqps = 200\\n697: \\t\\t\\t}\\n698: \\n699: \\t\\t\\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), grace)\\n700: \\t\\t\\t// We don't expect more than one token to be consumed\\n701:     "content": "450: \t\t}.InstallV2(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n451: \t}\n452: \n453: \tif s.openAPIV3Config != nil && !s.skipOpenAPIInstallation {\n454: \t\ts.OpenAPIV3VersionedService = routes.OpenAPI{\n455: \t\t\tV3Config: s.openAPIV3Config,\n456: \t\t}.InstallV3(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer, s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux)\n457: \t}\n458: \n459: \ts.installHealthz()\n460: \ts.installLivez()\n461: \n462: \t// as soon as shutdown is initiated, readiness should start failing\n463: \treadinessStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated.Signaled()\n464: \terr := s.addReadyzShutdownCheck(readinessStopCh)\n465: \tif err != nil {\n466: \t\tklog.Errorf(\"Failed to install readyz shutdown check %s\", err)\n467: \t}\n468: \ts.installReadyz()\n469: \n470: \tcomponentName := \"apiserver\"\n471: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentStatusz) {\n472: \t\tstatusz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, statusz.NewRegistry(s.EffectiveVersion, statusz.WithListedPaths(s.ListedPaths())))\n473: \t}\n474: \n475: \tif utilfeature.DefaultFeatureGate.Enabled(zpagesfeatures.ComponentFlagz) {\n476: \t\tif s.Flagz != nil {\n477: \t\t\tflagz.Install(s.Handler.NonGoRestfulMux, componentName, s.Flagz)\n478: \t\t}\n479: \t}\n480: \n481: \treturn preparedGenericAPIServer{s}\n482: }\n483: \n484: // Run spawns the secure http server. It only returns if stopCh is closed\n485: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\n486: //\n487: // Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid\n488: // breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.\n489: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) Run(stopCh <-chan struct{}) error {\n490: \tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\n491: \treturn s.RunWithContext(ctx)\n492: }\n493: \n494: // RunWithContext spawns the secure http server. It only returns if ctx is canceled\n495: // or the secure port cannot be listened on initially.\n496: // This is the diagram of what contexts/channels/signals are dependent on each other:\n497: //\n498: // |                                   ctx\n499: // |                                    |\n500: // |           ---------------------------------------------------------\n501: // |           |                                                       |\n502: // |    ShutdownInitiated (shutdownInitiatedCh)                        |\n503: // |           |                                                       |\n504: // | (ShutdownDelayDuration)                                    (PreShutdownHooks)\n505: // |           |                                                       |\n506: // |  AfterShutdownDelayDuration (delayedStopCh)   PreShutdownHooksStopped (preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh)\n507: // |           |                                                       |\n508: // |           |-------------------------------------------------------|\n509: // |                                    |\n510: // |                                    |\n511: // |               NotAcceptingNewRequest (notAcceptingNewRequestCh)\n512: // |                                    |\n513: // |                                    |\n514: // |           |----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|\n515: // |           |                        |              |                                          |\n516: // |        [without                 [with             |                                          |\n517: // | ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  ShutdownSendRetryAfter]  |                                          |\n518: // |           |                        |              |                                          |\n519: // |           |                        ---------------|                                          |\n520: // |           |                                       |                                          |\n521: // |           |                      |----------------|-----------------------|                  |\n522: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |\n523: // |           |         (NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   (WatchRequestWaitGroup::Wait)   |\n524: // |           |                      |                                        |                  |\n525: // |           |                      |------------------|---------------------|                  |\n526: // |           |                                         |                                        |\n527: // |           |                         InFlightRequestsDrained (drainedCh)                      |\n528: // |           |                                         |                                        |\n529: // |           |-------------------|---------------------|----------------------------------------|\n530: // |                               |                     |\n531: // |                       stopHttpServerCtx     (AuditBackend::Shutdown())\n532: // |                               |\n533: // |                       listenerStoppedCh\n534: // |                               |\n535: // |      HTTPServerStoppedListening (httpServerStoppedListeningCh)\n536: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) RunWithContext(ctx context.Context) error {\n537: \tstopCh := ctx.Done()\n538: \tdelayedStopCh := s.lifecycleSignals.AfterShutdownDelayDuration\n539: \tshutdownInitiatedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.ShutdownInitiated\n540: \n541: \t// Clean up resources on shutdown.\n542: \tdefer s.Destroy()\n543: \n544: \t// If UDS profiling is enabled, start a local http server listening on that socket\n545: \tif s.UnprotectedDebugSocket != nil {\n546: \t\tgo func() {\n547: \t\t\tdefer utilruntime.HandleCrashWithContext(ctx)\n548: \t\t\tklog.Error(s.UnprotectedDebugSocket.RunWithContext(ctx))\n549: \t\t}()\n550: \t}\n551: \n552: \t// spawn a new goroutine for closing the MuxAndDiscoveryComplete signal\n553: \t// registration happens during construction of the generic api server\n554: \t// the last server in the chain aggregates signals from the previous instances\n555: \tgo func() {\n556: \t\tfor _, muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal := range s.GenericAPIServer.MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() {\n557: \t\t\tselect {\n558: \t\t\tcase <-muxAndDiscoveryCompletedSignal:\n559: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n560: \t\t\tcase <-stopCh:\n561: \t\t\t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"haven't completed %s, stop requested\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\n562: \t\t\t\treturn\n563: \t\t\t}\n564: \t\t}\n565: \t\ts.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Signal()\n566: \t\tklog.V(1).Infof(\"%s has all endpoints registered and discovery information is complete\", s.lifecycleSignals.MuxAndDiscoveryComplete.Name())\n567: \t}()\n568: \n569: \tgo func() {\n570: \t\tdefer delayedStopCh.Signal()\n571: \t\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", delayedStopCh.Name())\n572: \n573: \t\t<-stopCh\n574: \n575: \t\t// As soon as shutdown is initiated, /readyz should start returning failure.\n576: \t\t// This gives the load balancer a window defined by ShutdownDelayDuration to detect that /readyz is red\n577: \t\t// and stop sending traffic to this server.\n578: \t\tshutdownInitiatedCh.Signal()\n579: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", shutdownInitiatedCh.Name())\n580: \n581: \t\ttime.Sleep(s.ShutdownDelayDuration)\n582: \t}()\n583: \n584: \t// close socket after delayed stopCh\n585: \tshutdownTimeout := s.ShutdownTimeout\n586: \tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n587: \t\t// when this mode is enabled, we do the following:\n588: \t\t// - the server will continue to listen until all existing requests in flight\n589: \t\t//   (not including active long running requests) have been drained.\n590: \t\t// - once drained, http Server Shutdown is invoked with a timeout of 2s,\n591: \t\t//   net/http waits for 1s for the peer to respond to a GO_AWAY frame, so\n592: \t\t//   we should wait for a minimum of 2s\n593: \t\tshutdownTimeout = 2 * time.Second\n594: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] using HTTP Server shutdown timeout\", \"shutdownTimeout\", shutdownTimeout)\n595: \t}\n596: \n597: \tnotAcceptingNewRequestCh := s.lifecycleSignals.NotAcceptingNewRequest\n598: \tdrainedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.InFlightRequestsDrained\n599: \t// Canceling the parent context does not immediately cancel the HTTP server.\n600: \t// We only inherit context values here and deal with cancellation ourselves.\n601: \tstopHTTPServerCtx, stopHTTPServer := context.WithCancelCause(context.WithoutCancel(ctx))\n602: \tgo func() {\n603: \t\tdefer stopHTTPServer(errors.New(\"time to stop HTTP server\"))\n604: \n605: \t\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh := notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\n606: \t\tif s.ShutdownSendRetryAfter {\n607: \t\t\ttimeToStopHttpServerCh = drainedCh.Signaled()\n608: \t\t}\n609: \n610: \t\t<-timeToStopHttpServerCh\n611: \t}()\n612: \n613: \t// Start the audit backend before any request comes in. This means we must call Backend.Run\n614: \t// before http server start serving. Otherwise the Backend.ProcessEvents call might block.\n615: \t// AuditBackend.Run will stop as soon as all in-flight requests are drained.\n616: \tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\n617: \t\tif err := s.AuditBackend.Run(drainedCh.Signaled()); err != nil {\n618: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to run the audit backend: %v\", err)\n619: \t\t}\n620: \t}\n621: \n622: \tstoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err := s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(stopHTTPServerCtx, shutdownTimeout)\n623: \tif err != nil {\n624: \t\treturn err\n625: \t}\n626: \n627: \thttpServerStoppedListeningCh := s.lifecycleSignals.HTTPServerStoppedListening\n628: \tgo func() {\n629: \t\t<-listenerStoppedCh\n630: \t\thttpServerStoppedListeningCh.Signal()\n631: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", httpServerStoppedListeningCh.Name())\n632: \t}()\n633: \n634: \t// we don't accept new request as soon as both ShutdownDelayDuration has\n635: \t// elapsed and preshutdown hooks have completed.\n636: \tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh := s.lifecycleSignals.PreShutdownHooksStopped\n637: \tgo func() {\n638: \t\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Name())\n639: \t\tdefer notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signal()\n640: \n641: \t\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain\n642: \t\t<-delayedStopCh.Signaled()\n643: \n644: \t\t// Additionally wait for preshutdown hooks to also be finished, as some of them need\n645: \t\t// to send API calls to clean up after themselves (e.g. lease reconcilers removing\n646: \t\t// itself from the active servers).\n647: \t\t<-preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signaled()\n648: \t}()\n649: \n650: \t// wait for all in-flight non-long running requests to finish\n651: \tnonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\n652: \tgo func() {\n653: \t\tdefer close(nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh)\n654: \t\tdefer klog.V(1).Info(\"[graceful-termination] in-flight non long-running request(s) have drained\")\n655: \n656: \t\t// wait for the delayed stopCh before closing the handler chain (it rejects everything after Wait has been called).\n657: \t\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\n658: \n659: \t\t// Wait for all requests to finish, which are bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\n660: \t\t// once NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait is invoked, the apiserver is\n661: \t\t// expected to reject any incoming request with a {503, Retry-After}\n662: \t\t// response via the WithWaitGroup filter. On the contrary, we observe\n663: \t\t// that incoming request(s) get a 'connection refused' error, this is\n664: \t\t// because, at this point, we have called 'Server.Shutdown' and\n665: \t\t// net/http server has stopped listening. This causes incoming\n666: \t\t// request to get a 'connection refused' error.\n667: \t\t// On the other hand, if 'ShutdownSendRetryAfter' is enabled incoming\n668: \t\t// requests will be rejected with a {429, Retry-After} since\n669: \t\t// 'Server.Shutdown' will be invoked only after in-flight requests\n670: \t\t// have been drained.\n671: \t\t// TODO: can we consolidate these two modes of graceful termination?\n672: \t\ts.NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup.Wait()\n673: \t}()\n674: \n675: \t// wait for all in-flight watches to finish\n676: \tactiveWatchesDrainedCh := make(chan struct{})\n677: \tgo func() {\n678: \t\tdefer close(activeWatchesDrainedCh)\n679: \n680: \t\t<-notAcceptingNewRequestCh.Signaled()\n681: \t\tif s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod <= time.Duration(0) {\n682: \t\t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] not going to wait for active watch request(s) to drain\")\n683: \t\t\treturn\n684: \t\t}\n685: \n686: \t\t// Wait for all active watches to finish\n687: \t\tgrace := s.ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod\n688: \t\tactiveBefore, activeAfter, err := s.WatchRequestWaitGroup.Wait(func(count int) (utilwaitgroup.RateLimiter, context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\n689: \t\t\tqps := float64(count) / grace.Seconds()\n690: \t\t\t// TODO: we don't want the QPS (max requests drained per second) to\n691: \t\t\t//  get below a certain floor value, since we want the server to\n692: \t\t\t//  drain the active watch requests as soon as possible.\n693: \t\t\t//  For now, it's hard coded to 200, and it is subject to change\n694: \t\t\t//  based on the result from the scale testing.\n695: \t\t\tif qps < 200 {\n696: \t\t\t\tqps = 200\n697: \t\t\t}\n698: \n699: \t\t\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), grace)\n700: \t\t\t// We don't expect more than one token to be consumed\n701: ",

Output

  "content": "{\"content\":\"120: \\n121: \\t// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not\\n122: \\t// include trailing \\\"/\\\".  This is used for operations that work on the\\n123: \\t// entire collection (listing and watching).\\n124: \\t//\\n125: \\t// KeyRootFunc and KeyFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\\n126: \\tKeyRootFunc func(ctx context.Context) string\\n127: \\n128: \\t// KeyFunc returns the key for a specific object in the collection.\\n129: \\t// KeyFunc is called for Create/Update/Get/Delete. Note that 'namespace'\\n130: \\t// can be gotten from ctx.\\n131: \\t//\\n132: \\t// KeyFunc and KeyRootFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\\n133: \\tKeyFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)\\n134: \\n135: \\t// ObjectNameFunc returns the name of an object or an error.\\n136: \\tObjectNameFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error)\\n137: \\n138: \\t// TTLFunc returns the TTL (time to live) that objects should be persisted\\n139: \\t// with. The existing parameter is the current TTL or the default for this\\n140: \\t// operation. The update parameter indicates whether this is an operation\\n141: \\t// against an existing object.\\n142: \\t//\\n143: \\t// Objects that are persisted with a TTL are evicted once the TTL expires.\\n144: \\tTTLFunc func(obj runtime.Object, existing uint64, update bool) (uint64, error)\\n145: \\n146: \\t// PredicateFunc returns a matcher corresponding to the provided labels\\n147: \\t// and fields. The SelectionPredicate returned should return true if the\\n148: \\t// object matches the given field and label selectors.\\n149: \\tPredicateFunc func(label labels.Selector, field fields.Selector) storage.SelectionPredicate\\n150: \\n151: \\t// EnableGarbageCollection affects the handling of Update and Delete\\n152: \\t// requests. Enabling garbage collection allows finalizers to do work to\\n153: \\t// finalize this object before the store deletes it.\\n154: \\t//\\n155: \\t// If any store has garbage collection enabled, it must also be enabled in\\n156: \\t// the kube-controller-manager.\\n157: \\tEnableGarbageCollection bool\\n158: \\n159: \\t// DeleteCollectionWorkers is the maximum number of workers in a single\\n160: \\t// DeleteCollection call. Delete requests for the items in a collection\\n161: \\t// are issued in parallel.\\n162: \\tDeleteCollectionWorkers int\\n163: \\n164: \\t// Decorator is an optional exit hook on an object returned from the\\n165: \\t// underlying storage. The returned object could be an individual object\\n166: \\t// (e.g. Pod) or a list type (e.g. PodList). Decorator is intended for\\n167: \\t// integrations that are above storage and should only be used for\\n168: \\t// specific cases where storage of the value is not appropriate, since\\n169: \\t// they cannot be watched.\\n170: \\tDecorator func(runtime.Object)\\n171: \\n172: \\t// CreateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during creation.\\n173: \\tCreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy\\n174: \\t// BeginCreate is an optional hook that returns a \\\"transaction-like\\\"\\n175: \\t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\\n176: \\t// but before AfterCreate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\\n177: \\t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\\n178: \\t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\\n179: \\tBeginCreate BeginCreateFunc\\n180: \\t// AfterCreate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n181: \\t// created and before it is decorated, optional.\\n182: \\tAfterCreate AfterCreateFunc\\n183: \\n184: \\t// UpdateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during updates.\\n185: \\tUpdateStrategy rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\\n186: \\t// BeginUpdate is an optional hook that returns a \\\"transaction-like\\\"\\n187: \\t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\\n188: \\t// but before AfterUpdate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\\n189: \\t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\\n190: \\t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\\n191: \\tBeginUpdate BeginUpdateFunc\\n192: \\t// AfterUpdate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n193: \\t// updated and before it is decorated, optional.\\n194: \\tAfterUpdate AfterUpdateFunc\\n195: \\n196: \\t// DeleteStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during deletion.\\n197: \\tDeleteStrategy rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\\n198: \\t// AfterDelete implements a further operation to run after a resource is\\n199: \\t// deleted and before it is decorated, optional.\\n200: \\tAfterDelete AfterDeleteFunc\\n201: \\t// ReturnDeletedObject determines whether the Store returns the object\\n202: \\t// that was deleted. Otherwise, return a generic success status response.\\n203: \\tReturnDeletedObject bool\\n204: \\t// ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate is an optional function to determine whether\\n205: \\t// an update from existing to obj should result in a delete.\\n206: \\t// If specified, this is checked in addition to standard finalizer,\\n207: \\t// deletionTimestamp, and deletionGracePeriodSeconds checks.\\n208: \\tShouldDeleteDuringUpdate func(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, existing runtime.Object) bool\\n209: \\n210: \\t// TableConvertor is an optional interface for transforming items or lists\\n211: \\t// of items into tabular output. If unset, the default will be used.\\n212: \\tTableConvertor rest.TableConvertor\\n213: \\n214: \\t// ResetFieldsStrategy provides the fields reset by the strategy that\\n215: \\t// should not be modified by the user.\\n216: \\tResetFieldsStrategy rest.ResetFieldsStrategy\\n217: \\n218: \\t// Storage is the interface for the underlying storage for the\\n219: \\t// resource. It is wrapped into a \\\"DryRunnableStorage\\\" that will\\n220: \\t// either pass-through or simply dry-run.\\n221: \\tStorage DryRunnableStorage\\n222: \\t// StorageVersioner outputs the <group/version/kind> an object will be\\n223: \\t// converted to before persisted in etcd, given a list of possible\\n224: \\t// kinds of the object.\\n225: \\t// If the StorageVersioner is nil, apiserver will leave the\\n226: \\t// storageVersionHash as empty in the discovery document.\\n227: \\tStorageVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner\\n228: \\n229: \\t// ReadinessCheckFunc checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\\n230: \\t// The field is optional, if set needs to be thread-safe.\\n231: \\tReadinessCheckFunc func() error\\n232: \\n233: \\t// DestroyFunc cleans up clients used by the underlying Storage; optional.\\n234: \\t// If set, DestroyFunc has to be implemented in thread-safe way and\\n235: \\t// be prepared for being called more than once.\\n236: \\tDestroyFunc func()\\n237: \\n238: \\t// corruptObjDeleter implements unsafe deletion flow to enable deletion\\n239: \\t// of corrupt object(s), it makes an attempt to perform a normal\\n240: \\t// deletion flow first, and if the normal deletion flow fails with a\\n241: \\t// corrupt object error then it proceeds with the unsafe deletion\\n242: \\t// of the object from the storage.\\n243: \\t// NOTE: it skips precondition checks, finalizer constraints, and any\\n244: \\t// after delete hook defined in 'AfterDelete' of the registry.\\n245: \\t// WARNING: This may break the cluster if the resource has\\n246: \\t// dependencies. Use when the cluster is broken, and there is no\\n247: \\t// other viable option to repair the cluster.\\n248: \\tcorruptObjDeleter rest.GracefulDeleter\\n249: }\\n250: \\n251: // Note: the rest.StandardStorage interface aggregates the common REST verbs\\n252: var _ rest.StandardStorage = &Store{}\\n253: var _ rest.StorageWithReadiness = &Store{}\\n254: var _ rest.TableConvertor = &Store{}\\n255: var _ GenericStore = &Store{}\\n256: \\n257: var _ rest.SingularNameProvider = &Store{}\\n258: \\n259: var _ rest.CorruptObjectDeleterProvider = &Store{}\\n260: \\n261: const (\\n262: \\tOptimisticLockErrorMsg        = \\\"the object has been modified; please apply your changes to the latest version and try again\\\"\\n263: \\tresourceCountPollPeriodJitter = 1.2\\n264: )\\n265: \\n266: // NamespaceKeyRootFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths\\n267: // to resource directories enforcing namespace rules.\\n268: func NamespaceKeyRootFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string) string {\\n269: \\tkey := prefix\\n270: \\tns, ok := genericapirequest.NamespaceFrom(ctx)\\n271: \\tif ok && len(ns) > 0 {\\n272: \\t\\tkey = key + \\\"/\\\" + ns\\n273: \\t}\\n274: \\treturn key\\n275: }\\n276: \\n277: // NamespaceKeyFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths to\\n278: // a resource relative to the given prefix enforcing namespace rules. If the\\n279: // context does not contain a namespace, it errors.\\n280: func NamespaceKeyFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string, name string) (string, error) {\\n281: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go%3FL120-280\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "120: \n121: \t// KeyRootFunc returns the root etcd key for this resource; should not\n122: \t// include trailing \"/\".  This is used for operations that work on the\n123: \t// entire collection (listing and watching).\n124: \t//\n125: \t// KeyRootFunc and KeyFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\n126: \tKeyRootFunc func(ctx context.Context) string\n127: \n128: \t// KeyFunc returns the key for a specific object in the collection.\n129: \t// KeyFunc is called for Create/Update/Get/Delete. Note that 'namespace'\n130: \t// can be gotten from ctx.\n131: \t//\n132: \t// KeyFunc and KeyRootFunc must be supplied together or not at all.\n133: \tKeyFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string) (string, error)\n134: \n135: \t// ObjectNameFunc returns the name of an object or an error.\n136: \tObjectNameFunc func(obj runtime.Object) (string, error)\n137: \n138: \t// TTLFunc returns the TTL (time to live) that objects should be persisted\n139: \t// with. The existing parameter is the current TTL or the default for this\n140: \t// operation. The update parameter indicates whether this is an operation\n141: \t// against an existing object.\n142: \t//\n143: \t// Objects that are persisted with a TTL are evicted once the TTL expires.\n144: \tTTLFunc func(obj runtime.Object, existing uint64, update bool) (uint64, error)\n145: \n146: \t// PredicateFunc returns a matcher corresponding to the provided labels\n147: \t// and fields. The SelectionPredicate returned should return true if the\n148: \t// object matches the given field and label selectors.\n149: \tPredicateFunc func(label labels.Selector, field fields.Selector) storage.SelectionPredicate\n150: \n151: \t// EnableGarbageCollection affects the handling of Update and Delete\n152: \t// requests. Enabling garbage collection allows finalizers to do work to\n153: \t// finalize this object before the store deletes it.\n154: \t//\n155: \t// If any store has garbage collection enabled, it must also be enabled in\n156: \t// the kube-controller-manager.\n157: \tEnableGarbageCollection bool\n158: \n159: \t// DeleteCollectionWorkers is the maximum number of workers in a single\n160: \t// DeleteCollection call. Delete requests for the items in a collection\n161: \t// are issued in parallel.\n162: \tDeleteCollectionWorkers int\n163: \n164: \t// Decorator is an optional exit hook on an object returned from the\n165: \t// underlying storage. The returned object could be an individual object\n166: \t// (e.g. Pod) or a list type (e.g. PodList). Decorator is intended for\n167: \t// integrations that are above storage and should only be used for\n168: \t// specific cases where storage of the value is not appropriate, since\n169: \t// they cannot be watched.\n170: \tDecorator func(runtime.Object)\n171: \n172: \t// CreateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during creation.\n173: \tCreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy\n174: \t// BeginCreate is an optional hook that returns a \"transaction-like\"\n175: \t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\n176: \t// but before AfterCreate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\n177: \t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\n178: \t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\n179: \tBeginCreate BeginCreateFunc\n180: \t// AfterCreate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\n181: \t// created and before it is decorated, optional.\n182: \tAfterCreate AfterCreateFunc\n183: \n184: \t// UpdateStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during updates.\n185: \tUpdateStrategy rest.RESTUpdateStrategy\n186: \t// BeginUpdate is an optional hook that returns a \"transaction-like\"\n187: \t// commit/revert function which will be called at the end of the operation,\n188: \t// but before AfterUpdate and Decorator, indicating via the argument\n189: \t// whether the operation succeeded.  If this returns an error, the function\n190: \t// is not called.  Almost nobody should use this hook.\n191: \tBeginUpdate BeginUpdateFunc\n192: \t// AfterUpdate implements a further operation to run after a resource is\n193: \t// updated and before it is decorated, optional.\n194: \tAfterUpdate AfterUpdateFunc\n195: \n196: \t// DeleteStrategy implements resource-specific behavior during deletion.\n197: \tDeleteStrategy rest.RESTDeleteStrategy\n198: \t// AfterDelete implements a further operation to run after a resource is\n199: \t// deleted and before it is decorated, optional.\n200: \tAfterDelete AfterDeleteFunc\n201: \t// ReturnDeletedObject determines whether the Store returns the object\n202: \t// that was deleted. Otherwise, return a generic success status response.\n203: \tReturnDeletedObject bool\n204: \t// ShouldDeleteDuringUpdate is an optional function to determine whether\n205: \t// an update from existing to obj should result in a delete.\n206: \t// If specified, this is checked in addition to standard finalizer,\n207: \t// deletionTimestamp, and deletionGracePeriodSeconds checks.\n208: \tShouldDeleteDuringUpdate func(ctx context.Context, key string, obj, existing runtime.Object) bool\n209: \n210: \t// TableConvertor is an optional interface for transforming items or lists\n211: \t// of items into tabular output. If unset, the default will be used.\n212: \tTableConvertor rest.TableConvertor\n213: \n214: \t// ResetFieldsStrategy provides the fields reset by the strategy that\n215: \t// should not be modified by the user.\n216: \tResetFieldsStrategy rest.ResetFieldsStrategy\n217: \n218: \t// Storage is the interface for the underlying storage for the\n219: \t// resource. It is wrapped into a \"DryRunnableStorage\" that will\n220: \t// either pass-through or simply dry-run.\n221: \tStorage DryRunnableStorage\n222: \t// StorageVersioner outputs the <group/version/kind> an object will be\n223: \t// converted to before persisted in etcd, given a list of possible\n224: \t// kinds of the object.\n225: \t// If the StorageVersioner is nil, apiserver will leave the\n226: \t// storageVersionHash as empty in the discovery document.\n227: \tStorageVersioner runtime.GroupVersioner\n228: \n229: \t// ReadinessCheckFunc checks if the storage is ready for accepting requests.\n230: \t// The field is optional, if set needs to be thread-safe.\n231: \tReadinessCheckFunc func() error\n232: \n233: \t// DestroyFunc cleans up clients used by the underlying Storage; optional.\n234: \t// If set, DestroyFunc has to be implemented in thread-safe way and\n235: \t// be prepared for being called more than once.\n236: \tDestroyFunc func()\n237: \n238: \t// corruptObjDeleter implements unsafe deletion flow to enable deletion\n239: \t// of corrupt object(s), it makes an attempt to perform a normal\n240: \t// deletion flow first, and if the normal deletion flow fails with a\n241: \t// corrupt object error then it proceeds with the unsafe deletion\n242: \t// of the object from the storage.\n243: \t// NOTE: it skips precondition checks, finalizer constraints, and any\n244: \t// after delete hook defined in 'AfterDelete' of the registry.\n245: \t// WARNING: This may break the cluster if the resource has\n246: \t// dependencies. Use when the cluster is broken, and there is no\n247: \t// other viable option to repair the cluster.\n248: \tcorruptObjDeleter rest.GracefulDeleter\n249: }\n250: \n251: // Note: the rest.StandardStorage interface aggregates the common REST verbs\n252: var _ rest.StandardStorage = &Store{}\n253: var _ rest.StorageWithReadiness = &Store{}\n254: var _ rest.TableConvertor = &Store{}\n255: var _ GenericStore = &Store{}\n256: \n257: var _ rest.SingularNameProvider = &Store{}\n258: \n259: var _ rest.CorruptObjectDeleterProvider = &Store{}\n260: \n261: const (\n262: \tOptimisticLockErrorMsg        = \"the object has been modified; please apply your changes to the latest version and try again\"\n263: \tresourceCountPollPeriodJitter = 1.2\n264: )\n265: \n266: // NamespaceKeyRootFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths\n267: // to resource directories enforcing namespace rules.\n268: func NamespaceKeyRootFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string) string {\n269: \tkey := prefix\n270: \tns, ok := genericapirequest.NamespaceFrom(ctx)\n271: \tif ok && len(ns) > 0 {\n272: \t\tkey = key + \"/\" + ns\n273: \t}\n274: \treturn key\n275: }\n276: \n277: // NamespaceKeyFunc is the default function for constructing storage paths to\n278: // a resource relative to the given prefix enforcing namespace rules. If the\n279: // context does not contain a namespace, it errors.\n280: func NamespaceKeyFunc(ctx context.Context, prefix string, name string) (string, error) {\n281: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"700: \\t\\t\\t// We don't expect more than one token to be consumed\\n701: \\t\\t\\t// in a single Wait call, so setting burst to 1.\\n702: \\t\\t\\treturn rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(qps), 1), ctx, cancel\\n703: \\t\\t})\\n704: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] active watch request(s) have drained\\\",\\n705: \\t\\t\\t\\\"duration\\\", grace, \\\"activeWatchesBefore\\\", activeBefore, \\\"activeWatchesAfter\\\", activeAfter, \\\"error\\\", err)\\n706: \\t}()\\n707: \\n708: \\tgo func() {\\n709: \\t\\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\\\", \\\"name\\\", drainedCh.Name())\\n710: \\t\\tdefer drainedCh.Signal()\\n711: \\n712: \\t\\t<-nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh\\n713: \\t\\t<-activeWatchesDrainedCh\\n714: \\t}()\\n715: \\n716: \\tklog.V(1).Info(\\\"[graceful-termination] waiting for shutdown to be initiated\\\")\\n717: \\t<-stopCh\\n718: \\n719: \\t// run shutdown hooks directly. This includes deregistering from\\n720: \\t// the kubernetes endpoint in case of kube-apiserver.\\n721: \\tfunc() {\\n722: \\t\\tdefer func() {\\n723: \\t\\t\\tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signal()\\n724: \\t\\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] pre-shutdown hooks completed\\\", \\\"name\\\", preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Name())\\n725: \\t\\t}()\\n726: \\t\\terr = s.RunPreShutdownHooks()\\n727: \\t}()\\n728: \\tif err != nil {\\n729: \\t\\treturn err\\n730: \\t}\\n731: \\n732: \\t// Wait for all requests in flight to drain, bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\\n733: \\t<-drainedCh.Signaled()\\n734: \\n735: \\tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\\n736: \\t\\ts.AuditBackend.Shutdown()\\n737: \\t\\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\\\"[graceful-termination] audit backend shutdown completed\\\")\\n738: \\t}\\n739: \\n740: \\t// wait for stoppedCh that is closed when the graceful termination (server.Shutdown) is finished.\\n741: \\t<-listenerStoppedCh\\n742: \\t<-stoppedCh\\n743: \\n744: \\tklog.V(1).Info(\\\"[graceful-termination] apiserver is exiting\\\")\\n745: \\treturn nil\\n746: }\\n747: \\n748: // NonBlockingRun spawns the secure http server. An error is\\n749: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.\\n750: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.\\n751: //\\n752: // Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid\\n753: // breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.\\n754: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) NonBlockingRun(stopCh <-chan struct{}, shutdownTimeout time.Duration) (<-chan struct{}, <-chan struct{}, error) {\\n755: \\tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\\n756: \\treturn s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(ctx, shutdownTimeout)\\n757: }\\n758: \\n759: // NonBlockingRunWithContext spawns the secure http server. An error is\\n760: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.\\n761: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.\\n762: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) NonBlockingRunWithContext(ctx context.Context, shutdownTimeout time.Duration) (<-chan struct{}, <-chan struct{}, error) {\\n763: \\t// Use an internal stop channel to allow cleanup of the listeners on error.\\n764: \\tinternalStopCh := make(chan struct{})\\n765: \\tvar stoppedCh <-chan struct{}\\n766: \\tvar listenerStoppedCh <-chan struct{}\\n767: \\tif s.SecureServingInfo != nil && s.Handler != nil {\\n768: \\t\\tvar err error\\n769: \\t\\tstoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err = s.SecureServingInfo.Serve(s.Handler, shutdownTimeout, internalStopCh)\\n770: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n771: \\t\\t\\tclose(internalStopCh)\\n772: \\t\\t\\treturn nil, nil, err\\n773: \\t\\t}\\n774: \\t}\\n775: \\n776: \\t// Now that listener have bound successfully, it is the\\n777: \\t// responsibility of the caller to close the provided channel to\\n778: \\t// ensure cleanup.\\n779: \\tgo func() {\\n780: \\t\\t<-ctx.Done()\\n781: \\t\\tclose(internalStopCh)\\n782: \\t}()\\n783: \\n784: \\ts.RunPostStartHooks(ctx)\\n785: \\n786: \\tif _, err := systemd.SdNotify(true, \\\"READY=1\\\\n\\\"); err != nil {\\n787: \\t\\tklog.Errorf(\\\"Unable to send systemd daemon successful start message: %v\\\\n\\\", err)\\n788: \\t}\\n789: \\n790: \\treturn stoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, nil\\n791: }\\n792: \\n793: // installAPIResources is a private method for installing the REST storage backing each api groupversionresource\\n794: func (s *GenericAPIServer) installAPIResources(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, typeConverter managedfields.TypeConverter) error {\\n795: \\tvar resourceInfos []*storageversion.ResourceInfo\\n796: \\tfor _, groupVersion := range apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions {\\n797: \\t\\tif len(apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version]) == 0 {\\n798: \\t\\t\\tklog.Warningf(\\\"Skipping API %v because it has no resources.\\\", groupVersion)\\n799: \\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n800: \\t\\t}\\n801: \\n802: \\t\\tapiGroupVersion, err := s.getAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo, groupVersion, apiPrefix)\\n803: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n804: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n805: \\t\\t}\\n806: \\t\\tif apiGroupInfo.OptionsExternalVersion != nil {\\n807: \\t\\t\\tapiGroupVersion.OptionsExternalVersion = apiGroupInfo.OptionsExternalVersion\\n808: \\t\\t}\\n809: \\t\\tapiGroupVersion.TypeConverter = typeConverter\\n810: \\t\\tapiGroupVersion.MaxRequestBodyBytes = s.maxRequestBodyBytes\\n811: \\n812: \\t\\tdiscoveryAPIResources, r, err := apiGroupVersion.InstallREST(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer)\\n813: \\n814: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n815: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to setup API %v: %v\\\", apiGroupInfo, err)\\n816: \\t\\t}\\n817: \\t\\tresourceInfos = append(resourceInfos, r...)\\n818: \\n819: \\t\\t// Aggregated discovery only aggregates resources under /apis\\n820: \\t\\tif apiPrefix == APIGroupPrefix {\\n821: \\t\\t\\ts.AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroupVersion(\\n822: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroupVersion.Group,\\n823: \\t\\t\\t\\tapidiscoveryv2.APIVersionDiscovery{\\n824: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tFreshness: apidiscoveryv2.DiscoveryFreshnessCurrent,\\n825: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tVersion:   groupVersion.Version,\\n826: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tResources: discoveryAPIResources,\\n827: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n828: \\t\\t\\t)\\n829: \\t\\t} else {\\n830: \\t\\t\\t// There is only one group version for legacy resources, priority can be defaulted to 0.\\n831: \\t\\t\\ts.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroupVersion(\\n832: \\t\\t\\t\\tgroupVersion.Group,\\n833: \\t\\t\\t\\tapidiscoveryv2.APIVersionDiscovery{\\n834: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tFreshness: apidiscoveryv2.DiscoveryFreshnessCurrent,\\n835: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tVersion:   groupVersion.Version,\\n836: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tResources: discoveryAPIResources,\\n837: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n838: \\t\\t\\t)\\n839: \\t\\t}\\n840: \\n841: \\t}\\n842: \\n843: \\ts.RegisterDestroyFunc(apiGroupInfo.destroyStorage)\\n844: \\n845: \\tif s.FeatureGate.Enabled(features.StorageVersionAPI) &&\\n846: \\t\\ts.FeatureGate.Enabled(features.APIServerIdentity) {\\n847: \\t\\t// API installation happens before we start listening on the handlers,\\n848: \\t\\t// therefore it is safe to register ResourceInfos here. The handler will block\\n849: \\t\\t// write requests until the storage versions of the targeting resources are updated.\\n850: \\t\\ts.StorageVersionManager.AddResourceInfo(resourceInfos...)\\n851: \\t}\\n852: \\n853: \\treturn nil\\n854: }\\n855: \\n856: // InstallLegacyAPIGroup exposes the given legacy api group in the API.\\n857: // The <apiGroupInfo> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\\n858: // underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\\n859: func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallLegacyAPIGroup(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {\\n860: \\tif !s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.Has(apiPrefix) {\\n861: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"%q is not in the allowed legacy API prefixes: %v\\\", apiPrefix, s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.List())\\n862: \\t}\\n863: \\n864: \\topenAPIModels, err := s.getOpenAPIModels(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo)\\n865: \\tif err != nil {\\n866: \\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"unable to get openapi models: %v\\\", err)\\n867: \\t}\\n868: \\n869: \\tif err := s.installAPIResources(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels); err != nil {\\n870: \\t\\treturn err\\n871: \\t}\\n872: \\n873: \\t// Install the version handler.\\n874: \\t// Add a handler at /<apiPrefix> to enumerate the supported api versions.\\n875: \\tlegacyRootAPIHandler := discovery.NewLegacyRootAPIHandler(s.discoveryAddresses, s.Serializer, apiPrefix)\\n876: \\t// No peer-to-peer discovery for legacy API group.\\n877: \\twrapped := discoveryendpoint.WrapAggregatedDiscoveryToHandler(legacyRootAPIHandler, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager)\\n878: \\ts.Handler.GoRestfulContainer.Add(wrapped.GenerateWebService(\\\"/api\\\", metav1.APIVersions{}))\\n879: \\ts.registerStorageReadinessCheck(\\\"\\\", apiGroupInfo)\\n880: \\n881: \\treturn nil\\n882: }\\n883: \\n884: // InstallAPIGroups exposes given api groups in the API.\\n885: // The <apiGroupInfos> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\\n886: // underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\\n887: func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) error {\\n888: \\tfor _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {\\n889: \\t\\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions) == 0 {\\n890: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"no version priority set for %#v\\\", *apiGroupInfo)\\n891: \\t\\t}\\n892: \\t\\t// Do not register empty group or empty version.  Doing so claims /apis/ for the wrong entity to be returned.\\n893: \\t\\t// Catching these here places the error  much closer to its origin\\n894: \\t\\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group) == 0 {\\n895: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot register handler with an empty group for %#v\\\", *apiGroupInfo)\\n896: \\t\\t}\\n897: \\t\\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version) == 0 {\\n898: \\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"cannot register handler with an empty version for %#v\\\", *apiGroupInfo)\\n899: \\t\\t}\\n900: \\t}\\n901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL700-900\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "700: \t\t\t// We don't expect more than one token to be consumed\n701: \t\t\t// in a single Wait call, so setting burst to 1.\n702: \t\t\treturn rate.NewLimiter(rate.Limit(qps), 1), ctx, cancel\n703: \t\t})\n704: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] active watch request(s) have drained\",\n705: \t\t\t\"duration\", grace, \"activeWatchesBefore\", activeBefore, \"activeWatchesAfter\", activeAfter, \"error\", err)\n706: \t}()\n707: \n708: \tgo func() {\n709: \t\tdefer klog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] shutdown event\", \"name\", drainedCh.Name())\n710: \t\tdefer drainedCh.Signal()\n711: \n712: \t\t<-nonLongRunningRequestDrainedCh\n713: \t\t<-activeWatchesDrainedCh\n714: \t}()\n715: \n716: \tklog.V(1).Info(\"[graceful-termination] waiting for shutdown to be initiated\")\n717: \t<-stopCh\n718: \n719: \t// run shutdown hooks directly. This includes deregistering from\n720: \t// the kubernetes endpoint in case of kube-apiserver.\n721: \tfunc() {\n722: \t\tdefer func() {\n723: \t\t\tpreShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Signal()\n724: \t\t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] pre-shutdown hooks completed\", \"name\", preShutdownHooksHasStoppedCh.Name())\n725: \t\t}()\n726: \t\terr = s.RunPreShutdownHooks()\n727: \t}()\n728: \tif err != nil {\n729: \t\treturn err\n730: \t}\n731: \n732: \t// Wait for all requests in flight to drain, bounded by the RequestTimeout variable.\n733: \t<-drainedCh.Signaled()\n734: \n735: \tif s.AuditBackend != nil {\n736: \t\ts.AuditBackend.Shutdown()\n737: \t\tklog.V(1).InfoS(\"[graceful-termination] audit backend shutdown completed\")\n738: \t}\n739: \n740: \t// wait for stoppedCh that is closed when the graceful termination (server.Shutdown) is finished.\n741: \t<-listenerStoppedCh\n742: \t<-stoppedCh\n743: \n744: \tklog.V(1).Info(\"[graceful-termination] apiserver is exiting\")\n745: \treturn nil\n746: }\n747: \n748: // NonBlockingRun spawns the secure http server. An error is\n749: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.\n750: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.\n751: //\n752: // Deprecated: use RunWithContext instead. Run will not get removed to avoid\n753: // breaking consumers, but should not be used in new code.\n754: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) NonBlockingRun(stopCh <-chan struct{}, shutdownTimeout time.Duration) (<-chan struct{}, <-chan struct{}, error) {\n755: \tctx := wait.ContextForChannel(stopCh)\n756: \treturn s.NonBlockingRunWithContext(ctx, shutdownTimeout)\n757: }\n758: \n759: // NonBlockingRunWithContext spawns the secure http server. An error is\n760: // returned if the secure port cannot be listened on.\n761: // The returned channel is closed when the (asynchronous) termination is finished.\n762: func (s preparedGenericAPIServer) NonBlockingRunWithContext(ctx context.Context, shutdownTimeout time.Duration) (<-chan struct{}, <-chan struct{}, error) {\n763: \t// Use an internal stop channel to allow cleanup of the listeners on error.\n764: \tinternalStopCh := make(chan struct{})\n765: \tvar stoppedCh <-chan struct{}\n766: \tvar listenerStoppedCh <-chan struct{}\n767: \tif s.SecureServingInfo != nil && s.Handler != nil {\n768: \t\tvar err error\n769: \t\tstoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, err = s.SecureServingInfo.Serve(s.Handler, shutdownTimeout, internalStopCh)\n770: \t\tif err != nil {\n771: \t\t\tclose(internalStopCh)\n772: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, err\n773: \t\t}\n774: \t}\n775: \n776: \t// Now that listener have bound successfully, it is the\n777: \t// responsibility of the caller to close the provided channel to\n778: \t// ensure cleanup.\n779: \tgo func() {\n780: \t\t<-ctx.Done()\n781: \t\tclose(internalStopCh)\n782: \t}()\n783: \n784: \ts.RunPostStartHooks(ctx)\n785: \n786: \tif _, err := systemd.SdNotify(true, \"READY=1\\n\"); err != nil {\n787: \t\tklog.Errorf(\"Unable to send systemd daemon successful start message: %v\\n\", err)\n788: \t}\n789: \n790: \treturn stoppedCh, listenerStoppedCh, nil\n791: }\n792: \n793: // installAPIResources is a private method for installing the REST storage backing each api groupversionresource\n794: func (s *GenericAPIServer) installAPIResources(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo, typeConverter managedfields.TypeConverter) error {\n795: \tvar resourceInfos []*storageversion.ResourceInfo\n796: \tfor _, groupVersion := range apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions {\n797: \t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.VersionedResourcesStorageMap[groupVersion.Version]) == 0 {\n798: \t\t\tklog.Warningf(\"Skipping API %v because it has no resources.\", groupVersion)\n799: \t\t\tcontinue\n800: \t\t}\n801: \n802: \t\tapiGroupVersion, err := s.getAPIGroupVersion(apiGroupInfo, groupVersion, apiPrefix)\n803: \t\tif err != nil {\n804: \t\t\treturn err\n805: \t\t}\n806: \t\tif apiGroupInfo.OptionsExternalVersion != nil {\n807: \t\t\tapiGroupVersion.OptionsExternalVersion = apiGroupInfo.OptionsExternalVersion\n808: \t\t}\n809: \t\tapiGroupVersion.TypeConverter = typeConverter\n810: \t\tapiGroupVersion.MaxRequestBodyBytes = s.maxRequestBodyBytes\n811: \n812: \t\tdiscoveryAPIResources, r, err := apiGroupVersion.InstallREST(s.Handler.GoRestfulContainer)\n813: \n814: \t\tif err != nil {\n815: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to setup API %v: %v\", apiGroupInfo, err)\n816: \t\t}\n817: \t\tresourceInfos = append(resourceInfos, r...)\n818: \n819: \t\t// Aggregated discovery only aggregates resources under /apis\n820: \t\tif apiPrefix == APIGroupPrefix {\n821: \t\t\ts.AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroupVersion(\n822: \t\t\t\tgroupVersion.Group,\n823: \t\t\t\tapidiscoveryv2.APIVersionDiscovery{\n824: \t\t\t\t\tFreshness: apidiscoveryv2.DiscoveryFreshnessCurrent,\n825: \t\t\t\t\tVersion:   groupVersion.Version,\n826: \t\t\t\t\tResources: discoveryAPIResources,\n827: \t\t\t\t},\n828: \t\t\t)\n829: \t\t} else {\n830: \t\t\t// There is only one group version for legacy resources, priority can be defaulted to 0.\n831: \t\t\ts.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager.AddGroupVersion(\n832: \t\t\t\tgroupVersion.Group,\n833: \t\t\t\tapidiscoveryv2.APIVersionDiscovery{\n834: \t\t\t\t\tFreshness: apidiscoveryv2.DiscoveryFreshnessCurrent,\n835: \t\t\t\t\tVersion:   groupVersion.Version,\n836: \t\t\t\t\tResources: discoveryAPIResources,\n837: \t\t\t\t},\n838: \t\t\t)\n839: \t\t}\n840: \n841: \t}\n842: \n843: \ts.RegisterDestroyFunc(apiGroupInfo.destroyStorage)\n844: \n845: \tif s.FeatureGate.Enabled(features.StorageVersionAPI) &&\n846: \t\ts.FeatureGate.Enabled(features.APIServerIdentity) {\n847: \t\t// API installation happens before we start listening on the handlers,\n848: \t\t// therefore it is safe to register ResourceInfos here. The handler will block\n849: \t\t// write requests until the storage versions of the targeting resources are updated.\n850: \t\ts.StorageVersionManager.AddResourceInfo(resourceInfos...)\n851: \t}\n852: \n853: \treturn nil\n854: }\n855: \n856: // InstallLegacyAPIGroup exposes the given legacy api group in the API.\n857: // The <apiGroupInfo> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\n858: // underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\n859: func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallLegacyAPIGroup(apiPrefix string, apiGroupInfo *APIGroupInfo) error {\n860: \tif !s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.Has(apiPrefix) {\n861: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"%q is not in the allowed legacy API prefixes: %v\", apiPrefix, s.legacyAPIGroupPrefixes.List())\n862: \t}\n863: \n864: \topenAPIModels, err := s.getOpenAPIModels(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo)\n865: \tif err != nil {\n866: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to get openapi models: %v\", err)\n867: \t}\n868: \n869: \tif err := s.installAPIResources(apiPrefix, apiGroupInfo, openAPIModels); err != nil {\n870: \t\treturn err\n871: \t}\n872: \n873: \t// Install the version handler.\n874: \t// Add a handler at /<apiPrefix> to enumerate the supported api versions.\n875: \tlegacyRootAPIHandler := discovery.NewLegacyRootAPIHandler(s.discoveryAddresses, s.Serializer, apiPrefix)\n876: \t// No peer-to-peer discovery for legacy API group.\n877: \twrapped := discoveryendpoint.WrapAggregatedDiscoveryToHandler(legacyRootAPIHandler, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager, s.AggregatedLegacyDiscoveryGroupManager)\n878: \ts.Handler.GoRestfulContainer.Add(wrapped.GenerateWebService(\"/api\", metav1.APIVersions{}))\n879: \ts.registerStorageReadinessCheck(\"\", apiGroupInfo)\n880: \n881: \treturn nil\n882: }\n883: \n884: // InstallAPIGroups exposes given api groups in the API.\n885: // The <apiGroupInfos> passed into this function shouldn't be used elsewhere as the\n886: // underlying storage will be destroyed on this servers shutdown.\n887: func (s *GenericAPIServer) InstallAPIGroups(apiGroupInfos ...*APIGroupInfo) error {\n888: \tfor _, apiGroupInfo := range apiGroupInfos {\n889: \t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions) == 0 {\n890: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"no version priority set for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n891: \t\t}\n892: \t\t// Do not register empty group or empty version.  Doing so claims /apis/ for the wrong entity to be returned.\n893: \t\t// Catching these here places the error  much closer to its origin\n894: \t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Group) == 0 {\n895: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"cannot register handler with an empty group for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n896: \t\t}\n897: \t\tif len(apiGroupInfo.PrioritizedVersions[0].Version) == 0 {\n898: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"cannot register handler with an empty version for %#v\", *apiGroupInfo)\n899: \t\t}\n900: \t}\n901: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes@d820c046f5c520aab51ec850b263524b1fa5a4e9/-/blob/staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go%3FL700-900"
  }
}
