118. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Kubernetes apiserver Library: Subsystem Architecture and Extension Guide\n\n## 1. Subsystem Purpose and Boundaries\n\nThe `k8s.io/apiserver` library (staged at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/`) is the reusable framework that powers every Kubernetes API server\u2014both `kube-apiserver` itself and any extension API server built with it (e.g., `sample-apiserver`, aggregated API servers, CRD-backed servers). It is not a standalone binary; it is a library that consumer binaries compose into a running server.\n\n**What it owns:**\n- The HTTP handler chain (authentication, authorization, audit, rate-limiting, timeouts)\n- The routing layer that maps URL paths to `rest.Storage` implementations\n- Storage abstraction over etcd\n- Admission control pipeline (mutating + validating)\n- Discovery endpoints (`/api`, `/apis`, `/openapi/v2`, `/openapi/v3`)\n- Server lifecycle (startup hooks, graceful shutdown, health probes)\n\n**What it does not own:**\n- The concrete `rest.Storage` implementations for Kubernetes resources (those live in `pkg/registry/` in the main tree)\n- The etcd client itself (provided by `go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3`)\n- RBAC rule data (provided by admission plugins and authorizer implementations in `pkg/auth/`)\n- The aggregation layer (in `staging/src/k8s.io/kube-aggregator/`)\n\n**Upstream dependencies:**\n- `k8s.io/apimachinery` \u2014 runtime types, serialization codecs, scheme, `GroupVersionKind`\n- `k8s.io/client-go` \u2014 REST client, informers (used by post-start hooks and delegating authenticators)\n- `k8s.io/component-base` \u2014 feature gates, metrics, tracing\n- `github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3` \u2014 the GoRestful container used for `/api` and `/apis` routes\n\n**Downstream consumers:**\n- `cmd/kube-apiserver` \u2014 composes the library with Kubernetes-specific storage, admission, and auth\n- Any aggregated API server using `RecommendedOptions` (e.g., `staging/src/k8s.io/sample-apiserver/`)\n- CRD infrastructure in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/`\n\n---\n\n## 2. Key Components and Responsibilities\n\n### 2.1 `pkg/server` \u2014 Core Server Bootstrap\n\n**`Config` struct** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go:112`)\n\nThe central configuration object. Its most important fields are:\n\n```go\ntype Config struct {\n    SecureServing    *SecureServingInfo          // TLS listener config\n    Authentication   AuthenticationInfo          // authenticator.Request implementation\n    Authorization    AuthorizationInfo           // authorizer.Authorizer implementation\n    AdmissionControl admission.Interface         // admission chain\n    FlowControl      utilflowcontrol.Interface   // priority-and-fairness (nil = MaxInFlight)\n    AuditBackend     audit.Backend\n    AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\n    BuildHandlerChainFunc func(http.Handler, *Config) http.Handler  // extension point\n    RESTOptionsGetter     genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter         // etcd connection options\n    MaxRequestsInFlight        int  // default 400\n    MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight int // default 200\n    RequestTimeout             time.Duration // default 60s\n    MaxRequestBodyBytes         int64  // default 3MB\n    ...\n}\n```\n\n`NewConfig()` (line 397) initializes the config with safe defaults, including setting `BuildHandlerChainFunc` to `DefaultBuildHandlerChain`. The pattern `NewConfig \u2192 Complete \u2192 New` is the canonical construction path.\n\n**`completedConfig.New()`** (line 779) instantiates a `GenericAPIServer`. It:\n1. Builds an `APIServerHandler` by calling `BuildHandlerChainFunc` around the `Director`\n2. Copies all delegation target's post-start hooks, pre-shutdown hooks, and health checks into the new server\n3. Registers several built-in post-start hooks (informer factory, priority-and-fairness, object count tracker)\n\n**`GenericAPIServer` struct** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go:110`)\n\nThe runtime server object. Key exported fields:\n- `Handler *APIServerHandler` \u2014 the HTTP handler tree\n- `LoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config` \u2014 privileged in-cluster config for post-start hooks\n- `Authorizer authorizer.Authorizer` \u2014 used by admission plugins that need to re-authorize\n- `AuditBackend audit.Backend` \u2014 flushed on graceful shutdown\n- `StorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager` \u2014 tracks storage versions of installed resources\n- `DiscoveryGroupManager` / `AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager` \u2014 manage `/api` and `/apis` responses\n\n**`DelegationTarget` interface** (line 312)\n\nEnables server chaining (e.g., apiextensions-apiserver delegates to kube-apiserver):\n\n```go\ntype DelegationTarget interface {\n    UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler  // the downstream's Director\n    PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry\n    PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n    HealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker\n    PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer\n    MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{}\n    Destroy()\n}\n```\n\nWhen requests fall through the upstream server's registered routes, they are forwarded to the `DelegationTarget.UnprotectedHandler()`. This is how the aggregation layer, apiextensions server, and core API server form a chain.\n\n**`APIGroupInfo`** (line 72)\n\nCarries everything needed to install a group's REST handlers:\n- `PrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion` \u2014 preferred order for serving\n- `VersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage` \u2014 maps version \u2192 resource \u2192 storage\n- `Scheme *runtime.Scheme`, `NegotiatedSerializer`, `ParameterCodec` \u2014 serialization context\n\n### 2.2 `pkg/server/handler.go` \u2014 HTTP Handler Structure\n\n```go\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\n    FullHandlerChain   http.Handler          // entire filter chain; ServeHTTP delegates here\n    GoRestfulContainer *restful.Container    // handles /api and /apis routes\n    NonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux  // handles /healthz, /readyz, /metrics, etc.\n    Director           http.Handler          // routes between GoRestful and NonGoRestful\n}\n```\n\nThe `Director` (`handler.go:115`) inspects the URL path against registered GoRestful WebServices. If the path has a registered prefix (e.g., `/apis/apps/v1`), the request goes to `GoRestfulContainer.Dispatch`; otherwise it falls through to `NonGoRestfulMux`. This design avoids the 404-forcing behavior of go-restful for paths it does not own.\n\n### 2.3 `pkg/server/config.go` \u2014 `DefaultBuildHandlerChain`\n\nThe default filter chain (line 1028) is the canonical example of how all the security and policy filters compose. Filters wrap each other like middleware; they are applied inside-out (the last `handler = ...` call in the function is the outermost wrapper, executed first). Reading the complete chain from outermost to innermost:\n\n```\nWithAuditInit                    \u2190 attaches empty AuditContext to request context\nWithPanicRecovery                \u2190 catches panics, logs stack, returns 500\nWithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete      \u2190 503s requests until routes are registered\nWithRequestReceivedTimestamp     \u2190 stamps arrival time in context\nWithRequestInfo                  \u2190 parses URL into RequestInfo (verb/resource/namespace/name)\nWithRoutine (optional)           \u2190 runs downstream handler in a separate goroutine\nWithHTTPLogging                  \u2190 access log\nWithRetryAfter (optional)        \u2190 sends 429 + Retry-After during shutdown\nWithHSTS                         \u2190 injects Strict-Transport-Security header\nWithCacheControl                 \u2190 sets Cache-Control: no-store\nWithProbabilisticGoaway (opt.)   \u2190 randomly sends HTTP/2 GOAWAY for load rebalancing\nWithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown (opt.) \u2190 rate-limits watch drain during shutdown\nWithWaitGroup                    \u2190 tracks non-long-running requests for graceful drain\nWithRequestDeadline              \u2190 sets context deadline from RequestTimeout\nWithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests \u2190 enforces deadline in a goroutine\nWithWarningRecorder              \u2190 thread-safe Warning: header recorder\nWithCORS                         \u2190 Cross-Origin Resource Sharing\n  [tracing (optional)]           \u2190 OpenTelemetry trace propagation\nWithAuthentication               \u2190 calls authenticator.Request, stores user in context\nWithAudit                        \u2190 records audit events at RequestReceived/ResponseComplete\nWithImpersonation / WithConstrainedImpersonation \u2190 handles Impersonate-* headers\nWithPriorityAndFairness / WithMaxInFlightLimit  \u2190 admission queuing\nWithAuthorization                \u2190 calls authorizer.Authorize; 403 on deny\n[core API handler \u2014 GoRestful or NonGoRestful]\n```\n\nKey detail: authorization runs *after* authentication but *before* the API handler. Audit wraps authorization (so both allow and deny are logged). Priority-and-fairness queuing runs between impersonation and authorization, so the queue decision is made with the effective user already set.\n\n### 2.4 `pkg/endpoints` \u2014 Route Installation\n\n**`APIGroupVersion`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/groupversion.go:50`)\n\nTakes a map of `rest.Storage` objects and registers HTTP routes for them. Its `InstallREST(container)` method constructs an `APIInstaller`, calls `installer.Install()`, then adds the resulting WebService to the GoRestful container.\n\n**`APIInstaller`** (`installer.go:62`)\n\nIterates over every storage object in the group-version, inspects which `rest.*` interfaces it implements, and registers routes accordingly. For each resource, it builds an `action` struct for each enabled verb, then calls the corresponding handler constructor (`handlers.GetResource`, `handlers.CreateResource`, etc.). It also populates the APIResource discovery document and registers metrics.\n\nAn `action` carries:\n```go\ntype action struct {\n    Verb          string              // \"GET\", \"POST\", \"WATCH\", etc.\n    Path          string              // GoRestful route path\n    Params        []*restful.Parameter\n    Namer         handlers.ScopeNamer\n    AllNamespaces bool\n}\n```\n\n### 2.5 `pkg/registry/rest` \u2014 Storage Interfaces\n\nAll REST storage objects implement a subset of the composable interfaces in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go`. Each interface corresponds to one HTTP verb:\n\n| Interface | HTTP Method | Key Method |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Getter` | GET (single) | `Get(ctx, name, *GetOptions) (Object, error)` |\n| `GetterWithOptions` | GET + subpath | `Get(ctx, name, runtime.Object) (Object, error)` |\n| `Lister` | GET (collection) | `List(ctx, *ListOptions) (Object, error)` |\n| `Creater` | POST | `Create(ctx, obj, validateFn, *CreateOptions) (Object, error)` |\n| `NamedCreater` | POST (subresource) | `Create(ctx, name, obj, ...) (Object, error)` |\n| `Updater` | PUT | `Update(ctx, name, UpdatedObjectInfo, ...) (Object, bool, error)` |\n| `GracefulDeleter` | DELETE | `Delete(ctx, name, validateFn, *DeleteOptions) (Object, bool, error)` |\n| `CollectionDeleter` | DELETE (collection) | `DeleteCollection(ctx, validateFn, ...)` |\n| `Watcher` | GET ?watch=true | `Watch(ctx, *ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)` |\n| `Connecter` | * (custom) | `Connect(ctx, id, opts, Responder) (http.Handler, error)` |\n| `Redirector` | GET (proxy) | `ResourceLocation(ctx, id) (*url.URL, RoundTripper, error)` |\n\n`StandardStorage` is a composite of `Getter`, `Lister`, `CreaterUpdater`, `GracefulDeleter`, `CollectionDeleter`, and `Watcher`. Most main resources implement it.\n\n`Connecter` is the escape hatch for custom HTTP protocols (e.g., `exec`, `port-forward`, `log`). It returns an `http.Handler` that takes over the connection for the duration of the request.\n\n### 2.6 `pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go` \u2014 `Store`\n\nThe canonical implementation of `StandardStorage` is `registry.Store` (line 100). It composes:\n- `Storage DryRunnableStorage` \u2014 wraps the etcd `storage.Interface` with dry-run support\n- `CreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy` \u2014 resource-specific defaulting and validation on create\n- `UpdateStrategy rest.RESTUpdateStrategy` \u2014 resource-specific update semantics\n- `DeleteStrategy rest.RESTDeleteStrategy` \u2014 resource-specific delete semantics\n- Lifecycle hooks: `BeginCreate`, `AfterCreate`, `BeginUpdate`, `AfterUpdate`, `AfterDelete`\n- `Decorator func(runtime.Object)` \u2014 post-storage decoration (cannot be watched)\n- `PredicateFunc` \u2014 builds the `storage.SelectionPredicate` for label/field filtering\n\nStrategy types are the point where resource-specific logic lives. `RESTCreateStrategy.PrepareForCreate` runs defaulting; `RESTCreateStrategy.Validate` runs validation before writing to etcd.\n\n### 2.7 `pkg/storage` \u2014 Storage Abstraction\n\nThe top-level interface is `storage.Interface` (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go:169`):\n\n```go\ntype Interface interface {\n    Versioner() Versioner\n    Create(ctx, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\n    Delete(ctx, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions, validateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\n    Watch(ctx, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\n    Get(ctx, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\n    GetList(ctx, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\n    GuaranteedUpdate(ctx, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\n        preconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\n    ReadinessCheck() error\n    Stats(ctx) (Stats, error)\n    RequestWatchProgress(ctx) error\n    GetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx) (uint64, error)\n    CompactRevision() int64\n}\n```\n\n`GuaranteedUpdate` is the core primitive for conflict-free updates: it loops calling `tryUpdate` with the current object until the compare-and-swap succeeds. This is what backs `Updater.Update` in `Store`.\n\nThe `Versioner` sub-interface (`interfaces.go:46`) manages `ResourceVersion` as an opaque uint64 in etcd and as an opaque string in the API.\n\nThe etcd3 implementation lives at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/`. A watch cache implementation wraps it at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/`, serving most list/watch requests from an in-memory cache to reduce etcd load.\n\n### 2.8 `pkg/admission` \u2014 Admission Control\n\n**`interfaces.go`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go`)\n\n```go\ntype Interface interface {\n    Handles(operation Operation) bool\n}\ntype MutationInterface interface {\n    Interface\n    Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error\n}\ntype ValidationInterface interface {\n    Interface\n    Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error\n}\n```\n\n`Attributes` carries all decision-relevant metadata: `GetOperation()` (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE/CONNECT), `GetObject()`, `GetOldObject()`, `GetUserInfo()`, `IsDryRun()`, and `GetReinvocationContext()` for re-invocation.\n\n**`chain.go`** \u2014 `chainAdmissionHandler` is a slice of `Interface` values that implements both `MutationInterface` and `ValidationInterface` by iterating and short-circuiting on the first error.\n\n**Re-invocation**: `ReinvocationContext` tracks whether any plugin set `ShouldReinvoke()`. The handler loop re-runs mutating plugins once if requested, allowing plugins that depend on object state modified by earlier plugins to see the updated values.\n\n**`PluginInitializer`** \u2014 used to inject shared resources (informer factories, clients) into plugins during startup.\n\n### 2.9 `pkg/endpoints/filters` and `pkg/server/filters` \u2014 Filter Implementations\n\nKey filter implementations (all return `http.Handler`):\n\n| Filter | Package | Key behavior |\n|---|---|---|\n| `WithAuthentication` | `endpoints/filters` | Calls `authenticator.Request.AuthenticateRequest`; stores `user.Info` in context via `genericapirequest.WithUser`; deletes `Authorization` and proxy headers on success |\n| `WithAuthorization` | `endpoints/filters` | Calls `authorizer.Authorize`; annotates audit with decision; returns 403 on deny |\n| `WithAudit` | `endpoints/filters` | Evaluates audit policy; intercepts `ResponseWriter` to capture status code; emits events at `RequestReceived`, `ResponseComplete`, `Panic` stages |\n| `WithRequestInfo` | `endpoints/filters` | Parses URL into `RequestInfo` struct; stores in context |\n| `WithImpersonation` | `endpoints/filters` | Validates `Impersonate-*` headers against authorization; replaces user in context |\n| `WithPanicRecovery` | `server/filters` | `recover()` on panics; writes 500 response |\n| `WithMaxInFlightLimit` | `server/filters` | Two semaphores (mutating/non-mutating); 429 when full |\n| `WithPriorityAndFairness` | `server/filters` | Flow-control: classifies requests into FlowSchemas, queues them, executes with work estimation |\n| `WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests` | `server/filters` | Runs handler in goroutine; returns 504 if context deadline exceeded |\n| `WithWaitGroup` | `server/filters` | Tracks non-long-running requests in `SafeWaitGroup` for graceful drain |\n| `WithRetryAfter` | `server/filters` | After shutdown is initiated, returns 429 + `Retry-After: 1` |\n\n---\n\n## 3. End-to-End Request Flow\n\nThis section traces a `GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/default/deployments/my-deploy` request.\n\n### 3.1 Network Ingress\n\nThe TLS listener (configured via `SecureServingInfo`) accepts the TCP connection. `s.SecureServingInfo.Serve(s.Handler, shutdownTimeout, stopCh)` begins serving via the `APIServerHandler.ServeHTTP` method, which delegates to `FullHandlerChain`.\n\n### 3.2 Handler Chain Traversal (outermost to innermost)\n\n1. **`WithAuditInit`** \u2014 allocates an empty `AuditContext` in the request context.\n2. **`WithPanicRecovery`** \u2014 wraps the rest in a `defer recover()`.\n3. **`WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete`** \u2014 if routes aren't all registered yet, returns 503.\n4. **`WithRequestReceivedTimestamp`** \u2014 stamps `time.Now()` in context.\n5. **`WithRequestInfo`** \u2014 parses the URL: sets `Verb=get`, `Resource=deployments`, `Namespace=default`, `Name=my-deploy`, `APIGroup=apps`, `APIVersion=v1` into a `RequestInfo` stored in context.\n6. **`WithHTTPLogging`** \u2014 prepares access log entry.\n7. **`WithHSTS`**, **`WithCacheControl`** \u2014 add response headers.\n8. **`WithWaitGroup`** \u2014 adds request to `NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup`.\n9. **`WithRequestDeadline`** \u2014 sets `context.WithDeadline` using `RequestTimeout` (default 60s).\n10. **`WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests`** \u2014 spawns goroutine; starts 60-second timer.\n11. **`WithWarningRecorder`** \u2014 attaches `WarningRecorder` to context.\n12. **`WithCORS`** \u2014 handles preflight if needed.\n13. **`WithAuthentication`** \u2014 calls `authenticator.Request.AuthenticateRequest(req)`. On success, deletes `Authorization` header, stores `user.Info` in context via `genericapirequest.WithUser`.\n14. **`WithAudit`** \u2014 evaluates audit policy; emits `StageRequestReceived` event.\n15. **`WithImpersonation`** \u2014 no impersonation headers present; passes through.\n16. **`WithPriorityAndFairness`** (or `WithMaxInFlightLimit`) \u2014 classifies request; may queue it. On admission, proceeds.\n17. **`WithAuthorization`** \u2014 calls `GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)` to extract `RequestInfo + user.Info` into `authorizer.Attributes`. Calls `authorizer.Authorize`. On `DecisionAllow`, annotates audit and proceeds.\n\n### 3.3 Director Routing\n\nThe request reaches the `Director`. It finds that `/apis/apps/v1` matches a registered GoRestful WebService prefix. It calls `goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)`.\n\nGoRestful matches the route `/apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments/{name}` with verb GET, and invokes the registered route handler built by `APIInstaller`.\n\n### 3.4 Resource Handler\n\nThe handler is `handlers.GetResource(getter, exporter, scope)`, constructed during `APIInstaller.Install()`. It:\n1. Extracts `namespace` and `name` from the GoRestful path parameters via `scope.Namer`.\n2. Calls `getter.Get(ctx, name, &metav1.GetOptions{})` \u2014 this is `store.Get()` in `registry.Store`.\n3. `store.Get()` calls `s.Storage.Get(ctx, key, opts, obj)` \u2014 the etcd3 storage (possibly via the watch cache).\n4. The storage layer may serve the request from the in-memory watch cache if the resource version requirement permits.\n5. On success, `handlers.GetResource` negotiates the response content type and serializes the object.\n\n### 3.5 Admission on Mutating Requests\n\nFor a `POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/default/deployments`, the flow diverges at step 3.4:\n\n1. `handlers.CreateResource` decodes the body into the internal object type.\n2. It calls `createStrategy.PrepareForCreate(ctx, obj)` to apply defaults.\n3. It runs admission via `admissionControl.Admit(ctx, attrs, objInterfaces)` \u2014 the `chainAdmissionHandler` iterates mutating plugins. If any plugin calls `attrs.GetReinvocationContext().SetShouldReinvoke()`, the chain re-runs once.\n4. It calls `createStrategy.Validate(ctx, obj)` for resource-specific validation.\n5. It runs admission via `admissionControl.Validate(ctx, attrs, objInterfaces)` \u2014 validating plugins run.\n6. If all pass, `store.Create(ctx, obj, validateFn, opts)` calls `s.Storage.Create(ctx, key, obj, out, ttl)` to write to etcd.\n\n### 3.6 Audit Completion and Response\n\nAfter the handler returns, `WithAudit` intercepts the captured HTTP status code from the wrapped `ResponseWriter` and emits a `StageResponseComplete` event. `WithWaitGroup` decrements the wait group.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Failure Modes, Limits, and Design Tradeoffs\n\n### 4.1 etcd Contention and `GuaranteedUpdate`\n\n`storage.Interface.GuaranteedUpdate` retries indefinitely on compare-and-swap failures. Under high write contention for a single object, this can cause goroutine pile-up. The `RequestTimeout` (default 60s) bounds total request duration, but tight deadlines on hot objects may produce spurious `504` responses before the update succeeds. The `OptimisticLockErrorMsg` constant (store.go:262) surfaces to API clients as a 409 Conflict.\n\n### 4.2 Watch Cache Staleness\n\nThe watch cache serves most list and watch requests without hitting etcd directly. However, caching introduces lag between a write and when a watch client sees it. The `RequestWatchProgress` method on `storage.Interface` can nudge the watch stream to emit a progress bookmark. List requests with `resourceVersion=0` may return data slightly older than the latest etcd revision; those with `resourceVersion=\"\"` are serialized through etcd for linearizable reads.\n\n### 4.3 Priority-and-Fairness vs MaxInFlight\n\nWhen `FlowControl` is nil, the server uses two fixed semaphores (`MaxRequestsInFlight=400` for non-mutating, `MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight=200` for mutating, per `config.go:443`). This is simple but applies all-or-nothing pressure equally across all callers.\n\nPriority-and-fairness (`WithPriorityAndFairness`) classifies requests into FlowSchemas with per-schema queuing and work estimation. It is significantly more complex, adds CPU overhead for work estimation, and requires running informers to watch `FlowSchema` and `PriorityLevelConfiguration` objects. Disabling it (by not providing `FlowControl`) removes this dynamism but makes the server more predictable under simple load patterns.\n\n### 4.4 Graceful Shutdown Signal Graph\n\n`RunWithContext` (genericapiserver.go:536) maintains a carefully ordered set of lifecycle signals:\n\n```\nctx.Done()\n  \u2192 ShutdownInitiated    (/readyz starts failing; load balancers drain)\n  \u2192 AfterShutdownDelayDuration  (after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses)\n  + PreShutdownHooksStopped\n  \u2192 NotAcceptingNewRequest\n  \u2192 [drain NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup + WatchRequestWaitGroup]\n  \u2192 InFlightRequestsDrained\n  \u2192 AuditBackend.Shutdown() + HTTP listener stops\n```\n\nA misconfigured or slow pre-shutdown hook delays `NotAcceptingNewRequest`, which in turn delays the HTTP server shutdown. Hooks that make API calls back to the server can deadlock if the server is already refusing new requests.\n\n### 4.5 Request Body Size\n\nThe `MaxRequestBodyBytes` limit (default 3MB, config.go:466) is enforced by the HTTP layer before decoding. Objects that serialize larger than this cannot be created or updated. For CRDs with large embedded JSON, this is a common limit to hit. The value is tied to etcd's recommended object size, since the object is later encoded as protobuf and stored in etcd.\n\n### 4.6 Serial vs Parallel Admission\n\n`chainAdmissionHandler.Admit` (chain.go:31) runs plugins serially and returns on the first error. There is no parallel execution of admission plugins. This keeps the admission order deterministic and avoids races on shared state, but it means a slow plugin (e.g., a webhook with high latency) blocks the entire request. Webhook plugins have their own timeout configuration.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Extension Points and Customization Hooks\n\n### 5.1 `BuildHandlerChainFunc` \u2014 Custom Filter Chains\n\nThe `Config.BuildHandlerChainFunc` field (config.go:186) allows replacing `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` entirely:\n\n```go\nconfig.BuildHandlerChainFunc = func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *server.Config) http.Handler {\n    handler := myCustomMiddleware(apiHandler)\n    return server.DefaultBuildHandlerChain(handler, c)\n}\n```\n\nAn alternative is `BuildHandlerChainWithStorageVersionPrecondition` (config.go:1022), which wraps with `WithStorageVersionPrecondition` before calling the default chain.\n\n**Risk**: Replacing the chain entirely and omitting authentication or authorization bypasses all security controls. The filter order documented in section 2.3 is critical\u2014moving audit outside of authentication, for example, would miss logging auth failures.\n\n### 5.2 `DelegationTarget` \u2014 Server Chaining\n\nCustom servers that need to extend the API can be inserted before `kube-apiserver` in the delegation chain:\n\n```go\nmyServer, _ := myConfig.New(\"my-server\", kubeAPIServer)   // delegates to kube-apiserver\n```\n\nUnmatched requests pass through to the delegate via `UnprotectedHandler()`. The `PrepareRun` call propagates through the chain. This is how `kube-aggregator`, `apiextensions-apiserver`, and `kube-apiserver` form the standard three-layer chain.\n\n**Risk**: The delegate's `UnprotectedHandler()` bypasses the delegating server's filter chain. Any security decision made by the outer server's chain is not re-applied. This is intentional (to avoid double-authenticating) but means the chain ordering must be carefully designed.\n\n### 5.3 `rest.Storage` Interfaces \u2014 Custom Resource Backends\n\nAny type implementing `rest.Storage` can be registered. The full capability set is composed by implementing additional optional interfaces:\n- Add `Getter`, `Lister`, `Creater`, etc. to enable specific verbs\n- Implement `Connecter` for custom HTTP protocols (exec, port-forward)\n- Implement `Redirector` for proxy semantics\n- Implement `StorageWithReadiness` to participate in readiness checks\n\nThe `Store` struct provides a batteries-included implementation. For resources that require non-etcd backends (e.g., aggregated metrics, or resources backed by external systems), implement `storage.Interface` directly or provide a custom `rest.Storage` that calls the external system.\n\n**Risk**: Implementing `Connecter` means the returned `http.Handler` runs within the request context after all filters have run but with no additional protection. If it performs privileged operations, it must implement its own access controls.\n\n### 5.4 `admission.MutationInterface` / `admission.ValidationInterface` \u2014 Admission Plugins\n\nCustom admission plugins register via `admission.RegisterPlugin` and are initialized with `PluginInitializer`. They receive `Attributes` containing the full request context and can:\n- Modify the object in-place (mutating only)\n- Call `attrs.AddAnnotation()` to log metadata into the audit record\n- Trigger re-invocation via `attrs.GetReinvocationContext().SetShouldReinvoke()`\n\nWebhook-based admission (in-tree in `plugin/pkg/admission/webhook/`) uses this same interface, calling external HTTP servers.\n\n**Risk**: Mutating plugins that call back to the same API server via the loopback client can trigger re-entrant admission. In-tree, this is mitigated by exempt service accounts, but custom plugins must take care to avoid infinite loops.\n\n### 5.5 `authenticator.Request` \u2014 Pluggable Authentication\n\nThe `Config.Authentication.Authenticator` field takes any `authenticator.Request` implementation. The standard composition uses `authenticatorunion.New` to chain multiple authenticators (token file, service account JWT, OIDC, client certs, bootstrap tokens). Custom implementations can be prepended.\n\n**Risk**: A custom authenticator that returns a non-nil user for requests it cannot verify will silently allow unauthenticated access with an arbitrary identity.\n\n### 5.6 `authorizer.Authorizer` \u2014 Pluggable Authorization\n\nThe `Config.Authorization.Authorizer` field accepts any `authorizer.Authorizer`. The standard implementation chains RBAC, Node, ABAC, and webhook authorizers. The `DecisionNoOpinion` return value allows chaining: if the first authorizer has no opinion, the next one is consulted.\n\n`GetAuthorizerAttributes` (authorization.go:101) derives attributes from `RequestInfo` and `user.Info` in the context, optionally including field/label selectors when the `AuthorizeWithSelectors` feature gate is enabled.\n\n### 5.7 Post-Start and Pre-Shutdown Hooks\n\n```go\nserver.AddPostStartHook(\"my-component\", func(ctx PostStartHookContext) error {\n    // runs in a goroutine after the server begins listening\n    go myController.Run(ctx.Done())\n    return nil\n})\nserver.AddPreShutdownHook(\"my-component\", func() error {\n    // runs synchronously before NotAcceptingNewRequest is signaled\n    myController.Deregister()\n    return nil\n})\n```\n\nHooks participate in readiness: the `readyz` endpoint fails until all post-start hooks have completed (for hooks registered with the readiness check). `LivezGracePeriod` allows `livez` to return true during startup even if hooks are still running.\n\n### 5.8 `RESTOptionsGetter` \u2014 Storage Configuration\n\n`genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter.GetRESTOptions(GroupResource, example)` provides storage configuration per resource. Custom implementations can route different resources to different backends, apply different encryption or compaction settings, or inject a storage decorator.\n\n`StoreOptions.AttrFunc` (options.go:58) provides the field extraction function for field selectors on a per-resource basis.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Operational Implications\n\n### 6.1 Performance Considerations\n\n**Serialization cost**: Content negotiation happens per request. JSON serialization is the default; protobuf is significantly smaller and faster for cluster-internal clients. Enabling CBOR (behind `CBORServingAndStorage` feature gate) is an active area of work.\n\n**Watch cache amplification**: Each watch from a client adds a goroutine that fans out events. The server tracks `StorageObjectCountTracker` (registered as a post-start hook) to estimate object sizes for work estimation in priority-and-fairness.\n\n**Admission latency**: In-tree admission plugins are sub-millisecond. Webhook admission adds an HTTP round-trip, typically 5-50ms. The server serializes plugin execution (section 4.6); with 10+ webhooks, admission can dominate request latency.\n\n**MaxInFlight vs Priority-and-Fairness**: The `WithMaxInFlightLimit` semaphores are O(1) to check. Priority-and-fairness (`WithPriorityAndFairness`) classifies requests by inspecting `RequestInfo` and matching against FlowSchemas \u2014 this is more expensive. Avoid registering hundreds of FlowSchemas.\n\n### 6.2 Reliability and Health Probes\n\nThree probe endpoints exist with different semantics:\n- `/healthz` \u2014 server is not broken (ping + basic checks). Used by kubelet restartPolicy.\n- `/livez` \u2014 server should not be restarted. Fails only on catastrophic internal failure. Tolerates `LivezGracePeriod` after startup.\n- `/readyz` \u2014 server can accept traffic. Fails during startup (informers not synced), during shutdown (once `ShutdownInitiated` fires), and if storage is not ready.\n\nThe `StorageReadinessHook` (genericapiserver.go:863) polls `ReadinessCheck()` on all registered storage objects and contributes to `/readyz`. If etcd is unavailable, `/readyz` fails but `/healthz` may still pass.\n\n### 6.3 Graceful Shutdown Timing\n\nThe shutdown sequence (section 4.4) has two user-configurable durations:\n- `ShutdownDelayDuration`: Time after `ShutdownInitiated` before `NotAcceptingNewRequest`. During this window, the server still accepts requests but advertises unhealthy on `/readyz`, allowing load balancers to drain.\n- `ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod`: Maximum time to wait for watch requests to drain. Set to zero (default) to skip watch drain entirely, which is faster but may drop in-progress watches.\n\nSetting `ShutdownSendRetryAfter: true` changes the shutdown behavior: the HTTP listener stays open until all non-long-running requests drain, returning 429 + `Retry-After: 1` for new requests. This prevents clients from seeing `connection refused` but increases the shutdown window.\n\n### 6.4 Maintainability: The Delegation Chain Coupling\n\nThe `DelegationTarget` pattern enables composability but creates tight coupling around hook, health check, and discovery namespace. Adding a hook in the wrong server in the chain may lead to it running multiple times (if both the upstream and delegate register the same name), or not at all (if the server is not in the expected position). The name conflict check in `AddPostStartHook` (config.go:608) is the guard.\n\nThe `MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals` mechanism exists specifically to prevent 404 races during startup: routes registered late in `PrepareRun` would otherwise be absent from the discovery document for a brief window, causing clients to receive 404s for valid resources.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Important Files and Directories\n\n```\nstaging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/server/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 config.go               # Config, RecommendedConfig, DefaultBuildHandlerChain, completedConfig.New\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 genericapiserver.go     # GenericAPIServer, APIGroupInfo, DelegationTarget, PrepareRun, Run\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 handler.go              # APIServerHandler, director (GoRestful \u2194 NonGoRestful routing)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 hooks.go                # PostStartHookFunc, AddPostStartHook, RunPostStartHooks\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 lifecycle.go            # lifecycleSignals (shutdown state machine channel set)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 options/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 recommended.go      # RecommendedOptions (Etcd, Auth, Audit, Admission, etc.)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 filters/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 maxinflight.go      # WithMaxInFlightLimit\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 priority-and-fairness.go  # WithPriorityAndFairness\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 timeout.go          # WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 waitgroup.go        # WithWaitGroup\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 watch_termination.go  # WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/endpoints/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 groupversion.go         # APIGroupVersion.InstallREST\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 installer.go            # APIInstaller.Install \u2014 verb \u2192 handler mapping\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 filters/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 authentication.go   # WithAuthentication\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 authorization.go    # WithAuthorization, GetAuthorizerAttributes\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 audit.go            # WithAudit (multi-stage event recording)\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 requestinfo.go      # WithRequestInfo\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 request_deadline.go # WithRequestDeadline\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/registry/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 rest/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 rest.go             # Storage, Getter, Lister, Creater, Updater, GracefulDeleter,\n\u2502   \u2502                           #   Watcher, Connecter, StandardStorage interfaces\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 generic/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 options.go          # RESTOptions, RESTOptionsGetter, StoreOptions\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 registry/\n\u2502           \u2514\u2500\u2500 store.go        # Store (StandardStorage implementation; strategies, hooks)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/storage/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 interfaces.go           # storage.Interface, Versioner, Preconditions, GuaranteedUpdate\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 etcd3/                  # etcd3 storage.Interface implementation\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 cacher/                 # watch-cache wrapper (serves reads from memory)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/admission/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 interfaces.go           # Attributes, MutationInterface, ValidationInterface,\n\u2502   \u2502                           #   ReinvocationContext, PluginInitializer\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 chain.go                # chainAdmissionHandler (serial chain with re-invocation)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/authentication/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 authenticator/\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 interfaces.go       # authenticator.Request, authenticator.Token, Response\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/authorization/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 authorizer/\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 interfaces.go       # authorizer.Authorizer, Attributes, Decision constants\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 pkg/audit/\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 types.go                # audit.Sink, audit.Backend interfaces\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Summary of Extension Tradeoffs\n\n| Extension Point | Flexibility | Risk | Primary File |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `BuildHandlerChainFunc` | Full control over filter order | Easy to skip critical filters | `pkg/server/config.go:186` |\n| `DelegationTarget` | Multi-server chaining | Coupling of hooks, health checks; double-filter risk | `pkg/server/genericapiserver.go:312` |\n| `rest.Storage` interfaces | Per-verb customization | Custom Connecter bypasses filters | `pkg/registry/rest/rest.go` |\n| `registry.Store` strategies | Resource-level defaulting/validation | Strategy bugs persist silently in etcd | `pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go:173` |\n| `storage.Interface` | Fully custom backend | Must handle versioning, watch, conflict detection | `pkg/storage/interfaces.go:169` |\n| `MutationInterface` | Object mutation before persistence | Re-entrant API calls; ordering dependencies | `pkg/admission/interfaces.go:129` |\n| `ValidationInterface` | Reject invalid state | Serial execution; webhook latency adds up | `pkg/admission/interfaces.go:138` |\n| `authenticator.Request` | Custom credential formats | Wrong implementation leaks identity | `pkg/authentication/authenticator/interfaces.go` |\n| `authorizer.Authorizer` | Custom policy engines | `DecisionNoOpinion` silently passes to next | `pkg/authorization/authorizer/interfaces.go` |\n| Post-start hooks | Async initialization | Slow hooks delay readiness; blocking hooks deadlock | `pkg/server/config.go:596` |\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/documentation.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Kubernetes apiserver Library: Subsystem Architecture and Extension Guide\n\n## 1. Subsystem Purpose and Boundaries\n\nThe `k8s.io/apiserver` library (staged at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/`) is the reusable framework that powers every Kubernetes API server\u2014both `kube-apiserver` itself and any extension API server built with it (e.g., `sample-apiserver`, aggregated API servers, CRD-backed servers). It is not a standalone binary; it is a library that consumer binaries compose into a running server.\n\n**What it owns:**\n- The HTTP handler chain (authentication, authorization, audit, rate-limiting, timeouts)\n- The routing layer that maps URL paths to `rest.Storage` implementations\n- Storage abstraction over etcd\n- Admission control pipeline (mutating + validating)\n- Discovery endpoints (`/api`, `/apis`, `/openapi/v2`, `/openapi/v3`)\n- Server lifecycle (startup hooks, graceful shutdown, health probes)\n\n**What it does not own:**\n- The concrete `rest.Storage` implementations for Kubernetes resources (those live in `pkg/registry/` in the main tree)\n- The etcd client itself (provided by `go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3`)\n- RBAC rule data (provided by admission plugins and authorizer implementations in `pkg/auth/`)\n- The aggregation layer (in `staging/src/k8s.io/kube-aggregator/`)\n\n**Upstream dependencies:**\n- `k8s.io/apimachinery` \u2014 runtime types, serialization codecs, scheme, `GroupVersionKind`\n- `k8s.io/client-go` \u2014 REST client, informers (used by post-start hooks and delegating authenticators)\n- `k8s.io/component-base` \u2014 feature gates, metrics, tracing\n- `github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3` \u2014 the GoRestful container used for `/api` and `/apis` routes\n\n**Downstream consumers:**\n- `cmd/kube-apiserver` \u2014 composes the library with Kubernetes-specific storage, admission, and auth\n- Any aggregated API server using `RecommendedOptions` (e.g., `staging/src/k8s.io/sample-apiserver/`)\n- CRD infrastructure in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/`\n\n---\n\n## 2. Key Components and Responsibilities\n\n### 2.1 `pkg/server` \u2014 Core Server Bootstrap\n\n**`Config` struct** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/config.go:112`)\n\nThe central configuration object. Its most important fields are:\n\n```go\ntype Config struct {\n    SecureServing    *SecureServingInfo          // TLS listener config\n    Authentication   AuthenticationInfo          // authenticator.Request implementation\n    Authorization    AuthorizationInfo           // authorizer.Authorizer implementation\n    AdmissionControl admission.Interface         // admission chain\n    FlowControl      utilflowcontrol.Interface   // priority-and-fairness (nil = MaxInFlight)\n    AuditBackend     audit.Backend\n    AuditPolicyRuleEvaluator audit.PolicyRuleEvaluator\n    BuildHandlerChainFunc func(http.Handler, *Config) http.Handler  // extension point\n    RESTOptionsGetter     genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter         // etcd connection options\n    MaxRequestsInFlight        int  // default 400\n    MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight int // default 200\n    RequestTimeout             time.Duration // default 60s\n    MaxRequestBodyBytes         int64  // default 3MB\n    ...\n}\n```\n\n`NewConfig()` (line 397) initializes the config with safe defaults, including setting `BuildHandlerChainFunc` to `DefaultBuildHandlerChain`. The pattern `NewConfig \u2192 Complete \u2192 New` is the canonical construction path.\n\n**`completedConfig.New()`** (line 779) instantiates a `GenericAPIServer`. It:\n1. Builds an `APIServerHandler` by calling `BuildHandlerChainFunc` around the `Director`\n2. Copies all delegation target's post-start hooks, pre-shutdown hooks, and health checks into the new server\n3. Registers several built-in post-start hooks (informer factory, priority-and-fairness, object count tracker)\n\n**`GenericAPIServer` struct** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go:110`)\n\nThe runtime server object. Key exported fields:\n- `Handler *APIServerHandler` \u2014 the HTTP handler tree\n- `LoopbackClientConfig *restclient.Config` \u2014 privileged in-cluster config for post-start hooks\n- `Authorizer authorizer.Authorizer` \u2014 used by admission plugins that need to re-authorize\n- `AuditBackend audit.Backend` \u2014 flushed on graceful shutdown\n- `StorageVersionManager storageversion.Manager` \u2014 tracks storage versions of installed resources\n- `DiscoveryGroupManager` / `AggregatedDiscoveryGroupManager` \u2014 manage `/api` and `/apis` responses\n\n**`DelegationTarget` interface** (line 312)\n\nEnables server chaining (e.g., apiextensions-apiserver delegates to kube-apiserver):\n\n```go\ntype DelegationTarget interface {\n    UnprotectedHandler() http.Handler  // the downstream's Director\n    PostStartHooks() map[string]postStartHookEntry\n    PreShutdownHooks() map[string]preShutdownHookEntry\n    HealthzChecks() []healthz.HealthChecker\n    PrepareRun() preparedGenericAPIServer\n    MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals() map[string]<-chan struct{}\n    Destroy()\n}\n```\n\nWhen requests fall through the upstream server's registered routes, they are forwarded to the `DelegationTarget.UnprotectedHandler()`. This is how the aggregation layer, apiextensions server, and core API server form a chain.\n\n**`APIGroupInfo`** (line 72)\n\nCarries everything needed to install a group's REST handlers:\n- `PrioritizedVersions []schema.GroupVersion` \u2014 preferred order for serving\n- `VersionedResourcesStorageMap map[string]map[string]rest.Storage` \u2014 maps version \u2192 resource \u2192 storage\n- `Scheme *runtime.Scheme`, `NegotiatedSerializer`, `ParameterCodec` \u2014 serialization context\n\n### 2.2 `pkg/server/handler.go` \u2014 HTTP Handler Structure\n\n```go\ntype APIServerHandler struct {\n    FullHandlerChain   http.Handler          // entire filter chain; ServeHTTP delegates here\n    GoRestfulContainer *restful.Container    // handles /api and /apis routes\n    NonGoRestfulMux    *mux.PathRecorderMux  // handles /healthz, /readyz, /metrics, etc.\n    Director           http.Handler          // routes between GoRestful and NonGoRestful\n}\n```\n\nThe `Director` (`handler.go:115`) inspects the URL path against registered GoRestful WebServices. If the path has a registered prefix (e.g., `/apis/apps/v1`), the request goes to `GoRestfulContainer.Dispatch`; otherwise it falls through to `NonGoRestfulMux`. This design avoids the 404-forcing behavior of go-restful for paths it does not own.\n\n### 2.3 `pkg/server/config.go` \u2014 `DefaultBuildHandlerChain`\n\nThe default filter chain (line 1028) is the canonical example of how all the security and policy filters compose. Filters wrap each other like middleware; they are applied inside-out (the last `handler = ...` call in the function is the outermost wrapper, executed first). Reading the complete chain from outermost to innermost:\n\n```\nWithAuditInit                    \u2190 attaches empty AuditContext to request context\nWithPanicRecovery                \u2190 catches panics, logs stack, returns 500\nWithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete      \u2190 503s requests until routes are registered\nWithRequestReceivedTimestamp     \u2190 stamps arrival time in context\nWithRequestInfo                  \u2190 parses URL into RequestInfo (verb/resource/namespace/name)\nWithRoutine (optional)           \u2190 runs downstream handler in a separate goroutine\nWithHTTPLogging                  \u2190 access log\nWithRetryAfter (optional)        \u2190 sends 429 + Retry-After during shutdown\nWithHSTS                         \u2190 injects Strict-Transport-Security header\nWithCacheControl                 \u2190 sets Cache-Control: no-store\nWithProbabilisticGoaway (opt.)   \u2190 randomly sends HTTP/2 GOAWAY for load rebalancing\nWithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown (opt.) \u2190 rate-limits watch drain during shutdown\nWithWaitGroup                    \u2190 tracks non-long-running requests for graceful drain\nWithRequestDeadline              \u2190 sets context deadline from RequestTimeout\nWithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests \u2190 enforces deadline in a goroutine\nWithWarningRecorder              \u2190 thread-safe Warning: header recorder\nWithCORS                         \u2190 Cross-Origin Resource Sharing\n  [tracing (optional)]           \u2190 OpenTelemetry trace propagation\nWithAuthentication               \u2190 calls authenticator.Request, stores user in context\nWithAudit                        \u2190 records audit events at RequestReceived/ResponseComplete\nWithImpersonation / WithConstrainedImpersonation \u2190 handles Impersonate-* headers\nWithPriorityAndFairness / WithMaxInFlightLimit  \u2190 admission queuing\nWithAuthorization                \u2190 calls authorizer.Authorize; 403 on deny\n[core API handler \u2014 GoRestful or NonGoRestful]\n```\n\nKey detail: authorization runs *after* authentication but *before* the API handler. Audit wraps authorization (so both allow and deny are logged). Priority-and-fairness queuing runs between impersonation and authorization, so the queue decision is made with the effective user already set.\n\n### 2.4 `pkg/endpoints` \u2014 Route Installation\n\n**`APIGroupVersion`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/endpoints/groupversion.go:50`)\n\nTakes a map of `rest.Storage` objects and registers HTTP routes for them. Its `InstallREST(container)` method constructs an `APIInstaller`, calls `installer.Install()`, then adds the resulting WebService to the GoRestful container.\n\n**`APIInstaller`** (`installer.go:62`)\n\nIterates over every storage object in the group-version, inspects which `rest.*` interfaces it implements, and registers routes accordingly. For each resource, it builds an `action` struct for each enabled verb, then calls the corresponding handler constructor (`handlers.GetResource`, `handlers.CreateResource`, etc.). It also populates the APIResource discovery document and registers metrics.\n\nAn `action` carries:\n```go\ntype action struct {\n    Verb          string              // \"GET\", \"POST\", \"WATCH\", etc.\n    Path          string              // GoRestful route path\n    Params        []*restful.Parameter\n    Namer         handlers.ScopeNamer\n    AllNamespaces bool\n}\n```\n\n### 2.5 `pkg/registry/rest` \u2014 Storage Interfaces\n\nAll REST storage objects implement a subset of the composable interfaces in `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/registry/rest/rest.go`. Each interface corresponds to one HTTP verb:\n\n| Interface | HTTP Method | Key Method |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Getter` | GET (single) | `Get(ctx, name, *GetOptions) (Object, error)` |\n| `GetterWithOptions` | GET + subpath | `Get(ctx, name, runtime.Object) (Object, error)` |\n| `Lister` | GET (collection) | `List(ctx, *ListOptions) (Object, error)` |\n| `Creater` | POST | `Create(ctx, obj, validateFn, *CreateOptions) (Object, error)` |\n| `NamedCreater` | POST (subresource) | `Create(ctx, name, obj, ...) (Object, error)` |\n| `Updater` | PUT | `Update(ctx, name, UpdatedObjectInfo, ...) (Object, bool, error)` |\n| `GracefulDeleter` | DELETE | `Delete(ctx, name, validateFn, *DeleteOptions) (Object, bool, error)` |\n| `CollectionDeleter` | DELETE (collection) | `DeleteCollection(ctx, validateFn, ...)` |\n| `Watcher` | GET ?watch=true | `Watch(ctx, *ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)` |\n| `Connecter` | * (custom) | `Connect(ctx, id, opts, Responder) (http.Handler, error)` |\n| `Redirector` | GET (proxy) | `ResourceLocation(ctx, id) (*url.URL, RoundTripper, error)` |\n\n`StandardStorage` is a composite of `Getter`, `Lister`, `CreaterUpdater`, `GracefulDeleter`, `CollectionDeleter`, and `Watcher`. Most main resources implement it.\n\n`Connecter` is the escape hatch for custom HTTP protocols (e.g., `exec`, `port-forward`, `log`). It returns an `http.Handler` that takes over the connection for the duration of the request.\n\n### 2.6 `pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go` \u2014 `Store`\n\nThe canonical implementation of `StandardStorage` is `registry.Store` (line 100). It composes:\n- `Storage DryRunnableStorage` \u2014 wraps the etcd `storage.Interface` with dry-run support\n- `CreateStrategy rest.RESTCreateStrategy` \u2014 resource-specific defaulting and validation on create\n- `UpdateStrategy rest.RESTUpdateStrategy` \u2014 resource-specific update semantics\n- `DeleteStrategy rest.RESTDeleteStrategy` \u2014 resource-specific delete semantics\n- Lifecycle hooks: `BeginCreate`, `AfterCreate`, `BeginUpdate`, `AfterUpdate`, `AfterDelete`\n- `Decorator func(runtime.Object)` \u2014 post-storage decoration (cannot be watched)\n- `PredicateFunc` \u2014 builds the `storage.SelectionPredicate` for label/field filtering\n\nStrategy types are the point where resource-specific logic lives. `RESTCreateStrategy.PrepareForCreate` runs defaulting; `RESTCreateStrategy.Validate` runs validation before writing to etcd.\n\n### 2.7 `pkg/storage` \u2014 Storage Abstraction\n\nThe top-level interface is `storage.Interface` (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/interfaces.go:169`):\n\n```go\ntype Interface interface {\n    Versioner() Versioner\n    Create(ctx, key string, obj, out runtime.Object, ttl uint64) error\n    Delete(ctx, key string, out runtime.Object, preconditions *Preconditions, validateDeletion ValidateObjectFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object, opts DeleteOptions) error\n    Watch(ctx, key string, opts ListOptions) (watch.Interface, error)\n    Get(ctx, key string, opts GetOptions, objPtr runtime.Object) error\n    GetList(ctx, key string, opts ListOptions, listObj runtime.Object) error\n    GuaranteedUpdate(ctx, key string, destination runtime.Object, ignoreNotFound bool,\n        preconditions *Preconditions, tryUpdate UpdateFunc, cachedExistingObject runtime.Object) error\n    ReadinessCheck() error\n    Stats(ctx) (Stats, error)\n    RequestWatchProgress(ctx) error\n    GetCurrentResourceVersion(ctx) (uint64, error)\n    CompactRevision() int64\n}\n```\n\n`GuaranteedUpdate` is the core primitive for conflict-free updates: it loops calling `tryUpdate` with the current object until the compare-and-swap succeeds. This is what backs `Updater.Update` in `Store`.\n\nThe `Versioner` sub-interface (`interfaces.go:46`) manages `ResourceVersion` as an opaque uint64 in etcd and as an opaque string in the API.\n\nThe etcd3 implementation lives at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/etcd3/`. A watch cache implementation wraps it at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/storage/cacher/`, serving most list/watch requests from an in-memory cache to reduce etcd load.\n\n### 2.8 `pkg/admission` \u2014 Admission Control\n\n**`interfaces.go`** (`staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/admission/interfaces.go`)\n\n```go\ntype Interface interface {\n    Handles(operation Operation) bool\n}\ntype MutationInterface interface {\n    Interface\n    Admit(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error\n}\ntype ValidationInterface interface {\n    Interface\n    Validate(ctx context.Context, a Attributes, o ObjectInterfaces) error\n}\n```\n\n`Attributes` carries all decision-relevant metadata: `GetOperation()` (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE/CONNECT), `GetObject()`, `GetOldObject()`, `GetUserInfo()`, `IsDryRun()`, and `GetReinvocationContext()` for re-invocation.\n\n**`chain.go`** \u2014 `chainAdmissionHandler` is a slice of `Interface` values that implements both `MutationInterface` and `ValidationInterface` by iterating and short-circuiting on the first error.\n\n**Re-invocation**: `ReinvocationContext` tracks whether any plugin set `ShouldReinvoke()`. The handler loop re-runs mutating plugins once if requested, allowing plugins that depend on object state modified by earlier plugins to see the updated values.\n\n**`PluginInitializer`** \u2014 used to inject shared resources (informer factories, clients) into plugins during startup.\n\n### 2.9 `pkg/endpoints/filters` and `pkg/server/filters` \u2014 Filter Implementations\n\nKey filter implementations (all return `http.Handler`):\n\n| Filter | Package | Key behavior |\n|---|---|---|\n| `WithAuthentication` | `endpoints/filters` | Calls `authenticator.Request.AuthenticateRequest`; stores `user.Info` in context via `genericapirequest.WithUser`; deletes `Authorization` and proxy headers on success |\n| `WithAuthorization` | `endpoints/filters` | Calls `authorizer.Authorize`; annotates audit with decision; returns 403 on deny |\n| `WithAudit` | `endpoints/filters` | Evaluates audit policy; intercepts `ResponseWriter` to capture status code; emits events at `RequestReceived`, `ResponseComplete`, `Panic` stages |\n| `WithRequestInfo` | `endpoints/filters` | Parses URL into `RequestInfo` struct; stores in context |\n| `WithImpersonation` | `endpoints/filters` | Validates `Impersonate-*` headers against authorization; replaces user in context |\n| `WithPanicRecovery` | `server/filters` | `recover()` on panics; writes 500 response |\n| `WithMaxInFlightLimit` | `server/filters` | Two semaphores (mutating/non-mutating); 429 when full |\n| `WithPriorityAndFairness` | `server/filters` | Flow-control: classifies requests into FlowSchemas, queues them, executes with work estimation |\n| `WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests` | `server/filters` | Runs handler in goroutine; returns 504 if context deadline exceeded |\n| `WithWaitGroup` | `server/filters` | Tracks non-long-running requests in `SafeWaitGroup` for graceful drain |\n| `WithRetryAfter` | `server/filters` | After shutdown is initiated, returns 429 + `Retry-After: 1` |\n\n---\n\n## 3. End-to-End Request Flow\n\nThis section traces a `GET /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/default/deployments/my-deploy` request.\n\n### 3.1 Network Ingress\n\nThe TLS listener (configured via `SecureServingInfo`) accepts the TCP connection. `s.SecureServingInfo.Serve(s.Handler, shutdownTimeout, stopCh)` begins serving via the `APIServerHandler.ServeHTTP` method, which delegates to `FullHandlerChain`.\n\n### 3.2 Handler Chain Traversal (outermost to innermost)\n\n1. **`WithAuditInit`** \u2014 allocates an empty `AuditContext` in the request context.\n2. **`WithPanicRecovery`** \u2014 wraps the rest in a `defer recover()`.\n3. **`WithMuxAndDiscoveryComplete`** \u2014 if routes aren't all registered yet, returns 503.\n4. **`WithRequestReceivedTimestamp`** \u2014 stamps `time.Now()` in context.\n5. **`WithRequestInfo`** \u2014 parses the URL: sets `Verb=get`, `Resource=deployments`, `Namespace=default`, `Name=my-deploy`, `APIGroup=apps`, `APIVersion=v1` into a `RequestInfo` stored in context.\n6. **`WithHTTPLogging`** \u2014 prepares access log entry.\n7. **`WithHSTS`**, **`WithCacheControl`** \u2014 add response headers.\n8. **`WithWaitGroup`** \u2014 adds request to `NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup`.\n9. **`WithRequestDeadline`** \u2014 sets `context.WithDeadline` using `RequestTimeout` (default 60s).\n10. **`WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests`** \u2014 spawns goroutine; starts 60-second timer.\n11. **`WithWarningRecorder`** \u2014 attaches `WarningRecorder` to context.\n12. **`WithCORS`** \u2014 handles preflight if needed.\n13. **`WithAuthentication`** \u2014 calls `authenticator.Request.AuthenticateRequest(req)`. On success, deletes `Authorization` header, stores `user.Info` in context via `genericapirequest.WithUser`.\n14. **`WithAudit`** \u2014 evaluates audit policy; emits `StageRequestReceived` event.\n15. **`WithImpersonation`** \u2014 no impersonation headers present; passes through.\n16. **`WithPriorityAndFairness`** (or `WithMaxInFlightLimit`) \u2014 classifies request; may queue it. On admission, proceeds.\n17. **`WithAuthorization`** \u2014 calls `GetAuthorizerAttributes(ctx)` to extract `RequestInfo + user.Info` into `authorizer.Attributes`. Calls `authorizer.Authorize`. On `DecisionAllow`, annotates audit and proceeds.\n\n### 3.3 Director Routing\n\nThe request reaches the `Director`. It finds that `/apis/apps/v1` matches a registered GoRestful WebService prefix. It calls `goRestfulContainer.Dispatch(w, req)`.\n\nGoRestful matches the route `/apis/apps/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/deployments/{name}` with verb GET, and invokes the registered route handler built by `APIInstaller`.\n\n### 3.4 Resource Handler\n\nThe handler is `handlers.GetResource(getter, exporter, scope)`, constructed during `APIInstaller.Install()`. It:\n1. Extracts `namespace` and `name` from the GoRestful path parameters via `scope.Namer`.\n2. Calls `getter.Get(ctx, name, &metav1.GetOptions{})` \u2014 this is `store.Get()` in `registry.Store`.\n3. `store.Get()` calls `s.Storage.Get(ctx, key, opts, obj)` \u2014 the etcd3 storage (possibly via the watch cache).\n4. The storage layer may serve the request from the in-memory watch cache if the resource version requirement permits.\n5. On success, `handlers.GetResource` negotiates the response content type and serializes the object.\n\n### 3.5 Admission on Mutating Requests\n\nFor a `POST /apis/apps/v1/namespaces/default/deployments`, the flow diverges at step 3.4:\n\n1. `handlers.CreateResource` decodes the body into the internal object type.\n2. It calls `createStrategy.PrepareForCreate(ctx, obj)` to apply defaults.\n3. It runs admission via `admissionControl.Admit(ctx, attrs, objInterfaces)` \u2014 the `chainAdmissionHandler` iterates mutating plugins. If any plugin calls `attrs.GetReinvocationContext().SetShouldReinvoke()`, the chain re-runs once.\n4. It calls `createStrategy.Validate(ctx, obj)` for resource-specific validation.\n5. It runs admission via `admissionControl.Validate(ctx, attrs, objInterfaces)` \u2014 validating plugins run.\n6. If all pass, `store.Create(ctx, obj, validateFn, opts)` calls `s.Storage.Create(ctx, key, obj, out, ttl)` to write to etcd.\n\n### 3.6 Audit Completion and Response\n\nAfter the handler returns, `WithAudit` intercepts the captured HTTP status code from the wrapped `ResponseWriter` and emits a `StageResponseComplete` event. `WithWaitGroup` decrements the wait group.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Failure Modes, Limits, and Design Tradeoffs\n\n### 4.1 etcd Contention and `GuaranteedUpdate`\n\n`storage.Interface.GuaranteedUpdate` retries indefinitely on compare-and-swap failures. Under high write contention for a single object, this can cause goroutine pile-up. The `RequestTimeout` (default 60s) bounds total request duration, but tight deadlines on hot objects may produce spurious `504` responses before the update succeeds. The `OptimisticLockErrorMsg` constant (store.go:262) surfaces to API clients as a 409 Conflict.\n\n### 4.2 Watch Cache Staleness\n\nThe watch cache serves most list and watch requests without hitting etcd directly. However, caching introduces lag between a write and when a watch client sees it. The `RequestWatchProgress` method on `storage.Interface` can nudge the watch stream to emit a progress bookmark. List requests with `resourceVersion=0` may return data slightly older than the latest etcd revision; those with `resourceVersion=\"\"` are serialized through etcd for linearizable reads.\n\n### 4.3 Priority-and-Fairness vs MaxInFlight\n\nWhen `FlowControl` is nil, the server uses two fixed semaphores (`MaxRequestsInFlight=400` for non-mutating, `MaxMutatingRequestsInFlight=200` for mutating, per `config.go:443`). This is simple but applies all-or-nothing pressure equally across all callers.\n\nPriority-and-fairness (`WithPriorityAndFairness`) classifies requests into FlowSchemas with per-schema queuing and work estimation. It is significantly more complex, adds CPU overhead for work estimation, and requires running informers to watch `FlowSchema` and `PriorityLevelConfiguration` objects. Disabling it (by not providing `FlowControl`) removes this dynamism but makes the server more predictable under simple load patterns.\n\n### 4.4 Graceful Shutdown Signal Graph\n\n`RunWithContext` (genericapiserver.go:536) maintains a carefully ordered set of lifecycle signals:\n\n```\nctx.Done()\n  \u2192 ShutdownInitiated    (/readyz starts failing; load balancers drain)\n  \u2192 AfterShutdownDelayDuration  (after ShutdownDelayDuration elapses)\n  + PreShutdownHooksStopped\n  \u2192 NotAcceptingNewRequest\n  \u2192 [drain NonLongRunningRequestWaitGroup + WatchRequestWaitGroup]\n  \u2192 InFlightRequestsDrained\n  \u2192 AuditBackend.Shutdown() + HTTP listener stops\n```\n\nA misconfigured or slow pre-shutdown hook delays `NotAcceptingNewRequest`, which in turn delays the HTTP server shutdown. Hooks that make API calls back to the server can deadlock if the server is already refusing new requests.\n\n### 4.5 Request Body Size\n\nThe `MaxRequestBodyBytes` limit (default 3MB, config.go:466) is enforced by the HTTP layer before decoding. Objects that serialize larger than this cannot be created or updated. For CRDs with large embedded JSON, this is a common limit to hit. The value is tied to etcd's recommended object size, since the object is later encoded as protobuf and stored in etcd.\n\n### 4.6 Serial vs Parallel Admission\n\n`chainAdmissionHandler.Admit` (chain.go:31) runs plugins serially and returns on the first error. There is no parallel execution of admission plugins. This keeps the admission order deterministic and avoids races on shared state, but it means a slow plugin (e.g., a webhook with high latency) blocks the entire request. Webhook plugins have their own timeout configuration.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Extension Points and Customization Hooks\n\n### 5.1 `BuildHandlerChainFunc` \u2014 Custom Filter Chains\n\nThe `Config.BuildHandlerChainFunc` field (config.go:186) allows replacing `DefaultBuildHandlerChain` entirely:\n\n```go\nconfig.BuildHandlerChainFunc = func(apiHandler http.Handler, c *server.Config) http.Handler {\n    handler := myCustomMiddleware(apiHandler)\n    return server.DefaultBuildHandlerChain(handler, c)\n}\n```\n\nAn alternative is `BuildHandlerChainWithStorageVersionPrecondition` (config.go:1022), which wraps with `WithStorageVersionPrecondition` before calling the default chain.\n\n**Risk**: Replacing the chain entirely and omitting authentication or authorization bypasses all security controls. The filter order documented in section 2.3 is critical\u2014moving audit outside of authentication, for example, would miss logging auth failures.\n\n### 5.2 `DelegationTarget` \u2014 Server Chaining\n\nCustom servers that need to extend the API can be inserted before `kube-apiserver` in the delegation chain:\n\n```go\nmyServer, _ := myConfig.New(\"my-server\", kubeAPIServer)   // delegates to kube-apiserver\n```\n\nUnmatched requests pass through to the delegate via `UnprotectedHandler()`. The `PrepareRun` call propagates through the chain. This is how `kube-aggregator`, `apiextensions-apiserver`, and `kube-apiserver` form the standard three-layer chain.\n\n**Risk**: The delegate's `UnprotectedHandler()` bypasses the delegating server's filter chain. Any security decision made by the outer server's chain is not re-applied. This is intentional (to avoid double-authenticating) but means the chain ordering must be carefully designed.\n\n### 5.3 `rest.Storage` Interfaces \u2014 Custom Resource Backends\n\nAny type implementing `rest.Storage` can be registered. The full capability set is composed by implementing additional optional interfaces:\n- Add `Getter`, `Lister`, `Creater`, etc. to enable specific verbs\n- Implement `Connecter` for custom HTTP protocols (exec, port-forward)\n- Implement `Redirector` for proxy semantics\n- Implement `StorageWithReadiness` to participate in readiness checks\n\nThe `Store` struct provides a batteries-included implementation. For resources that require non-etcd backends (e.g., aggregated metrics, or resources backed by external systems), implement `storage.Interface` directly or provide a custom `rest.Storage` that calls the external system.\n\n**Risk**: Implementing `Connecter` means the returned `http.Handler` runs within the request context after all filters have run but with no additional protection. If it performs privileged operations, it must implement its own access controls.\n\n### 5.4 `admission.MutationInterface` / `admission.ValidationInterface` \u2014 Admission Plugins\n\nCustom admission plugins register via `admission.RegisterPlugin` and are initialized with `PluginInitializer`. They receive `Attributes` containing the full request context and can:\n- Modify the object in-place (mutating only)\n- Call `attrs.AddAnnotation()` to log metadata into the audit record\n- Trigger re-invocation via `attrs.GetReinvocationContext().SetShouldReinvoke()`\n\nWebhook-based admission (in-tree in `plugin/pkg/admission/webhook/`) uses this same interface, calling external HTTP servers.\n\n**Risk**: Mutating plugins that call back to the same API server via the loopback client can trigger re-entrant admission. In-tree, this is mitigated by exempt service accounts, but custom plugins must take care to avoid infinite loops.\n\n### 5.5 `authenticator.Request` \u2014 Pluggable Authentication\n\nThe `Config.Authentication.Authenticator` field takes any `authenticator.Request` implementation. The standard composition uses `authenticatorunion.New` to chain multiple authenticators (token file, service account JWT, OIDC, client certs, bootstrap tokens). Custom implementations can be prepended.\n\n**Risk**: A custom authenticator that returns a non-nil user for requests it cannot verify will silently allow unauthenticated access with an arbitrary identity.\n\n### 5.6 `authorizer.Authorizer` \u2014 Pluggable Authorization\n\nThe `Config.Authorization.Authorizer` field accepts any `authorizer.Authorizer`. The standard implementation chains RBAC, Node, ABAC, and webhook authorizers. The `DecisionNoOpinion` return value allows chaining: if the first authorizer has no opinion, the next one is consulted.\n\n`GetAuthorizerAttributes` (authorization.go:101) derives attributes from `RequestInfo` and `user.Info` in the context, optionally including field/label selectors when the `AuthorizeWithSelectors` feature gate is enabled.\n\n### 5.7 Post-Start and Pre-Shutdown Hooks\n\n```go\nserver.AddPostStartHook(\"my-component\", func(ctx PostStartHookContext) error {\n    // runs in a goroutine after the server begins listening\n    go myController.Run(ctx.Done())\n    return nil\n})\nserver.AddPreShutdownHook(\"my-component\", func() error {\n    // runs synchronously before NotAcceptingNewRequest is signaled\n    myController.Deregister()\n    return nil\n})\n```\n\nHooks participate in readiness: the `readyz` endpoint fails until all post-start hooks have completed (for hooks registered with the readiness check). `LivezGracePeriod` allows `livez` to return true during startup even if hooks are still running.\n\n### 5.8 `RESTOptionsGetter` \u2014 Storage Configuration\n\n`genericregistry.RESTOptionsGetter.GetRESTOptions(GroupResource, example)` provides storage configuration per resource. Custom implementations can route different resources to different backends, apply different encryption or compaction settings, or inject a storage decorator.\n\n`StoreOptions.AttrFunc` (options.go:58) provides the field extraction function for field selectors on a per-resource basis.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Operational Implications\n\n### 6.1 Performance Considerations\n\n**Serialization cost**: Content negotiation happens per request. JSON serialization is the default; protobuf is significantly smaller and faster for cluster-internal clients. Enabling CBOR (behind `CBORServingAndStorage` feature gate) is an active area of work.\n\n**Watch cache amplification**: Each watch from a client adds a goroutine that fans out events. The server tracks `StorageObjectCountTracker` (registered as a post-start hook) to estimate object sizes for work estimation in priority-and-fairness.\n\n**Admission latency**: In-tree admission plugins are sub-millisecond. Webhook admission adds an HTTP round-trip, typically 5-50ms. The server serializes plugin execution (section 4.6); with 10+ webhooks, admission can dominate request latency.\n\n**MaxInFlight vs Priority-and-Fairness**: The `WithMaxInFlightLimit` semaphores are O(1) to check. Priority-and-fairness (`WithPriorityAndFairness`) classifies requests by inspecting `RequestInfo` and matching against FlowSchemas \u2014 this is more expensive. Avoid registering hundreds of FlowSchemas.\n\n### 6.2 Reliability and Health Probes\n\nThree probe endpoints exist with different semantics:\n- `/healthz` \u2014 server is not broken (ping + basic checks). Used by kubelet restartPolicy.\n- `/livez` \u2014 server should not be restarted. Fails only on catastrophic internal failure. Tolerates `LivezGracePeriod` after startup.\n- `/readyz` \u2014 server can accept traffic. Fails during startup (informers not synced), during shutdown (once `ShutdownInitiated` fires), and if storage is not ready.\n\nThe `StorageReadinessHook` (genericapiserver.go:863) polls `ReadinessCheck()` on all registered storage objects and contributes to `/readyz`. If etcd is unavailable, `/readyz` fails but `/healthz` may still pass.\n\n### 6.3 Graceful Shutdown Timing\n\nThe shutdown sequence (section 4.4) has two user-configurable durations:\n- `ShutdownDelayDuration`: Time after `ShutdownInitiated` before `NotAcceptingNewRequest`. During this window, the server still accepts requests but advertises unhealthy on `/readyz`, allowing load balancers to drain.\n- `ShutdownWatchTerminationGracePeriod`: Maximum time to wait for watch requests to drain. Set to zero (default) to skip watch drain entirely, which is faster but may drop in-progress watches.\n\nSetting `ShutdownSendRetryAfter: true` changes the shutdown behavior: the HTTP listener stays open until all non-long-running requests drain, returning 429 + `Retry-After: 1` for new requests. This prevents clients from seeing `connection refused` but increases the shutdown window.\n\n### 6.4 Maintainability: The Delegation Chain Coupling\n\nThe `DelegationTarget` pattern enables composability but creates tight coupling around hook, health check, and discovery namespace. Adding a hook in the wrong server in the chain may lead to it running multiple times (if both the upstream and delegate register the same name), or not at all (if the server is not in the expected position). The name conflict check in `AddPostStartHook` (config.go:608) is the guard.\n\nThe `MuxAndDiscoveryCompleteSignals` mechanism exists specifically to prevent 404 races during startup: routes registered late in `PrepareRun` would otherwise be absent from the discovery document for a brief window, causing clients to receive 404s for valid resources.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Important Files and Directories\n\n```\nstaging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/server/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 config.go               # Config, RecommendedConfig, DefaultBuildHandlerChain, completedConfig.New\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 genericapiserver.go     # GenericAPIServer, APIGroupInfo, DelegationTarget, PrepareRun, Run\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 handler.go              # APIServerHandler, director (GoRestful \u2194 NonGoRestful routing)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 hooks.go                # PostStartHookFunc, AddPostStartHook, RunPostStartHooks\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 lifecycle.go            # lifecycleSignals (shutdown state machine channel set)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 options/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 recommended.go      # RecommendedOptions (Etcd, Auth, Audit, Admission, etc.)\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 filters/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 maxinflight.go      # WithMaxInFlightLimit\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 priority-and-fairness.go  # WithPriorityAndFairness\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 timeout.go          # WithTimeoutForNonLongRunningRequests\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 waitgroup.go        # WithWaitGroup\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 watch_termination.go  # WithWatchTerminationDuringShutdown\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/endpoints/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 groupversion.go         # APIGroupVersion.InstallREST\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 installer.go            # APIInstaller.Install \u2014 verb \u2192 handler mapping\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 filters/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 authentication.go   # WithAuthentication\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 authorization.go    # WithAuthorization, GetAuthorizerAttributes\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 audit.go            # WithAudit (multi-stage event recording)\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 requestinfo.go      # WithRequestInfo\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 request_deadline.go # WithRequestDeadline\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/registry/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 rest/\n\u2502   \u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 rest.go             # Storage, Getter, Lister, Creater, Updater, GracefulDeleter,\n\u2502   \u2502                           #   Watcher, Connecter, StandardStorage interfaces\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 generic/\n\u2502       \u251c\u2500\u2500 options.go          # RESTOptions, RESTOptionsGetter, StoreOptions\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 registry/\n\u2502           \u2514\u2500\u2500 store.go        # Store (StandardStorage implementation; strategies, hooks)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/storage/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 interfaces.go           # storage.Interface, Versioner, Preconditions, GuaranteedUpdate\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 etcd3/                  # etcd3 storage.Interface implementation\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 cacher/                 # watch-cache wrapper (serves reads from memory)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/admission/\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 interfaces.go           # Attributes, MutationInterface, ValidationInterface,\n\u2502   \u2502                           #   ReinvocationContext, PluginInitializer\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 chain.go                # chainAdmissionHandler (serial chain with re-invocation)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/authentication/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 authenticator/\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 interfaces.go       # authenticator.Request, authenticator.Token, Response\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 pkg/authorization/\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 authorizer/\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 interfaces.go       # authorizer.Authorizer, Attributes, Decision constants\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 pkg/audit/\n    \u2514\u2500\u2500 types.go                # audit.Sink, audit.Backend interfaces\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. Summary of Extension Tradeoffs\n\n| Extension Point | Flexibility | Risk | Primary File |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| `BuildHandlerChainFunc` | Full control over filter order | Easy to skip critical filters | `pkg/server/config.go:186` |\n| `DelegationTarget` | Multi-server chaining | Coupling of hooks, health checks; double-filter risk | `pkg/server/genericapiserver.go:312` |\n| `rest.Storage` interfaces | Per-verb customization | Custom Connecter bypasses filters | `pkg/registry/rest/rest.go` |\n| `registry.Store` strategies | Resource-level defaulting/validation | Strategy bugs persist silently in etcd | `pkg/registry/generic/registry/store.go:173` |\n| `storage.Interface` | Fully custom backend | Must handle versioning, watch, conflict detection | `pkg/storage/interfaces.go:169` |\n| `MutationInterface` | Object mutation before persistence | Re-entrant API calls; ordering dependencies | `pkg/admission/interfaces.go:129` |\n| `ValidationInterface` | Reject invalid state | Serial execution; webhook latency adds up | `pkg/admission/interfaces.go:138` |\n| `authenticator.Request` | Custom credential formats | Wrong implementation leaks identity | `pkg/authentication/authenticator/interfaces.go` |\n| `authorizer.Authorizer` | Custom policy engines | `DecisionNoOpinion` silently passes to next | `pkg/authorization/authorizer/interfaces.go` |\n| Post-start hooks | Async initialization | Slow hooks delay readiness; blocking hooks deadlock | `pkg/server/config.go:596` |\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/documentation.md",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

1. WRITE /workspace/documentation.md
# Kubernetes apiserver Library: Subsystem Architecture and Extension Guide

## 1. Subsystem Purpose and Boundaries

The `k8s.io/apiserver` library (staged at `staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/`) is the reusable framework that powers every Kubernetes API server—both `kube-apiserver` itself and any extension API server built with it (e.g., `sample-apiserver`, aggregated API servers, CRD-backed servers). It is not a standalone binary; it is a library that consumer binaries compose into a running server.

**What it owns:**
- The HTTP handler chain (authentication, authorization, audit, rate-limiting, timeouts)
- The routing layer that maps URL paths to `rest.Storage` implementations
- Storage abstraction over etcd
- Admission control pipeline (mutating + validating)
- Discovery endpoints (`/api`, `/apis`, `/openapi/v2`, `/openapi/v3`)
- Server lifecycle (startup hooks, graceful shutdown, health probes)

**What it does not own:**
- The concrete `rest.Storage` implementations for Kubernetes resources (those live in `pkg/registry/` in the main tree)
- The etcd client itself (provided by `go.etcd.io/etcd/client/v3`)
- RBAC rule data (provided by admission plugins and authorizer implementations in `pkg/auth/`)
- The aggregation layer (in `staging/src/k8s.io/kube-aggregator/`)

**Upstream dependencies:**
- `k8s.io/apimachinery` — runtime types, serialization codecs, scheme, `GroupVersionKind`
- `k8s.io/client-go` — REST client, informers (used by post-start hooks and delegating authenticators)
- `k8s.io/component-base` — feature gates, metrics, tracing
- `github.com/emicklei/go-restful/v3` — the GoRestful container used for `/api` and `/apis` routes

**Downstream consumers:**
- `cmd/kube-apiserver` — composes the library with Kubernetes-specific storage, admission, and auth
- Any agg
... (truncated)
